Driven into a corner by the invading mobile suits of the Principality of Zeon, the Federation Forces developed this melee combat MS based on the development plan known as Operation V, which was intended to turn the tide of the war.
Because it was designed for combat data collection and versatile functionality, it has a Core Block system compatible with the Guncannon and Guntank, Luna Titanium alloy armor, a beam rifle with the power of a warship's main gun, and other technologies that were cutting edge at the time of its creation.
It was among the best of the mobile weapons used during the One Year War.
It was so versatile that it could fight everywhere from space to undersea with almost no modifications, and was even able to enter the atmosphere on its own.
The Gundam and its superior performance continued to pile up victories as the main weapon of the White Base Corps, and it was widely feared by the Zeon Forces, who nicknamed it the "Federation's White Devil."

Driven into a corner by the invading mobile suits of the Principality of Zeon, the Federation Forces developed this medium-range support MS based on the development plan known as Operation V, which was intended to turn the tide of the war.
It is equipped with a Core Block system compatible with the Gundam and Guntank.
Because its primary function is to provide medium-range support during combat, its mobility is inferior to the Gundam's.
However, its armor is thick enough that it does not require a shield, and its weapons include 240mm cannons and a beam rifle.
The prototype proved so effective in the hands of the White Base Corps that a few of them were manufactured and deployed in combat at the end of the war.

Driven into a corner by the invading mobile suits of the Principality of Zeon, the Federation Forces developed this artillery MS based on the development plan known as Operation V, which was intended to turn the tide of the war.
It is equipped with a Core Block system compatible with the Gundam and Guncannon.
The first of the RX series to be developed, it was used to provide rear support to the Gundam and Guncannon.
But because the Federation Forces knew little about MS development at the time, from a technical standpoint it is not a pure mobile suit.
It gives the strong impression of a ground weapon, and uses tank treads rather than legs for its movement.
Originally equipped with two seats, one for a pilot and one for a gunner, it was later remodeled to allow a single person to perform both functions.

Driven into a corner by the invading mobile suits of the Principality of Zeon, the Federation Forces developed this special fighter as part of the MS development plan known as Operation V, which was intended to turn the tide of the war.
It can turn into a Core Block that serves as the piloting core of an RX series prototype mobile suit.
As well as being extremely maneuverable, it is also capable of vertical take-off and landing, and can operate both inside and outside the atmosphere.
It was created as an escape pod for data recovery and pilot survival, and though it could also be used as a fighter, it initially lacked the firepower to handle anti-MS combat.
A Core Booster was later developed to increase its performance.

A support fighter created by attaching a booster to the Federation Forces' Core Fighter, greatly enhancing its firepower and cruising range.
Armed with mega particle cannons powerful enough to destroy a mobile suit with a single shot, it can hold its own even against an MS.
Two prototypes were deployed with the White Base Corps during the One Year War, and were used to support the RX series.

Driven into a corner by the invading mobile suits of the Principality of Zeon, the Federation Forces developed the melee combat MS Gundam based on the development plan known as Operation V, which was intended to turn the tide of the war.
This general-purpose mass-produced MS was created as a simplified version of the Gundam, omitting the Core Block system and other advanced features.
Due to the emphasis placed on mass production during its development, its performance and generator output are relatively low.
Though unable to carry the Gundam's beam rifle, it can use beam weapons despite its low output, and was able to outgun the Principality of Zeon's MS.
At the end of the One Year War, it was mass-produced to serve as the main force in counteroffensive operations, and spawned many different variations as the standard MS of the Federation Forces.
The RGM series would go on to serve as Federation standbys for many years.

This space combat pod was developed as a simple type of mobile suit, to supplement the Federation's predicted shortfall in GM production during the One Year War.
Originally, it was nothing more than military equipment for industrial labor.
But after the decision was made to mass-produce it for combat use, it was fitted with reinforced armor and a Guntank cannon over a series of prototype stages.
Several different variations exist.
This machine uses fuel cells as a power source and does not need to cool down after returning from a mission.
It also has a high-mobility vernier system that allows it to work in tandem with GMs, making it a valuable asset on the battlefield.

The first mass-produced mobile suit of the Principality of Zeon, which served as the basis for the mainline Zaku II that Zeon later developed.
Compared to existing space weapons, its fuel consumption was much lower thanks to the AMBAC effect created by its four limbs.
The introduction of the mobile suit, with its overwhelming space mobility, maneuverability, and versatility, was a radical departure from the weapon concepts of the time.
This machine served as the Principality of Zeon's main unit during the early stages of the One Year War, and was deployed during Operation British as well as in defensive positions towards the end of the war.

This general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit was produced in greater numbers than any model in the Principality of Zeon's Zaku series.
Able to operate both in space and on the ground with minor modifications, it has abundant options in terms of equipment and features superior versatility, extensibility, and ease of production, as well as excellent handling and reliability.
It could be considered the greatest masterpiece in the history of the mobile suit.
Deployed as Zeon's mainline MS during the One Year War, it would continue to see use on the front lines long after successor machines were developed.

This customized machine is a variation of the Principality of Zeon's Zaku II, based on the Zaku II F and remodeled for use by commanders and veteran pilots.
It is painted red to match the personal colors of Char Aznable, an ace also known as the "Red Comet."
Its key features include a head-mounted blade antenna to signify it as a commander type, 30% higher engine output, and increased propellant.
These additions do little to improve the machine's performance, however, and the rumors that it moves an amazing three times faster than normal are largely a result of the skill of its pilot.
It can use a variety of armaments depending on the situation, allowing it to adapt to different types of battle.
In addition to its versatility, extensibility, and ease of production, it also has excellent handling and reliability.

A Principality of Zeon mass-produced ground combat mobile suit, developed from the Zaku II for use under gravity.
It has better mobility and maneuverability than the Zaku II, as well as reinforced armor.
Thanks to its internal sub-thrusters, its movement is also greatly improved, giving it high mobility even in swamps and rocky areas.
The retractable electromagnetic heat rod in its right arm also gives this high-performance machine an advantage in melee combat.
However, as its internal weapons lacked versatility and were difficult to handle, this machine required a certain degree of expertise to pilot.

A Principality of Zeon mass-produced ground combat mobile suit that uses a combination of thermonuclear jets and chemical rockets for hovering movement.
It has far higher mobility than a conventional ground combat MS, and combines that speed with heavy armor to achieve superior performance.
While it was expected to become a mainline ground combat MS, it was ultimately deployed in smaller numbers than the Zaku series, though its excellent basic design led to the creation of a number of variations.
During the One Year War, pre-production units were deployed to the Odessa base, where they engaged in heated battles against the White Base Corps.

A Principality of Zeon mass-produced space mobile suit, in which the heavy ground combat MS Dom was modified for space use by replacing the hover systems with thrusters and the leg engines with thermonuclear rockets.
Though temporarily produced to fill a perceived gap in space fighting strength, its superior handling and ease of production led to it becoming a mainline MS, and it served alongside the Zaku II until the end of the war.

A joint project of the Zeonic, Zimmad, and MIP companies to develop a worthy successor to the Zaku II, the main mobile suit of the Principality of Zeon.
The final product was completed in the final days of the war, and represented the pinnacle of Zeon's MS technology.
It was also the first Zeon MS to truly adopt portable beam weaponry.
Though similar in size to the Dom, its thrust was enhanced, giving it greater maneuverability and mobility.
Its beam weapons also gave the Gelgoog a leg up in both close and long-range combat, allowing it to overwhelm the Federation's mainline MS during the last stages of the One Year War.

As part of a joint project by the Zeonic, Zimmad, and MIP companies to develop a worthy successor to the Zaku II, the main mobile suit of the Principality of Zeon, 25 pre-production Gelgoogs were produced.
One of these was painted red, the personal color of ace Char Aznable, otherwise known as the "Red Comet."
In addition to being the first of Zeon's mobile suits to adopt portable beam weaponry, it also had enhanced thrust, giving it mobility and maneuverability rivaling that of the Federation's strongest MS, the Gundam.

A general-purpose prototype mobile suit developed as part of the Principality of Zeon's Second Stage Main MS Development Plan.
This machine is specialized for melee combat, and carries a custom beam saber and a large shield with built-in weapons.
Highly maneuverable and easy to pilot, it was a candidate to succeed the Zaku II, but ultimately lost out to the Gelgoog and its higher overall performance.
One of the prototypes was customized for use by M'Quve.

This mass-produced amphibious mobile suit, developed by the Principality of Zeon, was the first true undersea MS in history.
Powered by a water-cooled fusion reactor, it was the first MS to be successfully equipped with internal mega particle cannons.
The machine also had heavy armor that allowed it to withstand water pressure, arms that could be retracted into its shoulders to minimize resistance when moving underwater, and a weapon known as a "freeziard" that could disable underwater mines.

This machine was one of the Principality of Zeon's mass-produced amphibious mobile suits.
In addition to being easy to produce thanks to its use of Zaku II components, it featured a two-seater cockpit that made it the ideal machine for amphibious MS training.
While it boasts high maneuverability on land, and enough speed and agility to move while jumping, it suffers compared to other amphibious MS when it comes to cruising range and undersea mobility.
On the other hand, its simple shape, minimal waste-heat generation, and stealth-specialized coloring and coating materials made it difficult to detected with thermal sensors.
For these reasons, the Acguy was often deployed for coastal recon and scouting operations in which secrecy was essential.

An amphibious mobile suit developed by the Principality of Zeon to succeed the Gogg.
This machine is designed to operate both on land and in water, with a hybrid cooling system that combines water and air cooling.
In the water it uses hydrojet rocket engines, and on land it uses thermonuclear jet engines and high-mobility verniers, making it extremely mobile in both environments.

An amphibious mobile suit developed by the Principality of Zeon to succeed the Gogg.
This unit is painted red, the personal color of Zeon ace pilot Char Aznable, also known as the "Red Comet."
Designed to operate both on land and in water, this machine has a hybrid cooling system that combines water and air cooling.
In the water it uses hydrojet rocket engines, and on land it uses thermonuclear jet engines and high-mobility verniers, making it extremely mobile in both environments.
Versatile enough to fight on equal terms with melee combat mobile suits, this S type also features improved reactor output, maneuverability, and armor materials, making it vastly superior to the normal model.

One of the Principality of Zeon's amphibious mobile suit prototypes.
Its shape is different of that of other amphibious MS, making it essentially a hybrid of mobile suit and mobile armor.
In addition to heavy armor all over, it has four mega particle cannons on the front, four on the back, and one on its head.
At the time, its firepower was said to be equal to that of an entire MS company.
However, this also made it the biggest of all amphibious MS, and its weight of over 200 tons rendered walking impossible, meaning that hovering was its only mode of travel over land.
To compensate for its low mobility, it has a special shape that is symmetrical on its front and back.
While it would be hard to call it a mobile weapon, it does function in a similar way to a movable battery.

A Principality of Zeon weapon described as a heavy mobile gun platform, and equipped with an experimental Minovsky craft system that allows it to float for a short period of time.
Its unique shape and four freely-moving legs also make vertical take-off and landing possible.
Armed with twin mega particle cannons above and below, it is also equipped with an anti-MS electromagnetic wave weapon known as the Adzam Leader, and other advanced technology that would become the basis for later mobile armors.
This prototype was deployed to Odessa for combat testing during the One Year War.

One of the Principality of Zeon's earliest prototype mobile armors, it was designed for high speed and firepower.
Utilizing advanced technology like a highly accurate diffuse beam cannon and arm-mounted heat sickles as its primary weapons, this machine possessed incredible combat capabilities for a prototype.
However, problems with its maneuverability and acceleration led to it being abandoned before it could be tested.

A mass-produced mobile armor developed by the Principality of Zeon for high-speed space combat.
Its powerful thrusters gave it superior mobility to anything else available at the time of the One Year War.
It was equipped with a number of different weapons, including quadruple missile launchers, a huge mega particle cannon, and sharp claw arms capable of seizing enemy machines.
With its high fighting strength, it proved the effectiveness of mobile armors in outer space.

A prototype heavy mobile armor developed by the Principality of Zeon for anti-fortress combat.
It was exceptionally large compared to the other mobile weapons of the One Year War.
An extremely powerful mega particle cannon was installed in its center, flanked by another 28 smaller cannons giving omnidirectional coverage.
It was also the first mobile weapon to be equipped with beam-deflecting I-Field generators, giving it incredible offensive and defensive performance.
This required an extremely high power output, however, making cooling a problem and limiting the amount of time it could remain in the field.
This mobile armor was designed for three pilots, but its control system could be switched over to a single pilot.

The first Principality of Zeon mobile armor for use by Newtypes, equipped with psycommu weapons.
It was scaled up to the size of an assault craft in order to accommodate the psycommu system, and separated into three blocks so that damaged sections could be detached to reduce the danger of explosions.
This increased the chances of recovering data and pilots.
Equipped with special Newtype weapons, it can perform all-range attacks, using wired psycommu to control its mega particle cannons and hit the enemy from every angle.
Because the machine can't be completely controlled using psycommu, it performs best with multiple pilots on board.

A prototype mobile armor for Newtypes, operated almost entirely via the psycommu system.
This was the first machine built completely for Newtype use.
It carries 10 unmanned attack terminals known as "bits," each with a mono-eye and a mega particle cannon.
Newtypes can use their psycho-waves to guide the bits from afar, performing all-range attacks.
However, this requires exceptionally high Newtype abilities.
Its first use in battle was an attack on the Sea of Solomon, in which it obliterated many Federation ships by making all-range attacks from beyond the Federation Forces' detection range, causing their soldiers to fear the "Ghost of Solomon."

A psycommu-equipped mobile suit developed at the end of the One Year War to be the final word in MS technology.
It was named the Zeong after its nation of origin.
It had 5-barrel mega particle cannons on each arm, which could be controlled via wired psycommu to perform all-range attacks.
This wired control allowed even ordinary pilots without Newtype abilities to operate the machine to some degree.
The head contains both a cockpit escape pod and a mega particle cannon, allowing it to fight independently of the body.
However, these additions were only stopgap measures to increase the survival rate of its invaluable Newtype pilots.
When the "Red Comet," Char Aznable, received the Zeong during the Battle of A Baoa Qu, its legs and the armor on its arms were incomplete, making the Zeong only 80% complete when it was first thrust into combat.

This is the main battle tank of the Principality of Zeon, but Zeon's lack of know-how regarding the development of ground combat vehicles is apparent in its unique appearance and capabilities.
It has a distinct form with an unusually elevated turret.
This turret, known as the Magella Top, can separate from the rest of the vehicle and is capable of independent flight.
But the meager remaining weaponry of the Magella Base, and the short flight time, poor stability, and reduced firing accuracy of the airborne Magella Top, make this an extremely unwieldy weapon.
On the battlefield, it largely served as support for MS and infantry, and had some degree of success in ground combat.

A Principality of Zeon atmospheric fighter, developed in the colonies to ensure air superiority during the Earth invasion operation.
Because the Principality of Zeon lacked know-how in developing terrestrial aircraft, this fighter's poor aerodynamics had to be forcefully supplemented with attitude control verniers in order to compensate for its poor balance.
On the other hand, the cockpit's elevated position allows for a favorable field of vision, and its mobility is very high.
Used mainly as a carrier-based fighter for the Gaw, it formed the core of Zeon's terrestrial aerial power.

A Principality of Zeon atmospheric fighter, developed in the colonies to ensure air superiority during the Earth invasion operation.
Because the Principality of Zeon lacked know-how in developing terrestrial aircraft, this fighter's poor aerodynamics had to be forcefully supplemented with attitude control verniers in order to compensate for its poor balance.
On the other hand, the cockpit's elevated position allows for a favorable field of vision, and its mobility is very high.
The machine used by Garma Zabi, the Earth Occupation Force commander, was painted in his personal brown colors and had slightly better performance than the standard version.

A Principality of Zeon space fighter produced in low numbers, whose tandem cockpits also serve as escape capsules capable of propulsion for brief periods.
Equipped with multiple attitude control verniers, it has high mobility, but lacks the efficiency of the AMBAC system used in mobile suits and cannot remain in combat for long periods of time.
For this reason, among others, it was deployed and used mainly for base defense.

The main battle tank used by the Federation Ground Force, armed with twin 155mm smoothbore cannons, and featuring a unique form thanks to its two-barreled gun turret.
Already obsolete by the time of the One Year War, it suffered heavy losses at the hands of Principality of Zeon mobile suits.
However, with the right tactics, this high-performance tank was still capable of destroying a Zaku II.
Because it was formally adopted in U.C.0061, it was dubbed the Type 61.

The result of a desire for the perfection of a humanoid mobile weapon equipped with a compact fusion reactor, the previously developed MS-04 Bugu ultimately proved too expensive to mass produce.
Owing to these circumstances, the mobile suit was redesigned for mass production and combat deployment, becoming the MS-05 Zaku I.

The improved version of the MS-06F Zaku II piloted by Char Aznable at the Battle of Loum.
This commander type mobile suit is painted Char's signature red, and its head is adorned with a decorative horn.
Despite the associated risks, Char arranged for its limiter to be removed, allowing him to utilize the unit's thrust capabilities to their fullest.

A close combat MS developed by the EFF.
It is equipped with beam weapons and its backpack can be mounted with a shoulder cannon.
In THE ORIGIN, the Gundam initially did not have a Core Block System until it was upgraded and installed in Jaburo.

The RGM-79KC GM Interceptor Custom, developed to defend fleets, equipped with the additional weapon unit, Fellow Booster, on its back.
The Fellow Booster serves to both expand the operational range of mobile suits in outer space and enhance their firepower.

Under the FSWS Project, new developments were being aggressively pursued even for mobile suit weapons.
From these efforts, the Frame Launcher, a composite weapon formed by combining a gatling gun with a missile launcher, became the Heavy Gundam's main weapon.
In addition, a new beam rifle and hyper bazooka were also developed.

A general-purpose prototype mobile suit developed by the Federation Forces, this was Unit 1 of the RX-78 Gundam produced under Operation V.
Its core capabilities are identical to those of Unit 2.
During testing in Side 7, the unit was severely damaged in an assault by a Zeon mobile suit squad. To maintain secrecy, it was later destroyed by allied forces.
However, the operational data, including beam weapon data, gathered from this unit was incorporated into Unit 2.

The third Gundam built under the Federation Forces mobile suit development plan known as Operation V.
It was targeted and partially destroyed in an ambush by Zeon special forces while being evacuated from Side 7 along with the rest of the RX series.
It is thought that the White Base retrieved the machine to salvage it for spare parts.
After this, however, it was improved with the addition of a new learning computer and a laser accelerator for its thermonuclear reactor.
The end result was a machine fully upgraded for Newtypes.
After serving as a testbed for magnet coating, it was given a standard loadout and stationed aboard a Pegasus-class ship.
Exact details of this Gundam's service record are largely unknown.

Essentially an early GM unit upgraded for seasoned pilots.
It sports a visor to protect the head sensors during close combat, as well as extra thrusters and enhanced armor and armament.
The result is a great increase in overall fighting ability, not just sniping.
Because this was a high-class type customized for skilled pilots, its cost was significant.
Only a few were produced and deployed, some of which defended Jaburo during the Gryps Conflict.

The F Type is a variation of the Zaku II developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War.
This machine was customized for the personal use of Space Strike Fleet commander Dozle Zabi.
Famously large in stature, Dozle had the cockpit expanded, and ordered a slew of meticulous tune-ups.
During the Battle of Solomon, a Federation Forces attack is thought to have incinerated this machine in its hangar.

A variation of the Zaku II developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War.
This is a later version of the Zaku II R, which was praised by many ace pilots for its high performance.
However, its complex operational requirements and high cost prevented mass production, resulting in these machines being rarer than F types.
The MS-06R-2 version inherits the Zaku exterior, but records indicate that the interior utilizes many Gelgoog components.
Its advanced specs make it the pinnacle of the Zaku series.
This particular unit belongs to Johnny Ridden, also known as the "Crimson Lightning."
The red color scheme reflects the pilot's personal colors.

A specialized combat mobile suit developed by the Principality of Zeon for an attack on Jaburo.
Based on a design from the prototype stage of the amphibious MS Acguy, it has been redesigned for this purpose.
In addition to two heat rods on each arm for close combat, it also has a compound mono-eye camera composed of countless sensor arrays for scoping out enemies.
Note that the heat rods in its arms can be exchanged for the iron nails used by the Z'Gok.

One of 25 pre-production units of the Gelgoog, a Principality of Zeon general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit.
Belonging to "Crimson Lightning" Johnny Ridden, this machine sports a striking red coat of paint, and is equipped with a high-mobility backpack that lets it execute hit-and-run tactics with lethal efficiency.

A simple mass-produced mobile suit for support and anti-air use, developed under the Principality of Zeon's Pezun Project during the One Year War.
With three wheels instead of leg units, it is virtually a movable battery.
The wheel units can be replaced by high-mobility vernier types, enabling use in space.
Its no-frills design makes it relatively cheap to build, but despite being slated for mass-production, it was assigned exclusively to base defense, meaning that only a handful of units ever saw the light of day.

A general-purpose prototype mobile suit developed under the Principality of Zeon's Pezun Project.
Based on the Gelgoog, it was designed for higher performance and incorporated the close combat capabilities of the melee-specialized Gyan, the Gelgoog's main rival for selection as a next-generation mainline MS.
Two versions were developed, the A type for ground combat and the B type for space.
The A type was lighter and had stronger thrusters to enable atmospheric flight, as well as thermonuclear jet engines on its arms.
The hope was to make multi-dimensional mobile combat possible, but the final product could not realize the planned specs, and development was canceled.

A prototype assault mobile suit that was developed on asteroid base Pezun.
The Gasshia proved to be an effective amphibious machine in close-quarter combat and soon saw rapid deployment to the field.
Its unique Hammer Gun was often called the Mountain Blitzer.

A prototype mobile suit created by Zimmad, in hopes that it would be formally adopted as the mainline MS of the Principality of Zeon.
It was originally developed as the EMS-04 Zudah, a high-performance machine with excellent mobility, which employed a Saturn engine that could run on extremely heavy propellants such as lead, zinc, and bismuth.
Together with the AMBAC system, this high thrust and acceleration enabled it to make extremely rapid turns, which ironically exceeded the machine's limits and caused it to break up in mid-air during an official test flight.
This disaster led to the EMS-04's official rejection.
However, it underwent structural improvements and was resurrected as the supposedly all-new EMS-10 Zudah, a high-performance machine far surpassing the Zaku.
Tragically, the defects of the original design could not be resolved, and it is not even certain whether the planned upgrade work was actually carried out.
Four prototype units were stationed aboard the test support ship Jotunheim for assessment by the 603rd Technical Evaluation Unit.
The ill-fated machine broke apart yet again, ending all chances of mass production for good.
This is the first unit, assigned to Lt.
Commander Jean Luc Duvall.
The horn on its head indicates that it is a commander machine.

A prototype mobile tank developed by the Principality of Zeon.
Though covered in thick armor, this behemoth can reach speeds of 110 km/hour.
Its 30cm cannon is capable of firing a variety of rounds, including APFSDS and incendiary shells, giving it show-stopping offensive muscle.
It could truly be described as a super-dreadnought tank.
Its upper section can change into mobile suit form.
This lets it use its shovel arm units for melee combat, and it can carry weapons such as a Zaku machine gun for close combat against MS.
There were high hopes for its great fighting strength.
However, the top military brass decided not to adopt the machine after its initial functional evaluation.
Two years later, the 603rd Technical Evaluation Unit tested it on Earth, but the vehicle was never recovered afterwards.
This field deployment was therefore actually a form of disposal.

An experimental fusion plasma cannon for use in fleet battles, developed by the Principality of Zeon to vaporize enemy warships from outside their firing range.
Its 200-meter barrel, lined with supplementary injectors, fires a plasmoid discharge with an output of 150,000 kW, enough to destroy a Federation Magellan-class battleship with a single shot.
Its maximum range under Minovsky particle interference is approximately 300 km.
Even at the outer limits of this range, the Jormungand is ten times more powerful than the largest warship gun.
High hopes were held for this weapon, leading to field testing by the 603rd Technical Evaluation Unit during the Battle of Loum.
Problems arose, however, one being the high susceptibility of the superheated plasma shots to magnetic interference.
A spotter unit was needed to relay target data for precise aiming.
The other problem was the introduction of the mobile suit, a humanoid weapon that the military was also developing at the same time.
This ultimately deprived the Jormungand of a chance to prove its worth, and it was never fired in battle again.

A prototype mobile suit created by Zimmad, in hopes that it would be formally adopted as the mainline MS of the Principality of Zeon.
It was originally developed as the EMS-04 Zudah, a high-performance machine with excellent mobility, which employed a Saturn engine that could run on extremely heavy propellants such as lead, zinc, and bismuth.
Together with the AMBAC system, this high thrust and acceleration enabled it to make extremely rapid turns, which ironically exceeded the machine's limits and caused it to break up in mid-air during an official test flight.
This disaster led to the EMS-04's official rejection.
However, it underwent structural improvements and was resurrected as the supposedly all-new EMS-10 Zudah, a high-performance machine far surpassing the Zaku.
Tragically, the defects of the original design could not be resolved, and it is not even certain whether the planned upgrade work was actually carried out.
Four prototype units were stationed aboard the test support ship Jotunheim for assessment by the 603rd Technical Evaluation Unit.
The ill-fated machine broke apart yet again, ending all chances of mass production for good.

A giant mobile armor made using parts from a super-dreadnought MA developed by the Principality of Zeon, connected to the mass-produced MA Bigro that was created for high-speed space combat.
The ammunition depot in its hull section can supply weapons and ammunition to mobile pods on the front lines, and perform emergency maintenance so that they can fight continuously.
Its armament includes the high-output mega particle cannon and claw arms of the Bigro core unit, while the hull section known as the "Ad Unit" can deploy beam-disrupting screens and fire anti-ship missiles.
This machine displayed great efficacy in chaotic battlefields.
But with a height of 138 meters and a length of 203 meters, its maneuverability was poor, and it had difficulty dealing with attacks from any direction other than directly ahead.

A mobile pod developed for space combat by the Principality of Zeon as the tide of war started to take an unfavorable turn.
It was introduced as a decisive battle weapon to fill the gap left by the painful shortage of mobile suits and veteran pilots at the end of the One Year War.
This machine makes use of existing parts and weapons such as the Zaku machine gun, thereby keeping production costs down.
Its control system is also relatively easy to master, and so it is used mainly by new recruits and rookie pilots.
Though its performance was said to trump that of the Federation Forces mobile pod Ball, its mobility and armor were inferior to those of a mobile suit, and it could not completely fill the gap caused by Zeon's MS shortage.

A "mobile diver system" and its corresponding control unit, developed by the Principality of Zeon to launch surprise attacks from satellite orbit.
The control unit is based on the amphibious mobile suit Z'Gok, which has exceptional pressure and shock resistance and is equipped with hybrid thermonuclear engines.
One arm is replaced by a sensor arm so that it can control the hastily constructed Logistics Weapon Container (LWC).
For the third test conducted by the 603rd Technical Evaluation Unit, the machine was fitted with a Kuhblume diffuse beam cannon, and then dropped into the stratosphere to intercept an enemy fleet that was being launched from Jaburo.
Though using this weapon for more than five seconds risked destroying the machine, it succeeded in downing five enemy ships.
After detaching the Kuhblume, however, the control unit lost all its fighting capabilities.
At this point, a Federation attack sent the Ze'Gok hurtling into the sea.

A joint project of the Zeonic, Zimmad, and MIP companies to develop a worthy successor to the Zaku II, the main mobile suit of the Principality of Zeon.
The final product was completed in the final days of the war, and represented the pinnacle of Zeon's MS technology.
It was also the first Zeon MS to truly adopt portable beam weaponry.
Though similar in size to the Dom, its thrust was enhanced, giving it great maneuverability and mobility.
Its beam weapons also gave the Gelgoog a leg up in both close and long-range combat, allowing it to overwhelm the Federation's mainline MS during the last stages of the One Year War.
Captain Herbert Von Kuspen brought this machine aboard the test support ship Jotunheim.
Painted in grey colors, it has a high-mobility backpack and a blade antenna on its head to indicate that it is a commander machine.
A mobile suit with an exterior designed to resemble those of the enemy Federation Forces' GMs, it is used on special missions.

Meant to improve the anti-MS capabilities of the RTX-44 which served as a prototype for the Guntank, this machine boasts far more weaponry than the base machine, enabling a wider variety of attacks.
Its most distinctive feature is the ability to change into assault gun form by deploying its front sub-crawler while sliding its upper body to achieve a forward-bent posture.
This gives it mobility surpassing that of most mobile suits.
However, its development was halted after data on it was leaked to the Principality of Zeon.
This prevented mass production and forced the Federation Forces to self-destruct the remaining prototypes in order to maintain secrecy.

A ground combat mobile suit produced using surplus Gundam parts as the Federation Forces were rushing to deploy MS in battle.
This was one of the first prototypes to emerge from the early stages of the MS mass production plan.
Because experimental mass production of this model took place before operational data on the Gundam had been collected, space combat equipment was omitted from its design, limiting the environments in which it was able to operate.
Even so, under gravity, this high-performance machine was said to have specs rivaling those of the Gundam.
The fact that it was made with surplus Gundam parts led to inconsistent performance, and while a limiter was installed to reduce these discrepancies, the result was performance less than that of the Gundam.
Thus it also had a "MAX Mode" system, which could disengage the limiter to maximize the machine's performance.
This machine was deployed on the front lines in Southeast Asia as part of the Kojima Battalion's 08th MS Team, and so the digits "08" are printed on its shield.

Because much of the armor and internal parts of the Federation Forces' Gundam Ground Type were recycled or reproduced from surplus Gundam parts, it lacked a stock of replacement parts, and so bases often had to make field repairs using parts from other machines and equipment.
For example, when Karen Joshua's machine lost its head in battle with an Acguy, head parts from a GM Ground Type were used to repair it.

This one-off remodel was created using various parts, including some from a GM Ground Type, to repair a Gundam Ground Type that was heavily damaged during an attempt to capture the Principality of Zeon's Apsaras II.
The Gundam Ground Type was experimentally produced in small numbers during the early stages of the Federation Forces' MS mass production plan.
Because much of its armor and internal parts were recycled or reproduced from surplus Gundam parts, it lacked a stock of replacement parts, and so bases often had to make field repairs using parts from other machines and equipment.
When Shiro Amada's Gundam Ground Type was incapacitated in the battle against the Apsaras II, his ideas were combined with the combat data collected up to that point to perform a complete overhaul, in the hope of improving the original machine's ground combat performance.
Ez8, or "Extra-Zero-Eight," is not an official model number.
This refers to the "81" on the machine's chest armor, which represents the first unit of the 08th MS Team, or in other words the commander's machine.

This one-off remodel was created using various parts, including some from a GM Ground Type, to repair a Gundam Ground Type that was heavily damaged during an attempt to capture the Principality of Zeon's Apsaras II.
The Gundam Ground Type was experimentally produced in small numbers during the early stages of the Federation Forces' MS mass production plan.
Because much of its armor and internal parts were recycled or reproduced from surplus Gundam parts, it lacked a stock of replacement parts, and so bases often had to make field repairs using parts from other machines and equipment.
When Shiro Amada's Gundam Ground Type was incapacitated in the battle against the Apsaras II, his ideas were combined with the combat data collected up to that point to perform a complete overhaul, in the hope of improving the original machine's ground combat performance.
Ez8, or "Extra-Zero-Eight," is not an official model number.
This refers to the "81" on the machine's chest armor, which represents the first unit of the 08th MS Team, or in other words the commander's machine.

A ground combat mobile suit manufactured by the Federation Forces using the production lines of the Gundam Ground Type.
This version was modified with special weaponry and coloring.
Because its intended role was different of that of other machines, it was given the name "Sniper."
But rather than being specially customized, it was merely tuned to match its primary weapon, the long-range beam rifle.
This rifle gave it firepower equal to that of a warship's main gun.
With an extremely long range exceeding that of existing beam rifles, this proved a very formidable weapon.
But because the energy that passed through the body of the machine exceeded the limit estimates, it required a heat sink with special energy circuits that could cool the body and barrel, and it was tuned with this in mind.

A pre-production version of the Federation Forces general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit GM.
It was experimentally produced using the production lines at Luna II.
Because this machine was developed as a prototype GM, its backpack has no beam saber, and its specifications are very different from the mass-produced model.
The few units that were produced were deployed to various Federation Forces facilities.

The Type K is a variation of the space combat pod Ball, which the Federation Forces developed as a simple type of mobile suit during the One Year War.
It had four manipulators instead of two, and was carried aboard cargo ships for both work and defense.
Its overall performance fell short of a mobile suit, however, and soldiers in the field mocked it as a "moving coffin."
Because the weapon on top of its body could be replaced, some types were equipped with a 180mm cannon instead of the standard fifteen-caliber.

One of the improved variations of the Principality of Zeon's mass-produced ground combat mobile suit Gouf produced in the middle and late stages of the One Year War.
This machine boasted vastly improved long-range offense, maneuverability, and maintenance.
The 5-barrel 75mm machine gun in its left hand was changed to a normal manipulator, while the right hand's built-in heat rod was wired to reduce overall weight.
The reduced firepower could be supplemented with a triple 35mm gatling cannon, a gatling shield able to mount a heat saber, and other optional equipment that made the machine a force to be reckoned with in ground combat.
The few units produced were primarily given to ace pilots, who used them to great success on the battlefield.

One of the improved variations of the Principality of Zeon's mass-produced ground combat mobile suit Gouf produced in the middle and late stages of the One Year War.
This machine boasted vastly improved long-range offense, maneuverability, and maintenance.
The 5-barrel 75mm machine gun in its left hand was changed to a normal manipulator, while the right hand's built-in heat rod was wired to reduce overall weight.
The reduced firepower could be supplemented with a triple 35mm gatling cannon, a gatling shield able to mount a heat saber, and other optional equipment that made the machine a force to be reckoned with in ground combat.
The few units produced were primarily given to ace pilots, who used them to great success on the battlefield.

One of the flying variations of the Principality of Zeon's mass-produced ground combat mobile suit Gouf.
This machine was the culmination of a long-held plan to create a mobile suit capable of flight.
The Gouf's legs and waist are fitted with thermonuclear jet engines, giving it stable flight performance.
It is also equipped with a gatling shield able to mount a heat saber and other optional weapons in a bid to improve its long-distance aerial attacks.
But because its cruising range and mobility left much to be desired, the MS flight plan was suspended after only a few units had been produced.
The problems of increased mobility and operating radius were resolved by the Dom, whose thermonuclear hovering provided greater speed of movement.

When development of the Gelgoog was delayed, the Principality of Zeon adapted the Dom for space use as a replacement next-generation mainline mobile suit, and this test machine was developed to acquire the necessary data.
Intended to test the mobility and maintenance issues of remodeling the legs with thermonuclear rocket engines, it was thrown into battle in Federation territory near Earth to collect data on how it would fare in combat against GMs.
The data it collected was lost, however, after it sustained heavy damage in a fight against Shiro Amada's Ball Type K.

A prototype mobile armor for fortress attacks, developed in absolute secrecy by the Principality of Zeon.
This massive weapon was developed to attack the Federation headquarters at the Jaburo base.
While the Apsaras I was an unarmed flying test machine, the Apsaras II was created to test the compatibility of the Minovsky craft system and the mega particle cannon.
It was believed that combining the high-flying capabilities of the Minovsky craft system with the tremendous destructive power of a large mega particle cannon would prove devastating to enemy defenses.

Technical Rear Admiral Ginius Sakhalin completed the final Apsaras model in spite of the order to cease production.
The unresolved issues with power shortages in the Minovsky craft system of the Apsaras I and II were solved so effectively with the inclusion of three Rick Dom generators that it was possible to add a second Minovsky craft system.
The three legs that extended from underneath the machine improved stability when firing, while the firepower of the large mega particle cannon could be adjusted in a way that would allow it to be used like a multi-barrel laser.
These improvements resulted in a machine that was completely different from the previous Apsaras models.

A combat helicopter used by the Principality of Zeon.
Its armaments included autocannons and wire-guided missiles.
These craft were called upon to defend a pillbox base when it was attacked by the Federation's 08th MS team.

A ground combat mobile suit manufactured by the Federation Forces using the production lines of the Gundam Ground Type.
This was a precursor to the RGM-79 GM that later became the Federation's mainline machine, and was given the GM name even though its construction was completely different.
Though most of its equipment was the same as that of a Gundam Ground Type, it lacked a chest vulcan and backpack, and was given a lower-output generator.
By retaining the Luna Titanium armor, however, it provided an overall performance advantage over the Federation's mainline GM.
Furthermore, because this machine was created after the Federation had already established a production format, spare parts were readily available, making both maintenance and the supply of parts to each team easier.
Large numbers were deployed on the Odessa front and in the attack on the Apsaras development base.

A mass-produced version of the Federation Forces prototype mobile suit Guntank.
It omitted the Core Block system of the original, in favor of a rotating upper body.
It also had a more efficient ammunition feed for its 120mm cannons, making it better suited for its original role of providing long-range support to front-line MS teams.
Though it was called a mass production type, this machine was not produced in great numbers, though a few were deployed to bombard tunnels hidden in the mountains during the attack on the Apsaras development base.

One of these combat support vehicles was assigned to every Federation Ground Force mobile suit team.
Its main mission was to provide rear support, perform enemy recon and gather data.
Its most distinctive feature is its sonar system, used to detect foes based on the sounds transmitted through the ground.
Even in the presence of Minovsky particles, which disrupt radio waves and neutralize radar, this can determine enemy positions at long distances, enabling early warning and precise tactical planning.
It is also equipped with a retractable antenna, a rotating periscope, and a rear storage rack for carrying ammunition and extra weapons.

A high-performance Gundam-type mobile suit, developed at the Augusta base on Earth as part of the Federation Forces' next-generation development plan known as the G-4 Project.
Its drive and control systems incorporate the very latest technology for use by Newtypes.
The entire drive system uses magnet coating technology, and a panoramic monitor provides a wider field of view during visual-range combat.
It also features a linear seat that reduces damage from impact and vibration, reducing the strain on the pilot.
Originally, this machine was slated for assignment to the 13th Independent Unit, rumored to be a Newtype corps.
However, it was attacked by a Zeon special forces team during its final testing, resulting in its partial destruction and preventing its deployment.

A high-performance Gundam-type mobile suit, developed at the Augusta base on Earth as part of the Federation Forces' next-generation development plan known as the G-4 Project.
Its drive and control systems incorporate the very latest technology for use by Newtypes.
The entire drive system uses magnet coating technology, and a panoramic monitor provides a wider field of view during visual-range combat.
It also features a linear seat that reduces damage from impact and vibration, reducing the strain on the pilot.
Furthermore, as part of the extra armament plan known as the FSWS Project, a set of Chobham armor was created as optional equipment for this machine.
This bulletproof armor includes layers of titanium alloy sheeting and nylon micro-mesh, sandwiched between steel plating.
The armor detaches as it is destroyed, thus absorbing more of the impact.

The Federation Forces developed the medium-range support mobile suit Guncannon based on the development plan known as Operation V, which was intended to turn the tide of the war.
This mass-produced type omits the Core Block system, uses components from the standard GM series, and changes the armor materials to titanium ceramic composite.
This greatly reduces costs, while retaining the powerful bombardment capabilities and sturdy armor of the base machine.
Though it has no melee weapons, this machine performs very well as a support MS.
By the end of the war, direct combat between MS had become the norm, and so support machines like this one were gradually being phased out.
Although it was a mass production type, only a few were ever produced.

One of the GM variations developed by the Federation Forces at the end of the One Year War. 
To meet the pressing need for better mobility, sweeping design changes were made, resulting in the simultaneous creation of the G type for ground use and the GS type for space use.
Designed mainly for the specialized task of base defense, its performance is tailored for local warfare, with the absolute minimum of required functionality. 
One of its distinctive features is a laser communications system, which allows multiple machines to coordinate despite Minovsky particle interference.
This GS type was originally meant to operate in relatively large areas of space, including areas around colonies.
However, its operating time at maximum output was quite short, which often prevented it from fully realizing its potential.

The final variation of the Federation Forces general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit GM to be developed during the One Year War.
Based on the GM Command series, this custom machine is enhanced to the very limit, making it the pinnacle of the GM family.
Its head visor contains a combination optical sensor and precision targeting laser.
This also serves to protect the main camera, cool the head unit, and reduce noise levels to enhance long-range sniping.
The machine is not designed exclusively for sniping, however.
Instead, it is meant to improve overall performance while retaining versatility.
In terms of catalog specs, the performance of this machine was even higher than that of the Gundam.
However, it was only completed at the end of the war, and very few units were produced.
Its production costs were also extremely high for a mass-produced machine, and it had few direct successors aside from testbeds for evaluation purposes.

The final variation of the mass-produced mobile suit Zaku II, developed by the Principality of Zeon at the end of the One Year War as part of the United Maintenance Plan.
While it boasts thruster output rivaling that of the S type used by ace pilots, its handling is similar to that of the F type, making this an underrated masterpiece.
Because its propellant capacity is unchanged, however, its operating time at maximum thrust is halved.
This machine retains the Zaku II's famed versatility and ease of maintenance, and it is even compatible with the components used in Federation machines.
This makes it possible to take requests from pilots into account, leading to unique customizations at deployment sites, such as machines with extra armor added to their heads.
Because it rolled out at the end of the One Year War, very few units were actually produced or deployed, let alone used in missions.

A redesigned version of the space combat mobile suit Rick Dom, developed by the Principality of Zeon at the end of the One Year War as part of the United Maintenance Plan.
A number of improvements have been made with space operations in mind, including structural changes to remove superfluous aerodynamic elements.
As well as a simpler production process, increased durability, enhanced thruster output, and various other basic improvements, the machine has also become much easier to control, placing less strain on its pilot.
Because the war ended before mass production could begin in earnest, very few units were actually produced or deployed, but its high performance led to its continued use long after the One Year War.

A variation of the general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit Gelgoog, which was developed by the Principality of Zeon at the end of the One Year War.
This version was redesigned as part of the United Maintenance Plan, preserving the high mobility of the original Gelgoog while upgrading it for precision shooting.
The result was a machine with extremely high performance in terms of both mobility and firing accuracy.
Intended for use by commanders, this machine is equipped with a higher-performance fire control system and a generator with increased output.
The rear skirt contains a set of vernier units and many attitude control thrusters, while propellant tanks can be attached to its backpack for extended operating time at maximum thrust.
These tanks are removed during combat under gravity, where they would actually decrease the machine's maneuverability instead.

A high-performance assault mobile suit produced in small numbers at the end of the One Year War, as part of the Principality of Zeon's United Maintenance Plan.
This is the E type (for "einhauen," or "assault"), designed to fight alone against other MS and break through the enemy's defensive lines.
Its basic concept is to perform hit-and-run attacks using the powerful vernier thrusters all over its body, eventually breaking through the enemy's lines and capturing their base.
Thus it is designed for maximum mobility and maneuverability, but its lightweight construction leaves much to be desired in the armor department.
Thanks to its block construction, the machine can be assembled in a simple facility, and so it was assigned mainly to special forces who smuggled it into enemy territory and used it for reconnaissance and sabotage operations.
The name "Kampfer" is German for "fighter."

A high-performance assault mobile suit produced in small numbers at the end of the One Year War, as part of the Principality of Zeon's United Maintenance Plan.
This is the E type (for "einhauen," or "assault"), designed to fight alone against other MS and break through the enemy's defensive lines.
Its basic concept is to perform hit-and-run attacks using the powerful vernier thrusters all over its body, eventually breaking through the enemy's lines and capturing their base.
Thus it is designed for maximum mobility and maneuverability, but its lightweight construction leaves much to be desired in the armor department.
Thanks to its block construction, the machine can be assembled in a simple facility, and so it was assigned mainly to special forces who smuggled it into enemy territory and used it for reconnaissance and sabotage operations.
The name "Kampfer" is German for "fighter."

A comprehensively redesigned version of the mass-produced amphibious mobile suit Gogg, developed by the Principality of Zeon at the end of the One Year War as part of the United Maintenance Plan. 
It features a host of major alterations, including a sweeping reduction in weight. This cuts a large contrast with its heavily armored ancestor, allowing the Hy-Gogg to maneuver far better both on land and undersea.
Its armament has also been improved, and the beam cannons in its arms are capable of rapid fire thanks to the use of energy capacitor technology. 
Optional equipment includes hand missile units for the arms and a back-mounted jetpack for water take-offs. 
These features give the Hy-Gogg better performance in nearly every respect.
Because it was developed at the end of the One Year War,only a handful of units were produced, and it was usedmainly by special forces teams.

A redesigned version of the mass-produced amphibious mobile suit Z'Gok, developed by the Principality of Zeon at the end of the One Year War as part of the United Maintenance Plan.
Designed for lower cost, it also features more efficient internal construction and integrates many new technologies to improve on the base machine's performance.
Its ground combat and melee capabilities have received further polish, and for an amphibious machine, it displays superb close combat strength on land.
The beam cannons in its arms are capable of rapid fire thanks to the use of energy capacitor technology, further increasing its fighting strength.
Because it was developed at the end of the One Year War, only a handful of units were produced, and it was used mainly by special forces teams.

The mobile suit that ended up as the prototype (early development model) for the MS-18E Kampfer.
The MS-18E inherited not only the "raid" concept and block construction of this model, but also its hefty thruster equipment.
Differences included the shape of the shoulder, chest and head sections (including the antenna), as well as the armor being thicker, and the rear being equipped with skirt armor.

A Federation Forces prototype mobile suit secretly developed for ground combat.
This highly experimental machine was designed to gather data on anti-MS melee combat, and it omits features such as the Core Block system adopted in the RX-78, dramatically reducing its weight for the sake of maximum speed and maneuverability.
Its armament consists primarily of easy-to-handle projectile weapons, as well as special beam daggers that boast high output despite their limited reach.
In addition to this close-quarters loadout, the machine has a special coating that gives it some stealth capability.
Three units were produced, with the second scheduled for assignment to the White Base.
Due to enemy interference, however, it never reached its destination.

A ground combat mobile suit developed by the Principality of Zeon's Earth Occupation Force, positioned in between the Gouf and the Dom.
Though classified as a general-purpose machine, it excels in close combat, and like the Gouf it specializes in anti-MS fighting.
The completed prototype was found to perform favorably, though it was hard to produce.
Plans for mass production were ultimately scrapped, leaving only eight units in existence.
Some speculate that this was due to political reasons, such as the Earth Occupation Force's lack of authority and the fact that the homeland was already focusing on the development of a next-generation mainline MS.
Most of the prototype units were assigned to special forces teams, while the others were used as testbeds for new weapons.

A machine that the Earth Federation Force created specifically to test the EXAM system that a Zeon Newtype research facility developed. It is built based on a Gundam Ground Type but has since been modified and enhanced.
The unit is designed with compatibility with the EXAM system in mind, around the concept of solo assaults and high-mobility combat. It is equipped with a variety of mobility-enhancing features, multiple built-in weapons, and has undergone extensive tuning for it to excel in close quarters.
Only the unit number 1 has the GM-type head from initial testing installed on it.

A machine that the Earth Federation Force created specifically to test the EXAM system that a Zeon Newtype research facility developed. It is built based on a Gundam Ground Type but has since been modified and enhanced.
The unit is designed with compatibility with the EXAM system in mind, around the concept of solo assaults and high-mobility combat. It is equipped with a variety of mobility-enhancing features, multiple built-in weapons, and has undergone extensive tuning for it to excel in close quarters.
Only the unit number 1 has the GM-type head from initial testing installed on it.

A Federation Forces prototype mobile suit developed with the aid of Chlust Morses, a scientist who defected from Zeon's Flanagan Agency.
This was the first Federation MS equipped with the special control technology known as the EXAM system.
This machine was originally based on a GM Ground Type, but its performance was insufficient to realize the EXAM system's specs.
Eventually, the GM head which housed the system was transferred to a Gundam Ground Type, which then underwent extensive modification.
This included an improved generator, and magnet coating for greater maneuverability.
In terms of weaponry, chest-mounted machine guns and wire-guided missiles provided a substantial increase in firepower from the base model.
Its most distinctive feature, however, was the aforementioned EXAM system.
When activated, this provided a potent advantage in combat.
Unfortunately, it was highly unstable.
During a test run, the system went out of control, with grave consequences to the test pilot.
The system continued fighting, with no distinction between friend and foe.
Because of this catastrophe, a limiter was added, and the machine was assigned to pilot Yuu Kajima.
Though this machine was used under gravity, it can also be equipped with a backpack for combat operations in space.

An experimental mobile suit developed by the Flanagan Agency, which conducted Newtype research for the Principality of Zeon, in order to test the special control technology known as the EXAM system.
Based on the ground combat MS Efreet, it has undergone dramatic improvements.
The head unit, which houses the EXAM system, has been enlarged to facilitate cooling.
Its thrusters have also been upgraded to handle the demands of the activated system, resulting in a highly mobile machine.
As for its combat capabilities, it has been tuned for melee combat to suit the personal tastes of test pilot Nimbus Schterzen.
When used in tandem with the EXAM system, the result is a highly effective fighting unit.
The red-painted shoulders, incidentally, were another of Nimbus's requests.

A Federation Forces prototype mobile suit developed with the aid of Chlust Morses, a scientist who defected from Zeon's Flanagan Agency.
This was the second Federation MS equipped with the special control technology known as the EXAM system, and unlike the first unit, it was based from the start on a Gundam Ground Type.
It thus used a Gundam head.
Its performance is basically the same as the first unit, with the chief differences being compatibility with a beam rifle and the addition of space combat equipment.
Note that the EXAM system limiter has not been installed, and due to the system's incomplete nature, it automatically activates when it detects a dangerous situation.
This greatly increases its abilities, but the system will shut down after a certain interval has elapsed.
This version of the machine, with its red-painted shoulders, reflects the personal tastes of the Zeon pilot Nimbus Schterzen who stole the Blue Destiny Unit 2.

A machine that the Earth Federation Forces created specifically to test the EXAM system that a Zeon Newtype research facility developed. It is built based on a Gundam Ground Type but has since been modified and enhanced.
The unit is designed, with compatibility with the EXAM system in mind, around the concept of solo assaults and high-mobility combat.
To excel in close quarters, it is equipped with a variety of mobility-enhancing features, multiple built-in weapons, and has undergone extensive tuning for melee combat.
Unit 2 was stolen by Zeon forces. Like Unit 3, it is capable of space combat.

A Federation Forces prototype mobile suit developed with the aid of Chlust Morses, a scientist who defected from Zeon's Flanagan Agency.
This was the third Federation MS equipped with the special control technology known as the EXAM system, and it was originally created as a spare for the first and second units.
Its performance was basically the same as the first unit, with an EXAM system limiter installed to prevent it running wild, and it was assigned to Yuu Kajima as a replacement for the lost Unit 1.
After a number of missions on Earth, this machine was assigned to the pursuit of Unit 2, which had been stolen by Nimbus.
Thus it was hastily modified for use in space.
Unit 3 was slated to receive the iconic blue color scheme of its series.
However, Chlust's untimely death meant that it remained in its default colors.

A machine that the Earth Federation Forces created specifically to test the EXAM system that a Zeon Newtype research facility developed. It is built based on a Gundam Ground Type but has since been modified and enhanced.
The unit is designed, with compatibility with the EXAM system in mind, around the concept of solo assaults and high-mobility combat.
To excel in close quarters, it is equipped with a variety of mobility-enhancing features and multiple built-in weapons. In particular, Unit 3 has undergone extensive tuning for space combat and versatility.

A high-performance unit based on the late-stage mass production GMs, this model was developed to gather data for the purpose of improving the GM series of mobile suits.
As these suits were produced to collect actual combat data on various types of experimental equipment, they do not possess any notable characteristics.
They do boast high-level, well-balanced suit performance, however, as well as multiple hardpoints that allow for extensive equipment expansion.

As the mobile suit development project steadily progressed to mass production while utilizing feedback from Operation V, this model was developed in response to repeated mobile suit deployment requests from the front lines, where soldiers lacked the means to fight back against Zeon's mobile suits and were desperate for their own units.
Though only a limited number was manufactured, these units entered early production as ground type mobile suits and were assembled using parts left over from the prototype Gundam development.
Though its development details give the impression of a primitive unit, owing to its components having been sourced from Gundams, these models were actually high-performance mobile suits that achieved impressive results on the battlefield.

This mobile suit was the result of the Federation, after having successfully mass produced GMs, combining their technologies to research and develop the next generation of mobile suits.
As a prototype that was completed by incorporating various contemporary technologies, the RX-80, which served as a template, was an incredibly appealing high-performance unit.
However, the costs of producing and operating such a unit rendered it difficult to deploy as a mass production model.
As a result, it was requisitioned by a certain research organization to serve as an experimental unit.
Once requisitioned, this mobile suit became a test unit and was fitted with the special EXAM-adjacent HADES system in order to substantially bolster its performance, receiving modifications in line with this system as well.

An experimental machine based on the Zaku series, developed by the Principality of Zeon as a testbed for Newtype mobile suits.
This was part of a plan to put Newtypes to military use.
During its development, there were problems with the miniaturization of its psycommu system and arm-mounted beam cannons.
Ultimately, the arms were redesigned to include large wired beam cannons, resulting in a rather distinctive look.
This prototype was code named "Bishop."
Three units were created, and data was collected by a test corps based on the flagship Lemuria.
When Annerose Rosenheim, a Marchosias pilot who also happened to be aboard the Lemuria, was discovered to possess Newtype potential, this mysterious fourth unit was hastily constructed from a reserve unit and spare parts.

A federation forces Gundam-type mobile suit assigned to the moore brotherhood, an organization made up of former redidents of the destroyed side 4.
Its design differs from the FA-78-1 version and includes an array of experimental systems and equipment.
This machine is equipped with extraordinarily heavy armament compared to other MS of the time.
Its right arm bears a large twin beam rifle which employs an energy pack system.
In addition to a shield, the left arm carries five rocket launchers.
Furthermore, the backpack holds missile pods and a large beam cannon, as well as six spare energy packs, and its sub-arms allow the machine to carry a total of four shields.
Thrusters and apogee motors are attached to the shoulders, legs, and elsewhere.
Combined with the backpack's large rocket boosters, these give it thrust rivaling that of a high mobility type MS.

A federation forces Gundam-type mobile suit assigned to the moore brotherhood, an organization made up of former redidents of the destroyed side 4.
Its design differs from the FA-78-1 version and includes an array of experimental systems and equipment.
This machine is equipped with extraordinarily heavy armament compared to other MS of the time.
Its right arm bears a large twin beam rifle which employs an energy pack system.
In addition to a shield, the left arm carries five rocket launchers.
Furthermore, the backpack holds missile pods and a large beam cannon, as well as six spare energy packs, and its sub-arms allow the machine to carry a total of four shields.
Thrusters and apogee motors are attached to the shoulders, legs, and elsewhere.
Combined with the backpack's large rocket boosters, these give it thrust rivaling that of a high mobility type MS.

An EFF mass-produced mobile suit developed using Gundam data.
Io Fleming piloted one of the units deployed to the Moore Brotherhood.
The joints are covered in sealing to protect them from debris in Thunderbolt Sector.
In addition to a large backpack, extra sensors are added to the head unit for exploration in shoal zones in addition to other general modifications.
The large backpack houses two sub-arms that can carry two shields and energy packs for its beam spray gun.
The cockpit can also eject using the Core Block system in case of emergency.
Its core block can furthermore purge an emergency pod from its main unit, all to ensure the pilot's safety.
This function is what saved Io when his GM was sniped by Daryl.

A high-mobility Zaku II equipped with the Reuse P Device that was developed by the Living Dead Division and nicknamed the Psycho Zaku by Captain Burroughs due to its overly long official name.
It was developed from the Zaku II High Mobility Type, a test machine for a drive system that enables a mobile suit to be controlled by pilots via direct transmission of their brainwaves called the Reuse P Device.
As Ensign Daryl Lorenz's personal machine, it engaged in battle with the Full Armor Gundam.

A high-mobility Zaku II equipped with the Reuse P Device that was developed by the Living Dead Division and nicknamed the Psycho Zaku by Captain Burroughs due to its overly long official name.
It was developed from the Zaku II High Mobility Type, a test machine for a drive system that enables a mobile suit to be controlled by pilots via direct transmission of their brainwaves called the Reuse P Device.
As Ensign Daryl Lorenz's personal machine, it engaged in battle with the Full Armor Gundam.

A mass-produced Zaku, otherwise known as the Zaku II.
Synonymous with the concept of mobile suits and the mainstay of the Principality of Zeon's armed forces, this unit played an active role in the Thunderbolt Sector at the end of the One Year War.
The units deployed to the Living Dead Division have their joints and power tubes covered in sealing to protect from debris and also have added shock-absorbers in the knees.
In Thunderbolt Sector, Zaku II's are mainly used for defensive missions, lurking in shoal zones and sniping invading enemy units.
For that reason, claws were added to the underside of the Zaku II's legs that can be fixed to debris and other objects to serve as footholds.
Its large backpack enables high mobility and is fitted with a propellant tank for thrusters.
Its weapons are a standard shield, a Zaku machine gun, heat hawk, and an improved Zaku bazooka.
Sergeant Major Daryl Lorenz was piloting a Zaku II when he first encountered Ensign Io of the Earth Federation.

An EFF model of the RX series developed at the same time as the Gundam.
This mass-produced mobile suit specializes in mid-range supporting fire.
Four units were deployed to the Moore Brotherhood.
These were manned by child soldiers assigned as supplemental pilots and were used as bulwarks (i.e., support units) to get the Full Armor Gundam, tasked with destroying the enemy fleet's Living Dead Division and handling their enemy's ace sniper, to the frontlines.
Its joints are covered in sealing to protect them from debris in Thunderbolt Sector and its head unit has added sensors to aid in exploration of shoal zones.
A pair of low-recoil cannons and thrusters are mounted on its large backpack.
A beam rifle is also part of its standard equipment.

A mobile pod created by the EFF and characterized by its spherical shape.
Its main armament is the low-recoil cannon mounted to its top.
The units deployed to the Moore Brotherhood were equipped with landing sleds, a machine gun on each manipulator, four rear thrusters, and one thruster located the bottom.
An additional 12 units were deployed to the Beehive to make up for the forces lost in the offensive against the Living Dead Division.

20 units were deployed to the Moore Brotherhood to be used as bulwarks (i.e., support units) to get the Full Armor Gundam, tasked with destroying the enemy fleet's Living Dead Division and handling their enemy's ace sniper, to the frontlines.
The joints are covered in sealing to protect them from debris in Thunderbolt Sector and extra sensors were added to the head unit for exploring shoal zones.
Mounted on its large backpack are thrusters and a sub-arm on its left shoulder.
On its right shoulder is a cannon with a targeting sensor.
Thrusters have also been added to the calves.
Its standard armaments are a beam spray gun and shield.
The cockpit can eject using the Core Block system in case of emergency.
Its core block can furthermore jettison an emergency pod.
These units were piloted by the child soldiers assigned as supplemental pilots.

This mobile suit is said to have been the peak accomplishment among mass-produced mobile suits developed during the One Year War and is the first of Zeon's mobile suits to be equipped with beam weapons as standard equipment.
The units deployed to the Siren Mobile Fleet have their joints covered in sealing to protect them in shoal zones.
Its large backpack is fitted with a beam generator and propellant tanks equipped with thrusters.
Its firepower and high mobility are basically on par with the Gundam.
Its main weapons are a beam naginata, a dedicated beam rifle, and a shield.
After rescuing Fisher's Rick Dom, the Siren Mobile Fleet arrived in Thunderbolt Sector as reinforcements for the Living Dead Division, but the might of the Full Armor Gundam left them all but completely annihilated.
However, thanks to the efforts of the Psycho Zaku, the Moore Brotherhood was also destroyed, and Federation soldier Io and his comrades were captured.

A high-mobility mobile suit mass-produced after the One Year War.
This version of the MS-09 Dom was modified for space use, and like the Dom, it features a cross-shaped field of view and a mono-eye capable of three-dimensional scanning.
The units deployed to the Living Dead Division have their joints covered in sealing to protect them in shoal zones.
Its large backpack has added high-mobility thrusters and a propellant tank.
The Dried Fish units were mainly used for sniping missions using the aptly named "big guns."
These units equipped with heat hawks and MMP-50 machine guns and were piloted by Hoover and Fisher.

Given the moniker "Old Zaku," this was the first mobile suit ever developed by the Principality of Zeon for actual combat use.
By the end of the One Year War, it was still in service but lost its position as Zeon's primary mobile suit to its successor, the Zaku II.
Sergeant Major Daryl Lorenz also piloted a Zaku I to snipe down the Full Armor Gundam after it destroyed a Zaku II piloted by his friend.
The Zaku Is deployed to the Living Dead Division have their joints covered in sealing to protect them in shoal zones.
Its backpack is equipped with a propellant tank for increased mobility and a sub-arm to hold firearms.
The left shoulder is armored with spike-like protrusions, while the right shoulder armor is removed and grips are attached.
Claws located on the underside of its feet can be fixed to debris and other objects to serve as footholds.
Its weapons are a Zaku machine gun, heat hawk, and crackers.

A special night combat variation of the ground combat prototype mobile suit Efreet developed by the Principality of Zeon.
This machine was equipped with unique new technological systems, in an attempt to capitalize on the superior ground combat capabilities of the Efreet series.
It has enhanced thrusters that allow for high mobility, as well as a katana-shaped cold blade and throwable cold kunai that give it a massive advantage in melee combat.
Its most distinctive feature, however, is a special jamming system that provides stealth against enemy radar and makes this machine an excellent choice for covert activities.
This machine was developed during the One Year War, but because it was hidden at Odessa when the base was seized by the Federation Forces, the Principality ultimately lost the performance and technology of this machine forever.

A mysterious state-of-the-art mobile suit piloted by Machu.
It participates in Clan Battle as part of the Pomeranians.
Suddenly appearing at Side 6 in U.C. 0085, the mobile suit joins in Clan Battle.
A mysterious, state-of-the-art mobile suit. Omega Psycommu has not been activated.

An experimental mobile suit, tested in absolute secrecy as part of the Gundam Development Project conducted by the Federation Forces and Anaheim Electronics.
This successor to the Gundam concept that had proved so successful during the One Year War was equipped with a learning computer, a Core Block system, and other technologies.
Its performance was almost 30% better than the Gundam's, but because its environmental adaptation was somewhat lacking, it was designed with the ability to switch out its main parts for a Core Fighter II.
This allowed it to operate both on land and in space.

The Gundam GP01 was an experimental mobile suit, tested in absolute secrecy as part of the Gundam Development Project conducted by the Federation Forces and Anaheim Electronics.
After it was heavily damaged in a space battle against a team of Gelgoog Marines, it underwent large-scale repairs at Anaheim Electronics' Von Braun plant, and was converted to a space version.
