It was originally thought that its versatility could be maintained by simple methods such as switching out its main parts for a Core Fighter II, but given the damage to its body, the decision was made to convert it into a pure space version.
At the same time, a variety of plans and previous operating data were used to completely overhaul the rear of the Core Fighter II, equipping it with boost pods, and to expand the movement range of the vernier thrusters on the upper body.
This led to dramatic improvements in mobility and maneuverability.

An experimental mobile suit, tested in absolute secrecy as part of the Gundam Development Project conducted by the Federation Forces and Anaheim Electronics.
This heavy assault machine was developed as an MS with the ultimate in offensive strength, which could annihilate enemies and their bases in any environment or situation.
Its most distinctive feature is a nuclear weapon known as the Atomic Bazooka.
To withstand the blasts of its own attacks, all its armor has anti-heat and anti-impact features, and it must carry a special shield with an internal cooling system.
Each shoulder is equipped with high-output thrusters to aid in attacks and escapes, and the machine possesses better reaction speed and mobility than its appearance would imply.
It was to have had its tactical nuclear strike capabilities tested at the Torrington base, but was seized and lost when the Delaz Fleet attacked the base.

An experimental machine, tested in absolute secrecy as part of the Gundam Development Project conducted by the Federation Forces and Anaheim Electronics.
It was developed as the mobile suit unit of the mobile weapon Dendrobium.
To help it combine with the armed base Orchis, it was given tail binders to improve its handling and mobility, and its cockpit used a panoramic monitor and linear seat to help the pilot control its vast array of weapons.
It also featured folding arms, necessary for it to work in tandem with the armed base Orchis.
Because it took a long time to resupply the armed base, the mobile suit unit was outfitted with the capabilities to hold its own in solo space combat.

An experimental machine that combined the features of a mobile suit and mobile armor, tested in absolute secrecy as part of the Gundam Development Project conducted by the Federation Forces and Anaheim Electronics.
It was loaded with every weapon imaginable, in an effort to fulfill the Gundam Development Project's concept of "history's strongest mobile weapon."
Because it was assumed that this machine would be used mainly to defend bases in space, the armed base Orchis was developed into a massive weapon storage unit.
It was highly mobile thanks to its huge propellant supply, and its armament included a mega beam cannon with incredible firepower.
Combined with a beam weapon-nullifying I-Field generator, these gave it overwhelming combat capabilities.

An experimental machine that combined the features of a mobile suit and mobile armor, tested in absolute secrecy as part of the Gundam Development Project conducted by the Federation Forces and Anaheim Electronics.
It was loaded with every weapon imaginable, in an effort to fulfill the Gundam Development Project's concept of "history's strongest mobile weapon."
Because it was assumed that this machine would be used mainly to defend bases in space, the armed base Orchis was developed into a massive weapon storage unit.
It was highly mobile thanks to its huge propellant supply, and its armament included a mega beam cannon with incredible firepower.
Combined with a beam weapon-nullifying I-Field generator, these gave it overwhelming combat capabilities.

An experimental machine that combined the features of a mobile suit and mobile armor, tested in absolute secrecy as part of the Gundam Development Project conducted by the Federation Forces and Anaheim Electronics.
It was loaded with every weapon imaginable, in an effort to fulfill the Gundam Development Project's concept of "history's strongest mobile weapon."
Because it was assumed that this machine would be used mainly to defend bases in space, the armed base Orchis was developed into a massive weapon storage unit.
It was highly mobile thanks to its huge propellant supply, and its armament included a mega beam cannon with incredible firepower.
Combined with a beam weapon-nullifying I-Field generator, these gave it overwhelming combat capabilities.

As per the Gundam Development Project—part of the Federation Forces Reorganization Plan implemented after the One Year War—an effort was made to realize the conceptual most powerful mobile suit by referencing an assault mobile suit developed long ago in the Principality of Zeon and recreating it using current technology.
Equipped with explosive propulsion by way of the Sturm Booster integrated into its propellant tank and outfitted with a high-powered long beam rifle and high-spec close combat capabilities alike, this mobile suit could wage a brutal assault in short order, potentially annihilating enemy forces and claiming victory in the blink of an eye.
However, as many of its designs overlapped with those of the GP01, it was ultimately excluded from the Gundam Development Project.
With development officially discontinued, Anaheim Co. had no choice but to continue working on it independently.
Anaheim's backroom business partner, the Cima Fleet, ended up making some changes to its exterior design and functionality and rebranding it as Gerbera Tetra, so few are familiar with it in this form.

One of the GM variations developed by the Federation Forces after the One Year War.
This machine was developed to serve as a testbed for a large prototype backpack and accompanying shock absorber units for use with the Gundam GP01, a mobile suit developed as part of the Gundam Development Project.
Based on a later production version of the GM, this machine was refitted to collect data for the development of new MS models.
While it ultimately boasted output and mobility superior to that of the GM Type C, only a few units were manufactured for testing purposes, and it was never given an official model number of its own.

One of the GM variations developed by the Federation Forces after the One Year War.
This machine had enhanced sub-thrusters and improved generator output, in order to enhance its overall performance as a mass production model.
With many of these specially tuned for ace pilots, this machine's production cost made it difficult to produce in large numbers.
But with almost twice the thrust of other GMs, and generator output on par with that of a Gundam type, it was the Federation's highest class of mass-produced machine up until the Delaz Conflict.

One of the GM variations developed by the Federation Forces after the One Year War.
Based on the concept of a simplified mass-produced version of the RX-77 Guncannon, it could be considered the culmination of the GM Cannon, Guncannon Mass Production Type, and other medium-range support mobile suits developed since the middle stages of the war.
Technological advances and production improvements allowed for the installation of a high-output generator, which in turn allowed for the inclusion of beam cannons on both of its shoulders.
Its exterior also boasted composite armor of the type as Chobham armor, while the rest of its basic components were designed to be compatible with those of a GM type MS.
This made it a virtually perfect support machine in terms of production cost and performance, but because it was meant to operate in tandem with the GM Custom, only a few units were ever actually deployed.

A riot control mobile suit specially developed by the Titans.
This was a cutting-edge GM-series machine, which followed in the tradition of the GM Custom designed for use by ace pilots.
Developed to be the mainline MS of the Federation's elite Titans unit, it had exceptionally high specs for a mass production type, and was markedly better than the GM series used by the regular forces.
While it was the first MS used by the Titans, it was already obsolete by the time of the Gryps Conflict and did not see combat on the front lines.
The black and blue coloring of this machine would later become the official "Titans colors," the standard colors used on their MS.

A prototype mobile suit developed for medium and long-range artillery support, it was designed to be a "movable battery."
Its thermonuclear jet engines and hovering features give it greater mobility than its heavy appearance would imply.
Still, its exceedingly low maneuverability makes close combat impossible.
Boasting an extremely long-range foldable 680mm cannon as its main weapon, this machine was intended for heavy bombardment.
It seats a pilot and a gunner, though its control system can be switched over to a single person.

A variation of the Principality of Zeon's general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit Gelgoog, which was developed at the end of the One Year War.
Improved for use by marine amphibious units, this machine was modified with optional propellant tanks to simplify the landing process at the beginning of an operation, increasing the amount of time it could spend in the field.
Because the situation made it impossible to obtain beam rifles, and due to the need to adapt to a wide variety of different battle conditions, its standard weapons were a 90mm machine gun and a unique spike shield that it could use for close combat.
In U.C.0083, a number of these were deployed as the main force of the Cima Fleet, with many of them seeing action.

This is the marine version of the Principality of Zeon's general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit Gelgoog, which was developed at the end of the One Year War.
It has been customized for use by commanders, with a head-mounted blade antenna that distinguishes it from the standard type and the addition of vulcan cannons.
To improve the machine's performance, it was given additional main thrusters and can carry a greater number of propellant tanks.
This is also the only machine in the Delaz Fleet to carry a large beam rifle, and it uses a special shield in place of the standard type's spike shield.
Because it is only used by the marine amphibious unit known as the Cima Fleet, this is essentially Cima Garahau's custom machine.

A modified version of a prototype mobile suit that was created as part of the Gundam Development Project conducted by the Federation Forces and Anaheim Electronics.
It was developed with the aim of making it the strongest mobile suit in terms of anti-MS combat.
It excels at melee and close combat, and exhibits outstanding mobility and acceleration when equipped with its sturm booster.
Originally stored at Anaheim Electronics, it was secretly turned over to the Cima Fleet through a convoluted route, and became the personal machine of Cima Garahau.

The Delaz Fleet of the Zeon remnants created this mass-produced mobile suit using a combination of body parts from the Zaku II F2, and parts from the high-speed space fighter-bomber Gattle.
Because it was designed with space combat in mind, its legs were removed in favor of propellant tanks.
However, it falls short of a normal MS as an AMBAC unit, and compensates for that deficiency with the inclusion of attitude control thruster pods on its shoulders.
The removal of its legs, the consumable part with the highest failure rate, made for a machine that was extremely easy to maintain and cost-efficient.
The fact that modified versions of this machine were sighted even after the Delaz Conflict is a testament to its service life.

A space combat mobile armor developed by the Principality of Zeon at the end of the One Year War, based on data from previous mobile armors.
Armed with a built-in large mega particle cannon and a special weapon known as a plasma leader, among other weaponry, it could easily be considered the culmination of Zeon MA technology.
As opposed to the Bigro and its focus on anti-ship combat, the Val-Walo was also designed with anti-MS combat in mind.
It boasts reinforced armor and extremely impressive mobility, giving it stellar overall combat capabilities.
After the One Year War, the former Zeon pilot Kelley Layzner hid this machine in the lowest levels of Von Braun, and repaired it in an effort to join the Delaz Fleet.

A massive space combat mobile armor developed independently by Axis, using the newest technology of the time.
It is equipped with a number of beam weapons and high-output thrusters, and features Wired Claw Arm capable of unleashing an all-range attack even without Newtype capabilities.
Its defenses include a special device known as an I-Field generator that is able to disrupt enemy beam attacks.
But while it boasts overwhelming combat capabilities, it also places a huge burden on the pilot and requires serious skill to operate properly.
During the Delaz Conflict, it was sent as a parting gift for Operation Stardust from the Axis Advance Fleet to the Delaz Fleet.
Anavel Gato, the "Nightmare of Solomon," would use it in a fierce fight against the Federation fleet.

A massive space combat mobile armor developed independently by Axis, using the newest technology of the time.
It is equipped with a number of beam weapons and high-output thrusters, and features Wired Claw Arm capable of unleashing an all-range attack even without Newtype capabilities.
Its defenses include a special device known as an I-Field generator that is able to disrupt enemy beam attacks.
But while it boasts overwhelming combat capabilities, it also places a huge burden on the pilot and requires serious skill to operate properly.
During the Delaz Conflict, it was sent as a parting gift for Operation Stardust from the Axis Advance Fleet to the Delaz Fleet.
Anavel Gato, the "Nightmare of Solomon," would use it in a fierce fight against the Federation fleet.

A later production version of the GM, a general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit introduced at the end of the One Year War.
This machine was upgraded with improved output and other features, incorporating the latest technology available at the time.
It served as a temporary mainline machine for the Federation Forces.
In addition to providing data on MS operation during combat, it offered improved performance over the standard GM in terms of mobility and maneuverability.
This was a big contribution in terms of production know-how, and became the foundation for future Federation MS.
In U.C.0083, a number of these were deployed to the Torrington base on Earth, where they saw combat during the Delaz Conflict.

A later version of the F Type, the most versatile variation of the mass-produced mobile suit Zaku II that was developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War.
This machine reflected operating data obtained since the Earth landing operations.
While the most obvious design changes were external ones such as reinforced chest armor, it also featured a lighter body and enhanced thruster output, which resulted in improved mobility.
This machine was customized for Kimberlide base commander Neuen Bitter.
Its color has been changed and its head equipped with a blade antenna to show that it is a commander's machine.
Its backpack is fitted with rocket boosters, and it exhibited impressive jumping ability in battle against the Federation's Albion team.

The Gelgoog was developed as a next-generation successor to the Zaku II, the main mobile suit of the Principality of Zeon.
Of the 25 pre-production units, one was specially colored for pilot Anavel Gato, known as the "Nightmare of Solomon."
This machine was the first of Zeon's mobile suits to adopt portable beam weaponry, and was armed with a prototype beam rifle. 
It also had enhanced thrust, giving it greater mobility and maneuverability and high overall performance.

A later version of the F Type, the most versatile variation of the mass-produced mobile suit Zaku II that was developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War.
This machine reflected operating data obtained since the Earth landing operations.
While the most obvious design changes were external ones such as reinforced chest armor, it also featured a lighter body and enhanced thruster output, which resulted in improved mobility.
Because it offered excellent adaptability to environments with gravity, and was easier to pilot than existing machines, it's said that it could be thrown into standard military operations with virtually no modifications.
But because of breakdowns in Zeon's command structure and supply lines, it was disproportionately deployed to the Solomon sector and the African front.

Developed by the Principality of Zeon using the Dom and Dom Tropical Test Type as a base, this is a Dom-type variation created for use in the desert and tropics, using data gathered from various test machines.
It has thermonuclear hover engines with anti-dust filters in its legs for extended operating time in deserts and ease of maintenance.
Weapon latches all over its body make it possible to use other armaments such as a raketen bazooka and sturm fausts, giving it high versatility.
It was deployed mainly in Central Africa, and after the war, it was used in the Delaz Fleet's attack on the Torrington base.

The early model of the Gundam GP01.
Anticipated to become the buddy unit (escort) of the nuclear-powered GP02A, it is equipped with both increased armor and Chobham armor.

An experimental mobile suit tested in absolute secrecy as part of the Gundam Development Project conducted by the Federation Forces and Anaheim Electronics.
This machine was the trial stage of the GP series.
As an experimental machine, it features a variety of new equipment, including a radome-shaped Minovsky Particle Interference Wave Searcher mounted on its back that serves as a wide-area sensor.
Additionally, while it has no rapid-fire weaponry, its long-range beam rifle gives it the highest class of MS firepower available at the time.
This makes the machine extremely lethal from long range.
It also has large verniers for high-speed combat, a Core Block system, and other systems for improved mobility.
By incorporating all of Anaheim Electronics's current technologies, it was hoped that the end result would be an all-powerful MS.
But because cramming every last feature into a single machine put a huge burden on the pilot and resulted in a number of operational issues, it was decided that the various features would be divided among multiple machines, which led to the later development of the GP series.

As a successor to the GM Striker, this special experimental unit was developed after the One Year War and specialized in melee combat.
As its specifications were almost identical to those of the GM Striker Kai, it was also given heavy armor to withstand close-range fire, a large backpack, and improved propulsion via enhancements to its legs.

A Gundam developed for Asuna by Anaheim Electronics.
It is equipped with a number of experimental weapons, including a specially designed shield and a saber that emits beams from both ends of its grip.

A single-seat GM used at Ecole for training purposes.
Notable for its unusual head structure, this mobile suit was intended to be piloted solo by trainees, and it was designed from the beginning to accommodate pilot error such as accidental damage and loss of balance.

A functional test machine assigned to the Titans' T3 test corps.
Based on a GM Quel with a Gundam head, it was developed to test its psychological impact on Zeon remnants.
The basic frame was that of the GM Quel with various parts replaced and reinforced, such as an enhanced backpack and movable booster pod, as well as enhanced thrusters in its legs.
Other key features included a shield booster which combined the roles of shield, thruster, and propellant tank, and an experimental beam rifle that used energy packs.
As an experimental machine, it included a variety of elements, and was said to have potential rivaling that of RX-type mobile suits.

A form of the Titans mobile suit Gundam TR-1 "Hazel Custom" equipped with the G-Parts "Hrududu."
The Hrududu was developed as optional equipment for the Hazel Custom, based on the concept of the G-Fighter used by the RX-78-2 Gundam during the One Year War.
The Hrududu not only served as a supplemental propulsion system, but also included sub-sensors, extra armor, and high-mobility binders.
Hrududu parts could be attached to the Hazel Custom's beam rifle to form a long blade rifle, capable of both long-range sniping and melee combat thanks to the heat blade underneath its barrel.
This gave the machine performance rivaling that of a second-generation mobile suit.

A prototype version of the transformable mobile armor Asshimar created for zero-gravity testing, which was developed by the Federation Forces and then forcibly requisitioned by the Titans so that they could remodel it into their own machine.
Dramatic advances in mobile suit technology after the One Year War had made it possible to create the movable mechanisms and high-strength materials required for transformation.
This machine also incorporated "drum frames," movable parts made up of disk-shaped frames, and its various parts and blocks turned around these in order to transform.
This machine combined the mobility of an MA and the versatility of an MS, allowing it to utilize the strengths of both to suit the situation.
Its frame technology was a great influence on the later development of the movable frame.

An enhanced and upgraded version of the Titans transformable mobile armor Gaplant TR-5.
The shield boosters which were installed to reduce the strain on ordinary pilots were replaced with movable shield binders, restoring the Gaplant's original high acceleration.
This machine was also equipped with the G-Parts "Hrududu II," which was developed as a set of enhancement parts for the Hrairoo.
Its long blade rifle could be connected to the beam emitter inside the Hrududu II's claw unit to form a long-barrel beam cannon, dramatically increasing its offensive power.
This is the first unit of the Hrairoo, which was distinguished by its white-based coloring.

A high-performance transformable mobile suit developed as part of Project Zeta, which was spearheaded by the Anti Earth Union Group (AEUG).
Its lightweight structure is extremely sturdy, thanks to the new alloy known as Gundarium Gamma.
It is powered by advanced ultra-compact fusion reactors, and its transformation mechanisms use movable frame technology appropriated from the Titans.
The machine can change from MS form into a flight form known as a "waverider," allowing it to enter the atmosphere with only its standard equipment.
It retains its attack capabilities even while descending, and can shift immediately into combat operations under gravity, a truly revolutionary feature.
Its ranged weapons include a beam rifle that can also function as a long beam saber for close combat, and a hyper mega launcher, the most powerful MS weapon of its era.
This gives the Zeta Gundam great offensive strength as well as high versatility.

A prototype attack mobile suit developed as part of Project Zeta, which was spearheaded by the Anti Earth Union Group (AEUG).
Based on the Rick Dias design, it was originally planned to be a transformable MS.
The transformation mechanisms, however, were found to contain fatal flaws, resulting in its finalization as a non-transformable type.
The machine uses the latest movable frame technology, which mimics the human skeleton.
It is both nimble and lightweight, giving it top-class maneuverability by the standards of its time.
It is also very energy-efficient, and its surplus output can be diverted to support a giant mega particle cannon called the mega bazooka launcher.
This single unit therefore has immense offensive potential.
The machine is named after its developer M.
Nagano's notion of building a mobile suit which would be used on the front lines for a hundred years.
Note that "Hyaku-Shiki" means "Type 100."

A prototype transformable mobile suit developed as part of Project Zeta, which was spearheaded by the Anti Earth Union Group (AEUG).
At the time, research was being conducted to develop machines capable of exhibiting high performance in all imaginable combat environments, and this was a testbed for evaluating transformation mechanisms.
Its transformation features were given highest design priority, resulting in maneuverability superior to many machines of its era.
The tradeoff, however, was limited durability and firepower.
After a small number of these prototypes were built, production was halted, with the design never to enter mass production.
Since it was relatively easy to pilot, some units were upgraded for action on the front lines.

Developed to be the flagship mobile suit of the Federation's elite Titans special forces, this machine boasted high performance and versatility worthy of the Gundam legacy.
Its cockpit unit, which doubled as an ejection pod, contained a linear seat and panoramic monitor.
It also adopted a movable frame capable of imitating human movement.
These new technologies, which became standard equipment for its era, were incorporated into the outline of the RX-78.
Its armament was designed to encompass a wide range of variations.
In addition to standard weapons, it included a slew of experimental technology, from confiscated Zeon weaponry to deleted equipment from the Delaz Conflict.
The Anti Earth Union Group (AEUG) succeeded in capturing three Gundam Mk-II units from the Titans.
They repainted one of them and used the machine to gather operating data.
This data was sent to Anaheim Electronics for the purpose of making improvements and developing optional equipment, resulting in an AEUG version of the machine that boasted specs on par with the era's newest machines.

Developed to be the flagship mobile suit of the Federation's elite Titans special forces, this machine boasted high performance and versatility worthy of the Gundam legacy.
Its cockpit unit, which doubled as an ejection pod, contained a linear seat and panoramic monitor.
It also adopted a movable frame capable of imitating human movement.
These new technologies, which became standard equipment for its era, were incorporated into the outline of the RX-78.
Its armament was designed to encompass a wide range of variations.
In addition to standard weapons, it included a slew of experimental technology, from confiscated Zeon weaponry to deleted equipment from the Delaz Conflict.
The final version of this machine did not utilize many of the planned parts.
There were also enhancement plans for special missions, and drafts of a dedicated SFS (Sub Flight System).

An enhancement plan conceived by the AEUG to keep the Gundam Mk-II relevant in the face of newer enemy machines.
When  docked with the G-Defenser support weapon, its firepower, defenses, and thrust were all greatly enhanced.
The transformation mechanism was relatively simple, with the Gundam Mk-II housed in the space underneath the G-Defenser.
This allowed transformation to take place even in hectic space battlefields, with the ability to change forms as the situation demanded.
The process did, admittedly, allow a moment of vulnerability to the enemy.
When fully docked, however, the Super Gundam's grand silhouette was enough to inspire awe in friend and foe alike.
Its long rifle, a supremely powerful armament for its time, further leveled the playing field in fights with state-of-the-art machines.

A transformable fighter developed by Anaheim Electronics, as a support weapon for the Gundam Mk-II that was stolen by the AEUG.
It was based on the G-Armor concept used on an experimental basis during the One Year War.
With a pilot at its controls, this machine can act as a heavy attack craft for space combat, fighting on equal terms against mobile suits.
By docking with the Gundam Mk-II, it boosts the other machine's firepower, defenses, and thrust.
In this form, the G-Defenser detaches its cockpit section, which then acts as an independent Core Fighter.
The Gryps Conflict saw one of these units deployed on the front lines, where it was used in conjunction with the Gundam Mk-II to achieve outstanding combat results.

A mass-produced attack mobile suit developed in secret for the Anti Earth Union Group(AEUG).
Anaheim Electronics created this machine at the AEUG's request, using a team led by former Principality of Zeon engineers.
Despite its strong resemblance to Zeon mobile suits, its interior adopts the basic frame of a Federation MS.
The back binders are compatible with AMBAC for greater mobility, surpassing that of traditional suits.
Its use of lightweight yet sturdy Gundarium Gamma alloy for its armor, and new movable frame technology that resembles the skeleton of a human body, make this the trailblazer of the second-generation MS.
This particular unit belongs to AEUG ace Quattro Bajeena, and sports his favorite red paint scheme.
Later, in part due to the color's popularity, red was chosen as the standard Rick Dias color scheme to reduce the risk of friendly fire.
The code name for this machine during its development was "Gamma Gundam."
Though it is said to incorporate elements of the post-One Year War Gundam Development Project, the veracity of such claims is uncertain.

A mass-produced attack mobile suit developed in secret for the Anti Earth Union Group (AEUG).
Anaheim Electronics created this machine at the AEUG's request, using a team led by former Principality of Zeon engineers.Despite its strong resemblance to Zeon mobile suits, its interior adopts the basic frame of a Federation MS.
The back binders are compatible with AMBAC for greater mobility, surpassing that of traditional suits.
Its use of lightweight yet sturdy Gundarium Gamma alloy for its armor, and new movable frame technology that resembles the skeleton of a human body, make this the trailblazer of the second-generation MS.The code name for this machine during its development was "Gamma Gundam.
"Though it is said to incorporate elements of the post-One Year War Gundam Development Project, the veracity of such claims is uncertain.

A prototype mobile suit independently developed by Karaba, the AEUG's Earth-based support organization.
Karaba devoted all its engineering capacity to the creation of this machine, which is based on a Rick Dias received after the attack on Jaburo and also borrows avionics and other parts from the Nemo.
The machine is specialized for operation under gravity, and its legs have vectored-nozzle jet thrusters, allowing for hovering movement during ground combat.
In addition to the blade antenna on its head, the tips of its cooling fins include rod-type multi-sensors for enhanced communications, making the Dijeh a high-spec machine suitable for ace pilots.
This unit belongs to Amuro Ray, Karaba's own ace, who was involved in the final phases of its development.
Records indicate he also suggested its experimental weapons loadout, which was chosen for testing purposes.
In addition to the mono-eye in its head, there are also sockets for twin cameras, said to be holdovers from an initial plan to make the machine a Gundam-type MS.

A mass-produced mobile suit jointly developed by the AEUG and Anaheim Electronics.
As full-scale mass production of the expensive Rick Dias and Hyaku-Shiki was quickly rejected, and the Marasai was provided to the Titans for political reasons, it was this MS that ended up being supplied to the AEUG.
This was a top-tier mass production type, which incorporated the cutting-edge technology of its time.
It was able to operate in all kinds of environments using the same components, whether in space or under gravity, and from cold districts to tropics.
The Gundarium Gamma used for its armor provided a very high cost-performance ratio, largely due to advances in mass production achieved with the Marasai.
Its performance far exceeded that of the GM II on which it was supposedly based.
Some records suggest the staff behind the Nemo may previously have worked on the GM Sniper II.
Many hail the Nemo as the most balanced MS to take part in the Gryps Conflict, pointing to its importance as a  reference for next-generation mass production types.

A mainline mass-produced mobile suit, developed by the Federation Forces after the One Year War as a successor to the GM.
It incorporates a variety of improvements to increase its performance relative to the base machine.
These changes are relatively minor, and its performance is not much higher, but it has a new computer and enhanced communications systems, and some units even have retractable sub-thrusters.
It thus achieves improved performance, while retaining the original version's ease of production and maintenance.
The pilot of this unit belongs to the anti-Titans branch of the Federation Forces known as the Anti Earth Union Group (AEUG).
It uses a green color scheme to indicate its affiliation.

A mainline mass-produced mobile suit, developed by the Federation Forces after the One Year War as a successor to the GM.
It incorporates a variety of improvements to increase its performance relative to the base machine.
These changes are relatively minor, and its performance is not much higher, but it has a new computer and enhanced communications systems, and some units even have retractable sub-thrusters.
It thus achieves improved performance, while retaining the original version's ease of production and maintenance.
In the late U.C.0080s, the demand for simple mass production types spiked, and this machine was produced on a scale almost rivaling that of the Federation's mainline GM.

The first new mass-produced mobile suit designed by the Federation Forces after the One Year War.
It was developed at the lunar city of Granada by former Principality of Zeon engineers who also integrated Federation technology.
This was the first mass-produced MS to employ a panoramic monitor and linear seat, and it was a revolutionary attempt to blend Federation and Zeon technologies.
Though it received mediocre overall evaluations, its catalog specs were not unsatisfactory.
It could also use beam weapons, though it was unable to drive two types at the same time.
This is said to have been due to cost-cutting, a last-minute generator change, and other production issues.
Nonetheless, this machine was popular with pilots for its good handling and versatility, and due to its low cost it remained in use until the end of the Gryps Conflict.
Note that the Federation and Titans versions use different color schemes.
The green colors depicted here belong to the latter organization.

The first new mass-produced mobile suit designed by the Federation Forces after the One Year War.
It was developed at the lunar city of Granada by former Principality of Zeon engineers who also integrated Federation technology.
This was the first mass-produced MS to employ a panoramic monitor and linear seat, and it was a revolutionary attempt to blend Federation and Zeon technologies.
Though it received mediocre overall evaluations, its catalog specs were not unsatisfactory.
It could also use beam weapons, though it was unable to drive two types at the same time.
This is said to have been due to cost-cutting, a last-minute generator change, and other production issues.
Nonetheless, this machine was popular with pilots for its good handling and versatility, and due to its low cost it remained in use until the end of the Gryps Conflict.
Note that the Federation and Titans versions use different color schemes.
The blue colors depicted here belong to the former organization.

A variation of the Hizack, the first new mass-produced mobile suit designed by the Federation Forces after the One Year War.
It specialized in sniping, hiding in the shadows to take down enemies from afar.
This tactic earned it the nickname of "Hidden Hizack."
Because it was remodeled as a sniper MS, it has an inherent disadvantage in close combat.
This is somewhat offset by the shield on its right shoulder, which can retract and extend to cover a larger area.
The heat sinks and stabilizers on its backpack have been dropped in favor of a larger propellant tank, allowing longer operating time in the field.
Though this machine demonstrated the ability to snipe enemies from beyond their own range, the usefulness of sniping itself was not fully recognized during the Gryps Conflict, and so it played only a limited role.

An attack mobile suit developed by Anaheim Electronics as a successor to the Hizack, the first new mass-produced mobile suit designed by the Federation Forces after the One Year War.
It combines the movable frame technology obtained from the stolen Gundam Mk-II with the Gundarium Gamma armor used in the Rick Dias, plus a wealth of Zeon know-how.
While retaining the good handling of the Hizack, it resolves the previous machine's generator output issues, allowing it to drive multiple beam weapons at the same time.
Its armament options are also increased, and it can use anything from One Year War-era equipment to the latest new weapons, both Zeon and Federation.
This machine was initially slated for delivery to the AEUG, but for political reasons the first batch of machines from the Granada arsenal was instead provided to the Titans free of charge, effectively handing them over to the enemy.
The data collected from the resulting skirmishes, however, far surpassed what could have been learned in simulations.

A general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit based on the Gundam Mk-II, developed by the Federation Forces for improved overall performance while also emphasizing ease of production.
This well-balanced and versatile machine could share equipment with the Hizack and GM II.
Its production began at the end of the Gryps Conflict, and it was expected to replace the GM II as the mainline MS of the Federation Forces.
However, due to its lack of distinctive features, production priority was given to other state-of-the-art machines.

A prototype transformable mobile suit developed by the Federation's elite Titans special forces, with simplified transformation mechanisms to make mass production possible.
The genius Paptimus Scirocco contributed to its design.
Using the latest technology of the time, such as a movable frame and magnet coating, this machine can change from a humanoid MS form to a stingray-like MA form in just 0.5 seconds.
By actively changing its mass balance and behavioral characteristics, it can harass enemies with a wide range of attacks.
While most transformable MS lose their melee capabilities when they change, this machine is able to maintain them in either form.
This machine was classified as a prototype under Titans jurisdiction.
Its model number indicates that it was the ninth machine developed at Zedan's Gate, the former A Baoa Qu.

A prototype transformable mobile suit developed by the Federation's elite Titans special forces, based on a design concept from the genius Paptimus Scirocco.
The Federation's Luna II arsenal was used for its development.
This machine boasts high specs in both bombardment and melee combat.
Its powerful Fedayeen rifle is a match for the Super Gundam's long rifle, and its shoulder-mounted mega particle cannons are highly effective in both close and long-range combat.
Its formidable weapons and high performance give it tremendous fighting abilities.
Despite its vast potential as a mobile weapon, the frame's complex structure proved too difficult to produce efficiently.
Mass production was abandoned, leaving only a few prototype units.

A prototype mobile suit developed by the Federation's elite Titans special forces for local warfare within the atmosphere.
With a design focused on flight performance, this machine shattered all records for flight duration in a non-transformable MS.
Its lightweight and aerodynamic body, thermonuclear jet engines, and expanded propellant tanks gave it excellent atmospheric flight capabilities.
Its armament was reduced to the absolute minimum, and mega particle cannons installed in its arms.
This machine could also be used in space.
However, its emphasis on flight led to numerous problems in terms of defense and versatility.
Therefore, development and mass production were abandoned after the prototype was completed.

A transformable mobile armor for Newtypes, developed by the Federation Forces with the assistance of the Newtype Laboratories.
This specialized machine is equipped with a psycommu interface which enables Newtypes and Cyber-Newtypes to transmit their thoughts directly to its control system.
The onboard psycommu system allows the machine to emit psycho-waves to suppress the pilot's consciousness and stimulate their fighting urges.
The remote control features that previously caused such stress for Cyber-Newtype pilots are also improved, but compared to previous Newtype systems, they still tend to erode the pilot's mind.
There is confirmation that three of these machines were used during the Gryps Conflict, with the pink-colored second unit piloted by Rosamia Badam.
Further demonstrating its potential, its control program could be adjusted for use by ordinary soldiers as well as Newtypes and Cyber-Newtypes.

A handmade transformable mobile armor developed aboard the Jupitris, a resource transportation ship that traveled between Jupiter and Earth.
Its powerful thrusters were designed for working within Jupiter's intense gravitational field.
Advances in mobile suit technology were quickly adopted in the machines used for the exploitation of Jupiter, and they were the first to make practical use of AMBAC maneuvering and thruster units with variable construction.
The Messala was based on this concept, and while its frame structure and device distribution differed from the machines developed in the Earth Sphere, its transformation mechanisms were similar to those of other transformable MS and MA.
This machine was a one-off machine designed by the genius Paptimus Scirocco of the Jupitris.
He continued to make adjustments to his masterpiece even after being summoned from the Jupiter Sphere by the Titans.

A handmade reconnaissance mobile suit developed aboard the Jupitris, a resource transportation ship that traveled between Jupiter and Earth.
Its primary role is to support other machines by searching for enemies, and thus its combat abilities are limited compared to the rest of the PMX series.
Nevertheless, it finds its niche as a high-performance mobile radar.
Its head is equipped with a radome that contains various built-in sensors, and it has a laser sensor on its left arm.
In addition to its superb search capabilities, this machine can perform high-precision intelligence analysis by linking these sensors to its excellent data-processing systems.
This machine has no handheld weapons, and its armament is only suitable for close combat and melee attacks.
For this reason, it is thought to have been developed for reconnaissance in force.
Like the rest of the PMX series designed by Paptimus Scirocco, this machine operated outside of Titans jurisdiction as part of Scirocco's private forces.

A handmade medium-range attack mobile suit developed aboard the Jupitris, a resource transportation ship that traveled between Jupiter and Earth.
Its large frame and wide variety of optional weapons enabled it to hold its own in combat against everything from MS to battleships.
This machine was distinguished by its impressive firepower.
It surpassed any other MS used in the Gryps Conflict, and was feared by both AEUG and Axis forces.
However, mobility was sacrificed in its design, and its close combat capabilities were limited.
Like the rest of the PMX series designed by Paptimus Scirocco, this machine operated outside of Titans jurisdiction as part of Scirocco's private forces.
The name "Palace Athene" comes from the goddess of war.
It is thought that Scirocco chose this name because he believed that the new era would be built by women.

A handmade prototype mobile suit developed aboard the Jupitris, a resource transportation ship that traveled between Jupiter and Earth, for the personal use of its designer Paptimus Scirocco.
This was Scirocco's fourth machine after the Messala, Palace Athene, and Bolinoak Sammahn.
Though its weapons are fairly standard, it is armored with a huge mass of Gundarium alloy, and its body unit houses a generator whose output surpasses that of an entire MS.
Its front skirt armor contains "hidden arms" that can be used for close combat and melee attacks, and the 50 thrusters all over its body can adapt to any vector.
For such a large machine, it has surprising mobility and acceleration, and its unique technology gives this machine extraordinary specs.
Although it is equipped with a psycommu system, what Zeon and the Earth Federation call "Newtype ability" is defined as "psychic" in the Jupiter Sphere, and so their devices use a different approach from the Federation and Anaheim Electronics.

A mass-produced mobile suit based on the Galbaldy Alpha developed by the former Principality of Zeon at the end of the One Year War.
Taking note of its superb performance, the Federation Forces confiscated and modernized this machine, and then formally adopted it.
Based on the Gelgoog, the Galbaldy Alpha also incorporated the close combat capabilities of the melee-specialized Gyan.
Though it was a first-generation MS, its performance was so impressive that the only change made to the Beta version was lighter weight to increase its mobility.
However, the machine's high mobility also made it difficult to control, and it is thought that it was used only by a few ace pilots as the Gryps Conflict began.

A transformable mobile armor developed by the Federation Forces' Oakland Laboratory.
This was the first machine capable of unassisted flight within the atmosphere.
By transforming its upper body into a disk shape, it could change into an aerodynamic lifting body, and its powerful propulsion systems gave it mobility rivaling that of fighter aircraft.
Its transformation took a mere 0.5 seconds, making even a single unit capable of three-dimensional combat.
Between its powerful large beam rifle and the MS form's melee capabilities, this machine had well-balanced combat performance.
Having demonstrated the usefulness of transformable MA, it was eventually deployed to many Federation bases.

A transformable mobile armor developed primarily by the Federation Forces' Oakland Laboratory, and tested at the Augusta Laboratory.
Though its body shape disregards aerodynamics, it can fly thanks to its immense thrust.
Together with the sudden changes of thrust direction made possible by its active thruster units, and an extraordinary mobility with no consideration for G-forces, this gives it unconventional performance in every sense.
The use of directional propulsion systems such as movable shields makes this machine capable of abrupt acceleration and course changes, but the thrust generated during these trajectory changes creates such G-forces that only Cyber-Newtypes and other pilots able to withstand this stress can handle it.
Several prototypes of this machine were tested in combat, and a small number were subsequently produced and deployed in areas such as Dakar.

A huge transformable mobile armor for Newtypes developed by the Murasame Laboratory, the Japanese branch of the Federation Forces' Newtype Laboratories.
Designed to capture and defend bases, this formidable machine boasts wide-ranging firepower and is protected by I-Field generators.
Its main form is the so-called mobile fortress, which is capable of flight thanks to the Minovsky craft system.
Vulnerable points such as joints are covered in armor, giving it robust defenses worthy of the fortress description.
In MS form, it can demonstrate its maximum firepower, and can use its bare hands to fight other mobile suits in close combat situations.
This makes it a useful form for anti-MS battle.
Its most distinctive feature is the psycommu system used to control the machine and its various weapons, and registered Newtype pilots can even use the system to control the machine from the outside.
However, this system also forces the pilot to fight, placing a huge burden on their psyche.

A transformable mobile suit independently developed by Axis to solve the problems created by its lack of trained pilots, and designed with an emphasis on mass production.
At the end of the One Year War, Zeon remnants fled to the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter, taking refuge at the asteroid base Axis which was used for mining and as a transfer station for the Jupiter route.
They developed the Gaza A and Gaza B as worker machines in order to expand their living quarters.
This machine was made using the production facilities of the Gaza A and Gaza B.
Designed mainly for mass bombardment, it is equipped with a knuckle buster and an enhanced generator.
However, since it is primarily a worker machine with added transformation mechanisms so that it can be used as a mobile armor, its mobility and maneuverability are far from impressive.
Though Axis gave this machine the development code MMT-1, the Federation classified it as the AMX-003 to differentiate it from other Axis mobile weapons.

A prototype mobile suit for Newtypes, conceived of by Axis as its flagship machine and created using Zeon's highest technology.
It was designed to be an MS version of the Elmeth, a prototype Newtype mobile armor developed by the former Principality of Zeon.
Inheriting the Elmeth's concept of combining a main machine with bit attack terminals, it was equipped with miniaturized bits known as "funnels."
As a descendant of the original machine, it was given the code name "Elmeth 2."
It is said that the machine itself was controlled via the psycommu system, making this the highest class of Newtype weapon in the entire Gryps Conflict.
Its overwhelming mobility, maneuverability, and responsiveness made it a truly high-performance MS.
Since only pilots with high Newtype abilities could operate this machine, it effectively became the personal unit of Haman Karn.
A Flying Armor specially designed for the Gundam Mk-II, affording it a means of re-entering Earth's atmosphere.

A transformable mobile suit independently developed by Axis to solve the problems created by its lack of trained pilots, and designed with an emphasis on mass production.
At the end of the One Year War, Zeon remnants fled to the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter, taking refuge at the asteroid base Axis which was used for mining and as a transfer station for the Jupiter route.
They developed the Gaza A and Gaza B as worker machines in order to expand their living quarters.
The Gaza C was made using the production facilities of the Gaza A and Gaza B.
Designed mainly for mass bombardment, it is equipped with a knuckle buster and an enhanced generator.
However, since it is primarily a worker machine with added transformation mechanisms so that it can be used as a mobile armor, its mobility and maneuverability are far from impressive.
This particular unit is the personal machine of Haman Karn, the true leader of Axis, and is colored accordingly.

A mobile suit developed by Edvard Raven, an engineer who once served as an assistant to the chief engineer responsible for Gundam development, Tem Ray.
While developing the mobile suit that would succeed the Rick Dias—the first to meet second-generation specifications—the Z Project was launched, which dramatically enhanced mobility through a transformation mechanism, but at the cost of an accompanying deformation mechanism that introduced problems with frame strength, resulting in the project's discontinuance.
The concept of a mobile suit capable of operating both on land and in space continued to garner interest, however, and the unit was redesigned as a non-deformable data collector.
It has more design similarities with the Rick Dias than the Hyaku Shiki, but the head resembles that of the Z Gundam.
Experimental in nature, the many trials and errors of the Z Project are apparent throughout, but this particular unit's versatility is undeniable.
Its dubious defensive capabilities aside, one could say that it effectively established itself as a high-performance example among second-generation mobile suits.
Despite being piloted by Quattro Bajeena and allegedly decked out in red like the Rick Dias (calling to mind Char Aznable), the very existence of this mobile suit is often called into question.

A successor to the AEUG transformable mobile suit Zeta Gundam.
It uses the transformation mechanisms of the Methuss, another AEUG transformable MS, to simplify the complex structure of the Zeta Gundam.
This makes it easier to produce and operate.
Despite its simpler transformation, the machine's waverider form has far higher acceleration than other MS.
In this form, the generators throughout its body can also be connected to charge up its mega beam rifle, giving it power rivaling that of the Zeta Gundam's hyper mega beam launcher.
With specs surpassing those of contemporary MS, it promised good cost-performance as a mass production machine.
However, due to the AEUG's financial constraints at the time, the development of the more versatile ZZ Gundam was given higher priority.
Though this machine never entered production, its concept was later inherited by the mass-produced transformable MS ReZEL.

A mass-produced version of the AEUG's Zeta Gundam, redesigned for greater ease of production by eliminating its transformation mechanisms.
Its head unit was also simplified in an attempt to further reduce costs.
However, this was not as cost-effective as expected.
Mass production plans focused instead on the Hyaku Shiki Kai and other MS of the Hyaku Shiki series, and the production of this machine was abandoned at the prototype stage.

An advanced version of the AEUG's Hyaku-Shiki, with increased thrust and lighter weight.
The Hyaku-Shiki's wing binder backpack was replaced with drum-shaped directional thrusters and high-mobility vernier stabilizers, improving its mobility and maneuverability.
The machine's armament is also supplemented with fixed weapons such as pulse laser cannons and beam gatling guns, which can be attached to shoulder hardpoints.
As a result, it boasts high offensive strength and extensibility.

A machine independently developed by Anaheim Electronics, based on analysis of data from the Gundam Mk-II which the AEUG captured from the Titans during a raid on Green Noa 1.
Further development of the Gundam Mk-II's movable frame technology, combined with Gundarium Gamma and the AEUG's own proprietary technology, gave this machine an even greater ability to reproduce human body movements.
Its generator and armament were also improved, resulting in performance far surpassing that of the Gundam Mk-II.

This machine was developed to compensate for the low firepower of the AEUG's mass-produced mobile suit Nemo.
A Nemo II type was initially developed in an attempt to increase its power by raising its generator output, but it had problems that could not be resolved.
As a result, the Nemo II was redesigned into this support MS equipped with a beam cannon.
This gave it higher firepower even without an increase in generator output.

A version of the Zeta Gundam constructed by Anaheim Electronics for the resistance group Karaba, who were considering the purchase of transformable mobile suits for atmospheric use.
Based on the highly versatile Zeta Gundam, this machine can change into a waverider form with increased mobility, which can enter the atmosphere on its own and function as an SFS (Sub Flight System) in joint operations with other machines.
Karaba also requested the preparation of an optional booster, which extends the machine's cruising range and enables it to reach the operation area more quickly.
This allows it to reach any area on Earth within the minimum possible time.
Incidentally, the number in its name means that this is the third version of the machine, rather than the third unit produced.

A machine developed as the "ultimate Gundam," as part of the AEUG's Project Zeta.
Like the transforming and separating ZZ Gundam also developed under Project Zeta, it has a transformation mechanism made up of three units that can dock and separate.
It is also built with the very latest movable frame, making it a flexible and high-performance machine.
Its armament includes a high-output beam smartgun and a quasi-psycommu remote weapon called an incom that is stored in its head.
This gives the machine extremely high offensive power, while its thruster propulsion and the stabilators installed all over its body provide excellent mobility and maneuverability.
The quest for technological supremacy was very nearly achieved with this machine.
Its most distinctive trait is the installation of an artificial intelligence system called ALICE.
Which can analyze the combat situation on its own and, ultimately, take control of the entire machine and its complex systems.
By entrusting control to ALICE, it could rival the performance of a Newtype machine.
When the S Gundam was assigned to the Federation's elite Task Force Alpha, ALICE was configured to operate behind the scenes in order to conceal it from those who feared the spread of unmanned mobile suits.

The first machine developed by the Federation Forces at the asteroid base Pezun as part of the X series, a plan for the creation of next-generation general-purpose mobile suits.
As an MS, it used orthodox technologies to check its total balance and clearances from every angle, giving it well-balanced performance as well as good handling and operation.
However, its greatest feature is the ability to use a wide variety of options such as weapons and search units, thanks to its high-output generator.
Its basic forms are the "first armament" with handheld beam weapons, the "second armament" for long-range attack, and the "third armament" equipped with projectile weapons.
By selecting the right parts, it can adapt to any role and match the performance of a specialized MS.
This unit represents the third armament, equipped with handheld projectile weapons.
It was used mainly by the rebellious New Desides, formerly a Federation training corps.

MA prototype transformable mobile suit developed by the AEUG's Project Zeta.
For the sake of maximum offensive power and versatility, this machine was designed to combine the functions of the One Year War-era Gundam and G-Mecha.
This MS specializes in high output and high firepower, as per the trend at its creation, and is thus technologically state-of-the-art.
It is armed with a high mega cannon and various other powerful weapons, but its operating time with default equipment at maximum power is very short, and some claim that its endurance is less than 15 minutes.
Another feature is its transformation into the G-Fortress, a mobile armor form with 1.5 times the thrust of its MS form, giving it the utmost mobility.
It can also separate into a Core Top made up of its upper body and a Core Block, a Core Base made up of its lower body and a Core Block, and a Core Fighter that forms its abdomen and main cockpit.
These three sections also function as aerospace fighter craft.

A high-performance form of the prototype transformable mobile suit ZZ Gundam developed by the AEUG's Project Zeta.
It is covered with extra armor and has various enhancements to support the addition of full armor equipment.
Due to its complex transformation and docking mechanisms, the ZZ Gundam's defensive strength is somewhat low.
By adding extra armor to its structural weak points, its defenses are significantly improved, and its offensive strength is also enhanced with the addition of optional weapons.
This enhancement plan was used to combat the giant mobile suit Quin Mantha, the strongest weapon of the Neo Zeon forces.
During this decisive battle, it was able to show off its high performance against its opponent.
This machine is unable to transform or separate when wearing the extra armor.

An upgraded version of the general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit GM that was developed by the Federation Forces during the One Year War.
Following the original GM produced at the end of the war, and the minor changes that went into the GM II, this was a newly designed mainline mass production type.
This machine actively incorporated new technologies such as a backpack identical to that of the Gundam Mk-II, while retaining its original ease of production.
This enabled it to handle melee and close combat, and gave it better mobility and maneuverability than previous mass-produced MS.
Its handheld beam rifle and shield were improved with higher firepower and durability, and it had a full lineup of optional weapons.
Thanks to its high versatility, this machine could also function as a medium and long-range support type.
Its performance was high for a mass production type, so there were multiple production facilities.
However, due to constant adjustments to production numbers and changes in equipment, it was never extensively mass-produced.

An Axis transformable mobile suit developed as a successor to the mass-produced Gaza C.
It was created with a strong emphasis on ease of production, so that Axis could produce a huge number of units as swiftly as possible.
With the ability to change into a mobile armor form that functions as a movable battery, and a generator box that contains the main engine, the design concepts of this machine are excellent.
However, it is still designed purely for mass production, and its performance as a single unit is nothing spectacular.
Its MS form is especially weak in comparison to other mainline MS models of its era.
Nonetheless, the ability to use Gaza C production lines was a point in its favor, and its production and deployment gradually increased.
It was used by the Neo Zeon forces until the end of the war.

A transformable attack mobile suit that belongs to the mainline mass-produced Gaza series developed by Axis.
Based on worker machines, the Gaza series emphasized ease of production and were not perfectly suited for combat, with lingering vulnerabilities in their frame construction.
This machine, with its remodeled frame, was designed to handle genuine anti-MS combat.
Though it shared many parts with the Gaza D, this was essentially a newly designed machine.
In addition to high mobility in its MA form, it also had enhanced firepower thanks to the hyper knuckle buster that served as its main weapon.
Due to its high performance, this machine fought as part of the main force during the First Neo Zeon War.

A prototype general-purpose mobile suit developed by the Neo Zeon forces (formerly Axis) after the end of the Gryps Conflict in order to carry out a full-scale Earth invasion operation.
This machine was designed to fight on the front lines in tandem with the Zssa.
Former Principality of Zeon technicians who had dispersed throughout the Earth Sphere were reunited to develop this machine, and thus its form was heavily influenced by traditional Zeon tastes.
One example is its built-in close combat armament, thought to have been inspired by that of the mass-produced ground combat MS Gouf.
It also incorporates current technologies borrowed from Federation MS, such as the movable frame.
Its overall balance is excellent, and it is also easy for pilots to handle.
It was used up until the end of the First Neo Zeon War.

A mainline mobile suit developed and deployed by the Neo Zeon forces at the beginning of the First Neo Zeon War.
This machine was designed to be used in tandem with the Galluss J, taking on the role of rear support.
In addition to countless missiles all over its body, it can also be equipped with a booster pod to give it flight capabilities, enabling it to attack surface bases from above.
By making these additional functions thoroughly optional, the MS itself can take on different forms depending on the strategy and battle situation.
And by detaching optional parts at will, it can rid itself of deadweight such as damaged parts and empty weapons.
This flexibility is ultimately its greatest strength.
The Neo Zeon forces originally had very few support MS, and so this machine was widely deployed and used until the end of the First Neo Zeon War.

This high-performance mobile suit was developed by the Neo Zeon forces as a testbed for a quasi-psycommu system that could be used by ordinary soldiers and pilots with minimal Newtype ability.
Its most distinctive feature is the telescoping mechanism that allows it to extend its arms up to 50 meters.
These extensions consist of four cables and a flexible main pipe.
This machine was supposed to be able to perform all-range attacks within a 100-meter radius of its body, but the failure of the quasi-psycommu system prevented it from reaching its full potential.
As a result, while the machine's large thrusters gave it high mobility, the quasi-psycommu system was unable to perform a true all-range attack, and thus only two prototypes were built before production was abandoned.

A Neo Zeon machine developed for use by knights during the First Neo Zeon War.
Designed to be a successor to the Principality of Zeon's general-purpose prototype mobile suit Gyan, it had very high melee combat abilities.
This machine was customized for use by high-ranking commanders called "knights," and classified as an attack type with an emphasis on melee combat.
While its structure is relatively standard compared to Neo Zeon's other mainline MS, it is armed with a variety of close combat weapons such as a high-output beam saber and a bayonet-equipped beam rifle.
Its ranged combat performance is also very solid.
The prototype version of this machine showed promising performance when it was introduced into combat, but it never entered mass production.

A prototype mobile suit with docking and transformation mechanisms, developed by the Neo Zeon forces during the First Neo Zeon War.
It is comprised of two autonomous blocks, the upper "Bawoo Attacker" and lower "Bawoo Nutter," which can dock and separate.
It has high mobility and melee combat ability, and by separating into a manned Bawoo Attacker and an unmanned Bawoo Nutter operated via radio control, it can operate freely both inside and outside the atmosphere.
Its armament includes a beam rifle and beam sabers, unusually standard equipment for a Neo Zeon MS, but this also increases its versatility.
It also has mega particle cannons in its hybrid shield, and projectile weapons such as grenade launchers, giving it relatively heavy armament compared to the MS of its time.
This unit is a prototype assigned to Neo Zeon pilot Glemy Toto, and emblazoned on its front skirt armor are the Chinese characters for "dragon" and "flight."

The mass-produced version of the Bawoo, a prototype mobile suit with docking and transformation mechanisms that was developed by the Neo Zeon forces during the First Neo Zeon War.
It is comprised of two autonomous blocks, the upper "Bawoo Attacker" and lower "Bawoo Nutter," which can dock and separate.
It has high mobility and melee combat ability, and by separating into a manned Bawoo Attacker and an unmanned Bawoo Nutter operated via radio control, it can operate freely both inside and outside the atmosphere.
Its armament includes a beam rifle and beam sabers, unusually standard equipment for a Neo Zeon MS, but this also increases its versatility.
It also has mega particle cannons in its hybrid shield, and projectile weapons such as grenade launchers, giving it relatively heavy armament compared to the MS of its time.
Since the prototype showed promising results during combat, and due to its versatility and ease of operation by ordinary soldiers, this machine was mass-produced without any major changes aside from its green coloring.

An enhanced version of the Rick Dias developed by Anaheim Electronics, equipped with "Grye Binders" that contain beam cannons and vectored thrusters.
The result is a heavy assault mobile suit with increased offensive power and maximum speed.
Compared to the base machine, this version has upgraded back binders, rear skirts, and knee armor, with a maximum speed said to be 1.5 to 2.5 times that of the original.
It was initially designed for the personal use of AEUG pilot Quattro Bajeena, and research suggests that its initial code number was RMS-099SR, but deployment plans were suspended when Quattro switched to the Hyaku-Shiki instead.
Thus this machine ended up in the hands of Zeon remnants via backroom political dealings.
It is also said that some units were passed along to Karaba ace pilots, but this remains unverified.

This machine is a version of Neo Zeon's general-purpose mobile suit Zaku III, which was developed as the legitimate successor to the Zaku II, with customizations for use by Cyber-Newtypes.
As well as swapping in new optional equipment, it has additional improvements for even higher mobility.
In particular, its replacement backpack significantly increases its generator and thruster output.
In striving for the pinnacle of the general-purpose MS concept, it achieves high enough performance to compete against fourth-generation machines.
This was the personal unit of Neo Zeon pilot Mashymre Cello, and proved its worth by exhibiting great performance as part of Haman's forces at the end of the First Neo Zeon War.

An improved and enhanced version of the Principality of Zeon's mass-produced mobile suit Gelgoog.
Originally created at Axis for use as a training unit, it had high potential even though it was an upgrade to an older machine, and thus ended up being used in combat.
Compared to the base machine, it had higher overall performance, and while there were no major changes to its form, the improved armor materials and generator gave it better mobility.
Its armament differed greatly from the standard melee weapons of the base machine, consisting mainly of ranged weapons, with most of its fixed armament consisting of projectile weapons.
Its name, "Regelgu," is short for "Refined Gelgoog."

A transformable mobile armor that was rushed into combat by Neo Zeon to shore up its fighting strength at the end of the First Neo Zeon War.
Originally it was intended to be a large MA similar to the Principality of Zeon's Big-Zam, but because it was deployed before its construction was completed, temporary limbs had to be added to the completed body.
Designed to be a large mobile armor, it is equipped with a large generator and heavy weapons such as a high mega cannon, despite the small size of its body.
In MA form, it not only demonstrates maximum mobility, but can also link its generator directly to its weapons to further increase the output of its beams.
Three units of this machine were produced for the "3-D" mobile suit team, and participated in a joint operation between Neo Zeon and the Republic of Zeon forces.

This machine was developed exclusively for use by the Royal Guard, which was responsible for the personal protection of Axis VIPs.
It was created by upgrading the already highly regarded Galbaldy Beta, and specializing it for melee combat.
The Royal Guard usually operate in pairs when performing their escort duties.
This blue unit is tuned for use by Nee Gylen, whose duty is to protect the target's right flank.
Thus the machine's right shoulder has larger armor, and its right arm is bolstered with extra protection.
It can attack in tandem with the symmetrically designed Gaz-L, but often plays a more defensive role so as not to allow enemies near the target.

This machine was developed exclusively for use by the Royal Guard, which was responsible for the personal protection of Axis VIPs.
It was created by upgrading the already highly regarded Galbaldy Beta, and specializing it for melee combat.
The Royal Guard usually operate in pairs when performing their escort duties.
This red unit is tuned for use by Lance Gylen, whose duty is to protect the target's left flank.
Thus the machine's left shoulder has larger armor, and its left arm is bolstered with extra protection.
It can attack in tandem with the symmetrically designed Gaz-R, but often plays a more defensive role so as not to allow enemies near the target.

A fourth-generation mobile suit developed by Neo Zeon at the end of the First Neo Zeon War.
Designed for maximum output and firepower, this dinosaurian machine has extremely high fighting strength for a single unit.
It is a Newtype MS equipped with a psycommu system, which requires a Newtype or a Cyber-Newtype to display its full potential.
Therefore, the countless mega particle cannons and unique psycommu weapons installed throughout its body are all designed to be controlled using Newtype abilities.
The only unit that was deployed in combat was piloted by the Cyber-Newtype Chara Soon, and proved to be capable of fighting on equal terms with high-performance enemy machines.

Developed by Axis with the aim of turning the Newtype-use Mobile Armor Elmeth—pride of the One Year War—into a mobile suit.
The biggest hurdle to overcome was reducing the size and weight of the unmanned portable gun battery "bit," which was achieved by adopting the energy CAP method and making it possible to refill energy and fuel from the mobile suit itself.
The ability to carry psycommu weapon "Funnels" for all-range attacks was also implemented.
Its unique fuselage, with a terminal built into its rearward-extending head for transmitting and receiving psycho-waves, allows it to functionally double as a Newtype Machine.
By retooling the propulsion unit into a four-piece shoulder binder, its angle can be adjusted to achieve response and mobility rates that far outpace those of other mobile suits of the era in times of high mobility warfare.
Piloted by Haman Karn since the end of the Gryps War, her unparalleled piloting skill has exhibited such masterful and exemplary combat power that it has cemented a strong image of Axis among its contemporaries.

A prototype mobile suit created by Neo Zeon using spare parts from the Newtype mobile suit AMX-004 Qubeley, with the objective of mass production.
While the original AMX-004 had excellent performance, high Newtype abilities were required to operate it, so it effectively became Haman Karn's personal machine and was seldom deployed in the battlefield.
Thus this machine was remodeled and updated with mass production in mind, and also to discover more people with high Newtype abilities.
Though details were changed, its basic structure is unaltered from that of the AMX-004.
The thrusters concentrated in its shoulder binders, and the machine control performed via the psycommu system, give it superb mobility and responsiveness.
Its core feature is the use of psycommu weapons to perform all-range attacks, giving this machine very high fighting strength.
This particular unit had its psycommu system tuned for use by the Neo Zeon clone Newtype Elpeo Ple, and was painted in black colors.

A prototype mobile suit created by Neo Zeon using spare parts from the Newtype mobile suit AMX-004 Qubeley, with the objective of mass production.
While the original AMX-004 had excellent performance, high Newtype abilities were required to operate it, so it effectively became Haman Karn's personal machine and was seldom deployed in the battlefield.
Thus this machine was remodeled and updated with mass production in mind, and also to discover more people with high Newtype abilities.
Though details were changed, its basic structure is unaltered from that of the AMX-004.
The thrusters concentrated in its shoulder binders, and the machine control performed via the psycommu system, give it superb mobility and responsiveness.
Its core feature is the use of psycommu weapons to perform all-range attacks, giving this machine very high fighting strength.
This particular unit had its psycommu system tuned for use by the Neo Zeon clone Newtype Elpeo Ple, and was painted in black colors.

A prototype mobile suit created by Neo Zeon using spare parts from the Newtype mobile suit AMX-004 Qubeley, with the objective of mass production.
While the original AMX-004 had excellent performance, high Newtype abilities were required to operate it, so it effectively became Haman Karn's personal machine and was seldom deployed in the battlefield.
Thus this machine was remodeled and updated with mass production in mind, and also to discover more people with high Newtype abilities.
Though details were changed, its basic structure is unaltered from that of the AMX-004.
The thrusters concentrated in its shoulder binders, and the machine control performed via the psycommu system, give it superb mobility and responsiveness.
Its core feature is the use of psycommu weapons to perform all-range attacks, giving this machine very high fighting strength.
This particular unit had its psycommu system tuned for use by the Neo Zeon clone Newtype Ple-Two, and was painted in red colors.
It was also equipped with a psycommu controller that enabled remote control of the machine, making it an ideal testbed for the development of even stronger types.

A mass-produced mobile suit for Newtypes developed in secret by Glemy Toto, who was preparing to revolt against Neo Zeon regent Haman Karn.
Based on the Qubeley, it was redesigned for use by the Newtype Corps that Glemy planned to use as a trump card in his takeover of Neo Zeon.
Various improvements were made to increase its firepower relative to the base machine.
In addition to performing all-range attacks with its funnels, it was also equipped with active cannons, allowing it to combine all-range attacks with bombardment.
Due to the minimal differences between the pilots, there was no need to individually adjust their psycommu systems, and so its performance was consistent across all units.
This unit was painted in the grey colors of Glemy's forces, and deployed in actual combat as an escort to Ple-Two's Quin Mantha.
It was later repainted in dark blue prior to engaging Haman's Royal Guard.

A redesigned and mass-produced mobile suit based on the Qubeley, a mobile suit developed for Newtype use.
Glemy Toto, who spearheaded Neo Zeon's efforts to augment its Newtype forces with Cyber-Newtypes, developed it in secret as the trump card in his rebellion against Haman Karn.
While it takes many of its design cues from the Qubeley, it was also heavily redesigned to improve its performance and firepower, with fixed active cannons.

A fourth-generation mobile suit developed by Neo Zeon during the First Neo Zeon War.
Equipped with a quasi-psycommu system that lets ordinary pilots perform all-range attacks with mechanical assistance, it is a powerful machine with many beam weapons powered by a high-output generator Based on the design of the Psycho Gundam Mk-II, it was developed to be a miniaturized version for mass production.
Thanks to its large mobile armor-class generator and its built-in beam and projectile weapons, this huge machine stands more than 20 meters tall, and though technically an MS it has the firepower of a fortress.
The standard type has "beam hands" that can be remotely controlled via wires, and also function as anti-MS stun guns.
This machine defeated the Zaku III in a competition to select Neo Zeon's new mainline MS, and was then mass-produced in small numbers.
The first of these were assigned to the Space Wolf Team led by the veteran officer Rakan Dahkaran.

A fourth-generation mobile suit developed by Neo Zeon during the First Neo Zeon War.
Equipped with a quasi-psycommu system that lets ordinary pilots perform all-range attacks with mechanical assistance, it is a powerful machine with many beam weapons powered by a high-output generator. Based on the design of the Psyco Gundam Mk-II, it was developed to be a miniaturized version for mass production.
