Thanks to its large mobile armor-class generator and its built-in beam and projectile weapons, this huge machine stands more than 20 meters tall, and though technically an MS it has the firepower of a fortress.
The commander type has wireless "beam hands" that can be remotely controlled via laser communication, and when these are launched they reveal hidden arms that can hold extra weapons such as beam sabers.
This commander type was assigned to the veteran officer Rakan Dahkaran, and effectively became his personal machine.

The successor to the Psycho Gundam, a huge transformable Newtype mobile armor developed by the Federation's Murasame Laboratory.
Inheriting the base machine's concept of a mobile fortress for capturing and defending bases, it also adds new weapons that can be remotely operated via the psycommu system.
While the base machine could attack over a wide area by firing diffuse beams in a forward direction, this version has mega beam cannons all over its body, allowing it to fire volleys in every direction.
Its multiple reflector bits can not only deflect enemy beams, but also enable a kind of makeshift all-range attack, giving it overwhelming fighting strength.
This machine was used by the Titans at the end of the Gryps Conflict, and abandoned when it was badly damaged in battle.
It was subsequently recovered and repaired by Neo Zeon, who used it against the Federation Forces.

A huge Newtype mobile suit developed by Axis at the end of the First Neo Zeon War.
It was created to increase the output and armament of the mobile suit to its utmost limit.
Incorporating all the MS technology accumulated since the Principality of Zeon era, as well as technology from the Psycho Gundam Mk-II that was recovered during the Gryps Conflict, this enormous humanoid weapon was equipped with a large generator as well as mobility high enough for melee combat.
It boasted high offensive strength thanks to the mega particle cannons all over its body, and could perform all-range attacks using the funnels it carried on its back.
Its shoulder binders also housed a mega particle deflection system that could nullify enemy beam weapons.
This gave it fighting strength surpassing any MS of its era.
However, because extremely high Newtype abilities were required to control the machine, its funnels, and its many mega particle cannons, even the Neo Zeon forces had virtually no pilots who could meet this requirement.

The final production version of the mass-produced ground combat mobile suit Dom, which was developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War.
As a local warfare type specialized for desert combat, it was upgraded for improved ground combat capabilities.
Its hover system had longer operating time than the base machine, and its thrust was dramatically increased.
Though its mobility in the desert surpassed that of any contemporary MS, its overall performance was not quite as good.
This machine inherits the Dom's standard armament of a giant bazooka and heat saber, but some units also carry unique weapons.
To decrease air resistance during high-speed hovering movement, it does not carry a shield.
Due to the shifting war situation, this machine failed to reach the battlefield before the end of the One Year War, and was used by Zeon remnants and tribal groups on the African front instead.

The final production version of the mass-produced ground combat mobile suit Dom, which was developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War.
As a local warfare type specialized for desert combat, it was upgraded for improved ground combat capabilities.
Its hover system had longer operating time than the base machine, and its thrust was dramatically increased.
Though its mobility in the desert surpassed that of any contemporary MS, its overall performance was not quite as good.
This machine inherits the Dom's standard armament of a giant bazooka and heat saber, but some units also carry unique weapons.
To decrease air resistance during high-speed hovering movement, it does not carry a shield.
This unit is a customized version of the MS-09G created for the One Year War veteran known as Desert Rommel.
Its mobility is greatly improved, and it is painted in red and black colors.

A general-purpose mobile suit developed by Axis in preparation for an Earth invasion operation.
Designed for overwhelming power in ground combat, it was also versatile enough for use in space.
Together with its remarkable ease of production, this made it a mainline machine in the later part of the First Neo Zeon War.
Because it was descended from the Principality of Zeon's mass-produced ground combat MS Dom, this machine was developed at Axis as the "final form" of the Dom, and it has been claimed that the One Year War-era Rick Dom II was used as a base.
Its armament included close combat weapons such as a beam tomahawk, and triple beam cannons as standard equipment.
It also carried revolutionary vernier-equipped throwing weapons called tri-blades, whose blades rotated to slice through their targets.
Since it was so easy to maintain and expand, many different variations were considered, and there is evidence that a few such prototypes were actually built.

A worker mobile suit created by Gemon Bajack, who works as a junk dealer in the Side 1 colony of Shangri-La.
He constructed this "mobile worker" for use in his junk business.
Because it was made to carry huge junk parts, this machine has size and power rivaling that of a military MS.
Not only is its output remarkable for a civilian machine, but its fighting abilities are not far short of a combat MS.
Incorporating new systems such as a movable cockpit that can move around on rails, it has superb handling, and very high performance for a privately created MS.
This unit was used by Gemon himself, and painted in grey colors.

A worker mobile suit created by Gemon Bajack, who works as a junk dealer in the Side 1 colony of Shangri-La.
He constructed this "mobile worker" for use in his junk business.
Because it was made to carry huge junk parts, this machine has size and power rivaling that of a military MS.
Not only is its output remarkable for a civilian machine, but its fighting abilities are not far short of a combat MS.
Incorporating new systems such as a movable cockpit that can move around on rails, it has superb handling, and very high performance for a privately created MS.
This unit was used by the disguised Yazan Gable, and painted in wine-red colors.

An amphibious mobile suit developed by Neo Zeon for local warfare.
As with the Principality of Zeon's amphibious Hy-Gogg, the Capule's arms are retractable.
The use of a spherical form enables high-speed movement both above and below the water's surface, while simultaneously boosting the machine's ability to withstand extreme water pressure.

A large mobile worker from Moon Moon.
As a machine used for colony construction in the U.C. 0030s, it can be considered an early forerunner of mobile suits.
Standing approximately thirty meters tall, it is considerably larger than a typical mobile suit. With no system for walking, it relies on hovering for locomotion.
Most units were dismantled after they became obsolete, but one survived on the naturalist colony Moon Moon, where it was enshrined in ivy as an object of worship.

An AEUG mobile suit support mecha that combined a mega bazooka launcher with an SFS (Sub Flight System).
Able to operate both in and out of the atmosphere, it was equipped with basic living facilities, movable command center functions, and the ability to carry mobile suits, making it an extremely effective support weapon.
This was one of the few manned SFS of its time.
It could be be controlled either from the cockpit or by one of the mobile suits it was carrying.

A mobile suit developed by Neo Zeon engineers with the superb versatility of the Zaku series in mind.
Thanks to the cutting-edge technology used in its creation, it could achieve high extensibility by swapping in different option parts.
However, this design gave it inadequate basic performance compared to other Neo Zeon MS, which emphasized high output and firepower.
Defeated in trials against the heavily-armed Doven Wolf, it never achieved the status of a mainline machine, despite bearing the Zaku name.
Because it focuses on versatility and extensibility, this machine has no unique weapons of its own.
Instead, optional armaments can be flexibly swapped in as needed, supported by basic weapons such as a head-mounted mega particle cannon.
A few prototypes were deployed in combat, but failed to achieve any noteworthy results.

A modified Hizack equipped with an advanced EWAC system for reconnaissance and patrol.
Although it was developed by the Federation Forces, it fell into the possession of Neo Zeon's Blue Corps, which now operates it.

Aiming to achieve the ultimate firepower in a mobile suit, its specifications gear toward an increase in the basic capabilities of the Gundam ZZ, a fourth-generation mobile suit equipped with even a docking and transformation mechanism.
This is carried out by the Neo Core Fighter control unit, the Core Top upper block, and the Core Base lower block, the three of which combine to form a G-Fortress patrol unit that can then transform into the Mobile Suit Gundam ZZ.
With three generators on board, it is capable of equipping multiple high-output beam weapons, including the High Mega Cannon mounted on its head which exhibits power well beyond the standard for mobile suit armaments.
Although it's tough to spot alterations to this unit at a glance, its overall performance is greatly improved through the addition of armor, anti-beam coating for enhanced defensive capabilities, increased generator output, and a much larger backpack.

Following the Principality of Zeon's Earth Invasion Operation, the retaking of seized critical positions became an urgent priority for the Earth Federation Forces.
The GM Night Seeker was a mobile suit developed for the purpose of descending from super high altitude on missions to launch assaults on these key locations.

The RGM-79KC GM Interceptor Custom, developed to defend fleets, equipped with the additional weapon unit, Fellow Booster, on its back.
The Fellow Booster serves to both expand the operational range of mobile suits in outer space and enhance their firepower.

This customized machine is a variation of the Zaku II F developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War.
The most immaculate F types were chosen to receive minor upgrades, becoming new machines designated as the MS-06FS.
unlike the standard F type, the FS type has four head-mounted vulcan guns.
This particular unit belongs to Shin Matsunaga, also known as the "White Wolf of Solomon," hence its distinctive color scheme.
It has also been tuned up for maximum mobility.

A higher-performance version of the Zaku II J, a mass-produced ground combat mobile suit developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War.
To improve its terrestrial combat capabilities, the joints and leg frame were reinforced, since they bore extra stress under gravity.
The backpack was also upgraded and extra thrusters added, giving it far greater ground combat performance than the base model.
Despite these advances, priority was given to the mass-produced ground combat MS Gouf that was being developed at the same time, and only a few units of this machine were produced.

A variation of the Zaku II developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War.
This version is an improvement on the MS-06R-1, which was hampered by a limited operating time.
The R type was praised by many ace pilots for its high performance.
However, its complex operational requirements and high cost prevented mass production, with the result that these machines were rarer than F types.
This particular unit belongs to Anavel Gato, the "Nightmare of Solomon," and its body is painted in his personal colors.

A Gouf variant with enhanced mobility, this mobile suit is known as a combat engineer.
Production was carried out chiefly at California Base in North America, and though 22 were produced, three of these were never received.

Based on the general-purpose prototype mobile suit Gyan, this version was upgraded for combat deployment through the cooperation of Zimmad and Zeonic.
The major improvements were better mobility in space and a revised armament.
The missile launchers that were previously built into its shield were omitted, and instead its melee defenses were increased by changing the armor material.
Its firepower was enhanced with a distinctive prototype beam bayonet, whose tip could also be used as a beam saber.
Two completed prototypes were assigned to the flagship of Zeon's Armored Assault Force, but this machine was never mass-produced.

A heavy mobile gun platform developed by the Principality of Zeon as a supplement to its mobile suits, in order to maintain its fighting strength on the lunar surface.
This unique machine normally moved on caterpillar tracks, but during combat it could fly using the rocket engines on its underside.
It was assigned to the defense of the Granada base as a movable battery, with twin projectile cannons as its main armament.
However, the slow speed of its caterpillar tracks and the short flight time of its rocket engines made it a problematic machine.
It was later used as a base for the development of the Adzam.

This machine was developed in an attempt to miniaturize the Principality of Zeon's prototype Newtype mobile armor Braw-Bro.
It was based on the prototype MA Zakrello, with all upgrade work completed within roughly one month.
In addition to the Zakrello's diffuse beam cannon and heat sickles, it was also armed with two smaller versions of the Braw-Bro's wired psycommu mega particle cannons.
This machine was deployed at A Baoa Qu, and used as a psycommu trainer for Newtype pilots.

A modified version of the Principality of Zeon's mass-produced space mobile armor Bigro, designed for anti-ship attacks.
A large missile more than 100 meters long is attached to its underside.
To compensate for this extra weight, two boosters were added to supplement the machine's thrust, giving it enough mobility for hit-and-run tactics.
This machine has enough performance to take the vanguard and attack an enemy fleet in battle, though the weight of its extra equipment means that it requires a skilled pilot to perform attitude control when moving at high speeds.

This machine was prepared by former Chimaira Corps ace pilot Uma Lightning for his reunion with fellow Chimaira Corps ace Johnny Ridden, the "Crimson Lightning."
Although from the outside it resembles Zeon's general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit Gelgoog, its interior was created by Anaheim Electronics using the very best new components.
It features a Federation-made linear seat with arm rakers, and magnet coating even more efficient than the type used during the One Year War.
Though it looks like a Zeon MS, it makes extensive use of Federation Forces technology, and so this unusual machine could also be considered a Federation MS.
This unit was later repainted in Johnny Ridden's signature red colors and given to Led Wayline, a pilot assigned to the FSS.

A transformable mobile armor developed primarily by the Federation Forces' Oakland Laboratory, and tested at the Augusta Laboratory.
Though its body shape disregards aerodynamics, it can fly thanks to its immense thrust.
Together with the sudden changes of thrust direction made possible by its active thruster units, and an extraordinary mobility with no consideration for G-forces, this gives it unconventional performance in every sense.
The use of directional propulsion systems such as movable shields makes this machine capable of abrupt acceleration and course changes, but the thrust generated during these trajectory changes creates such G-forces that only Cyber-Newtypes and other pilots able to withstand this stress can handle it.
This particular unit is operated by Ingrid 0, a Cyber-Newtype member of the Chimaira Corps.
The movable shields on its arms are enlarged, and various other parts of the machine have been remodeled.
For some reason it is painted in Johnny Ridden's signature red colors, and the movable shields bear his emblem.

A form that adds supplemental thrusters to increase its cruising range and includes Chobham Armor.
It was known as the White The-O.

A work mobile suit manufactured by Monotone Mouse.
It was cobbled together with junk parts collected from scrapped mobile suits.
Although it was intended for colony repair work, it repurposes a generator and movable frame from a top-of-the-line combat mobile suit, granting it exceptionally high performance for a civilian machine.
Because it is a civilian machine, it does not have any firearms.
Nevertheless, it can still engage in mobile suit combat by utilizing seven work tools, including a rivet gun, wired bombs, and a magnet anchor.

A prototype mobile suit for Newtypes, personally designed by Amuro Ray of One Year War fame.
This machine was designed based on generations of Gundam-type data and equipped with the best technology available at the time.
It has incredible responsiveness due to its partial use of psycho-frame, a revolutionary material that incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality.
On its back are fin funnels, special Newtype weapons controlled through psycommu to unleash all-range attacks.
Combining three or more fin funnels allows them to be used as a beam weapon-nullifying I-Field generator, and thus these high-performance weapons combine both attack and defense.

A prototype mobile suit for Newtypes, personally designed by Amuro Ray of One Year War fame.
This machine was designed based on generations of Gundam-type data and equipped with the best technology available at the time.
It has incredible responsiveness due to its partial use of psycho-frame, a revolutionary material that incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality.
On its back are fin funnels, special Newtype weapons controlled through psycommu to unleash all-range attacks.
Combining three or more fin funnels allows them to be used as a beam weapon-nullifying I-Field generator, and thus these high-performance weapons combine both attack and defense.

A version of the Zeta Gundam, which was developed as part of Anaheim Electronics' Project Zeta, with a simplified transformation system. It replaced the Zeta Gundam's independent transformation ability, the biggest impediment to mass production, with a BWS (Back Weapon System) that allowed it to be converted into a fighter form.
During the Second Neo Zeon War, one prototype unit was deployed to the Federation's Londo Bell task force, where it was used in combat against the Neo Zeon forces.
Its name, "Re-GZ," stands for "Refined Gundam Zeta."

A general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit formally adopted by the Federation Forces in U.C. 0089 as the successor to the GM series.
Fundamentally designed for long service life and high versatility, it was able to operate in space, within colonies, and under gravity with virtually no modifications. Optional weaponry allowed it to fight in various conditions.
While maintaining the necessary level of armor strength, it also adopted a lightweight body that would improve its maneuverability and was meant to be easy to maintain, making it the perfect next-generation machine and a mainline MS that performed admirably for the Federation Forces for a long time.

A version of the Zeta Gundam, which was developed as part of Anaheim Electronics' Project Zeta, with a simplified transformation system. This is the fighter form of the Re-GZ, equipped with a BWS (Back Weapon System) that greatly improves its maneuverability and cruising range. Outfitted with a mega beam cannon and other high-power
weaponry, it possesses superior overall performance. 
But because the BWS must be detached when it changes into MS form, it typically can't change back after transforming.

Developed for the personal use of Char Aznable, the leader of the revived Neo Zeon, this prototype mobile suit for Newtypes was the culmination of all Zeon mobile suit research and development.
It was therefore a true symbol of the reborn Neo Zeon forces.
Because it was developed to be an outstanding MS, as well as a full-spec Newtype machine that could perform all-range attacks with remote weapons, it was heavily armored and packed with thrusters and apogee motors to give it high mobility.
The machine achieved incredible responsiveness thanks to its partial use of psycho-frame, a material that incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality.
Its fixed armament included a diffuse mega particle cannon, and with a total of six funnels on its back, it was a high-performance machine that was incredibly balanced.

Developed for the personal use of Char Aznable, the leader of the revived Neo Zeon, this prototype mobile suit for Newtypes was the culmination of all Zeon mobile suit research and development.
It was therefore a true symbol of the reborn Neo Zeon forces.
Because it was developed to be an outstanding MS, as well as a full-spec Newtype machine that could perform all-range attacks with remote weapons, it was heavily armored and packed with thrusters and apogee motors to give it high mobility.
The machine achieved incredible responsiveness thanks to its partial use of psycho-frame, a material that incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality.
Its fixed armament included a diffuse mega particle cannon, and with a total of six funnels on its back, it was a high-performance machine that was incredibly balanced.

A huge prototype mobile armor for Newtypes, developed by the revived Neo Zeon led by Char Aznable. It features a number of elements inspired by all the Newtype mobile armors and mobile suits that had existed up to that point.
All piloting and weapon controls are handed by psycommu, while its large funnels and shoulder-mounted wired psycommu mega arm cannons make all-range attacks possible. The
inclusion of a mega particle cannon in its mouth, among other weaponry, gives this machine incredible firepower. 
The sturm thruster units in its legs combine large propellant tanks and thrusters to give the machine overwhelming acceleration, but become dead weight in battle,
and can be detached at the pilot's discretion.

A general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit developed by the revived Neo Zeon led by Char Aznable. It would follow in the footsteps of the Principality of Zeon's Zaku II to become the mainline machine of Neo Zeon.
With a simple construction and balanced performance, its extensive equipment options gave it the ability to handle a variety of different situations despite its relatively low production cost. 
Of the 100 or so produced during the Second Neo Zeon War, 82 were deployed in combat.

A general-purpose mass-produced mobile suit developed by the revived Neo Zeon led by Char Aznable. It would follow in the footsteps of the Principality of Zeon's Zaku II to become
the mainline machine of Neo Zeon. With a simple construction and balanced performance, its extensive equipment options gave it the ability to handle a variety of different situations despite its relatively low production cost.
Of the 100 or so produced during the Second Neo Zeon War, 82 were deployed in combat.
This was the personal machine of Neo Zeon ace Rezin Schnyder, equipped with the blade antenna used to distinguish command units. In addition to being painted completely blue, its performance was enhanced to match its pilot's skill.

A prototype heavy mobile suit for Newtypes based on Neo Zeon's main mass-produced MS, the Geara Doga. It shares the Geara Doga's movable frame, and makes partial use of psycho-frame, a material that incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality. Equipped with a larger generator, it has approximately 1.6 times more output than
the Geara Doga, and higher overall performance than the base machine.
With a total of six funnels (three on each shoulder), it can unleash all-range attacks via psycommu, but because of its miniaturized psycommu system it is limited as a Newtype MS.
This particular unit is the personal machine of Cyber-Newtype Gyunei Guss, and features a large antenna on its head.

A prototype heavy mobile suit for Newtypes based on Neo Zeon's main mass-produced MS, the Geara Doga. It shares the Geara Doga's movable frame, and makes partial use of psycho-frame, a material that incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality. Equipped with a larger generator, it has approximately 1.6 times more output than the Geara Doga, and higher overall performance than the base machine.
With a total of six funnels (three on each shoulder), it can unleash all-range attacks via psycommu, but because of its miniaturized psycommu system it is limited as a Newtype MS.
This particular unit is the personal machine of the Newtype Quess Air, who became a Neo Zeon pilot. It is equipped with a mega gatling gun capable of rapid fire. 
Quess Air was a false name assumed by Quess Paraya, the daughter of an Earth Federation official, in a bid to keep her identity a secret after joining Neo Zeon.

A mobile suit for Newtypes assigned to the Federation's Londo Bell task force during the Second Neo Zeon War.
Originally developed by Anaheim Electronics at its Von Braun factory, this new MS was arranged for by the Londo Bell commander Bright Noa.
Its basic design is an updated average of all previous Gundam types, with an emphasis on reliability and resilience.
The resulting free space was used to surround the cockpit with psycho-frame material obtained from a captured Neo Zeon MS.
This revolutionary material incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality, permitting the control of special Newtype weapons known as fin funnels.
Combining three or more fin funnels allows them to be used as a beam weapon-nullifying I-Field generator.
Thanks to these high-performance psycommu weapons, this is the first Gundam type to be a full-spec Newtype machine.

A mobile suit for Newtypes assigned to the Federation's Londo Bell task force during the Second Neo Zeon War.
Originally developed by Anaheim Electronics at its Von Braun factory, this new MS was arranged for by the Londo Bell commander Bright Noa.
Its basic design is an updated average of all previous Gundam types, with an emphasis on reliability and resilience.
The resulting free space was used to surround the cockpit with psycho-frame material obtained from a captured Neo Zeon MS.
This revolutionary material incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality, permitting the control of special Newtype weapons known as fin funnels.
Combining three or more fin funnels allows them to be used as a beam weapon-nullifying I-Field generator.
Thanks to these high-performance psycommu weapons, this is the first Gundam type to be a full-spec Newtype machine.

Developed for the personal use of Char Aznable, the leader of the revived Neo Zeon, this prototype mobile suit for Newtypes was the culmination of all Zeon mobile suit research and development.
It was therefore a true symbol of the reborn Neo Zeon forces.
This machine greedily incorporates concepts from various machines of the Gryps Conflict era.
Though gigantic compared to other MS, it boasts high mobility and operating time thanks to its propellant tanks and movable vernier binders, which double as funnel pods.
It also has the flexibility to adapt to anti-MS combat.
It has incredible responsiveness due to its partial use of psycho-frame, a material that incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality.
It is armed with a large mega beam rifle that can fire both focused and diffuse beams, and five funnels in each of its funnel pods.
This remarkable machine has an unusually high level of balance

A prototype mobile armor for Newtypes developed by the revived Neo Zeon. It was created at the same time as the Sazabi, the personal mobile suit of Neo Zeon's leader.
All piloting and weapon controls are handed by psycommu. The machine can perform all-range attacks with funnels and the wired psycommu beam cannons on its shoulders, making it a prototype for the huge Newtype mobile armor Alpha Azieru.

Model Number: RGZ-91B A redesigned version of the simplified transformable mobile
suit Re-GZ, developed specifically for ace pilot Amuro Ray of the Federation's Londo Bell task force.
The BWS (Back Weapon System), which was developed as a simple transformation system for mass production, was integrated into the backpack so that it could transform without purging the BWS. It also had V-shaped forehead antennas for enhanced sensor functions, restoring the appearance and concept of the original Zeta Gundam and improving its overall performance.

A large prototype mobile armor for Newtypes, developed by Neo Zeon as an upgrade of the Alpha Azieru. It features a number of elements inspired by all the Newtype mobile armors and mobile suits that had existed up to that point.
Unlike the Alpha Azieru, in which all piloting and weapon controls are handed by psycommu, this machine is armed with large anti-ship misiles and other projectile weapons that do not depend on the psycommu system and thus give it greater versatility. It can still perform all-range attacks using the funnels that are remotely controlled by Newtype psycho-waves, so its high firepower is uncompromised. The hyper mega particle cannon in its abdomen, which is directly connected to its generator, is powerful enough to destroy a large battleship with one blast.
Although this machine is derived from the Alpha Azieru, many aspects are also based on the Neo Zeon mobile suit Nightingale.

A large prototype mobile armor for Newtypes, developed by Neo Zeon as an upgrade of the Alpha Azieru. It features a number of elements inspired by all the Newtype mobile armors and mobile suits that had existed up to that point.
Unlike the Alpha Azieru, in which all piloting and weapon controls are handed by psycommu, this machine is armed with large anti-ship misiles and other projectile weapons that do not depend on the psycommu system and thus give it greater versatility. It can still perform all-range attacks using the funnels that are remotely controlled by Newtype psycho-waves, so its high firepower is uncompromised. The hyper mega particle cannon in its abdomen, which is directly connected to its generator, is powerful enough to destroy a large battleship with one blast.
Although this machine is derived from the Alpha Azieru, many aspects are also based on the Neo Zeon mobile suit Nightingale.

A large prototype mobile armor for Newtypes, developed by Neo Zeon as an upgrade of the Alpha Azieru. It features a number of elements inspired by all the Newtype mobile armors and mobile suits that had existed up to that point.
Unlike the Alpha Azieru, in which all piloting and weapon controls are handed by psycommu, this machine is armed with large anti-ship misiles and other projectile weapons that do not depend on the psycommu system and thus give it greater versatility. It can still perform all-range attacks using the funnels that are remotely controlled by Newtype psycho-waves, so its high firepower is uncompromised. The hyper mega particle cannon in its abdomen, which is directly connected to its generator, is powerful enough to destroy a large battleship with one blast.
Although this machine is derived from the Alpha Azieru, many aspects are also based on the Neo Zeon mobile suit Nightingale.

A high-performance commander version of the Neo Zeon general-purpose, mass-produced mobile suit Geara Doga. Its frame is unchanged, but the armor material has been upgraded to Gundarium alloy, and its avionics have been revamped for better handling and improved overall performance.
However, its development was canceled at the prototype stage, because priority had been given to the massive Newtype mobile armor Alpha Azieru.

A prototype mobile suit for Newtypes based on the Neo Zeon general-purpose, mass-produced mobile suit Geara Doga. Its fixed armament includes a diffuse mega particle cannon in its abdomen, as well as three funnels in each shoulder which enable it to perform all-range attacks via the psycommu system. Despite its firepower, this machine proved inadequate as a Newtype MS due to its poor maneuverability. 
Nonetheless, the data acquired from it would later influence the development of the Jagd Doga.

A mass-produced version of the Federation Forces prototype Newtype mobile suit Nu Gundam. This was developed as a candidate for a next-generation MS to replace the Jegan.
Inheriting the standard construction of the Nu Gundam, and the revolutionary psycho-frame material that incorporates computer chips with basic psycommu functionality, this machine surpasses the Jegan in every respect.
It is also designed for use by ordinary pilots, and can be adapted to both normal and Newtype pilots by attaching either fin funnels or quasi-psycommu incom weapons to its backpack.
A plan was proposed for a small production run for use by the Federation's Londo Bell task force, to give it more fighting strength against the Neo Zeon forces. However, this plan was abandoned due to the Federation's lack of interest in new MS development and the early conclusion of the Second Neo Zeon War.

In the mass production plan for the Nu Gundam, little consideration was given to the sourcing of NT pilots, and the use of an Incom Pack was seen as the more practical option. However, as a full-spec version, a model equipped with fin funnels was proposed.

A form of the AEUG mobile suit Hyaku-Shiki Kai with extra armor.
Developed to enhance the base machine's armor and weaponry, it is equipped with multiple high-output beam weapons and projectile weapons.
It also has newly designed armor, and reflector panels that serve as an anti-beam defense mechanism.
It minimizes the decrease in mobility by adding thrusters to various parts, and can function as a standard Hyaku-Shiki Kai when the armor is purged.

A form of the AEUG mobile suit Gundam Mk-III with an extra armor system.
It preserves the Gundam Mk-III's emphasis on mobility by adding thrusters to make up for the additional weight of its extra armor.
It also has a firepower boost, thanks to the hyper beam cannons on its legs and a variety of other beam weapons.
By retaining the strengths of the base MS, while dramatically increasing its firepower and thrust, this machine achieves extremely good balance for a full armor type.

Following the Second Neo Zeon War, the Federation Space Force pursued a number of reconstruction plans.
One such initiative was the UC Project, tasking Anaheim Electronics with the secret development of a prototype mobile suit that would fully realize the potential of psycho-frame technology.
Unlike previous machines, this new prototype's movable frame was completely built from psycho-frame material.
This first "full psycho-frame" MS featured responsiveness unmatched by any mobile suit to date.
A great deal of advanced technology was invested into its weaponry, which included a beam magnum.
It's energy blast was several times stronger than that of a normal beam rifle, and so destructive that it was able to penetrate the heavy armor of a mobile armor with a single shot.
Its power was so great, in fact, that even a near miss could result in the destruction of any MS in the vicinity of its line of fire.
Called "Unicorn" because of its pure white exterior and single horn, this MS underwent testing at Anaheim's Industrial 7 colony.
Because of his close connections to Anaheim, Vist Foundation head Cardeas Vist was able to get a special system known as the La+ Program installed onboard, in a plot to hand it over to the Neo Zeon remnant organization known as the Sleeves.
Because its activation requires recognition of a registrant's biometrics, it became by default, the personal machine of Banagher Links.

The prototype Unicorn Gundam, secretly developed for the Federation Forces by Anaheim Electronics, features a system called NT-D which expands the inner frame of the mobile suit, transforming the machine itself.
At the same time, the joints of the armor slide open to reveal the psycho-frame beneath, giving it a vastly different appearance from its so-called Unicorn Mode.

The prototype Unicorn Gundam, secretly developed for the Federation Forces by Anaheim Electronics, features a system called NT-D which expands the inner frame of the mobile suit, transforming the machine itself.
At the same time, the joints of the armor slide open to reveal the psycho-frame beneath, giving it a vastly different appearance from its so-called Unicorn Mode.

One of the Refined Gundam Zeta series, created in an attempt to mass-produce the transformable Zeta Gundam, which was highly valued for its performance in the Gryps Conflict.
The previously developed Re-GZ used the Back Weapon System as a substitute for a transformation mechanism, but this machine was designed to achieve the flexible operation provided by the solo transformation of the Zeta Gundam, and borrowed from the Methuss to create a transformation mechanism that was simple and durable.
Because many of the internal components used are the same as those used in the Federation's main mass-produced MS Jegan, it costs considerably less to produce than previous machines.
Nevertheless, it continues to be as well-armed as its ancestor.

One of the Refined Gundam Zeta series, created in an attempt to mass-produce the transformable Zeta Gundam, which was highly valued for its performance in the Gryps Conflict.
This specialized version of the ReZEL has been tuned to suit ace pilots and team commanders.
The standard ReZEL has had its capabilities detuned to allow even rookie pilots to get adequate performance from the machine.
This model, however, has its thruster output limiters boosted to maximum.
Its internal frame is also reinforced to adjust for the burden this places on the machine, making it an enhanced version of the already speedy Zeta-series mobile suits.
Additionally, the whole machine has been tuned for greater reliability, giving it superior performance overall, and is normally equipped with a high-output mega beam launcher, putting its offensive capabilities ahead of the regular ReZEL.

A mass-produced mobile suit employed by the Neo Zeon remnants known as the Sleeves.
During the Second Neo Zeon War, Anaheim Electronics began developing an enhanced and updated mobile suit to replace the Geara Doga, in the event that the fight between the Earth Federation and the revived Neo Zeon led by Char Aznable turned into a extended conflict.
With the end of Char's Rebellion, Anaheim halted production of the machine that it was developing.
But the know-how obtained from the production and use of the Geara Doga, and the performance improvements provided by advancing technology, made it even easier to produce and maintain than was first expected.
It was also relatively simple to modify and enhance, easy to handle, and well-balanced, giving it high potential.
To raise the morale of the disorganized Neo Zeon forces, the external design was molded to mimic the old Zaku type, giving it an orthodox Zeon style.
These twin assets of propaganda and fighting potential meant that the Geara Zulu became a symbol of Zeon restoration.
Development was resumed in this spirit, culminating in the machine's long-awaited completion.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

Following the Second Neo Zeon War, the Federation Space Force pursued a number of reconstruction plans.
One such initiative was the UC Project, tasking Anaheim Electronics with the secret development of a prototype mobile suit that would fully realize the potential of psycho-frame technology.
Unlike previous machines, this new prototype's movable frame was completely built from psycho-frame material.
This first "full psycho-frame" MS featured responsiveness unmatched by any mobile suit to date.
Though this is the second unit of the Unicorn Gundam, it underwent functional testing in Earth's gravity before the first unit, and the adjustments made from that extra data gave it a higher level of completeness.
Its weapons were also more capable than those of the first unit, featuring special psycho-frame equipment called Armed Armor that increased its fighting strength and allowed it to stay on the battlefield for a longer period of time.
This machine was the true Unicorn Gundam that the UC Project originally set out to create.

The biggest differences between this machine and the first unit are its shining gold psycho-frame and its many-horned multi-blade antenna.
This gold mane resembles that of the lion, king of beasts.
It also has extra psycho-frame equipment called "armed armor," and the hyper-oscillating crushers of the Armed Armor VN, which take advantage of the toughness of the psycho-frame, resemble the claws of a beast.
These claws, combined with its overwhelming destructive power, make the Banshee truly like a lion.

Following the Second Neo Zeon War, the Federation Space Force pursued a number of reconstruction plans.
One such initiative was the UC Project, tasking Anaheim Electronics with the secret development of the Unicorn Gundam.
This is a version of the Unicorn Gundam 02 Banshee with improved overall performance.
The revolving launcher attached beneath the barrel of its main weapon, the beam magnum, uses different cartridges for different situations, allowing it to unleash a wide range of attacks.
For defense, its shield is equipped with the Armed Armor DE, which increases its mobility in space and allows for wide-area attacks.
It is also equipped with the Armed Armor XC, which is said to increase the affinity between the pilot and the machine.
As a weapon, the completed Banshee Norn does justice to its original form as a hunting machine.

The prototype Banshee Norn, secretly developed for the Federation Forces by Anaheim Electronics, features a system called NT-D which expands the inner frame of the mobile suit, transforming the machine itself.
At the same time, the joints of the armor slide open to reveal the psycho-frame beneath, giving it a vastly different appearance from its so-called Unicorn Mode.
The treasure of the Banshee Norn is its Armed Armor XC, which not only relaxes the conditions for the activation of Destroy Mode, but also maintains a heightened affinity between pilot and psycommu after the activation.
The expansion mechanisms of each body part work in the same fashion as the extra psycho-frame armaments added to the Banshee Norn, meaning that the lion-maned Armed Armor XC transforms the Banshee into an absolute beast.

The prototype Banshee Norn, secretly developed for the Federation Forces by Anaheim Electronics, features a system called NT-D which expands the inner frame of the mobile suit, transforming the machine itself.
At the same time, the joints of the armor slide open to reveal the psycho-frame beneath, giving it a vastly different appearance from its so-called Unicorn Mode.
The treasure of the Banshee Norn is its Armed Armor XC, which not only relaxes the conditions for the activation of Destroy Mode, but also maintains a heightened affinity between pilot and psycommu after the activation.
The expansion mechanisms of each body part work in the same fashion as the extra psycho-frame armaments added to the Banshee Norn, meaning that the lion-maned Armed Armor XC transforms the Banshee into an absolute beast.

Following the Second Neo Zeon War, this prototype mobile suit was developed by Anaheim Electronics to test the capabilities of the psycho-frame.
Using psycho-frame as part of the basic MS skeleton, or movable frame, allows for partial transmission of the pilot's psycho-waves to the machine, thus achieving a remarkable level of responsiveness.
In addition to its high response, the machine is equipped with two large thrusters on its back, giving it mobility far superior to ordinary MS.
Though its armaments are fairly standard, like all its other components they are expertly crafted with the highest technology.
When they are used in conjunction with the Sinanju's blinding mobility, a weapon of ferocious power is achieved.
This machine was stolen by Full Frontal, leader of the Neo Zeon remnant organization known as the Sleeves.
Its new coat of crimson and infamous speed convinced many a wary soldier it was the second coming of the Red Comet.
Conversely, to members of the disorganized Sleeves, it provided a welcome morale boost.
Data on the machine's strength and the improved responsiveness of its psycho-frame was the basis for the Unicorn Gundam's development.

The prototype Unicorn Gundam, secretly developed for the Federation Forces by Anaheim Electronics, features a system called NT-D which expands the inner frame of the mobile suit, transforming the machine itself.
At the same time, the joints of the armor slide open to reveal the psycho-frame beneath, giving it a vastly different appearance from its so-called Unicorn Mode.
Normally, when going into Destroy Mode, the Unicorn's psycho-frame glows red.
But when Banagher Links awakened as a Newtype, the thoughts of the people resonated with the psycho-frame and its color changed from red to green.
This light is thought to be the same as the light that appeared during Char's Rebellion, when humanity's collective unconscious resonated with the Nu Gundam's psycho-frame to create a physical force, allowing it to push back the Axis asteroid that threatened to crash into Earth.
However, little is known for sure.

The prototype Unicorn Gundam, secretly developed for the Federation Forces by Anaheim Electronics, features a system called NT-D which expands the inner frame of the mobile suit, transforming the machine itself.
At the same time, the joints of the armor slide open to reveal the psycho-frame beneath, giving it a vastly different appearance from its so-called Unicorn Mode.
Normally, when going into Destroy Mode, the Unicorn's psycho-frame glows red.
But when Banagher Links awakened as a Newtype, the thoughts of the people resonated with the psycho-frame and its color changed from red to green.
This light is thought to be the same as the light that appeared during Char's Rebellion, when humanity's collective unconscious resonated with the Nu Gundam's psycho-frame to create a physical force, allowing it to push back the Axis asteroid that threatened to crash into Earth.
However, little is known for sure.

A huge mobile armor designed for fortress attack and developed specifically for use by Full Frontal, commander-in-chief of the Neo Zeon remnant faction known as the Sleeves.
The Sinanju combines with it to serve as its core unit, a configuration attesting to the Neo Zeong's unprecedented size.
Once combined, the Red Comet's machine changes from a humanoid type to a monster with overwhelming power.
Multiple mega particle cannons are just the beginning of an expansive array of weapons bristling all over its body.
However, its true value lies in newly designed weapons that work together with the psycho-frame of the core unit.
In other words, the hull unit serves as a psycommu amplifier for the Sinanju itself.
Most noteworthy are the psycho-shard generators housed in the shoulders.
When deployed to its sides, they form a ring of light crystals, or psycho-shards.
These resonate with the psycho-frame of the Sinanju, allowing the Neo Zeong to produce a psycho-field-like phenomenon at will, without the aid of a separate machine.
When active, these generators can also cause enemy weaponry to explode.

A Federation Forces prototype transformable mobile suit based on the Delta Gundam, a transformable MS plan that was completed as the non-transformable Hyaku-Shiki due to weaknesses in its frame design.
Implementing technology from the Zeta Gundam series, the redesigned Delta finally works as a mass-produced transformable MS.
Like the original Hyaku-Shiki, priority was given to reducing machine weight and increasing maneuverability accordingly.
The thrusters on its waist give it incredible mobility, while the combat data obtained from Hyaku-Shiki types allows it to achieve elevated performance in every respect.
However, the beam rifle that had been developed as its main weapon would ultimately be scrapped in favor of that of the mass-produced ReZEL, due to lack of energy pack compatibility.

Following in the footsteps of the GM Striker, a variation of the Federation Forces mass-produced mobile suit GM, this remodeled GM II Desert Type boasts reinforced armor on its shoulders and left forearm, as well as enhanced close combat weaponry in the form of a twin beam spear.
A number of these machines were deployed to the Federation Forces' Torrington base, where they were used against the Zeon remnants.

A variation of the Federation Forces main mass-produced mobile suit, the Jegan, with some minor changes.
Though the Jegan was relatively old as of U.C.0096, this machine had excellent overall performance.
It also helped soften the effects of military downsizing, at a time when the Federation was trying to boost its fighting strength through the remodeling of existing machines as opposed to the development of new models.
External changes were limited to its backpack, and alterations to the waist and shoulder armor to support additional equipment, but careful modifications were made to its internal components and weaponry that served to raise the total specs of the machine.

A Federation Forces prototype mobile suit for heavy bombardment, created by adding extra weapons and armor to the earliest version of the Jegan.
With large anti-ship missiles and other projectile weapons as its main armaments, it adopted a side-by-side tandem cockpit, among a number of other improvements.
It was produced in small numbers as a test machine for the Stark Jegan.

An enhanced equipment form of the Jegan Type D, redesigned based on functional combat data from the Prototype Stark Jegan.
It boasted ample firepower and weapons that could be used at a variety of ranges, including a hyper bazooka and triple missile pods that drew heavily on the operational concepts of the GM III.
Additional equipment included multiple thrusters that gave it higher mobility than its massive silhouette would imply.
Because the utmost care was taken in balancing these thrusters against the rest of the machine, it can purge some of its additional equipment in battle and automatically adjust its propulsion.
Despite being a testbed specialized for bombardment, it ultimately became a machine that achieved solid overall performance and could change its equipment options to adapt flexibly to any combat scenario.

A mass-produced mobile suit developed by Anaheim Electronics as an escort to the Unicorn Gundam, as part of the Federation Forces' UC Project.
While the Unicorn Gundam was designed to quickly unleash its maximum power in one-on-one combat, its ability to last in extended fights was severely lacking, which was where the Jesta stepped in.
The Jesta was to focus on taking out ordinary opponents, creating opportunities for the Unicorn to go directly against Newtype foes.
Though based on the Jegan series and its ability to handle a variety of different combat scenarios, it boasted reinforced armor on its shoulders and legs, added propulsion to deal with the extra weight, and unique handheld weapons, giving it capabilities that put the Jegan to shame.

A mass-produced mobile suit developed by Anaheim Electronics as an escort to the Unicorn Gundam, as part of the Federation Forces' UC Project.
This machine was a heavy equipment version of the Jesta.
Loaded with additional armor that incorporated a number of different weapon types, and an upgraded version of its OS with enhanced fire control capabilities, this machine could do more than simply provide cover fire, and the overall improvements to its offensive capabilities allowed it to hold its own on the front lines.
It also featured explosive bolts that could be used to purge its added weapons and armor on a moment's notice.

A Federation Forces mass-produced transformable mobile suit, redesigned based on data from the Asshimar that was developed at the Oakland Laboratory during the Gryps Conflict.
It was easy both to produce and maintain.
To fulfill the Federation request that it be able to serve as an SFS (Sub Flight System), attempts were made to incorporate the same internal components that were standard to the Jegan series, and despite being a mass production model, it possessed the ability to fly unaided in atmosphere.
Six Garuda-class aircraft were deployed around the world to provide air defense as part of the Garuda Initiative.
This plan called for the creation of a MS dispatch system for emergency readiness, and these ultra-large Garuda transports could deploy large numbers of mobile suits.

A technology testbed created by modifying one of the remaining Byarlants developed by the Titans.
It pursued the same lightweight and aerodynamic qualities as the original, but had its thermonuclear jet engines enhanced and propellant tanks added in an attempt to improve its atmospheric flight capabilities.
In terms of weaponry, its overall performance was improved with the addition of arm-mounted claw arms similar to those of the Gabthley, and mega particle cannons that could be used as beam sabers.
The change from a mono-eye to a composite sensor made it look more like a Federation-made mobile suit.
With the addition of propellant tanks and other parts, the first unit served to obtain data for improvements.
Based on that feedback, the second unit was to be the completed version with the addition of Gaplant active thrusters and a new Type 94 cockpit block.

A ground support mobile suit developed by Karaba during the Gryps Conflict.
It was equipped with a specialized beam rifle and beam cannons on both shoulders, and used the Methuss's transformation mechanisms to mitigate the recoil when firing its beam cannons.
This MS was completed after the end of the Gryps Conflict, and deployed in small numbers to help protect the Torrington base.

A transformable mobile suit secretly developed by the Strategic Naval Research Institute (SNRI) for the exclusive use of the Federation Space Force's ECOAS special operations group, also known as the "Manhunters."
Its most distinctive feature is the use of an ultra-compact fusion reactor, which enabled the downsizing of the machine itself.
Its size is roughly half that of most mobile suits of its era.
It is the covert nature of the machine's missions which demanded this miniaturization, requiring the transportation of an eight-man special operations team, plus lightning speed and high mobility for rapid operational movement.
These astonishing design hurdles were leaped by eliminating dead space from the design.
Its forearms also depart from the norm.
They consist of anti-MS missile containers, plus simple manipulators equipped with high-powered devices known as "beam burners," which use mega particles to cut through both barriers and enemy MS.

The mainline mobile suit used by Neo Zeon in Char's Rebellion.
The Geara Doga is highly versatile, with very stable basic performance.
Even in U.C.0096, surviving machines could still be seen on the front lines of conflicts involving the Neo Zeon remnants known as the Sleeves.
The engraved markings on its wrists, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

The Geara Doga was the mainline mobile suit used by Neo Zeon in Char's Rebellion.
This variation, fitted with additional armament, is used by the Neo Zeon remnants known as the Sleeves as a heavy weapons type.
Its arsenal includes a beam sword-axe, sturm fausts, and other basic offensive tools.
The upgraded backpack is used with the massive Lange Bruno gun it carries, enhancing its firepower and allowing it to operate as a long-range support machine, able to work alongside both standard Geara Dogas and the newly developed Geara Zulu.
The engraved markings on its wrists, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

A version of the Geara Zulu used exclusively by Angelo Sauper, commander of the Guards who escort Full Frontal, the leader of the Neo Zeon remnants known as the Sleeves.
It sports a unique purple color scheme and various modifications to help it keep up with the mobility of Full Frontal's own Sinanju.
A pair of large boost pods are attached to its back, along with a heavy arms backpack which also includes a stabilizer and propellant tank.
These additions provide a significant boost in thruster output and mobility.
Its arsenal includes a "Lange Bruno gun plus," based on the long-range support projectile weapon developed for the Geara Doga and remodeled into a long-range beam launcher for use in space.
This was fitted in anticipation of providing combat support to the Sinanju.
Though the machine itself has racked up an impressive sortie count within the Sleeves, its confirmed kills are not nearly as high.
This is not due solely to its primary role as a support unit, but also because its pilot, Angelo Sauper, is forbidden from interfering while Full Frontal is on the battlefield.

An amphibious mobile suit developed by the Sleeves, a Neo Zeon remnant group, for use in Earth invasion operations.
It builds on the Geara Zulu's basic frame using amphibious MS data accumulated by the Principality of Zeon.
It is equipped with redesigned armor to reduce water pressure, a vest-like diving unit, a neck-mounted ballast tank, hydrojet thrusters on its back, swimming fins on its feet, and other features, that enable prolonged undersea operation in addition to high mobility both in the water and out.
The Zee Zulu wields a beam machine gun with a special firing mode to offset beam divergence undersea.
It also has heat-generating triple blades dubbed "iron nails," which are fashioned from a high molecular ceramic compound.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

A Newtype mobile suit based on the huge Quin Mantha introduced at the end of the First Neo Zeon War.
This updated version has been downsized considerably for use by the Neo Zeon remnant faction known as the Sleeves, without sacrificing the high performance specs of its ancestor.
It integrates a large variety of special technology, while building on a wealth of Zeon know-how to achieve miniaturization with artful craftsmanship.
The Kshatriya is intended solely for Newtypes, with a psycho-frame cockpit and dramatically downsized psycommu system.
Only half the size of the Quin Mantha, it avoids many of the problems faced by large MS.
Of the many armaments affixed to the suit, the most eye-catching would be the four large binders extending from the shoulders, each housing two mega particle cannons and six funnels, as well as a small sub-arm that contains a beam saber.
Though this formidable arsenal lives up to its pedigree in terms of raw firepower, its complicated nature dictates that only the most capable of Newtypes can take the controls.

A variation of the Galluss J, a mass-produced ground combat mobile suit developed for use in Earth invasion operations.
It drops the shoulder and finger missiles of its forebear for a beam cannon identical to the hand weapon of the Ga-Zowmn.
Additionally, it sports the aptly named "big gun," a weapon also used by the Zaku Cannon.
This arsenal makes it a perfect machine for heavy bombardment, as demonstrated in the attack on the Torrington base, where it excelled in an artillery support role.

A huge amphibious mobile armor, based on a design originally drafted by Haman Karn's Neo Zeon forces during the First Neo Zeon War, and then developed over six years by Zeon remnants.
Equipped with a Minovsky craft system and hover propulsion, this mobile and stealthy machine is able to cruise both on and under the water while emitting very little noise.
Additionally, it incorporates cutting-edge psycho-frame technology, thanks to assistance from Sleeves engineers.
Its arsenal includes shoulder-mounted diffuse mega particle cannons, whose beams can be directed using reflector bits to attack a wide area.
Enemy beam fire can also be deflected in this way, giving it robust defensive options.
Its head houses a High-Caliber Mega Particle Cannon for immense firepower.
Coupled with its formidable defenses, this makes the Shamblo a deadly battlefield behemoth.

A long-range sniper variation of the Principality of Zeon mobile suit Zaku I, created in order to re-use worn-out machines on Earth.
A few changes set this version apart from those used in the One Year War, including the addition of a blade antenna on the head and structural improvements to the barrel of its weapon, resulting in a beam sniper rifle capable of rapid fire.
Using this machine in conjunction with a Fat Uncle transport plane allows for high-altitude sniping and precise team coordination.
Zeon remnant leader Yonem Kirks used it in just this fashion during the attack on the Torrington base.

A variation of the close combat prototype mobile suit Efreet, which was developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War.
For over a decade after the One Year War's conclusion, many updates were made, such as tuning to boost its melee abilities in ground combat.
It bristles with fourteen heat darts, its main offensive weapons, which are perfect for encounters at either close or point-blank range.
Even in U.C.0096, its fighting prowess remains relevant, with the Efreet Schneid being employed by Zeon remnant forces during the attack on the Torrington base.

Developed by the Principality of Zeon using the Dom and Dom Tropical Test Type as a base, this is a Dom-type variation created for use in the desert and tropics, using data gathered from various test machines.
It has thermonuclear hover engines with anti-dust filters in its legs, for extended operating time in deserts and ease of maintenance.
Weapon latches all over its body make it possible to use other armaments such as a raketen bazooka and sturm fausts, giving it high versatility.
It was deployed mainly in Central Africa, and after the war, it was used in the Delaz Fleet's attack on the Torrington base.
In U.C.0096, some surviving units were used by the Zeon remnants to attack the Torrington base once again.

The final production version of the mass-produced ground combat mobile suit Dom, which was developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War.
As a local warfare type specialized for desert combat, it was upgraded for improved ground combat capabilities.
Its hover system had longer operating time than the base machine, and its thrust was dramatically increased.
Though its mobility in the desert surpassed that of any contemporary MS, its overall performance was not quite as good.
This machine inherits the Dom's standard armament of a giant bazooka and heat saber, but some units also carry unique weapons.
To decrease air resistance during high-speed hovering movement, it does not carry a shield.
Even in U.C.0096, this machine retains its land mobility, and it participates in the attack on the Torrington base.

A specialized combat mobile suit developed by the Principality of Zeon for an attack on Jaburo.
Based on a design from the prototype stage of the amphibious MS Acguy, it is a medium-range support machine from the Agg series.
Though this is essentially a redesigned version of a prototype, because it is based on a machine with high ground combat performance, the Juaggu has great mobility for a heavy MS.
It uses ranged projectiles for support attacks, as well as powerful tackles, giving it formidable medium-range combat abilities.
Even in U.C.0096, its phenomenal firepower and tough armor are still enough to overwhelm Federation Forces MS during the attack on the Federation capital at Dakar.

A specialized combat mobile suit developed by the Principality of Zeon for an attack on Jaburo, originally created as a sister model to the amphibious MS Z'Gok.
When the Z'Gok was officially approved by the Zeon brass, it is said this close combat MS was then given the green light to join the Jaburo attack.
Most of the Zogok's offensive tools are unique to this machine, such as the arm punch, which launches its square-cut four-fingered manipulator forward to penetrate enemy armor.
Its sole ranged attack utilizes boomerang cutters, which are less versatile than the weapons of existing amphibious MS, making the machine something of an acquired taste.
Thanks to 17 years of fighting experience, the unit used by the Zeon remnants in U.C.0096 holds even more power in its fists.

The prototype Unicorn Gundam that was secretly developed for the Federation Forces by Anaheim Electronics, enhanced for use in a decisive battle.
Its additional equipment went above and beyond its own custom weaponry to include the Stark Jegan's anti-ship missile launchers, the Kshatriya's beam gatling guns, and the large boosters of the Base Jabber Type 94, just to name a few.
Though it was meant to carry as many of the types of MS equipment remaining aboard the Nahel Argama as possible, it was ultimately a balanced and powerful machine.
Because it was originally assumed that the Unicorn Gundam would only be used in special circumstances, quickly unleashing its maximum power in one-on-one combat, it boasted overwhelming power but struggled as a standalone weapon due to its inability to last in extended fights.
The completion of the Full Armor Plan, however, succeeded in adding a level of firepower beyond what was standard for a mobile suit.

The prototype Unicorn Gundam that was secretly developed for the Federation Forces by Anaheim Electronics, enhanced for use in a decisive battle.
The activation of the Full Armor Unicorn Gundam's NT-D system expands the inner frame of the mobile suit, transforming the machine itself.
At the same time, the joints of the armor slide open to reveal the psycho-frame beneath, giving it a vastly different appearance from its so-called Unicorn Mode.
Its numerous weapons are attached in a way that prevents them from impeding the body's size changes when going in and out of Destroy Mode.
This ensures that the Gundam keeps the ability to exhibit its full offensive capabilities.

A prototype mobile suit based on Neo Zeon's fourth-generation MS Doven Wolf, which was captured at Axis after the First Neo Zeon War and then remodeled by Anaheim Electronics at the request of the Federation Forces.
Changes from the base machine include the omission of some weapons with direct connections to the generator, and updated alloys in the exterior armor for significant weight reduction.
Energy supply to the thrusters was improved, allowing it to maintain stable agility even at high output.
Its most distinctive features are quasi-psycommu weapons such as "wired hands" that can be launched to unleash wire-guided all-range attacks, and the incoms housed in its backpack.
Though intended only for testing, this MS proved itself more than fit for actual combat.

A specialized version of the Jegan Type D, a variation of the Federation Forces main mass-produced mobile suit with some minor changes.
This machine was developed exclusively for the ECOAS special operations group, also known as the "Manhunters."
Additional sensors and extra chest armor compared to a standard Jegan D Type boost its performance in special missions, and raise the survival rate of the pilot in combat.
This machine proved its true value when paired with ECOAS's special transformable MS Loto.

During the First Neo Zeon War, the Neo Zeon forces led by Haman Karn developed a prototype mobile suit known as the Hamma-Hamma.
Its concepts were later revived to create this prototype MS, using the Sleeves' mass-produced Geara Zulu as a base.
Both arms are fitted with the same quasi-psycommu weapons used by the Hamma-Hamma.
Known as incoms, these contain triple mega particle cannons and can detach from the main body to be remotely controlled via wire guidance.
Its most distinctive weapons, however, are its psycho-jammers, special devices which can be deployed around an area to interfere with psycommu functions.
This is a trump card against the Unicorn Gundam.

A Neo Zeon mobile suit for Newtypes and Cyber-Newtypes, developed by Neo Zeon during Char's Rebellion.
Boasting a high-output generator, psycho-frame technology, and funnel weapons, this machine's combat capabilities make it a highly feared presence on the battlefield.
This machine has been refurbished for use by the Neo Zeon remnant faction known as the Sleeves, replacing the arm lost in U.C.0093 with that of a Geara Doga, which uses the same frame.
Its color scheme has also been changed.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

After the Kshatriya was damaged by the Unicorn Gundam and then repaired, becoming the Kshatriya Besserung, it was given further upgrades by the Nahel Argama crew.
The two missing binders were replaced with giant propellant boosters, while the damaged head and cockpit block were switched out for Federation-made parts.
A beam gatling gun was integrated into the makeshift frame of the right leg, and a hyper beam javelin; one of the Unicorn Gundam's optional weapons, into the left forearm.
The funnels were modified to include warheads from small sturm fausts, as a countermeasure against enemy I-Fields.
One could say that this arsenal boasts more variety than previous versions.

A successor to Neo Zeon's mass-produced transformable mobile suit Gaza C, which was heavily used in the First Neo Zeon War.
The Gaza D's main weapon is the "knuckle buster," known for its high precision.
The machine itself is highly cost-efficient, resulting in the Neo Zeon remnant outfit known as the Sleeves repainting many of them for use in the conflict around Laplace's Box.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

A development of Neo Zeon's Gaza series, a line of mass-produced transformable mobile suits originally adapted from worker MS.
Its frame structure has been revamped, allowing it to change into a fighter-bomber form designed to attack warships and fortresses.
Its main weapon is a hyper knuckle buster, which foregoes the traditional direct connection to the generator in favor of an independent energy pack.
This makes the weapon more portable and versatile.
Many of the units produced during the final phases of the First Neo Zeon War were acquired by the Sleeves, whereupon their color scheme was altered.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

This mass-produced heavy mobile suit used by the remnant faction known as the Sleeves is identical to the Dreissen introduced during the First Neo Zeon War.
With its original armor of sturdy Gundarium alloy, combined with almost excessive generator output and propulsion, it still proudly holds its own against the modern mass-produced MS of U.C.0096.
The only changes are some partial upgrades, and new weapons based on the concepts of the Dom.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

A mass-produced mobile suit of the Zaku series, developed at Axis during the First Neo Zeon War.
Its heavy armor and built-in weaponry make it a highly versatile force in the field.
In U.C.0096, the Neo Zeon remnants known as the Sleeves altered the front armor to house hidden beam saber-wielding arm units, and made other changes for deployment in the Laplace Conflict.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

An assault mobile suit developed by the Neo Zeon remnants known as the Sleeves, based on the general-purpose Galluss J used by Axis during the First Neo Zeon War.
Due to its primary function of base penetration, a slew of major customizations have been made, such as the removal of its ancestor's bulky armor plating.
This resulted in a weight reduction of over 50%.
As the machine was designed to be compatible with the weapons of other mobile suits, its standard equipment includes no hand weapons, but depending on the mission it can sortie with as much armament mounted all over its body as its weapon payload will permit.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

A mass-produced rear support mobile suit developed during the First Neo Zeon War.
While its close combat capabilities are limited, the Zssa's small frame carries a host of missiles.
This puts firepower equivalent to that of a large, heavily armed mobile armor into a compact, mass-producible package.
The Sleeves version employed in U.C.0096 offsets the lack of anti-MS fighting ability by allowing the use of hand-held armaments intended for other suits, thus expanding its usefulness beyond pure support.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

A mass-produced transformable mobile suit developed by Axis during the First Neo Zeon War.
It is comprised of two autonomous blocks, the upper "Bawoo Attacker" and lower "Bawoo Nutter," which can dock and separate.
The machine is highly mobile, and its transformation and separation features further expand its offensive and evasive repertoire.
In U.C.0096, the Bawoo was resurrected to demonstrate its combat prowess once more, this time under the banner of the Sleeves.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

An upgraded version of the mainline mobile suit Gelgoog, developed by the Principality of Zeon at the end of the One Year War.
Though initially used for training, its combat record during the First Neo Zeon War led to its increased use in the battlefield.
After a few design alterations under Sleeves oversight, including a color change, this machine was deployed in the Laplace Conflict.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

A mass-produced mobile suit used by the Neo Zeon remnant outfit known as the Sleeves, largely identical to the Dra-C models used in the Delaz Conflict.
Modern updates include a new pink and purple color scheme, and a gatling gun mounted on its right arm.
This added firepower helps the MS keep up with modern machines.
The engraved markings on its wrists and chest, which gave the remnant faction its curious moniker, differ according to factors such as the team, pilot, and assigned mission.

A Federation Forces prototype transformable mobile suit that was completed as the non-transformable Hyaku-Shiki due to weaknesses in its frame design.
For the theoretical waverider form to be sufficiently mobile, as much excess weight as possible had to be cut.
Its armor sported the same coat of golden beam-resistant emulsion seen on the Hyaku-Shiki.
Though this and other specs made this transformable MS look like a world-beater, the frame strength flaws that emerged in the design phase made it impossible to build with the technology available at the time.
The blueprints themselves, however, were sound and complete, and they were used in U.C.0090 to develop the Delta Plus.

This prototype transformable mobile suit takes into account the unrealized Delta Gundam blueprints, as well as data compiled during the development of the Delta Plus.
Compared to its forebears, the machine sports a more developed, option-rich arsenal, including back-mounted proto fin funnels for all-range attacks.
It is well-equipped for any kind of combat, right out of the box.
The most distinctive feature, however, is its "NITRO" psycommu system, which grants normal humans combat abilities on par with Newtypes.
When it is used, it forcibly rewrites the pilot's brain to turn them into a Cyber-Newtype optimized for the system.
The blue flames that jet out of its drive sections are a byproduct of this process.

This prototype transformable mobile suit takes into account the unrealized Delta Gundam blueprints, as well as data compiled during the development of the Delta Plus.
Compared to its forebears, the machine sports a more developed, option-rich arsenal, including back-mounted proto fin funnels for all-range attacks.
It is well-equipped for any kind of combat, right out of the box.
The most distinctive feature, however, is its "NITRO" psycommu system, which grants normal humans combat abilities on par with Newtypes.
When it is used, it forcibly rewrites the pilot's brain to turn them into a Cyber-Newtype optimized for the system.
The blue flames that jet out of its drive sections are a byproduct of this process.

This prototype transformable mobile suit takes into account the unrealized Delta Gundam blueprints, as well as data compiled during the development of the Delta Plus.
Compared to its forebears, the machine sports a more developed, option-rich arsenal, including back-mounted proto fin funnels for all-range attacks.
It is well-equipped for any kind of combat, right out of the box.
The most distinctive feature, however, is its "NITRO" psycommu system, which grants normal humans combat abilities on par with Newtypes.
When it is used, it forcibly rewrites the pilot's brain to turn them into a Cyber-Newtype optimized for the system.
The blue flames that jet out of its drive sections are a byproduct of this process.

A prototype mobile suit based on Neo Zeon's fourth-generation MS Doven Wolf, which was captured at Axis after the First Neo Zeon War and then remodeled by Anaheim Electronics at the request of the Federation Forces.
Changes from the base machine include the omission of some weapons with direct connections to the generator, and updated alloys in the exterior armor for significant weight reduction.
Energy supply to the thrusters was improved, allowing it to maintain stable agility even at high output.
This machine was developed to test its quasi-psycommu capabilities, as well as the Federation-made funnel weapons that were being developed at the time.
The large wired funnel on its back had its own internal generator, and it contributed greatly to the development of later psycommu machines.

The second unit of the Byarlant Custom, a technology testbed created by modifying one of the remaining Byarlants developed by the Titans.
Its color scheme has been changed, it has movable binders containing beam cannons on its back, and the arms now use five-finger manipulators.
Furthermore, its new aero armor reduces air resistance during high-speed cruising.
A large booster unit can also be attached as an option.
