These enhancements achieve the original design goal of crafting a mobile suit that commands the skies.

The flight form of the Federation's mass-produced transformable mobile suit ReZEL Type C (Defenser a-Unit).
It is based on, and shares a name with, the Zeta Gundam's waverider form.
In addition to having a swift, simple transformation process, the control support features of the new OS adjust the flight controls so that they have the same feel as when the machine is in mobile suit form, making it very easy to pilot.
Compared to the standard type, it exhibits superior mobility thanks to thrusters that have been tuned for enhanced output.
This type of ReZEL also has back-mounted variable wing binders, which raise its atmospheric flight performance.

One of the Refined Gundam Zeta series, created in an attempt to mass-produce the transformable Zeta Gundam, which was highly valued for its performance in the Gryps Conflict.
This variation, known as the Type C, is modified for use by commanders and equipped with a defenser unit.
The defenser unit is one of several backpacks designed by the Federation Forces to enhance the ReZEL's performance, and can be switched out to suit the situation.
There are two types, designated a and b based on their armament.
The "b-Unit" houses a direct-drive generator to boost the operating efficiency of its beam weaponry, allowing it to rapidly fire full-power beam shots.
This capability makes the ReZEL a serious threat in mid- to long-range combat.

A heavily modified Sleeves version of the Bawoo, a mass-produced transformable mobile suit developed during the First Neo Zeon War.
Psycho-frame has been used around the cockpit of the upper "Rebawoo Attacker" and the nose section of the lower "Rebawoo Nutter," allowing perfect coordination between the separated blocks via the pilot's psycho-waves.
The striking crimson and graceful embellishments signify that this machine was originally meant for Full Frontal.
After the seizure of the Sinanju, however, the Rebawoo was assigned to another Sleeves pilot, seeing multiple combat sorties thereafter.

An improved version of the mass-produced Geara Doga, which served as the mainline mobile suit of the Neo Zeon forces during Char's Rebellion.
Resurrected by the Neo Zeon remnants known as the Sleeves, various upgrades have gone into this machine, including higher thruster output and meticulously increased responsiveness.
Its crimson body and blinding speed are ample hints of the second coming of the Red Comet.

The mass-produced Geara Doga served as the mainline mobile suit of the Neo Zeon forces during Char's Rebellion.
This machine has a unique purple color scheme, denoting its exclusive use by Angelo Sauper, a young officer of the Neo Zeon remnants known as the Sleeves.
This heavily armed version is equipped with large propellant tanks for extended missions, and a Lange Bruno gun for long-distance sniping.
The barrel of which has a sensor that links with the head's mono-eye for deadly accuracy.

A prototype mobile suit developed by the Neo Zeon remnant faction known as the Sleeves.
Its design concept was to recreate the high mobility psycommu system testbed developed by the Principality of Zeon during the One Year War, but with U.C.0096 technological advancements.
Using Angelo Sauper's Geara Zulu as a base, drastic alterations have been made.
Attached to its shoulders are massive arms with built-in mega particle cannons, which can be used as wire-guided remote weapons.
The legs have also been switched out for hefty thruster units, and propellant boosters for long-range cruising, bigger than the machine itself, can be mounted on its back.

The Narrative Gundam, fitted with equipment allowing for high mobility.
During its development, an emphasis was placed on upgrading its legs, and it can achieve an explosive acceleration by aiming all of its large boosters backward.
In addition, it operated on the front lines of the Phoenix Hunt mission and comes equipped with the Psycho Capture, a composite special weapon designed to capture the Phenex.

A fifth-generation mobile suit developed by Anaheim Electronics was prepared for Mafty in order to raid the Adelaide Conference.
It is a sibling unit to the Penelope but incorporates even more advanced technology due to its built-in Minovsky Flight Unit.
Its total height surpasses 26 meters and its arms are especially more massive when compared to standard mobile suits, resulting in a monstrous silhouette despite being named a Gundam.

A mobile suit developed by Anaheim Electronics Co.
It was deployed to the front lines by Kenneth to wipe out Mafty.
When the Odysseus Gundam is equipped with the FF (Fixed Flight) unit, it is designated the Penelope.
As one of the first mobile suits to incorporate a miniaturized Minovsky Flight, it boasts advanced solo flight capabilities within the atmosphere.
While it also possesses supersonic flight capabilities, it must first transform into its flight form to utilize them.

A mobile suit developed by Anaheim Electronics Co.
It was deployed to the front lines by Kenneth to wipe out Mafty.
When the Odysseus Gundam is equipped with the FF (Fixed Flight) unit, it is designated the Penelope.
As one of the first mobile suits to incorporate a miniaturized Minovsky Flight, it boasts advanced solo flight capabilities within the atmosphere.
While it also possesses supersonic flight capabilities, it must first transform into its flight form to utilize them.

A commander-type Messer with a blade antenna outfitted on its head and enhanced communication capabilities.
It was the unit piloted by Hathaway before he received the Xi Gundam. Its unique coloring denotes it as the mobile suit of the leader, Mafty Navue Erin.

This mobile suit was secretly developed by Anaheim Electronics Co. as the main machine of the anti-Federation organization known as Mafty. Though it has the characteristic structure of a Zeon MS, it carries weapons in line with the Federation's standards, such as a beam saber and a beam rifle. Its body is heavily armored, and the thrusters on its back enhance its mobility, granting it an overall performance rivaling that of the Federation's mass-produced Gustav Karl.

The primary mobile suit used by Mafty.
Specializing in guerrilla warfare in coordination with a Galcezon, when in free fall it can deploy the vectored tail stabilizer attached to its back to perform air braking and attitude control.
The suit's name changes depending on the parts included in its build. Gawman pilots a Type-F01.
The Messer Type-F02 is equipped with a minelayer unit attached to its vectored tail stabilizer for bombardment use.

The Messer Type-F02 is equipped with a minelayer unit attached to its vectored tail stabilizer for bombardment use.
It features the SFS (Sub-Flight System) used by Mafty.
By deploying the rear hatch and raising the grips on the center of the frame, up to two mobile suits can be loaded vertically.

A mass-production mobile suit deployed within the Earth Federation Forces in U.C. 0105.
Standing at 22 meters tall from head to toe, it is a large machine among mass-produced units.
In the process of going from the early units deployed in U.C. 0096 to the current mass-produced design, all aspects underwent complete reevaluations and improvements, including the construction of the frame, and the model name was changed from Type 13 to Type 00.

A mass-production mobile suit deployed within the Earth Federation Forces in U.C. 0105.
Standing at 22 meters tall from head to toe, it is a large machine among mass-produced units.
In the process of going from the early units deployed in U.C. 0096 to the current mass-produced design, all aspects underwent complete reevaluations and improvements, including the construction of the frame, and the model name was changed from Type 13 to Type 00.
It uses the EFF's cutting-edge SFS (Sub-Flight System).
The top of the unit is outfitted with space for up to two mobile suits loaded in parallel, and it is capable of vertical takeoff and landing.

The RX-104FF Penelope had been deployed by Kenneth as a trump card to eradicate Mafty.
TX-ff104, commonly referred to as the Alyzeus, was hastily assembled to be used by Lane Aim as a machine to train for high-speed flight while in Flight Form until the Penelope was deployed.

A prototype mass-production model of the RX-93 Nu-Gundam.
Its machine specs are approximately 80% of Nu-Gundam's.
Minor changes were made for this machine to function as the foundation of Alyzeus, and the block structure of the head part and groin joints were changed.

A prototype mobile suit developed as a testbed by S.N.R.I., a proponent of mobile suit miniaturization.
The F90 forms the base of the machine, and despite specializing in melee combat, it only comes equipped with basic weaponry, allowing excellent mobility.
However, by attaching and detaching various mission packs to the hardpoints covering its body, it can respond flexibly to a wide range of missions.

A mobile suit developed by S.N.R.I.(Strategic Naval Research Institute), which specializes in mobile suit miniaturization.
It has "achieved the maximum performance mobile suits are capable of at the present point in time (U.C.0123)."
Its entire structure, from basic frame to armor, is densely packed, and its Biosensor measures pilot skill, enabling it to fully demonstrate its capabilities.

A mobile suit modified from the first Crossbone Gundam to counter the Quavarze, the Jupiter Empire's latest mobile suit.
The only modifications made were to its weapons, and it is now equipped with a special weapon for close-range combat called the Screw Web, stored inside its rear skirt armor.

The primary mobile suit of the Jupiter Empire that became its basic model.
Optimized for the Jupiter Sphere, it has retractable legs to optimize thruster performance.
Its design, whereby its head, torso, and limbs were adapted for specific functions, was intended to facilitate the development of successor mobile suits while achieving miniaturization, higher performance, and efficient production.

A general-purpose transformable and dockable mobile suit independently developed by League Militaire.
Its weapons can be enhanced by combining its Core Fighter (middle section) with its Top Limb (upper section) and Bottom Limb (lower section).
In its mobile suit form, it's a high-performance machine surpassing BESPA's mobile suits.

The primary mobile suit of the Earth Federation Forces.
It is a downsized version of the Heavygun and is easier to produce.
A large number thereof is stationed on EFF ground bases.

The primary general-purpose mobile suit of the Earth Federation Forces.
It is equipped with a shot lancer for combat inside colonies.

A small, general-purpose mobile suit developed by BESPA, it was designed to use the Einerad, but also boasts relatively high performance on its own and excels in group combat.
By deploying the beam shields equipped on its arms, it can cover the Einerad's blind spots, which enabled it to overrun the Macedonia colony and Tampico.
Developed as a primary mobile suit to replace the Zoloat, several units were deployed by the Motorad Squadron and were the bane of the League Militaire's mobile suit squadrons.

The Mobile Fighter that represented Neo Japan.
As the Mobile Fighter developed for the 13th Gundam Fight, it was designed by Dr.Mikamura.
Notable for being able to switch combat modes in response to its pilot's emotions, it can also utilize its special move, the Shining Finger, upon reaching its second stage, Battle Mode.
In its third stage, Super Mode, all of its cooling covers fully open and the entire machine shines golden.
In this state, its pilot can unleash its full fighting potential.

A Mobile Fighter representing Neo Japan, designed and developed by Rain's father, Dr. Mikamura.
Its pilot is colonial martial arts champion Domon Kasshu, whose techniques it can accurately trace.
It has the ability to switch among three modes according to its pilot's emotions: Normal Mode, Battle Mode, and Super Mode.
Its special move is the Shining Finger, in which it uses its fists covered in liquid metal to deliver a crushing blow to the enemy Mobile Fighter's head.

The Mobile Fighter that represented Neo America.
As the Mobile Fighter developed for the 13th Gundam Fight, it was designed with an all-American motif, incorporating thematic elements such as American football, boxing, and gunslinging.
With its twin Gigantic Magnum pistols and a knock-out punch, it is a capable fighter at both close and long range.
It is particularly formidable at close combat, where it shifts to Boxer Mode and wears its shoulder armor like gloves.

A Gundam that can shift between two modes to adapt to the flow of battle.
The spaulders can be equipped as boxing gloves, doubling the strength of its punches.
It has the flexibility to remove its tough armor, entering Boxer Mode and gaining quicker footwork.

The Mobile Fighter that represented Neo France.
As the Mobile Fighter developed for the 13th Gundam Fight, it sports a chivalrous design fit for a knight.
It conceals countless Rose Bits under the cape draped over its left shoulder and is a dangerous fighter at any range.
A highly mobile machine, it swings the Chevalier Saber in close-quarters combat.

A Gundam modeled after knights of old that is noted for its high mobility and an elegant, refined fighting style.
When released, the Rose Bits stored beneath the cape-like shield on its left shoulder dance like flowers, disorienting enemies. Moreover, they can be used to fire beams from multiple angles, create a defensive matrix with its Beam Net, or even self-destruct, leaving enemies no chance of retaliation.

The Mobile Fighter that represented Neo China.
As the Mobile Fighter developed for the 13th Gundam Fight, it is designed to be piloted by a martial artist and is highly mobile.
Specializing in martial arts techniques, it fights with twelve beam flags, its extendable Dragon Claws, and other such martial arts-inspired weapons.

A Gundam built with the purpose of tracing Sai Saici's mastery of Shaolin Temple martial arts.
It excels at disorienting enemies with its dynamic movements as well as surprise attacks, and can conjure a protective barrier with its Feilong Flag.
Its Dragon Claw, an extendable weapon that resembles a dragon's head, is able to spit scorching flames or crush armor using its jaws.

The Mobile Fighter that represented Neo Russia.
As the Mobile Fighter developed for the 13th Gundam Fight, it boasts excellent power and armor.
Taking advantage of its powerful output, it specializes in brute-force, and the Graviton Hammer mounted on its left shoulder can crush a typical Mobile Fighter in a single blow.

A Gundam built with emphasis on power and armor.
It is a heavily-fortified, high-output machine with independent propulsion systems called Victor Engines installed throughout its frame.
As a result, its raw strength is roughly twice that of a normal Gundam and it uses its devastatingly powerful Graviton Hammer to batter its enemies.

A Mobile Fighter developed by Neo Germany for the 13th Gundam Fight.
This unit specializes in concealment and mobility, and by having this composition, the pilot Schwarz Bruder is able to utilize the German-style Ninjutsu he acquired to its fullest.
Ninjutsu, such as Disguise, where the Shadow Gundam disguises into other objects, and Invisibility, where it assimilates with its surroundings, are used to confuse the enemies during battles.
In addition, the Spiegel Blade equipped on the arm is so powerful that it can cut through the armor of a Mobile Fighter and by spinning with it in high speed, it creates a powerful technique, Sturm Und Drang, that destroys the enemy with a single blow.

The Mobile Fighter that represented Neo Hong Kong.
It was the winner of the 12th Gundam Fight.
The fact that it entered the tournament with hardly any upgrades proved the brilliance behind its original design.
Built to be piloted by Master Asia, it can perfectly trace his signature cloth-wielding fighting techniques.

A Mobile Fighter used by Master Asia, Undefeated of the East in the 13th Gundam Fight.
How this unit was developed remains a mystery.
One theory says that Master Asia used the Shuffle Heart he piloted during his time in the Shuffle Alliance and completed this unit with the combination of the DG (Dark Gundam) Cells.
The structure of the unit is able to effectively reproduce Master Asia's combat ability, generating multiple ultimate moves of The School of Master Asia.
Since DG Cells cannot erode organisms with a strong mentality, Master Asia is able to overcome the DG Cells with his robust spirit, and repair the unit using the DG Cells without being taken over by it.

A Mobile Fighter used by Master Asia, Undefeated of the East in the 13th Gundam Fight.
How this unit was developed remains a mystery.
One theory says that Master Asia used the Shuffle Heart he piloted during his time in the Shuffle Alliance and completed this unit with the combination of the DG (Dark Gundam) Cells.
The structure of the unit is able to effectively reproduce Master Asia's combat ability, generating multiple ultimate moves of The School of Master Asia.
Since DG Cells cannot erode organisms with a strong mentality, Master Asia is able to overcome the DG Cells with his robust spirit, and repair the unit using the DG Cells without being taken over by it.

The Mobile Fighter that represented Neo England.
This Neo England-made Gundam was the winner of the 9th through 11th Gundam Fights.
Shooting with a long beam gun is its specialty, and its accuracy is unmatched.
Chapman, its pilot, was an expert marksman who was hailed as a hero of Neo England.

The Mobile Fighter that represented Neo Italy.
It was one of the Mobile Fighters that was developed for the 13th Gundam Fight.
Its legs are equipped with 16 Satyricon Beams, and it specializes in leg-based attacks.

A type of mobile suit spawned by the Dark Gundam.
Although a single unit does not pose a significant threat on its own, they move in swarms and overwhelm their opponents with sheer numbers.
As humanoid mobile suits, they can also be disguised as Gundams like the Master Gundam and the Dragon Gundam and deployed as decoys.

A type of mobile suit spawned by the Dark Gundam.
As a Dark Army variant, it consists of a Dark Army mobile suit attached to an aerial unit with its legs folded behind its back.

A type of mobile suit spawned by the Dark Gundam.
As a Dark Army variant, it consists of a Dark Army mobile suit with bat-like wings attached to its back.
The Dark Gundam generated Dark Bats from tentacle-like Gundam Heads after it captured the space colony of Neo Japan.

A type of mobile suit spawned by the Dark Gundam.
As a Dark Army variant, it consists of a Dark Army mobile suit attached to a quadrupedal unit with its legs folded behind its back.
It uses a specialized machine rifle as its weapon.

When it was originally developed, it was conceived as a rival model to the Shining Gundam.
However, Dr.Kasshu intended it serve a far nobler purpose than a mere Mobile Fighter and developed it to restore the Earth.
Since its development was based on the three major theoretical abilities of self-regeneration, self-evolution, and self-replication, its potential far exceeds any single weapon.

Originally, it was the Ultimate Gundam, equipped with the three major theoretical abilities of self-regeneration, self-evolution, and self-replication.
In order to repair the damage its programming sustained when it crash landed on Earth, however, it began harnessing terrestrial energy for self-regeneration.
Since the Dark Gundam is controlled through psychic energy, it requires a powerful biological unit to serve as its core.

A mysterious mobile Fighter that was operated by Black Joker, a member of the Shuffle Alliance.
Its high performance outclasses even top-of-the-line Mobile Fighters.
It specializes in wrapping its enemies in chains of light.

A mysterious mobile Fighter that was operated by Ace of Clubs, a member of the Shuffle Alliance.
Its high performance outclasses even top-of-the-line Mobile Fighters.
It excels in melee combat due to its high mobility.
In addition, its shoulders are equipped with large beam cannons.

A mysterious mobile Fighter that was operated by Queen of Spades, a member of the Shuffle Alliance.
Its high performance outclasses even top-of-the-line Mobile Fighters.
Highly mobile, it attacks by throwing countless knives of light.

A mysterious mobile Fighter that was operated by Jack of Diamonds, a member of the Shuffle Alliance.
Its high performance outclasses even top-of-the-line Mobile Fighters.
It is armed with a powerful flamethrower.

A mobile Fighter piloted by Master Asia, Undefeated of the East, during the Shuffle Alliance era.
It was the King of Hearts' personal machine.
Among the machines piloted by the Shuffle Alliance, its performance is the highest, demonstrating unrivaled prowess in melee combat.

A Neo England unmanned mobile suit.
It is mass-produced to support the Royal Gundam.
It is armed with a beam rifle, but its performance is low.
Nevertheless, it uses a similar paint job to the Royal Gundam and the cover of fog to confuse its opponents, handing the Royal Gundam many victories.

A Neo Japan mass-produced mobile suit.
They were used to defend the area around the Tokyo Metropolitan Government in the ruins of Tokyo.
Equipped with beam guns and beam sabers, their performance is only notable for how standard it is.
However, their numbers paled in comparison to the Dark Army, and they failed to stop the latter's invasion.

A Mobile Fighter representing Neo Holland.
It survived the qualifying rounds by disguising itself as a windmill.
Its special move is the Nether Typhoon, a wind attack it creates with the giant fan blades mounted on its chest.

A Mobile Fighter representing Neo Nepal.
Its high-output thrusters enable it to engage in aerial combat.
In addition to concealing a Beam Saber, its staff is also eqquipped with a flamethrower.

A Mobile Fighter representing Neo Canada.
One of the most powerful combatants in the tournament, it specializes in grappling by bear-hugging its opponents and hurling them backwards.
The two Grizzly Axes mounted on both of its arms also serve as powerful weapons.

A Mobile Fighter representing Neo Denmark.
Its underwater combat power increases dramatically when it enters Fish Mode.
It captures its opponents in its Beam Net, then finishes them off with its Mermaid Trident.

A Mobile Fighter developed by Neo Japan for the 13th Gundam Final Fight.
This unit has all 11-months of tournament data of the Shining Gundam embedded into it, and its performance under normal condition matches up to the Shining Gundam's super mode.
Various parts and manipulators have been modified and improved, as well as the linkage with the pilot, Domon Kasshu, to achieve a higher accuracy of reflecting Domon's combat ability and style.
One of the greatest features, Hyper Mode, is activated by using the energy multiplier that amplifies the pilot's spirits and allows them to concentrate their energy.
When activated, the unit can perform its final move, Erupting Burning Finger, and The School of Master Asia's ultimate move, Sekiha Tenkyoken.

A Mobile Fighter developed by Neo Japan for the 13th Gundam Final Fight.
This unit has all 11-months of tournament data of the Shining Gundam embedded into it, and its performance under normal condition matches up to the Shining Gundam's super mode.
Various parts and manipulators have been modified and improved, as well as the linkage with the pilot, Domon Kasshu, to achieve a higher accuracy of reflecting Domon's combat ability and style.
One of the greatest features, Hyper Mode, is activated by using the energy multiplier that amplifies the pilot's spirits and allows them to concentrate their energy.
When activated, the unit can perform its final move, Erupting Burning Finger, and The School of Master Asia's ultimate move, Sekiha Tenkyoken.

A Mobile Fighter developed by Neo Sweden for the 13th Gundam Fight.
Although it has a feminine outlook as a whole, the unit has high-density mounting of high-performance parts and has been given a thorough weight reduction, making it one  of the most dynamic and agile Mobile Fighter.
There are many special weapons that take advantage of these characteristics, such as a Beam Hoop, which attacks the enemy by throwing a beam shaped-like disk, and Beam Ribbon, a whip-shaped beam with flexible attacks.
The main feature of this unit is the Berserker System, that ignores the controls of the pilot and makes them violent, drawing out the maximum capabilities of the pilot and the unit.
As a price to pay for this overwhelming ability, the burden on the pilot is enormous, and the duration is short and unstable.

A Mobile Fighter developed by Neo Japan for the battle with the Dark Gundam.
This unit is the same model used during the development and completion of the Shining Gundam, and has been redesigned to fight the Dark Gundam.
As compared to the Shining Gundam, which focuses on melee battles, it is a combination of a Mobile Suit's weapon concept and a Mobile Fighter's fuselage potential, making it more practical for battles.
It's left forearm has the ability to transform into a bow and fire a powerful beam, Bow Beam, and it is equipped with a long sword, Heat Naginata, that can cut through armor made of strong steel.
Its special move, Ultimate Rising Arrow, is a powerful attack that shoots through the target with a single blow.

The Noble Gundam, mutated by DG Cells.
It boasts a formidable arsenal, including 10 large-caliber beam cannons and Walter Fangs.
It also posseses the capability to escape the atmosphere on its own.

The Royal Gundam, mutated by DG Cells.
It can transform into something resembling a four-legged beast.
It can also fire a powerful electric shock called Grand Thunder from its Grand Horns, the giant spikes mounted on its back.

The Neros Gundam, mutated by DG Cells.
It can assume an avian-like flight mode, and its performance in aerial combat far surpasses other Mobile Fighters.
It can also perform a variety of attacks, including Heaven's Dart, Wind Flare, and Tornado.

The main reactor of the Colony Dark Gundam, mutated by DG Cells.
It confronted the new members of the Shuffle Alliance, wielding each of the Four Kings's techniques.
While it was destroyed by the Shuffle Alliance Attack, the combined technique of all five Shuffle Alliance members, it later regenerated after Domon approached its core.

A core-like entity that separated from the head of the evolved Dark Gundam.
Rain serves as its biological unit in Kyoji's stead.
Deep within the Colony Dark Gundam, it controls nearly every operation of the Dark Gundam.

A Neo Japan mobile fighter that participated in the 12th Gundam Fight.
Its Gundam Fighter was Urube Ishikawa.
Appears in Urube's reminiscence.
Equipped with two Long Swords.

A Neo Japan mobile fighter that participated in the 12th Gundam Fight.
Its Gundam Fighter was Urube Ishikawa.
Appears in Urube's reminiscence.
Equipped with two Long Swords.

A Neo Japan mobile fighter that participated in the 12th Gundam Fight.
Its Gundam Fighter was Urube Ishikawa.
Appears in Urube's reminiscence.
Equipped with two Long Swords.

A Gundam developed by the Colony Liberation Organization for special assignments.
This high-spec Mobile Suit was designed for maximum versatility by preeminent Beam Weapons researcher Doctor J.
It is able to transform into Bird Mode for high-speed flight, and the Gundanium Alloy used in its armor grants it uncannily high durability.
Its exceedingly powerful signature weapon, the Buster Rifle, can vaporize multiple Mobile Suits with a single shot.

A prototype unit created by the five Gundam Scientists 15 years prior to the start of Operation Meteor.
Created to be the supreme Gundam, it was developed with a razor-sharp focus on performance with little thought given to ease of production or practicality.
Wielding Twin Buster Rifles powerful enough to destroy whole colonies and a cockpit equipped with a performance- enhancing Zero System, it is peerless in terms of sheer combat ability.
Development was halted due to its overwhelming destructive power and the instability of the Zero System, but it was put into action after Quatre Raberba Winner obtained the schematics and completed the suit himself.

A Gundam developed by the Colony Liberation Organization for special assignments.
Designed by Professor G, an authority in stealth technology, this top secret, high-maintenance suit was developed for solo infiltration and guerilla warfare.
Its back-mounted Hyper Jammer allows the unit to approach unassuming enemies before cutting them down with its Beam Scythe, giving it the nickname the "Grim Reaper."

A Gundam developed by the Colony Liberation Organization for special assignments.
It was designed by Professor H, a household name in software and interface engineering, and developed with a focus on close-quarters combat.
With more robust armor than other Gundams, it comes equipped with twin Heat Shotels attached to its back.
Combining its shotels, shield, and backpack to form the Cross Crusher allows it to rend even the most heavily-armored opponents.
The Maganac Corps owes many of its victories to this Gundam's superior command and control capabilities.

A Gundam developed by the Colony Liberation Organization for special assignments.
It was designed for intense firefights based on the designs of Doctor S, specialist in weapons control systems.
With a powerful arsenal covering every inch of its body, the suit is more than enough to take an entire base by itself.
Its Beam Gatling Gun being the only weapon that does not use live ammunition, it has never been known to run out of bullets.

A Gundam developed by the Colony Liberation Organization for special assignments.
It was made for close-quarters combat based on the designs of propulsion systems specialist Master O.
Equipped with a Dragon Fang mounted on its right hand as well as a Beam Glaive, it ranks highly in close-quarters bouts even among other Gundams of its type.
The Dragon Fang itself comes furnished with flamethrowers, giving it extended range.
Its pilot, Chang Wufei, refers to it using the pet name "Nataku."

This mobile suit was developed at the behest of OZ leader Treize Khushrenada and based on the Tallgeese and five Gundams.
Its ability to transform allows it to fly at high speeds for extended periods of time.
In keeping with Treize's wishes, it does away with projectile weapons in favor of a Beam Sword and Heat Rod, granting it unmatched performance in close quarters.
A Zero System similar to the Wing Gundam Zero's grants it high performance along with a risk of driving the pilot completely insane.
The suit passed from the hands of Treize to Heero Yuy before being piloted by Milliardo Peacecraft.

A combat-spec Mobile Suit developed by the Gundam Scientists, the first of its kind.
In addition to being the blueprint for the Leo, it serves as the common ancestor to all Mobile Suits including the Gundams.
At the upper limits of Mobile Suit performance,it has power and mobility beyond the reach of later mass-produced units, but its unwieldy nature in the hands of the average pilot meant only one prototype survived when its development plans were destroyed.
Zechs Merquise awakened it from its slumber at the Corsica base in AC 195, piloting it himself to face off against the powerful Gundams after their arrival on Earth.
Its name is derived from the term "theurgist," meaning "wonder-worker."

The personal suit of World Nation leader Treize Khushrenada.
It was built by Zechs Merquise using spare parts from his own treasured Tallgeese with the intention of piloting it himself.
Though a Gundam-style head and altered color scheme distinguishes it from the original, its capabilities remain the same.
Treize's masterful piloting, in addition to its fearsome mobility, played a key role in his assault on the White Fang's Mobile Dolls.

Developed by the OZ as the first of its kind and geared towards versatility and ease of production, this Mobile Suit was manufactured in large quantities to form the Alliance's main strength and is deployed in most regions.
Interchangeable parts makes it flexible on a variety of battlefields, and its numerous variations give manifold strength to more factions than just the Alliance, allowing it to hold the line until the battle is decided.
Its name is taken from the astrological sign.

A space-ready version of the Alliance's mass-produced Leo.
Equipped with a zero-g backpack, it is specially tailored for space combat.
However, its overall capabilities are mostly unchanged, due to it using the same equipment as its Earth-based version.
This unit is employed by various factions including the Alliance, OZ, and White Fang as the primary weapon in many a battle.

The OZ's version of the Leo.
Essentially identical to the Alliance's own version in terms of performance.
These formed the OZ's core strength along with the Aries during the initial stages of the coup d'etat, but fell out of favor with the development of the Mobile Dolls.

The OZ's space-ready version of the Leo.
It is equipped with a zero-g backpack and especially tailored for space combat.
It is essentially identical to the Alliance's own version in terms of performance.
These, along with the Aries, formed the OZ's core strength during the initial stages of the coup d'etat, but fell out of favor with the development of the Mobile Dolls.

A cannon variant of the united Earth Sphere Alliance's mass-produced Mobile Suit, the Leo Ground Type.
Its shoulder-mounted cannons give it improved firepower and different armor plating to fit them.
They are primarily deployed in areas like the Mogadishu fortress for base defense.

A rear support Mobile Suit developed by OZ to provide backup to the Leo.
It also had optional parts like hover units developed alongside it.
It is capable of bipedal walking when it detaches the hover unit around its legs, enabling it to adapt to the needs of a given battlefield or situation.
It has large cannons mounted on its shoulders, giving it generous firepower support from the rear.
Individually, its performance is low and it does not possess any melee weapons, so it is effectively employed as a moving battery.

OZ's mass-produced aerial Mobile Suit employed by both the OZ and the Alliance.
This suit can transform into a high-speed flight mode by withdrawing its legs into its body.
Though not especially strong or sturdy compared to the Leo, it outstrips it in terms of maneuverability and range.
Its inability to stand against newer Mobile Suits and the debut of the Mobile Doll System made it a rare sight.
It takes its name from the astrological sign.

A Taurus model belonging to the Preventers.
This is Lucrezia Noin's personal Taurus that she has used since her time in the Sanc Kingdom.
After the rise of the Earth Sphere United Nation, with the entire world militarily disarmed, it became one of the scant few Mobile Suits left to oppose the Mariemaia Army's uprising.
It fought alongside Zechs Merquise's Tallgeese III and attacked the Mariemaia Army's stronghold in Brussels, going head to head against squadron after squadron of Serpents.

The OZ's version of the Aries.
It is essentially identical to the Alliance's own version in terms of performance.
Though it allowed the OZ to defeat the Alliance's Leos one after another during the initial stages of the coup d'etat, it fell out of favor with the development of the Mobile Dolls.

The Maganac Corps' primary suit, developed independently in the Middle East.
Though conceived with desert combat in mind, its extreme versatility makes it useful for space combat with minimal modification.
With performance roughly on-par with the united Earth Sphere Alliance's Leos, the Maganac is formidable in the hands of skilled pilots working together.
The Maganac Corps possesses 40 of these units, each with unique upgrades, making no two the same.
Its name is taken from the Tagalog "mag-anak," or "family."

The OZ's mass-produced Mobile Doll, developed by Chief Engineer Tubarov, is based on the Vayeate and Mercurius.
Designed with the Mobile Doll AI in mind, it does not feature a cockpit.
The wide Beam Cannon on its right arm and the Planet Defensors on its left shoulder give it superior offense and defense.
It is often found in groups of three to compensate for the blind spot created by its Planet Defensors.
Mass-produced as the primary weapon of the Romefeller Foundation, its high performance and group tactics give it a strong advantage over conventional Mobile Suits.
It takes its name from the astrological sign.

The White Fang's mass-produced Mobile Doll unit.
An upgraded version of the Virgo made with space operations in mind.
Its back-mounted booster pods grant it extreme maneuverability.
An increased number of Planet Defensors leave it with fewer blind spots than the Virgo.
Though originally developed by the OZ, its parts were recovered by the White Fang during its occupation of the Lunar Base.
Put into production as a primary unit, the White Fang made great use of them together with the Libra.

A prototype Mobile Suit developed by the Gundam Scientists while imprisoned by the OZ.
Designed for maximum offensive capability, it's unparalleled in combat ability when paired with the defense-oriented Mercurius.
Powered by a back-mounted Beam Generator, the Beam Cannon is its sole weapon and surpasses even the Wing Gundam's own Buster Rifle.
Later it would be manufactured with a Mobile Doll System under the White Fang.
It takes its name from an Egyptian goddess.

A prototype Mobile Suit developed by the five Gundam Scientists while imprisoned by the OZ.
Designed for maximum defensive capability, its back-mounted Planet Defensors generate an electromagnetic field used to protect its offensive partner the Vayeate.
It also comes equipped with a Beam Gun and Crash Shield, giving it an advantage in close quarters.
Later it would be manufactured with a Mobile Doll System under the White Fang.
It takes its name from the Roman god of thieves.

A space-ready Mobile Suit developed as a replacement for the Leo as OZ's standby.
Its ability to transform gives it high maneuverability and a heavy advantage against the Leo.
Though made for space combat, light modifications allow it to fight battles within the atmosphere.
Its specialized Large Beam Cannon "Taurus Cannon" is powerful enough to pierce Gundanium.
Manufactured as the primary Mobile Suit of OZ's space forces, its superior performance led to its adoption by other factions.
The first pick for the production line of Mobile Dolls, many fly without pilots.
It takes its name from the astrological sign.

The Sanc Kingdom's version of the Taurus.
Captured in secret by Lucrezia Noin and added to the Kingdom's forces, it's been colored white to make it easier to identify.
Its inherently high mobility makes it more than a match for a Mobile Doll in the hands of the right pilot.
Even after the fall of the Kingdom, it remains one of Noin's most prized suits.

The White Fang's Version of the Taurus.
It was captured from the OZ and is now part of the White Fang's forces.
Aside from its magenta coloring for identification purposes, there's not much to distinguish it from the OZ's own version.
While it served as a primary fighter during the start of the uprising, the mass production of the Virgo II led to its disappearance from the field.

An upgraded version of the Gundam Deathscythe.
This model was repaired and modified for zero-g operations by the Gundam Scientists after the original Deathscythe was destroyed by the OZ.
It comes equipped with Anti-Beam Active Cloak coating for enhanced defense as well as an upgraded Hyper Jammer for improved stealth.
A total weapon overhaul gives it Twin Beam Scythes as its primary weapons.

An upgrade from the Gundam Sandrock, modified by  Peacemillion's Mike Howard for use in space combat.
Exchanging its backpack for full-body vernier thrusters grants it improved mobility.
Though this change results in the loss of the Cross Crusher, the improved rate of fire of its Beam Machine Gun makes up for this in raw firepower.

An upgrade from the Gundam Heavyarms.
Abandoned on Earth, the Heavyarms was delivered to Peacemillion by Sally Po and modified for use in space by Mike Howard.
Among its enhancements are stronger backpack thrusters, increasing its maneuverability to make it viable in zero-g combat.
A notable change to its equipment is the Twin Beam Gatling, a twofold increase on the original's main weapon.

An upgraded version of the Shenlong Gundam.
Salvaged from the OZ Lunar Base, repairs and improvements were made on the original by the Gundam Scientists.
Altron, in keeping with its namesake two-headed dragon, is now equipped with Dragon Fangs on both hands and has had its Beam Glaive replaced with the Twin Beam Trident.
The Beam Cannon installed on its back compensates for its previous long-range handicap.

Wing Gundam's design from the manga, Glory of the Losers.
While it differs from the design of the same unit in the TV series, the two are treated as one and the same for the purposes of the manga.
It is largely similar to Wing Gundam Zero EW, with a different color scheme using the tricolor style.
The Wing Binders on its back have been made to look more mechanical, with complex parts.
Its main weapon, the Buster Rifle, has been made bigger and longer, and it has a firing capacity of 9 shots, including reserve energy packs.

Wing Gundam's design from the manga, Glory of the Losers.
While it differs from the design of the same unit in the TV series, the two are treated as one and the same for the purposes of the manga.
It is largely similar to Wing Gundam Zero EW, with a different color scheme using the tricolor style.
The Wing Binders on its back have been made to look more mechanical, with complex parts.
Its main weapon, the Buster Rifle, has been made bigger and longer, and it has a firing capacity of 9 shots, including reserve energy packs.

The first prototype Mobile Suit, developed in secret on the MO-V industrial satellite.
The cockpit serves as the core component, with the ability to mix-and-match modular G-Units to maximize performance across diverse environments.
Even at the base level its performance dwarfs that of mass-produced suits, and its rich arsenal includes an Accelerate Rifle and Beam Swords.
Its PX System boosts performance based on the pilot's psychological state for a short time, but its use requires ability, training, and calibrations unique to each pilot.

The second prototype Mobile Suit, developed in secret on the MO-V industrial satellite.
The cockpit serves as the core component, with the ability to mix-and-match modular G-Units to maximize performance across diverse environments.
The sole feature distinguishing it from the 01 is its blue coloring.

A customized OZ Prize Vayeate.
An improvement on the Vayeate developed by the OZ equipped with dual Beam Cannons and Beam Generators, it dwarfs the original in terms of sheer firepower.
These machines were used by the personal guard of OZ Prize's Valder Farkill.

The Gundam Deathscythe Hell's OVA and movie version.
With its Active Cloak reminiscent of a pair of otherworldly wings, this suit looks like a vision of the reaper itself.
Its Buster Shield has been replaced in favor of a compact shield attached to each arm.
While it was sent to the Sun for disposal after the battle with the Libra, it was recovered during the Barton Uprising to fight against the Mariemaia Army.
Despite the difference in design from the TV version, it is meant to be the exact same suit.

A high-stakes weapon developed by the United Nations Earth as a trump card to counter the Space Revolutionary Army's colony drops during the 7th Space War.
Possessing a Flash System to maximize Newtype pilots' combat abilities, it is able to control unmanned drones called Bit Mobile Suits.
It is also equipped with a Satellite System, allowing its energy supply to be transmitted directly from the Super Microwave facility D.O.M.E., located on the surface of the moon.
This energy supply also powers the Gundam X's greatest weapon, the large Satellite Cannon, which possesses the power to destroy even a colony with a single shot.
However, in order to receive energy via Super Microwave, a Newtype was required to access the SMW facility upon the system's first use.
In addition, this unit cannot be started up without a control device called G-Controller.
A unit known for its power, although difficult to operate.

A Mobile Suit reconstructed from the Gundam X, after it was severely damaged in the Fort Severn battle.
Repairing the Satellite Cannon, as well as other equipment used by the Gundam X, proved near impossible.
Kid Salsamille, the Freeden's mechanic, opted to make use of his stockpile of junk parts, painstakingly restoring the unit to a condition suitable for Mobile Suit combat.
The main Shield Buster Rifle armament was replaced with a Beam Machine Cannon, while a Large Beam Sword—as well as a spare—was also included.
Another significant change is the installation of a massive weapon called the Divider; though normally used as a shield, it can also be used as a propulsion unit thanks to the thrusters installed on both ends.
The Divider also possesses a 19-shot Beam Cannon, known as the Harmonica Cannon, a versatile and powerful firearm.

A transforming high mobility mobile suit developed by the United Nations Earth before the Gundam X was created.
Although there were issues with both the firepower and durability of these units when compared to those of other Gundams, their rate for taking fire turned out to be the lowest, and they were put to extensive use during the decisive battles of the last great war.

A mobile suit developed by the former United Nations Earth before the Gundam X was created.
Designed for intense combat, this mobile suit is equipped with multiple weapons and heavy armor.
Its main armament is the inner arm gatling on its left shoulder, though this weapon attaches to the left arm when used.

A Mobile Suit developed for Newtypes by the Space Revolution Army.
It was used by the Space Revolutionary Army to carry out Operation Lilac in the 7th Space War to gain control of Earth.
The remaining parts were collected by remnants of the Space Revolutionary Army and redeveloped secretly.
Since achieving balance was the focus of its design, both the left and right sides of the unit were designed symmetrically, and weapons that would cause imbalance to the unit were all installed internally.
The meticulous design enables high maneuverability during battle without breaking its balance.
The most prominent feature is Bit, a remote-controlled weapon that can only be used by those with excellent Newtype abilities.
It consists of a small Beam Cannon and moves in response to the brain waves of the pilot, but can also act independently, and can be used for simultaneous attacks on different targets and concentrated attacks from various positions in battle.

This mobile suit was developed by the Government Reconstruction Committee, comprised of the remnants of the former United Nations Earth, following the end of the 7th Space War.
It is equipped with a Trans-System that allows it to transform into a mobile armor.
Possessing enough power to be able to combine with the Virsago, the Ashtaron also wields a pair of gigantic atomic scissors for melee combat, and can even use them in its mobile armor form.

This mobile suit was developed by the Government Reconstruction Committee, comprised of the remnants of the former United Nations Earth, following the end of the 7th Space War.
Though the Virsago is installed with a Flash System, no compatible bit mobile suits were built.
Due to the staggering power of the Megasonic Cannon in its chest, it uses its strike claws to stabilize itself when firing.

A G-Bit stationed at the lunar base.
A bit mobile suit deployed by the will of D.O.M.E., it was utilized for self-defense.
It was given the task of guiding the people to the truth they deserved, while also repelling those scheming to invade the lunar base for nefarious purposes.

G-bits were unmanned units that Newtypes could control via the Flash System.
Newtypes were able to manipulate G-bits that had the same specifications and weapons as the mother unit.
Before the great war, Gundam squads consisted of one mother Gundam unit and around 12 bit mobile suits.
The GX-bits were the G-bits over which the Gundam X had control.

The former United Nations Earth's main mass production mobile suit.
Incredibly versatile, it was also easy to produce and maintain.
Enhanced units were also relatively easy to develop, and many variations were produced during the great war.
Its basic format is sometimes referred to as the DT-6800A for convenience.

A support type equipped with a large cannon on its right shoulder.
Though its mobility is slightly reduced, its supporting bombardments enable it to achieve great results on the battlefield.
Some versions of this mobile suit are equipped with bazookas.

A Daughtress unit with enhanced communications for commander use.
Though almost identical to regular units in terms of functionality, it is equipped with an additional antenna.

A high mobility commander type model of the former United Nations Earth-developed Daughtress.
Piloted by Ennil El, it boasts an enhanced sensor system, among other things.

A high mobility model of the former United Nations Earth-developed Daughtress.
Its hover capabilities help it travel over land at high speeds.
Used by Zakot's crew.

The former Space Revolutionary Army's main mass production mobile suit, utilized during the last great war.
Incredibly versatile, it was also easy to produce and maintain.
Enhanced units were also relatively easy to develop, and many variations were produced during the great war.
An enhanced land-type version of the Jenice, this was the former Space Revolutionary Army's main mass production mobile suit.

A specialized mobile suit supplied by Lumark of the sea Vultures and piloted by the freelancer Ennil El.
Characterized by the sub-camera on its head.

A mobile suit with distinctive horns atop its head.
The personal unit of the female mobile suit pilot Vedoba Morte, its name was derived from its horns.

An enhanced version of the Jenice Custom Ground Battle Type mobile suit developed by the former Space Revolutionary Army.
Following the war, these units were utilized by, among others, the vigilantes of Fort Severn city.

A mass production mobile suit developed by the former Space Revolutionary Army.
With better mobility than even the Jenice, this mobile suit is distinctive for its large legs, which have built-in high-output Verniers.
There is also the land-type use RMS-009G model.

A mass production mobile suit owned by the Vultures.
As an improved version of the Septem's ground warfare variant, the G type, the enhanced air intake systems and joints of these mobile suits render them especially dust-proof.
Most of the mobile suits used by Vultures in the post-war world were Septem Kai units.

A handmade mobile armor created by Lumark of the sea Vultures, assembled using parts he salvaged.
Though it possesses high mobility and acceleration, its handling and maneuverability are lacking, resulting in a propensity for sluggish motion.
This armor was piloted by Ennil El and used to reconnoiter Zonda Epta.

A high mobility mobile suit developed by the former Space Revolutionary Army.
Equipped with various verniers, it boasts exceptional acceleration and mobility.
There is also the land-type RMS-14G model.

A high mobility, mass production mobile suit owned by the Vultures.
These units were the result of the former Space Revolutionary Army gathering Octapes and customizing them for ground use.
The unit deployed to the ship of the Vulture Gritz Jo was designed with a white color scheme.

A mobile armor developed by the New United Nations Earth.
As production of the Balient was prioritized, manufacture of them was ended after roughly 30 were built.
Its max speed and turning performance outstrip those of the Air Master.

A super-heavyweight prototype unit developed by the New United Nations Earth, it has far heavier armor than regular mobile suits and is equipped with a field generator that neutralizes beam attacks.
This mobile suit overwhelms enemies simply by weaponizing its sheer enormity.
Second Lieutenant Milra Draid piloted one of these units.

A mass production mobile suit based on the Estardoth and developed jointly by the South Asian countries of Estardo, Northernbell, and Gastar.
Though developed to oppose the New United Nations Earth, this mobile suit was ultimately utilized by them once Gastar betrayed the other two nations to join them.

A colossal mobile armor operated by placing a Newtype inside its Psycommu duct.
This unit was scheduled to be introduced during Operation Lilac, the plan devised by the former Space Revolutionary Army to invade Earth, but the operation failed and the war ended.
Mayor Nomoa (once the Space Revolutionary Army scientist Dorat) of Fort Severn established the city at the mobile armor's crash site and kept it concealed there.

A colossal mobile armor operated by placing a Newtype inside its Psycommu duct.
This unit was scheduled to be introduced during Operation Lilac, the plan devised by the former Space Revolutionary Army to invade Earth, but the operation failed and the war ended.
Mayor Nomoa (once the Space Revolutionary Army scientist Dorat) of Fort Severn established the city at the mobile armor's crash site and kept it concealed there.

The first mobile suit mass produced by the New United Nations Earth after the war.
Developed partially to revitalize the UNE's air force, which had been ravaged by the last war, these units boast impressive flight capabilities.

A Newtype-use mobile armor that was developed by the former Space Revolutionary Army towards the end of the last great war and deployed in actual combat.
The unit piloted by Lancerow Dawell engaged in many fierce battles with Jamil and the Gundam X.
Its head functions as an escape pod.
Its model number reflects that it started development as a mobile suit.

A variant of a New United Nations Earth-developed heavily armed rear support mobile suit that was enhanced to specialize in melee combat.
Its right arm is equipped with a monofilament heat wire that can slice through even Luna Titanium alloy.
Second Lieutenant Duett Langraph piloted one of these units.

A mobile suit with a built-in Flash System that was developed by the New United Nations Earth.
Similarly to the Gundam-type mobile suits, it can control bit mobile suits.
Its matching bit mobile suits were specially named Bit Rasvehts.
While piloting one of these mobile suits in a clash with the DX, Second Lieutenant Abel Bauer awakened as a Newtype.

The first mass production mobile suit manufactured by the small South Asian nation of Estardo after they obtained the cooperation of Northernbell and Gastar.
It was developed using the mobile suits built by the former Space Revolutionary Army as reference.
These mobile suits ended up in museum exhibits in the post-war world.

A mobile armor used by the former Space Revolutionary Army's descent squads.
Equipped with a long-range particle cannon and an anti-air beam cannon, this armor is utilized as a portable gun battery for raids on key strongholds.
After the war, it was used by the Alternative Company among others.

A New United Nations Earth mobile suit boasting incredibly lightweight armor.
With agility that far outstrips that of regular mobile suits, it can run rings around its enemies and use its sole armament, a beam knife, to perform inescapable attacks in extremely close range melee combat.
Second Lieutenant Demar Griffe, who was scouted by the Frost brothers, piloted one of these units.

A Mobile Suit developed by the New United Nations Earth after the war using the latest technology based on Gundam X.
Since it inherits the concept and data of Gundam X, and the cockpit has been installed as it is.
The Satellite System can be started up without a Newtype.
In addition, the Satellite Cannon is upgraded to the Satellite System Mk-II, a two-barrelled Twin Satellite Cannon armed with overwhelming attack power.
With this improvement, the unit can fire multiple shots with the Twin Satellite Cannons with only one microwave energy absorption.
Other armaments have also been comprehensively strengthened to meet the performance level required for the strongest Mobile Suit in After War.
This unit also requires a specialized G-Controller to start up, but it can also be activated using the GX-9900 G-Controller.

A Mobile Suit developed by the New United Nations Earth after the war using the latest technology based on Gundam X.
Since it inherits the concept and data of Gundam X, and the cockpit has been installed as it is.
The Satellite System can be started up without a Newtype.
In addition, the Satellite Cannon is upgraded to the Satellite System Mk-II, a two-barrelled Twin Satellite Cannon armed with overwhelming attack power.
With this improvement, the unit can fire multiple shots with the Twin Satellite Cannons with only one microwave energy absorption.
Other armaments have also been comprehensively strengthened to meet the performance level required for the strongest Mobile Suit in After War.
This unit also requires a specialized G-Controller to start up, but it can also be activated using the GX-9900 G-Controller.

A successor to Gundam Virsago developed by the Government Reconstruction Committee to fight against the Space Revolutionary Army.
The Government Reconstruction Committee, established among the upper class of the former United Nations Earth, develops the unit with the intention to resurrect Gundam, a high-takes weapon that is said to be the symbol of the United Nations Earth in the previous Great War.
One of them is the Gundam Virsago.
This unit inherits its characteristics, and is able to adapt to the space environment together with improved overall combat ability.
Initially, the Gundam Virsago, which had a high overall ability is redeveloped to further specialize in its artillery combat ability.
Among them, the Mega Sonic Cannon, a powerful armament is added to the chest and two additions to the abdomen.
Also, the output has been enhanced and implemented as Triple Mega Sonic Cannon.
It is capable of doing a link-up with the Gundam Ashtaron Hermit Crab, where the unit receives microwave energy and transfers it to the consort unit to release a powerful attack, Satellite Launcher, which has the same potential as the Satellite Cannon.

A mobile suit modified to improve upon the Air Master's greatest asset-namely, its mobility.
Additionally, its armaments have also been upgraded, featuring one beam cannon on its nose and four on its boosters when in fighter mode.

A mobile suit manufactured as an improved version of the Gundam Leopard.
It is equipped with the Twin Beam Gatling, which has a similar albeit superior mechanism to the conventional Inner Arm Gatling, on both its arms.
In addition to boasting improved offensive capabilities, its head parts have also been refitted.

A successor to Gundam Ashtaron developed by the Government Reconstruction Committee to fight against the Space Revolutionary Army.
The Government Reconstruction Committee, established among the upper class of the former United Nations Earth, develops the unit with the intention to resurrect Gundam, a high- stakes weapon that can be said to be the symbol of the United Nations Earth in the previous Great War.
One of them is the Gundam Ashtaron.
This unit inherits its characteristics, and is able to adapt to the space environment together with improved overall combat ability.
Similar to the base unit, the transformation mechanism, Trans-System, is adopted.
However, unlike the base machine, the body of the Moblie Armor is composed of a gentle streamline.
It is capable of doing a link-up with the Gundam Virsago Chest Break, where the consort unit receives the microwave energy and releases a powerful attack, Satellite Launcher, which is attached to the Mobile Armor and has the same potential as the Satellite Cannon.

A new mobile suit model, it was the first developed by the Space Revolutionary Army after the war.
It boasts thick armor capable of withstanding beam attacks as well as high mobility thanks to the verniers mounted all over its frame.
The beam cutter equipped on its back rivals anti-ship artillery in firepower.

A next-generation mass production mobile suit developed by the New United Nations Earth.
As the latest model of the Daughtress-type, it sports a certain degree of variation in its armaments.
It was deployed by the Frost Guard.

A support mecha for Gundam-type mobile suits developed by the former United Nations Earth.
It is a high-mobility fighter of the Mobile Suit Carrier-type, capable of transporting a mobile suit while moving at high speeds.
It was scrapped during development, but Satyricon put it into operation after recovering and completing it.

A mobile suit developed by the Space Revolutionary Army after the war.
It features heavy armor capable of repelling beam attacks, combined with high mobility enabled by large verniers.

The Gundam Leopard mounted with optional equipment designed for underwater combat.
Also known as the underwater assault loadout.
The S-1 Unit, a modified submarine propulsion system created by Kid, allows the land-based Leopard to operate underwater.
Equipped with noise sensors and a towed sonar.
Its Inner Arm Gatling has been refitted with a micro-torpedo launcher.

A modified amphibious Daughtress.
During the war, countless prototypes were created until mass production became possible.
Able to hamper enemy attacks with its extendable arms.
The mobile suits among Doza's fleet were equipped with D-Navi Systems using dolphin brains.

An improved variation of the Daughseat.
Better adapted for underwater combat than its predecessor, with powerful hydrojet engines mounted on its legs.

An aerial combat variant equipped with a flight pack.
Though it was not among the most agile mobile suits in the air, due to the general lack of aerial combat forces across the board after the war, it was still able to deliver substantial results.

The combined mobile suit form of the G-Falcon and Gundam Double X for counter-MS operations.
Though the Gundam Double X was created post-war, its design was based on the Gundam X, allowing seamless combination with the G-Falcon.
This form can use both the G-Falcon and Gundam Double X's weapon systems, as well as the G-Falcon's thrusters as its main propulsion system, giving this mobile suit remarkable firepower and mobility.
However, the unbalanced form robs this mobile suit of agility, hindering melee capabilities.

The combined mobile suit form of the G-Falcon and Gundam X.
Greatly improves upon the mobility and cruising range of the Gundam X.
This allows the Gundam X, an exceptionally decisive weapon, to be promptly deployed wherever needed, making it possible to intercept a colony drop.
However, the war came to an end before the G-Falcon went into mass production, thus joint operation with Gundam-type mobile suits was never implemented at a large scale.

The combined form of the G-Falcon and Gundam Airmaster.
Designed for long-range travel with the Gundam Airmaster stowed within the G-Falcon's fuselage.
Greatly extends the Gundam Airmaster's cruising range, broadening its operational radius.
However, the war came to an end before the G-Falcon went into mass production, thus joint operation with Gundam-type mobile suits was never implemented at a large scale.

The combined form of the G-Falcon and Gundam Leopard.
Designed to remarkably expand the operational radius of the Gundam Leopard—a mobile suit with no flight capabilities—while also improving its maneuverability.
However, the war came to an end before the G-Falcon went into mass production, thus joint operation with Gundam-type mobile suits was never implemented at a large scale.

A Mobile Suit that emerged from the "White Doll" statue in the ruins of Vicinity, originally buried in the Mountain Cycle and worshipped by the local people.
When the forces of Dianna Counter attacked the city of Nocis, it suddenly reactivated and revealed itself to be the Turn-A Gundam.
This unit uses a drive system called IFBD (I-Field Beam Drive), and Nanomachines that can repair the unit's armor.
Except for the Turn X, it has no relation to other Mobile Suits.
The most prominent feature is the Moonlight Butterfly System, which scatters a large amount of nanomachines into the air, forming a Nanomachine Field on its back like the wings of a butterfly.
Once activated, the wings change color like an aurora, disintegrating everything they touch, and it can even cut through Mobile Suit armor.
It is unclear for what purpose the Moonlight Butterfly System was created, but it is believed its destructive power was the reason it was sealed away and hidden as a relic of the Dark History.

A small mobile suit that is a tandem two-seater.
It is armed with a weapon not unlike a jitte called the Joint Buster.
It can also wield the Eagail's Minchi Drill, and when it does, a sixth digit emerges from its arm to support the large size of the weapon.
The Militia call it the Armadillo.

A Mobile Suit that the Dianna Counter dug out from the Lunar's Mountain Cycle.
A total height of 40m, it is considerably larger than any other Mobile Suits.
The unit's surface is coated with Nanomachine, a lost technology on Moon.
In addition, all armaments are equipped in the head, making it an independent unit which also can be used as a fixed turret for base defense.
As it has an artillery body specializing in long-ranged support with only shooting weapons equipped on it, it uses its arms and legs when it comes to close combat.
Due to its unique form, it is known as Scarecrow in Militia.

One of the GAT-X series Mobile Suits, the Strike Gundam was a joint development between the Atlantic Federation and Morgenroete, Inc. in the fight against the ZAFT's Mobile Suits.
The GAT-X series is loaded with the latest in experimental Beam Weapon tech to offer performance that puts the ZAFT's mass-produced varieties to shame.
Special Phase Shift Armor offers near-total immunity to physical damage while active but shifts down once the battery's energy runs out, deactivating and returning the armor to its original gray color.
The suit's greatest characteristic is its ability to be fitted with multi-weapon Striker Pack units.
These packs can be swapped out as necessary, offering the Strike Gundam maximum flexibility on any battlefield.
Its standard complement of weapons includes a head-mounted "Igelstellung" Anti-Air Autocannon and a "Armor Schneider" Combat Knife on its hip.

A Strike Gundam Striker Pack variant, the Aile Striker Pack is intended for high-speed mid-range combat.
Combining four large thrusters with wings ensures maximum maneuverability in both zero-g environments and within the Earth's atmosphere.
Working as a back-mounted sheathe for its Beam Sabers, the pack offers serious offensive power when used in conjunction with the standard high-output Beam Rifle.

A Strike Gundam Striker Pack variant, the Sword Striker Pack is intended for close-quarters combat.
Designed with ship assaults in mind, its Anti-Ship Sword "Schwert Gewehr," vessel-capturing rocket anchor "Panzer Eisen," and Beam Boomerang "Midas Messer" with its unique trajectory helps it keep the advantage in close-quarter fights.
This makes the pack a high performer not only against other Mobile Suits, but also against ships and when taking bases.

A Strike Gundam Striker Pack variant, the Launcher Striker Pack deals heavily in projectile-based fights.
Its standard Mobile Suit-spec weapons include an absurdly powerful Large Beam Cannon "Agni" in addition to a Combo Weapon Pod featuring a Vulcan cannon and gun launchers for close-range defense.
While its weight means a tradeoff in mobility, the sheer amount of firepower sees it used to attack far-off fleets and fortresses.

A Strike Gundam Striker Pack variant, the Multiple Assault Striker Pack combines aspects of the Aile, Sword, and Launcher Packs.
Featuring high-output back thrusters, the Hyper Impulse Cannon "Agni," and the Anti-Ship Sword "Schwert Gewehr," its equipment takes a note from each pack.
This experimental pack, though high in sheer offensive power, is difficult to master, and the increase in weight sacrifices a great deal of maneuverability.

One of the GAT-X series Mobile Suits, the Aegis Gundam was a joint development between the Atlantic Federation and Morgenroete, Inc. in the fight against the ZAFT's Mobile Suits.
The GAT-X series is loaded with the latest in experimental Beam Weapon tech to offer performance that puts the ZAFT's mass-produced varieties to shame.
Special Phase Shift Armor offers near-total immunity to physical damage while active but shifts down once the battery's energy runs out, deactivating and returning the armor to its original gray color.
Its convertible frame, unique to the GAT-X series, allows the Aegis to transform into a Mobile Armor.
The suit's sparse complement of weapons including Beam Rifles and Beam Sabers keeps any hindrance to transformation minimal.
Its increased mobility while in Mobile Armor mode allows the Aegis to get in close, striking with its claws and Large-Caliber Beam Cannon "Scylla" before withdrawing.
This is an especially effective tactic in battles against warships.

One of the GAT-X series Mobile Suits, the Duel Gundam was a joint development between the Atlantic Federation and Morgenroete, Inc. in the fight against the ZAFT's Mobile Suits.
The GAT-X series is loaded with the latest in experimental Beam Weapon tech to offer performance that puts the ZAFT's mass-produced varieties to shame.
Special Phase Shift Armor offers near-total immunity to physical damage while active but shifts down once the battery's energy runs out, deactivating and returning the armor to its original gray color.
A standard suit designed with flexibility in mind, it elects to keep its weapons simple with Beam Sabers and Beam Rifles.
Being the nearest of the GAT-X series to completion, it was used as a base model for later suits.

A Duel Gundam with additional equipment.
The Assault Shroud refers to the ZAFT-developed equipment add-on system made for the GINN and modified for use with the Duel Gundam.
It comes equipped with additional armor for increased defense, along with shoulder-mounted Railgun "Shiva" and a 5-Tube Missile Pod for enhanced offensive power.
Though the extra equipment does add weight, the addition of more thrusters and stabilizing nozzle covers offer the lowest possible compromise to maneuverability and balance.

A Duel Gundam with additional equipment.
The Assault Shroud refers to the ZAFT-developed equipment add-on system made for the GINN and modified for use with the Duel Gundam.
It comes equipped with additional armor for increased defense, along with shoulder-mounted Railgun "Shiva" and a 5-Tube Missile Pod for enhanced offensive power.
Though the extra equipment does add weight, the addition of more thrusters and stabilizing nozzle covers offer the lowest possible compromise to maneuverability and balance.

A Duel Gundam with additional equipment.
The Assault Shroud refers to the ZAFT-developed equipment add-on system made for the GINN and modified for use with the Duel Gundam.
It comes equipped with additional armor for increased defense, along with shoulder-mounted Railgun "Shiva" and a 5-Tube Missile Pod for enhanced offensive power.
Though the extra equipment does add weight, the addition of more thrusters and stabilizing nozzle covers offer the lowest possible compromise to maneuverability and balance.

Part of a joint development between the Atlantic Federation and Morgenroete, Inc.
in the fight against the ZAFT's Mobile Suits.
It is one of the GAT-X series Mobile Suits.
Starting with its Beam Weapons, the GAT-X series is loaded with the latest in experimental tech to offer performance that puts the ZAFT's mass-produced varieties to shame.
Special Phase Shift Armor offers near-total immunity to physical damage while active but shifts down once the battery's energy runs out, deactivating and returning the armor to its original gray color.
Designed for long range assaults, it comes equipped with powerful projectile weapons 350mm Gun Launchers on each hip and a 94mm High-Energy Beam Rifle for maximum firepower.
Both weapons can be linked to create a unique firearm which can be used to launch devastating attacks at various targets.

One of the GAT-X series Mobile Suits, the Blitz Gundam was a joint development between the Atlantic Federation and Morgenroete, Inc. in the fight against the ZAFT's Mobile Suits.
The GAT-X series is loaded with the latest in experimental Beam Weapon tech to offer performance that puts the ZAFT's mass-produced varieties to shame.
Special Phase Shift Armor offers near-total immunity to physical damage while active but shifts down once the battery's energy runs out, deactivating and returning the armor to its original gray color.
Designed for solo top-secret missions, its Mirage Colloid stealth system uses a full-body coating of colloid particles to completely avoid both electronic and visual detection methods.
Geared towards assault and infiltration, its equipment includes the Offensive Shield System "Trikeros" and the wired rocket-propelled "Gleipnir" used to maximize its ability to manage enemies.

A second-gen GAT-X model, created by the Alliance based on the data from the first generation.
Featuring an improved OS and a step up from Phase Shift to Trans Phase Armor, this enhanced and upgraded series is an improvement on the first in nearly every way.
This projectile-focused suit expands on the Buster Gundam's development concept. Back-mounted Large Beam Cannons "Schlag" and a high-speed plasma-powered Bazooka "Todesblock" are just examples of its impressive weaponry.
Though less than agile due to its heavy loadout, a set of full body thrusters allows it to hover in place.

A second-gen GAT-X model, created by the Alliance based on the data from the first generation.
Featuring an improved OS and a step up from Phase Shift to Trans Phase Armor, this enhanced and upgraded series is an improvement on the first in nearly every way.
This assault-focused suit expands on the Blitz Gundam's development concept. Its energy-deflecting Geschmeidig Panzer system applies Mirage Colloid technology to deflect an enemy's beams by generating powerful magnetic fields.
This tech is exploited even further with the Plasma Cannon "Hresvelgr" whose beams can be curved in virtually any direction.
Additional weapons including Railguns and a handheld scythe give it incredible versatility allowing the Forbidden Gundam to switch seamlessly from long-range to close-range.
Flipping its backpack over its head to transform into assault mode allows it to fly at high speeds even within the Earth's atmosphere.

A second-gen GAT-X model, created by the Alliance based on the data from the first generation.
Featuring an improved OS and a step up from Phase Shift to Trans Phase Armor, this enhanced and upgraded series is an improvement on the first in nearly every way.
This suit expands on the Aegis Gundam's development concept, adding a Mobile Armor transformation in pursuit of vastly increased mobility.
With a shoulder-mounted 52mm Hyper Velocity Shield Cannon and spiked Mjolnir mace, this machine is made to go up against other Mobile Suits.
Able to fly through the atmosphere by shifting to Mobile Armor mode makes it a prime candidate for quick strikes.
It was also used as part of a sub-flight system for the flightless Calamity Gundam.

A mass-produced Orb union Mobile Suit.
Developed as part of Morgenroete's Astray series using data obtained during the development of the Alliance's GAT-X series, the suits hold a number of similarities.
Featuring lightweight foam metal armor for enhanced mobility and equipped with a backpack based on the Strike Gundam's own Aile Striker Pack, it has the ability to fly for short bursts of time.
Specialized for defense, it comes armed with lightweight Type 70 Beam Rifles and Type 70 Beam Sabers to help keep the enemy at bay.
Though developing an OS to handle the suit proved difficult at first, Kira Yamato's engineering led to its completion and to large numbers being adopted as one of Orb's primary units.

A mass-produced Alliance Mobile Suit developed based on data from the Strike Gundam, the first GAT-X series model.
With versatility and mass production in mind, it has been stripped of the series' frills such as larger armaments and Phase Shift Armor.
The suit's OS has also been improved, offering a more accessible control scheme for Naturals.
Though it has near-identical performance to the base model,standard compact Beam Weapons offer it a leg up over ZAFT's favored GINN.
These mass-produced suits served as the Alliance's main force during the opening stages of the conflict in Panama.

A Strike Gundam copycat created by Orb.
Under a veil of secrecy, Orb recovered a severely damaged Strike Gundam from the Marshall Islands. The resulting data and spare parts were used to create this suit.
Mostly identical to the original, it features unique upgrades made by Morgenroete, Inc.
Notable among these is an enhanced Power Extender for increased operational range and Phase Shift Armor for stronger defense.
As a result, its armor shifts from gray to red to indicate that the armor has activated.
Built to be the exclusive suit of Cagalli Yula Athha, daughter of the Orb union's leader, it was built on the Kusanagi and was later added to the Clyne Faction of PLANT.

Developed to be the premium command version of the ZAFT's BuCuE ground-based Mobile Suit.
Though slightly larger than the BuCuE, it retains the quadrupedal setup and treads of the former with the addition of greatly improved maneuverability.
The back-mounted turret now comes equipped with a 2-Barrel Beam Cannon.
Legs featuring claws for close-quarters combat and the enhanced output of its head-mounted Beam Saber makes it an effective anti-Mobile Suit fighter.
Its two-seater setup allows the pilot to control the suit while the copilot acts as a gunner, increasing its potential as an incredibly agile threat.
While on the African front, Andrew Waldfeld, "Desert Tiger" and his partner Aisha used this to back Kira Yamato's Strike Gundam into a corner.

A ZAFT ground-based quadrupedal Mobile Suit.
It was developed for use in high gravity environments, especially in desert regions.
With a quadrupedal setup that allows it to stay balanced on rough terrain and caterpillar treads for high speed on even surfaces, this suit stays incredibly maneuverable regardless of environment.
Conversely, its lack of standard Mobile Suit Manipulators requires the appropriate piece of equipment to be added to its back-mounted turret.
Later models were also equipped with a fixed Beam Saber.
This model features a 450mm 2-Barrel Railgun whose large projectiles can take out an Alliance Linear-Gun Tank in a single shot.

A transforming ZAFT projectile-based suit.
This suit was developed for long-range attacks on bases and to provide support on the battlefield.
Shoulder-mounted 2-Barrel Cannons and a wealth of other weaponry give it considerable offensive power.
