Low maneuverability combined with a lack of melee weapons means that these suits are often used to lay down supporting fire from the rear, or as gun batteries."

A primary ZAFT mass-produced Mobile Suit.
The first official ZAFT suit of its kind, it became the basis for every ZAFT model thereafter.
This model was developed to maximize both versatility and augmentability for operations in gravity-rich rather than zero-g environments.
It comes equipped with a Heavy Sword for close-quarters combat, a 76mm Heavy Assault Machine Gun for engaging the enemy, and a rich store of optional parts.
Said to be worth five of Alliance's favorite Moebius Mobile Armors in terms of sheer performance, it allowed the severely-outgunned PLANT to come out on top during the early stages of the battle.
Its endless number of variants customized for each environment and objective are a testament to the suit's versatility.

A GINN equipped with the Barrus Modified Heavy Ion Cannon, a large D-Type Beam Cannon intended for base invasions.
Unfortunately, due to its limited energy reserves, the cannon can only fire three times, making it unsuitable for combat against other Mobile Suits.
Miguel Aiman launched in a GINN with this armament when he was ordered to attack the Strike Gundam on Heliopolis.

A version of the ZAFT's GINN equipped with a D-Type heavy arsenal for capturing strongholds.
It packs a great deal more firepower in the form of Missile Launchers in each hand and Missile Pods in each leg.
Equipped for projectile-based fights, this variant is not known to carry melee weapons, and its weight generally rules out close-quarters engagements.
The highly-versatile GINN can fit into nearly any situation with a few simple changes to its equipment.

A GINN variant used for reconnaissance-in-force.
A modified version of the suit used for reconnaissance in space, it comes equipped with radomes on each shoulder and sensors on every part of its body to enhance its search capabilities.
Its extended operational range over the standard GINN also allows it to act as a long-range scout.
A two-seater cockpit gives the suit room for both an operator and an intel-gatherer.
Equipped with a sniper rifle, this suit's keen ability to search out the enemy gives it deadly accuracy.

A ZAFT command Mobile Suit, developed as the successor to the GINN.
Inheriting the GINN's versatility, it also has an increased number of full-body thrusters for greatly enhanced maneuverability.
The addition of an upgraded Heavy Sword and Vulcan-equipped shield brings a respectable boost to its overall performance.
Effective, but not quite ready for the production line, a small number of these found themselves reserved for select distribution to squadron leaders.
One of these was piloted by ZAFT Commander Rau Le Creuset as part of a mission to pursue the Archangel.

A ZAFT mass-produced Mobile Suit, developed as the successor to the GINN and CGuE.
While it retains the frame of the ZAFT's general-purpose machine, head-mounted sensor lights and machine guns reflect the technology and design sensibilities of the data retrieved from the stolen GAT-X series suits.
It also comes outfitted with the same Phase Shift Armor as the Alliance's mainstays as well as a Beam Rifle, a first for any of the ZAFT's mass-produced units.
The GuAIZ also boasts innovative weaponry including a shield-capable Beam Claw in addition to hip-mounted Rocket Anchors equipped with Beam Guns.
While these suits came in massive numbers to join the fights around Boaz and Jachin Due, they arrived too late to affect the end result.

The command version of ZAFT's GuAIZ mass-produced Mobile Suit.
With no notable modifications save for the white paint job, it retains the performance of the common version.
ZAFT Commander Rau Le Creuset took the first of these off the line, using it to engage the Strike Gundam at the Mendel Colony.

A ZAFT mass-produced Mobile Suit, developed as the successor to the GOOhN.
Though it shares the GOOhN's ability to cut down on water resistance by transforming, the time it takes to do this has been reduced.
In contrast to the GOOhN's torpedo-heavy approach, this suit comes equipped with claws on both arms as well as Phonon Maser Cannons to make it into an effective Mobile Suit fighter.

An amphibious Mobile Suit developed for the ZAFT's invasion of Earth.
Specialized for underwater combat, it can transform to cut down on water resistance as it moves at high speeds.
Its defining characteristic is a set of Lorenzini Sensors, which are modeled after a shark's sensory organs and used to detect electrical impulses in the water.
Intended to target Alliance ships and coastal bases using torpedoes, the GOOhN does not fare well in close-combat.

A ZAFT atmospheric Mobile Suit developed for mid-air battles in high gravity.
Based on the GINN and made to be as light as possible, it comes equipped with large wings stowed in its backpack.
Its aeroshell helmet cuts down on air resistance, allowing it to fight at high altitudes without support from a Guuru.
In addition to the standard 76mm Heavy Assault Machine Gun, its 90mm Anti-Air Shotguns for Maximum anti-air performance and chest-mounted Missile Launchers make it effective at anything from maintaining control of the skies to taking strongholds.

An Alliance atmospheric support fighter.
Based on the Alliance's favored fighter, the Spearhead, it was developed to support the Strike Gundam.
Its main role involves delivering Striker Packs to the Strike Gundam on the frontlines.
Its additional ability to use a Striker Pack's equipment gives this fighter unparalleled offensive power.
Two of these crafts were transferred to the Archangel from the Alliance's 8th Fleet.
Unit 1, piloted by Mu La Flaga, began working with the Strike Gundam and became an essential part of their forces.

A Skygrasper equipped with the Aile Striker Pack.
The Aile Striker's thrusters work as additional boosters, greatly increasing the craft's maneuverability and range.
It also sees a vast increase in firepower when equipped with the Strike Gundam's Beam Rifle and shield on each side.

A Skygrasper equipped with the Launcher Striker Pack.
The ability to use the Hyper Impulse Cannon "Agni" and combo weapons pod results in an exponential increase in firepower.
This is especially true for the mighty Agni, which can easily deal a fatal blow to the average Mobile Suit.

A Skygrasper equipped with the Sword Striker Pack.
Its ability to deploy the Anti-Ship Sword "Schwert Gewehr" for slashing attacks, or the Panzer Eisen to provide cover is somewhat hampered by the skill needed to utilize them effectively.

An Alliance general purpose space fighter.
This Alliance mainstay can perform vertical takeoffs and landings by pointing its vector nozzles downward and using VTOL nozzles to direct the engine exhaust.
Superior performance in the hands of a pilot with the right strategy put it on the level of ZAFT's own Mobile Suits.

A ZAFT anti-tank combat helicopter.
Without the tail rotor of a standard helicopter, it flies using only the main rotor on top of its body.
Though equipped with wired missiles and Vulcans, it makes a poor match for Mobile Suits and is thus restricted to strikes on armed vehicles, ground facilities, and reconnaissance.

The Alliance's favored tank.
It features main Linear Guns and room for a driver, operator, and gunner.
Its superior performance compared to other tanks makes it a match for a ZAFT ZuOOT in the hands of a veteran.
A common sight at the beginning of the war, their inability to stand against ZAFT's new Mobile Suits saw them quickly reduced to being mere cannon fodder.
Even as the focus shifted to Mobile Suits, these tanks stood on the frontlines of the ground war until the Alliance put their own suits into production.
The Orb union also deployed a considerable number of them.

The Alliance's Mobile Armor mainstay.
This unit was put onto the production line without the hard-to-master gunbarrels of the Moebius Zero.
Though it comes in objective-based variants, the standard version comes equipped with Linear Guns and Anti-Ship Missiles.
 Its superior maneuverability brought a healthy number of victories at the outset of the conflict, but the debut of the GINN Mobile Suit turned the tides and saw it quickly rendered obsolete.

The Alliance's mainstay Moebius Mobile Armor equipped with a nuclear missile.
The drop in mobility caused by switching out its Linear Guns with a large nuclear missile means the armor is no longer battle-ready.
This unit is notable for launching a nuclear attack on the Junius Colony, sparking the "Bloody Valentine" incident.
It was later used in the Battle of Boaz to deliver the nuclear payload which obliterated the asteroid.

An Alliance Mobile Armor fighter jet.
These suits were developed to secure the Alliance's foothold in space, taking the place of the Alliance's multipurpose Mistral pods that were initially deployed to fight the ZAFT's Mobile Suits.
Its four exterior wired gunbarrels act as thrusters, granting the suit an impressive rate of acceleration.
These barrels can also be detached and used in an all-range attack to hit the enemy from multiple directions.
Using the gunbarrels in this way, however, requires an incredible amount of spatial awareness that few pilots were capable of.
Its detachable nose section can also be used as a battering ram as well as to descend into the atmosphere.

A ZGMF-X series suit, developed based on data from stolen Alliance GAT-X series units and created as a joint project between ZAFT's Mobile Suit divisions.
With the devastating output provided by its Neutron Jammer Canceller nuclear reactor and virtually unlimited use of Phase Shift Armor, its performance far outstrips that of the GAT-X series.
Intended to hold its own against entire crowds of Mobile  Suits, its devastating arsenal includes Balaena Plasma Beam Cannons in each wing, Xiphias Railguns on each hip, and a multi-lock-on system with the ability to track multiple targets at once.
The sheer amount of offensive might is enough to spell instant doom for an entire fleet.
Deploying the wings on its back will shift the suit into HiMAT (High Mobility Aerial Tactics) mode, granting it superior maneuverability whether on- or off-planet.

The Freedom Gundam in a more powerful configuration.
In this form, the Freedom Gundam is docked with a METEOR large-scale weapons module designed to improve its combat abilities.
With a variety of powerful weapons, including a Large-Caliber Beam Cannon and Anti-Ship Missiles, the METEOR module provides staggering firepower via a simple docking process that can be easily disengaged in emergencies.
When undocked, the METEOR module can dock itself with the Eternal, a ship designed specifically to support the ZGMF-X Series, and serve as a gun turret.

A ZGMF-X series suit, developed based on data from stolen Alliance GAT-X series units and created as a joint project between ZAFT's Mobile Suit divisions.
With the devastating output provided by its Neutron Jammer Canceller nuclear reactor and virtually unlimited use of Phase Shift Armor, its performance far outstrips that of the GAT-X series.
The suit's most distinguishing characteristic by far is the Fatum-00 unit mounted on its back.
Though it can give an added boost compared to traditional propulsion units, it can also be detached for use in remote attacks or as a sub-flight system.
Armed with twin Lacerta Beam Sabers which can be combined into a single blade, as well as Bassel Beam Boomerangs concealed in both shoulders, this machine is capable of unleashing unconventional attacks when linked with a decoupled Fatum-00.

The Justice Gundam in a more powerful configuration.
In this form, the Justice Gundam is docked with a METEOR large-scale weapons module designed to improve its combat abilities.
With a variety of powerful weapons, including a Large-Caliber Beam Cannon and Anti-Ship Missiles, the METEOR module provides staggering firepower via a simple docking process that can be easily disengaged in emergencies.
When undocked, the METEOR module can dock itself with the Eternal, a ship designed specifically to support the ZGMF-X Series, and serve as a gun turret.

A ZGMF-X series suit, developed based on data from stolen Alliance GAT-X series units, and created as a joint project between ZAFT's Mobile Suit divisions.
With the devastating output provided by its Neutron Jammer Canceller nuclear reactor and virtually unlimited use of Phase Shift Armor, its performance far outstrips that of the GAT-X series.
Custom-tailored to use the quantum communication-based DRAGOON weapons system, its all-range attacks can operate all 11 units to dominate virtually every modern Mobile Suit.
Created with Rau Le Creuset's peerless spatial awareness in mind, this tool is one that only a select number of pilots can make full use of.
Despite the DRAGOON System's added weight, the suit's designation as a close-quarters fighter gives it maneuverability that belies its bulky appearance.

A ZGMF-X series suit, developed based on data from stolen Alliance GAT-X series units, and created as a joint project between ZAFT's Mobile Suit divisions.
With the devastating output provided by its Neutron Jammer Canceller nuclear reactor and virtually unlimited use of Phase Shift Armor, its performance far outstrips that of the GAT-X series.
Custom-tailored to use the quantum communication-based DRAGOON weapons system, its all-range attacks can operate all 11 units to dominate virtually every modern Mobile Suit.
Created with Rau Le Creuset's peerless spatial awareness in mind, this tool is one that only a select number of pilots can make full use of.
Despite the DRAGOON System's added weight, the suit's designation as a close-quarters fighter gives it maneuverability that belies its bulky appearance.

A ZAFT ground-based quadrupedal Mobile Suit.
It was developed for use in high gravity environments, especially in desert regions.
With a quadrupedal setup that allows it to stay balanced on rough terrain and caterpillar treads for high speed on even surfaces, this suit stays incredibly maneuverable regardless of environment.
Conversely, its lack of standard Mobile Suit manipulators requires the appropriate piece of equipment to be added to its back-mounted turret.
Later models were also equipped with a fixed Beam Saber.
This model comes equipped with a 400mm 13-Tube autotracking large Missile Pod for effective point control.

A configuration of the Strike Rouge with alternate equipment.
The Integrated Weapons Striker Pack, or IWSP for short, combines the mobility, close-range combat, and artillery-focused characteristics of the other packs into one.
 Initially developed by the PMP Company, the massive amounts of energy required simply to create the power pack saw the project left on the cutting room floor.
 Later falling into Orb's hands, they worked on it in secret, using their know-how in creating high-capacity battery technology to make it a reality.
However, it was only completed aboard the Kusanagi after the fall of the union.
The final version, equipped on the Strike Rouge,was abandoned after numerous test runs after pilots found its complex fire control system too much to handle.

A mass-produced Alliance Mobile Suit developed based on GAT-X series data.
This is the official mass-produced version of the Strike Gundam.
Equipped with the Striker Pack plug which had been removed from the Strike Dagger to make it easier to manufacture, its head-mounted sensors have also been replaced by the same high-spec variety used in the Strike Gundam.
Though Phase Shift and Trans Phase Armor have been left off the equation due to cost, its use of Laminated Armor provides a dramatic increase in resistance against Beam Weapons.
Though officially named the "Dagger," it was designated the "105 Dagger" to better reflect the Strike Gundam's model number of GAT-X 105

One of the equipment configurations for the 105 Dagger.
Using the Jet Striker pack optimizes it for high-speed aerial combat.
Thanks to the flight unit's wings and thrusters, the Dagger can now achieve atmospheric flight.
The mount latched beneath the wings can hold propellant tanks, along with a variety of additional equipment and weapons like Missile Launchers to bolster its offense.

One of the configurations for the 105 Dagger.
Armed with the Launcher Striker Pack, it boasts incredible firepower.
Its standard Mobile Suit-spec weapons include an absurdly powerful Large Beam Cannon "Agni" in addition to a Combo Weapon Pod featuring a Vulcan cannon and gun launchers for close-range defense.
While its weight means a tradeoff in mobility, the sheer amount of firepower sees it used to attack far-off fleets and fortresses.

A 105 Dagger equipped with the Gunbarrel Striker Pack.
Though originally developed to be Mu La Flaga's exclusive Striker unit, his and the Archangel's defection from the Alliance put the plan on hold.
The Alliance headquarters had intended to promote the Strike Gundam as a machine used by Naturals rather than one having to rely on Coordinators, by potentially having the heroic Lieutenant Commander La Flaga pilot the Strike Gundam in the upcoming battles.
Though an aptitude test proved that Morgan Chevalier was more than capable of handling the gunbarrels, he chose to take his beloved 105 Dagger into battle rather than the Strike Gundam.
This machine was used to cut through the enemy vanguard and deliver a shining victory in the Battle of Boaz.

A variant of the Alliance's mass-produced 105 Dagger.
Taking notes from the Buster Gundam and adding a Striker Pack to the 105 Dagger shifted its specialization to projectile-heavy fights.
It was discovered at the design stage that packs were not fully supported when simply switched out, which resulted in the upgrades to this model.
That being said, the parts common to each model allows them to be manufactured on the same line.
Though the Calamity Gundam's loadout was considered for this machine, the difficulty in mass-producing such heavy weaponry resulted in it being fitted with the Buster Gundam's loadout.

A variant of the Orb's M1 Astray.
This zero-g version of the M1 Astray was created to defend the Orb's Ame-no-Mihashira orbital satellite.
Designed strictly for operations in space, the highly simplified structure of its legs severely reduces its ability to walk in return for a 30% increase in AMBAC response.
Featuring additional and enhanced thrusters, this model has also been equipped with new propellant and coolant tanks on its back.
A Sniper Beam Rifle developed for long-range attacks while in orbit serves as its primary weapon, giving it deadly accuracy when coupled with its high-precision sensors and compensated aiming system.
This model was assigned to a small number of pilots onboard the Kusanagi, and it delivered a major victory to the union in the hands of the "God of Fist," Barry Ho.

An upgrade from the Alliance's mass-produced Long Dagger.
A few adjustments to the Long Dagger's OS makes this machine Natural-friendly.
With its high performance and improved production process, this machine could be mistaken for a mass-produced version of the Duel Gundam.
These suits were primarily assigned to ace pilots.

A Duel Dagger variant with additional armor.
Fortestra was the Alliance's response to the Assault Shroud, an incredibly useful piece of extra armor developed by ZAFT for use with its stolen Duel Gundam.
In addition to the Fortestra, this Duel Dagger comes equipped with extra armor, thrusters, and shoulder-mounted Linear Cannons for a massive boost in offense, defense, and propulsion.

A highly-maneuverable variant of the ZAFT's mass-produced GINN.
Equipped with enhanced and additional thrusters, this model is optimized for mobility and operational range.
Planned as a stopgap measure to remedy development delays with the ZAFT's promising new GuAIZ suits, it is configured for maximum reliability by using existing technology rather than beam or other specialized weapons.
Being based on the GINN made it easy to handle, and favorable responses from pilots resulted in steady demand even after the GuAIZ had gone into production.
The first unit off the line brought countless victories when piloted by Rau Le Creuset, even engaging in a fierce battle against Mu La Flaga in an Alliance Moebius Zero.

A variant of the ZAFT's MAss-produced CGuE.
Using data gathered from the GAT-X series, ZAFT equipped this test model with experimental Beam Weapons.
Optimized for maximum stability, the oscillators on these first-stage weapons easily surpass 10 meters in length.
To compensate for the sheer size of these weapons, both the power and cooling units of the suit have been replaced with high-output versions.
With performance as expected across the board, all later Beam Weapons were designed based on these units.
Shiho Hahnenfuss, the driving force in the creation of the oscillation system, was assigned to Joule Team after its development and piloted this suit in the Second Battle of Jachin Due.

A derivative of the Alliance's Calamity Gundam.
It was created during the Calamity Gundam's development stage as a part of the Rebuild 1416 General Development Program. 
The most conspicuous of its changes from the original is the replacement of the Long-Range Beam Cannon "Schlag" with Anti-Ship Sword "Schwert Gewehr." Contrasted with the mid-range weapons specialization of the original,it takes the Beam Boomerangs and Rocket Anchors of the Sword Striker to emphasize its function as a close-combat fighter. 
Completed at roughly the same time as the Calamity, the initial three-unit rollout saw two put to the test during the attempt to capture Victoria Base.

One of the Gundam Astray series of prototype Mobile Suits developed by Morgenroete Inc.(an Orb Company) utilizing misappropriated GAT-X series data.
They never adopted Phase Shift Armor, but by utilizing a foam metal for the armor and cutting back considerably on armor bulk, the Red Frame came out much lighter, giving it an incredible level of agility.
It also enabled the use of Beam Weapons that never made it to full adoption by ZAFT Mobile Suits at the time.
This is the second of the Gundam Astrays, intended to be a testbed for ways to allow Naturals to pilot Mobile Suits.
It utilizes an unfinished prototype OS.
It was built in the neutral colony Heliopolis, but when the colony was destroyed in a battle between ZAFT's Creuset Team and the Earth Alliance's forces, it was recovered by Lowe Guele of the Junk Guild, who made it his own personal machine from that point forward.
Due to the heavy energy consumption of Beam Sabers, it is equipped with a gigantic suit-sized katana called the Gerbera Straight, which it uses as its primary armament.

One of the Gundam Astray series of prototype Mobile Suits developed by Morgenroete Inc.(an Orb Company) utilizing misappropriated GAT-X series data. 
They never adopted Phase Shift Armor, but by utilizing a foam metal for the armor and cutting back considerably on armor bulk, the Blue Frame came out much lighter, giving it an incredible level of agility. 
It also enabled the use of Beam Weapons that never made it to full adoption by ZAFT Mobile Suits at the time. This is the third of the Gundam Astrays, intended to be used for testing modular options. Its on-board OS contains data pertaining to add-on parts. 
It was built in the neutral colony Heliopolis, but when the colony was destroyed in a battle between ZAFT's Creuset Team and the Earth Alliance's forces, it was recovered by Lowe Guele of the Junk Guild. Later, it was transferred to the ownership of Gai Murakumo, leader of the mercenary band Serpent Tail, who put it to great use in his battles against the Earth Alliance and ZAFT."

The overhauled Gundam Astray Gold Frame Amatsu.
The Gundam Astray Gold Frame Amatsu was moderately damaged in battle against the Red Frame and Blue Frame,after which it received an overhaul at Ame-no-Mihashira,an Orb union space station.
In addition to the Totsuka-no-Tsurugi models of solid swords stored in its torso, it had a close combat claw called the Tsumuha-no-Tachi installed on its left arm,among other extra armaments intended to shore it up in melee combat.
It encountered the Regenerate Gundam on a test run,leading to a fight where it got its legs destroyed.
After that, it had armor added to the fronts of its legs, and its feet were swapped out for high-heeled feet.
The name Mina comes from the name of its pilot,Rondo Mina Sahaku.

The upgraded Gundam Astray Blue Frame.
After getting critically damaged in battle against the Sociuses, Lowe Guele's group gave it a full restoration and overhaul.
It uses thruster-equipped shoulder units devised some time ago by Gai Murakumo, along with parts that Lowe produced to replace its damaged head and backpack.
Its new backpack is called Tactical Arms, and in addition to working as a thruster, it can be split apart and transformed for use as a Giant Sword or Gatling Gun.
It has Phase Shift Armor added to its most vital areas.
It also includes a system that activates only when it detects pressure on the outer armor— a system that just happens to be identical to Trans Phase Armor.
The L in the name comes from Lowe's first initial.

One of the Gundam Astray series of prototype Mobile Suits developed by Morgenroete Inc.
(an Orb Company) utilizing misappropriated GAT-X series data.
They never adopted Phase Shift Armor, but by utilizing a foam metal for the armor and cutting back considerably on armor bulk, the Gold Frame came out much lighter, giving it an incredible level of agility.
It also enabled the use of Beam Weapons that never made it to full adoption by ZAFT Mobile Suits at the time.
This is the first of the Gundam Astrays, and unlike the other two, its manipulators have plugs that adhere to Earth Alliance Force standards, enabling it to simply pick up and use GAT-X series weapons.
It was built in the neutral colony Heliopolis, but when the colony was destroyed in a battle between ZAFT's Creuset Team and the Earth Alliance's forces, it was ordered scuttled.
However, Rondo Gina Sahaku disregarded orders and removed it, leading to it falling into the hands of House Sahaku.

One of the prototype mobile suits from the Gundam Astray Series.
It was developed by Orb's Morgenroete, Inc. using data from the GAT-X Series.
It used the 350 mm Rail Bazooka "Gáe Bulg" in an attempt to escape from Heliopolis. However, the right arm burst due to improper maintenance.
The arm was removed to prevent further damage.

An even more improved, perfected version of the Gundam Astray Gold Frame Amatsu that was created on Rondo Gina Sahaku's instructions.
The improvements to the imperfect version include new armaments like the Maga-no-Ikutachi, a set of rear-mounted special armaments that utilize colloid technology and allow the unit to discharge enemy energy and absorb it into itself, as well as the experimental Maga no Shirahoko weapon, which fires out harpoons built with Phase Shift Armor materials.
Its drastic performance upgrades served Rondo Gina Sahaku well in fighting Lowe Guele's group.

An upgrade from the Gundam Astray Gold Frame.
Under Rondo Gina Sahaku's instructions, Orb retrieved and analyzed the remains of the Blitz Gundam, after its defeat by the Strike Gundam. This machine is the result of applying those findings to the Gold Frame.
Replacing the right arm it lost in the Heliopolis escape with the Blitz's right arm, it is also able to use the Blitz's Mirage Colloid weapon.
It is still far from perfect, but is more than good enough to go up against the latest Mobile Suits of the Earth Alliance and ZAFT.

The upgraded version of Elijah's GINN.
This is Elijah's GINN after sustaining moderate damage in battle against ex-ZAFT soldier Gud Vair, and subsequently getting a full repair and overhaul.
Since parts from Vair's GINN were used in its overhaul, it now has a blue-and-red color scheme.
It also has a slight improvement in performance due to the fact that Vair's GINN was a custom model.

A work Mobile Suit belonging to the Junk Guild.
Scrapped by ZAFT and overhauled into a machine for manual labor, this is employed for use by Lowe Guele's Junk Guild team.
It has a compact backpack that gives it less mobility and firepower than a regular GINN, while allowing for greater precision in its handling.
It has a manipulator on its left arm and a winch attached to its waist.
In emergencies, it uses an Anti-Armor Linear Gun, an overhauled version of the one used by the Moebius model, as well as a shield meant for the Red Frame.

A GINN belonging to Elijah Kiel, with the mercenary band Serpent Tail.
It has a custom blue paint job, but in terms of basic performance, it's on par with any other regular GINN.
It swaps out the GINN's standard head sensor array for a giant blade which it can use as a weapon.
It carries its weight among Serpent Tail's forces, and it fights an intense battle against ex-ZAFT soldier Gud Vair's GINN.

A ZAFT prototype transforming Mobile Suit.
This was developed based on the Aegis Gundam that ZAFT stole from the Earth Alliance Forces.
It has three modes: Mobile Suit form, assault Mobile Armor form, and Mobile Armor cruise mode.
Its backpack is its main body.
When Mobile Suit parts get destroyed in battle, it is capable of swapping in new parts and regenerating ad infinitum, allowing it to stay in the fight.
Because this machine was designed to fulfill nearly the same purpose as the METEORS, it is capable of attaching itself to any unit with connectors, Alliance or ZAFT, and taking control of the machine.
It is also equipped with a laser-acceleration system called the Lightcraft Propulsion that allows it to receive lasers from GENESIS Alpha that ignite its propellant and accelerate it drastically.

The Gundam Astray Blue Frame, equipped with the Full Weapon option set.
This is a form designed for fighting long engagements against large numbers of enemies.
It primarily uses solid-ammo weaponry like the M68 Cattus 500mm Recoilless Rifle and multiple sets of Missile Pods.
This allows it to make maximum use of energy-intensive Beam Weapons.

The Gundam Astray Blue Frame, equipped with the Scale System option set.
Equipped all over its form with scale motors, a propulsion system that uses microvibrations, giving it impressive mobility in water.
Its head is equipped with an underwater sensor, and its main weapon is the Super Cavitating Torpedoes.

The Gundam Astray Red Frame with a flight unit equipped.
The flight unit itself is an M1 Astray backpack that Lowe independently upgraded.
With it, the Red Frame gets a drastic improvement in its flight capabilities.
The unit's wings both have fuel tanks on them, allowing for greatly increased cruising range.
Installing the pseudopersonality computer 8 (Eight) enables the flight unit to fly unassisted, and a Mobile Suit can hold onto it for aerial travel.

A prototype Mobile Suit developed by ZAFT.
Based on the GuAIZ, it is a prototype with a Neutron Jammer Canceler loaded in its head, enabling it to have a nuclear drive system.
Due to its high output and near-infinite uptime, it also utilizes Phase Shift Armor technology and Pristis Beam Reamers, remote guided weapons that use the energy- intensive DRAGOON System.
Developed on the military's initiative, this unit was disassembled immediately after its testing and slated to be scrapped, except for its classified equipment.
However, Siegel Clyne arranged for it to be taken away in secret, and it became the primary Mobile Suit for Prayer Reverie, Reverend Malchio's student.

The repaired Dreadnought Gundam.
After the mercenary band Serpent Tail stole its head while it was being transported to Earth, it had a GuAIZ head affixed to replace it.
With the loss of the Neutron Jammer Canceler in its previous head, it cannot use a nuclear engine, and it uses normal batteries instead, as well as cutting back on the energy-intensive Phase Shift Armor.
It is also far more restricted in when it can use its remote guided Pristis Beam Reamers.
Serpent Tail subsequently returned its head.

A prototype Mobile Suit developed by the Eurasian Federation.
The Eurasian Federation (part of the Earth Alliance) developed this suit independently with technological assistance from the civilian Actaeon Industries.
It inherits the features of the GAT-X series developed by the Atlantic Federation, while also integrating the lightwave defense band that is the Eurasian Federation's specialty.
Its most distinctive feature is the Armure Lumiere installed in its arms and backpack, which deploys a beam shield around the suit that nullifies most attacks from outside the shield.
To reduce power consumption from the shield deployment, it has a short uptime of 300 seconds, but it makes up for this with a versatile mix of defensive and offensive capabilities, such as using it for melee attacks.
This is the first of three prototypes produced, and it was piloted by Canard Pars, a guinea pig for the Super Coordinator project.
He used it to fight against many Gundams, including the Red Frame.

The Dreadnought Gundam, with its extra module affixed.
This is how the Dreadnought Gundam looks with four newly- developed large DRAGOON units on its back.
The DRAGOON units were produced and affixed at Ame-no- Mihashira by Lowe Guele, based on design docs furnished by Andrew Waldfeld.
Each DRAGOON is connected to the main unit via cables.
Feeding energy through these cables is what enables the use of Phase Shift Armor.
It can also deploy beams between each turret, creating a defensive field.
The machine's name is one that Lowe gave it.
The "X" comes from the shape of the DRAGOON units; and the "Astray" comes from the fact that it is a weapon, yet is intended to be used peacefully, ergo it strays from its fundamental purpose.
Prayer Reverie piloted this machine and put its impressive might to full use in battle against the Super Hyperion Gundam stolen from the Eurasian Federation.

The upgraded Hyperion Gundam Unit 1.
This is the version of the Hyperion Gundam that runs on a nuclear engine.
After pilot Canard Pars took it from Artemis, it was refitted with a nuclear engine, a Rear Skirt, and a Neutron Jammer Canceler stolen from the lunar Ptolemaios Base.
With a power source that is for all intents and purposes, unlimited; it can use the Armure Lumiere with no time limit.
It fought fiercely against Prayer Reverie's X Astray, which also had a nuclear engine.

A prototype Mobile Suit developed by the Eurasian Federation.
The Eurasian Federation (part of the Earth Alliance) developed this suit independently with technological assistance from the civilian Actaeon Industries.
It inherits the features of the GAT-X series developed by the Atlantic Federation, while also integrating the lightwave defense band that is the Eurasian Federation's specialty.
Its most distinctive feature is the Armure Lumiere installed in its arms and backpack, which deploys a beam shield around the suit that nullifies most attacks from outside the shield.
To reduce power consumption from the shield deployment, it has a short uptime of 300 seconds, but it makes up for this with a versatile mix of defensive and offensive capabilities, such as using it for melee attacks.
This is the second of three prototypes produced.
While Unit 1 was primarily white, this model is primarily grey in color.
Balsam Arendo piloted it in pursuit of Canard Pars when he made off with Unit 1.

The Gundam Astray Blue Frame Second G, equipped with the Sniper Pack option set.
The Blue Frame Second G is an upgraded Mobile Suit based on Gai Murakumo's original intended upgrade blueprints for the Blue Frame.
It removes the rear Tactical Arms and head, replacing it with a regular type.
It keeps a solid metal sword hidden in its chest for close combat.
Since Lowe Guele's proposed plan was named "Second L," Gai uses his first initial for this and calls it the "Second G."
This is the Blue Frame Second G's long-range sniping configuration.
It carries a large folding Beam Rifle capable of curved fire, and it keeps a telescopic sniping sensor on its left shoulder.
It is a heavier model intended to out-range its opponents.
When an enemy closes in on it, it purges its backpack and engages up close.

The GuAIZ, exclusively customized for Meia Siva, who became the defense fleet commander of Jachin Due as a close associate of Patrick Zala.
Equipped with an Assault Shroud designed for the GuAIZ, it boasts enhanced mobility and defensive capabilities.
As a mobile suit developer, Siva also adjusted the unit that serves as its base herself, making it a literally exclusive machine that only exhibits high performance when she is the pilot.
Its main weapon is the Trishula, a proprietary beam trident.
Additionally, it is also conducting operational tests on the prototype weapon Damaru.
The Damaru was inspired by the GENESIS and has a cylindrical shape.
When it is hit by a beam cannon, the Damaru focuses and converts the energy internally, then refires it, resulting in a more powerful beam attack than the original.

A prototype large mobile armor developed by the Earth Alliance Forces to realize the conventional mobile armor concepts of "sufficient firepower for solo missions," "mobility that rivals fighters," and "heavy armor to withstand attack."
It is equipped with several large batteries to achieve three features: overwhelming firepower from its main battery, the capability of flight within the atmosphere, and a positron reflector.
However, since its power was unstable, either its main battery, flight capability, or positron reflector would become inoperable whenever its power output was reduced.
Due to this critical flaw, it was virtually abandoned by the Earth Alliance Forces in a hangar at Victoria Base.
It shares a similar design concept to the later-developed YMAF-X6BD Zamza-Zah.
Equipped with no fewer than five positron generators, it is capable of deploying its positron reflector in any direction in response to attack.
Nevertheless, remaining in its prototype stage and experiencing power output issues, it can only generate a shield in the direction its generators are deployed.
Despite its various flaws, it more or less remained in development at Victoria Base.
When the remnants of the Zala faction attacked, it was deemed suitable for short-term deployment to defend the base and was sent out accordingly.

A mobile suit rebuilt from the GAT-X105 Strike that survived the last great war using spare parts that belonged to the Rogue that were provided by Cagalli.
When Phase Shift is activated, the color of its armor conforms to the Alliance's Strike.
On the other hand, its Twin Eye is green because it incorporates spare parts from the Rouge.
It is designed to perform like the first-generation GAT-X105 Strike, and some of its components cannot keep up with Kira's abilities as an ace pilot.
Nevertheless, by replacing worn and deteriorated parts with new ones, it maintains a high level of performance at the limit of its design in terms of responsiveness and durability.
The DRAGOON Striker was completed at Orb and the Factory using design data from a prototype Striker Pack for DRAGOON operation planned by the Alliance.
Kira, having learned of the DRAGOON System's existence when he engaged the Providence, wanted to incorporate it on a trial basis.
The Dragoon Striker evolved from the AQM/E-X04 Gun Barrel Striker, which operated four Wired Gunbarrels, and comes equipped with three completely separate DRAGOONs.
During the design stage, it became clear that the Strike's generator did not produce sufficient power output.
However, by connecting multiple battery packs—the same used in the Perfect Strike—a bespoke solution was finally arrived at.
While the DRAGOON Striker had issues with the energy efficiency of its generator, its firepower was ideal.
After Kira lost the Freedom, he used the DRAGOON Striker to respond to emergencies, which proved useful in providing him with combat experience in a mobile suit equipped with the DRAGOON System.
It is possible that Kira's experience with the DRAGOON Striker was one reason he was able to adapt so quickly to the DRAGOON System in the Strike Freedom.

The original doctrine of the Orb Forces was to maintain a nonaggressive defense, but after suffering defeat at the hands of the Earth Alliance Forces when they invaded, reconsidering that doctrine became an imperative, and the Orb Forces began to adopt a proactive defense, preemptively striking the enemy before they approach the homeland.
As a result, aerial carriers capable of conducting foreign campaigns became a necessity, and the Orb Forces began to explore developing carrier-based machines as its primary mobile armor.
In the process, several designs were considered and prototyped in parallel, and this machine was one of the test models of the Murasame developed with mass production in mind, as well as one of the few machines actually built for testing from among the many considered.
As a prototype, some of its parts, such as its wings, are replaceable, enabling a variety of tests to be conducted with a single machine.
The version lent to Athrun, whom Lacus entrusted to handle the remnants of the Zala faction who descended to Earth, was selected after taking the nature of the mission into account based on the suitability of its parts for long operational time and long-distance flight.
Additionally, while its performance is extremely high, it is nevertheless too peaky, and only an ace pilot can draw out its true potential.
Consequently, these tests parts were not adopted in the mass production version of the Murasame.
Still, they succeeded in collecting valuable operational data in combat, which in turn was used to complete the mass production version.

A ZGMF-X series Mobile Suit developed by ZAFT, and the successor to the Freedom Gundam.
This suit is capable of extremely high output due to its nuclear-powered hybrid Hyper Deuterion System, and it reuses Second Stage Series technology based on the Freedom Gundam for an overall improvement in design.
The Super DRAGOON Mobile Weapon Wings on its back is an improvement to the DRAGOON System, and they can be used with its all-range attack and shooting weapons mounted all over its frame for immense destructive power.
The propulsion system has also been improved.
It is equipped with thrusters that utilize the Voiture Lumiere propulsion system developed by the Deep Space Survey and Development Organization (DSSD) for extremely high mobility.
The suit was originally developed by ZAFT, but was captured by the Clyne Faction immediately before the development completion.
Many adjustments were then made to it to suit the needs of its pilot, Kira Yamato.

A ZGMF-X series Mobile Suit developed by ZAFT, and the successor to the Freedom Gundam.
This suit is capable of extremely high output due to its nuclear-powered hybrid Hyper Deuterion System, and it reuses Second Stage Series technology based on the Freedom Gundam for an overall improvement in design.
The Super DRAGOON Mobile Weapon Wings on its back is an improvement to the DRAGOON System, and they can be used with its all-range attack and shooting weapons mounted all over its frame for immense destructive power.
The propulsion system has also been improved.
It is equipped with thrusters that utilize the Voiture Lumiere propulsion system developed by the Deep Space Survey and Development Organization (DSSD) for extremely high mobility.
The suit was originally developed by ZAFT, but was captured by the Clyne Faction immediately before the development completion.
Many adjustments were then made to it to suit the needs of its pilot, Kira Yamato.

One of the new Second Stage Series Mobile Suits developed by ZAFT after the signing of the Junius Treaty.
It inherits many of the capabilities of the ZGMF-X Series (the First Stage Series), and adds a number of new technologies, including an improved version of Phase Shift Armor known as the Variable Phase Shift Armor.
Since nuclear engines are banned under the Treaty, the Impulse instead utilizes a Deuterion Beam Charge System to charge its battery via a beam fired at an external receptor.
As long as a mothership capable of emitting a charging beam is nearby, the Impulse can operate indefinitely.
Though its main armaments are limited to a High-Energy Beam Rifle and Folding Laser Anti-Armor Knife, it can dock with packs from the special Silhouette System as the situation requires to acquire greater firepower.
The Impulse is comprised of a detachable three-part design, making it a highly resilient all-rounder.

One of the Impulse Gundam's equipment variants.
Attaching the Force Silhouette pack gives the Impulse high mid-range combat mobility.
With the addition of powerful thrusters and wings, the Impulse is now capable of flight.
The top of the backpack also includes two Vajra Beam Sabers developed for the Second Stage Series.

One of the Impulse Gundam's equipment variants.
Attaching the Force Silhouette pack gives the Impulse high mid-range combat mobility.
With the addition of powerful thrusters and wings, the Impulse is now capable of flight.
The top of the backpack also includes two Vajra Beam Sabers developed for the Second Stage Series.

One of the Impulse Gundam's equipment variants.
Attaching the Sword Silhouette pack makes the Impulse a powerful close-range combatant.
Its Excalibur Laser Anti-Ship Swords combine a physical cutting edge with a laser blade that can slice through any kind of armor.
Both can be combined at the hilt for its large, ambidextrous form.
The Sword Silhouette is also equipped with two powerful Flash Edge Beam Boomerangs, making it a formidable foe in both anti-Mobile Suit and anti-ship battles.

One of the Impulse Gundam's equipment variants.
Attaching the Blast Silhouette pack gives the Impulse access to powerful long-range weapons.
The pack is equipped with two Kerberos High-Energy Long-Range Beam Cannons, two 4-Tube Missile Launchers, and two Deluge Hyper Velocity Rail Cannons, all of which can be fired simultaneously to bring down fortresses and fleets in a flash.

One of the Second Stage Series machines developed at the request of PLANT Supreme Council Chairman Gilbert Dullindal.
Boasting supremely high performance courtesy of the hybrid-nuclear Hyper Deuterion System, it possesses even higher performance than other Second Stage Series suits.
It's equipped with a variety of armaments originally belonging to the Impulse Gundam's Silhouette System, giving it all-around performance to handle any situation.
In addition to powerful weaponry like its Palma Fiocina Palm Beam Cannon and High-Energy Long-Range Beam Cannon, the Destiny can also deploy the wings on its back for high-mobility atmospheric flight.
The Destiny also excels defensively, with an expandable Anti-Beam Shield, and a Solidus Fulgor beam shield generator.

One of the Second Stage Series machines developed at the request of PLANT Supreme Council Chairman Gilbert Dullindal.
Boasting supremely high performance courtesy of the hybrid-nuclear Hyper Deuterion System, it possesses even higher performance than other Second Stage Series suits.
It's equipped with a variety of armaments originally belonging to the Impulse Gundam's Silhouette System, giving it all-around performance to handle any situation.
In addition to powerful weaponry like its Palma Fiocina Palm Beam Cannon and High-Energy Long-Range Beam Cannon, the Destiny can also deploy the wings on its back for high-mobility atmospheric flight.
The Destiny also excels defensively, with an expandable Anti-Beam Shield, and a Solidus Fulgor beam shield generator.

One of the new Second Stage Series Mobile Suits developed by ZAFT after the signing of the Junius Treaty.
It inherits many of the capabilities of the ZGMF-X Series (the First Stage Series), and adds a number of new technologies, including an improved version of Phase Shift Armor known as the Variable Phase Shift Armor.
Since nuclear engines are banned under the Treaty, the Saviour instead utilizes a Deuterion Beam Charge System to charge its battery via a beam fired at an external receptor.
As long as a mothership capable of emitting a charging beam is nearby, the Saviour can operate indefinitely.
Besides standard armaments like Beam Sabers and a Beam Rifle, the Saviour is equipped with powerful Beam Weapons including two Amfortas Plasma Beam Cannons and two Super Fortis Beam Cannons, making it a powerful, high-speed combatant at both mid-range and up close.

One of the new Second Stage Series Mobile Suits developed by ZAFT after the signing of the Junius Treaty.
It inherits many of the capabilities of the ZGMF-X Series (the First Stage Series), and adds a number of new technologies, including an improved version of Phase Shift Armor known as the Variable Phase Shift Armor.
Since nuclear engines are banned under the Treaty, the Chaos instead utilizes a Deuterion Beam Charge System to charge its battery via a beam fired at an external receptor.
As long as a mothership capable of emitting a charging beam is nearby, the Chaos can operate indefinitely.
Developed with an emphasis on space combat, the Chaos has superior mobility thanks to its abundant thrusters.
It can also transform into a space flight-focused Mobile Armor mode to further improve its mobility.
Outfitted with a breadth and depth of powerful weapons, including Mobile Weapon Pods that use the DRAGOON System, the Chaos can launch powerful attacks at all ranges.

One of the new Second Stage Series Mobile Suits developed by ZAFT after the signing of the Junius Treaty.
It inherits many of the capabilities of the ZGMF-X Series (the First Stage Series), and adds a number of new technologies, including an improved version of Phase Shift Armor known as the Variable Phase Shift Armor.
Since nuclear engines are banned under the Treaty, the Gaia instead utilizes a Deuterion Beam Charge System to charge its battery via a beam fired at an external receptor.
As long as a mothership capable of emitting a charging beam is nearby, the Gaia can operate indefinitely.
Designed to excel at ground combat, the Gaia transforms into a Mobile Armor mode that greatly resembles the beastlike four-legged BuCUE for unparalleled speed when fighting in gravity.
In Mobile Suit mode, it is equipped with standard weapons like Beam Sabers and a Beam Rifle, allowing it to fight adequately in space.
In Mobile Armor mode, it uses large melee weapons like Griffon 2 Beam Blades and Beam Assault Cannons to improve on the BuCUE's lack of firepower.

One of the new Second Stage Series Mobile Suits developed by ZAFT after the signing of the Junius Treaty.
It inherits many of the capabilities of the ZGMF-X Series (the First Stage Series), and adds a number of new technologies, including an improved version of Phase Shift Armor known as the Variable Phase Shift Armor.
Since nuclear engines are banned under the Treaty, the Abyss instead utilizes a Deuterion Beam Charge System to charge its battery via a beam fired at an external receptor.
As long as a mothership capable of emitting a charging beam is nearby, the Abyss can operate indefinitely.
The Abyss was developed with an emphasis on amphibious combat, and can transform into a specialized underwater Mobile Armor mode that reduces water resistance for superior aquatic mobility, while also encasing its entire body in a durable shield.
Its many powerful weapons include the high-output Calidus Multi-Phase Beam Cannon and 3-Barrel Beam Cannons mounted on its shoulders.
In its Mobile Armor configuration, it can also attack with both Torpedoes and Beam Cannons, making it a fearsome opponent underwater.

A variant of the Strike Rouge.
This form is equipped with the multipurpose Ootori striker pack.
The Ootori, which is a striker pack exclusively for the Strike Rouge, was completed by Morgenroete, Inc.
through the use of original technologies.
It is based on the I.W.S.P., a pack that never saw actual combat use due to technical problems.
In addition to having atmospheric flight capabilities, this Mobile Suit is equipped with powerful artillery as well as a Large Anti-Ship Sword, all of which enable it to respond to changing tactical conditions without replacing the striker pack.

A variable mass-produced Mobile Suit of the Orb forces, which was developed as the successor of the M1 Astray.
Designed with the fact that Orb is an island nation in mind, its large wings give it unassisted atmospheric flight capabilities, so it can handle ground combat like the M1 Astray, and also aerial combat.
In addition, when it transforms into its more mobile Mobile Armor form, it effectively engages enemies at sea, proving itself highly capable of responding to invaders.
In terms of armaments, this suit is equipped with a Beam Rifle and Beam Saber that are improved from the M1 Astray's, and it boasts highly versatile combat capabilities.

A general-purpose Mobile Suit of the Orb forces.
Before his death, the former Orb representative Uzumi Nara Athha commissioned the development of this suit, and it was completed as a result of the combined efforts of Morgenroete, Inc.
The most distinctive feature of this suit is its golden beam reflecting armor Yata-no-Kagami, which enables it not only to completely nullify beam attacks, but also to automatically deflect beams back at enemy units tracked by its sensors.
This makes the armor a potent offensive system.
In addition, this suit has striker pack-like armament packs.
This includes the atmospheric aerial combat pack Oowashi, which has four jet engines and two rocket boosters for atmospheric subsonic flight.
However, the development of this line was delayed due to the sophistication of the required technologies and high costs, and plans were abandoned after completing one prototype.

A configuration of the Akatsuki.
Using the space combat pack Shiranui gives it superior space combat capabilities.
This suit's armament pack includes a Guided Mobile Beam Turret System equivalent to ZAFT's DRAGOON System, and it is capable of all-range attacks using its seven remotely guided turrets.
Beams can also be utilized between these turrets to form a beam barrier, which gives this system high defensive capabilities too.

A mass-produced Alliance Mobile Suit intended to serve as the successor to the Dagger L.
Though it carries forward the key features of the Strike Gundam and Dagger variants developed during the last war, the addition of large vernier thrusters across the frame greatly enhance its mobility.
Equipped with an improved Beam Rifle and the Offensive Shield Type E loaded with multipurpose missiles, the Windam balances versatility with its high offensive power.
Like the Dagger L, the Windam is also compatible with the Striker Pack system, allowing it to expand its capabilities to meet operation requirements.
Immediately following the "Break the World" incident, the Windam was mass-produced in order to replace the Dagger L and the Alliance's mainline machine, and deployed in numerous battlefields both on Earth and in space.

A mass-produced Alliance Mobile Suit intended to serve as the successor to the Strike Dagger.
As an improved version of the 105 Dagger, a limited- production adaptation of the Strike Gundam, the Dagger L carries forward the specs and design of the 105 Dagger, but with an even greater emphasis on ease of production.
It is equipped with a rapid-fire Beam Carbine and easy- to-use armaments like Beam Sabers.
It is also compatible with the Striker Pack system for high adaptability.
After mass production, Dagger Ls were widely distributed across Earth and space, and were still used in battles even after the introduction of its successor, the Windam.

Intended as the successor to the GINN and GuAIZ, the ZAKU Warrior is one of the next-generation Mobile Suits from the New Millennium Series designed for mass production following the signing of the Junius Treaty.
Easily produced with a high degree of overall power, its greatest and most unique feature is the Wizard System, allowing the ZAKU Warrior to equip different weapon packs to handle any situation the battlefield throws its way.
Its standard loadout includes the newly-developed Beam Assault Rifle and Beam Tomahawk, giving it even greater combat prowess than the GAT-X Series suits used during the previous great war.

An improved version of the ZAFT ZuOOT transforming artillery Mobile Suit, the GAZuOOT gives a power boost to the already-considerable firepower of the original.
The ammunition-based cannons on its shoulders have been replaced with Beam Cannons, and its manipulators replaced with 3-Barrel Cannons, giving it a boost in firepower that is even easier to use thanks to an improved fire control system.
In addition to serving as defensive units for ZAFT's military bases, they are also used to attack enemy deployments and serve as fixed gun turrets on ships.

An improved version of ZAFT's main mass production Mobile Suit, the GuAIZ.
Developed after the signing of the Junius Treaty, its core construction has no major changes from the GuAIZ, but more powerful thrusters and an improved backpack make it just as easy to produce as the base model while improving its overall performance.
Sporting improved weapons, including two Porcus IV Railguns on its waist, the GuAIZ R's composite shield now emits a Beam Saber instead of a Beam Claw.
It served as ZAFT's mainstay unit up until mass production of the Millennium Series began.

A mass-produced Mobile Suit of the Clyne Faction.
Originally developed by ZAFT as a candidate for its main second-generation suit, its hover drive, which required special skills, and other features proved unpopular with pilots.
Thus it was excluded from the mass production competition.
The design data was later leaked to the Clyne Faction, who made their own improvements, and manufactured several of these which were used in combat to defend Orb.
In spite of its bulky armor, it boasts superior maneuverability both in the atmosphere and in space due to the hover unit built into its legs and many thrusters.
The suit's armaments include the Giga-Launcher DR1 Multiplex Bazooka, which fires both beams and physical bullets; as well as the Screaming Nimbus, which emits high-energy particles to generate a defensive field that also has offensive properties.
In addition, armament expansion via the Wizard System is possible like with the ZAKU Warrior.
The suit is equipped with a compact EZ Wizard that consists of two thrusters and a Beam Saber.

A ZAFT transforming aerial combat Mobile Suit, the BABI's aerodynamic, cone-shaped head and high-output thrusters makes it an excellent atmospheric flyer.
It also has a simple flight transformation setup, giving it improved flight capabilities over the DINN.
Equipped with two different types of Beam Rifles and Aerial Gun-Launchers, it also has a Beam Cannon in its abdomen, fixing the DINN's biggest weakness: a lack of firepower.

A high-mobility variant of ZAFT's mass-produced  GINN Mobile Suit.
Unlike the GINN High Maneuver, which featured new-model thrusters, the High Maneuver Type 2 achieves better mobility through improving the existing thrusters and adding verniers.
Its weapons now include a powerful Sword and a rapid-fire Beam Carbine, bolstering its close-combat capabilities even further.
The High Maneuver Type 2 was used by the Sato-led terrorist group responsible for the "Break the World" incident to fight against Phantom Pain and ZAFT Mobile Suits.

A high mobility Mobile Armor of the Earth Alliance forces.
This Mobile Armor is the successor of the Mobile Armor Moebius Zero, a few of which were deployed early in the last great war.
Its performance has been greatly increased through the use of many new technologies.
Like the Moebius Zero, it is equipped with four wired Gun Barrels that give it the capability to launch remote all-range attacks.
However, its attack power has been increased by switching its armaments from projectile cannons to 2-Barrel Beam Cannons and equipping it with Anti-Ship Beam Cutters.
Unfortunately, like the Moebius Zero, this Mobile Armor is hard to control unless the pilot has exceptional spatial awareness.
As there are only a few pilots who can fully harness its performance, only a few units have ever been produced.

A prototype Mobile Armor of the Earth Alliance forces.
This Mobile Armor was developed by the munitions company Adukav Mechano-Industries for base defense.
Its unique structure consists of a combination of the upper body of the Strike Dagger, the main Mobile Suit used by the Alliance forces in the last great war, and six Mobile Armor legs.
The lower Mobile Armor portion also serves as a flight unit, making it capable of atmospheric flight by folding up its legs and entering flight mode.
It offers superior performance in defensive combat thanks to the Beam Cannons on the tip of each of its front legs, the 125mm 2-Barrel Smoothbore Gun on its back, and its Positron Reflector, which is capable of deflecting and nullifying Beam Cannons.

A prototype Mobile Armor of the Earth Alliance forces.
This Mobile Armor was developed by the munitions company Adukav Mechano-Industries.
Although it has a total length of almost 50 meters and a weight of over 500 tons, its high- output thrusters give it solid flight performance and mobility that rivals Mobile Suits.
This Mobile Armor also has armaments that include a Large Beam Cannon Gamzatov mounted on each leg, other ranged weapons, and Vasiliev claws that oscillates at high speed to destroy targets, making this unit capable of combat at any range.
The Zamza-Zah also possesses a defensive weapon in the form of a Positron Reflector capable of deflecting and nullifying Beam Cannons, so it boasts high combat capabilities both offensively and defensively.

An Alliance walking heavy assault fortress.
This massive Mobile Weapon, intended to be the final, ZAFT-obliterating ace in the hole for the Alliance, was developed with the aid of the military-industrial complex known as Logos.
Following in the footsteps of previous weapons like the Zamza-Zah and Gells-Ghe, the Destroy Gundam boasts both near-impenetrable defenses courtesy of its Positron Reflectors and ranged weapons installed across its frame, allowing it to easily subdue enemy installations solo.
The Detachable Beam Cannons Sturm Faust on its forearms contain Split Beam Guns, allowing it to attack foes from any direction.
By transforming into its fortress Mobile Armor mode, the Destroy can use its firepower to its fullest while offsetting its inherently limited mobility.
Because of the high level of spatial awareness required for effective use, the Destroy is used exclusively by Extended pilots.

A ZGMF-X series Mobile Suit developed by ZAFT, and the successor to the Justice Gundam.
This suit is capable of extremely high output due to its nuclear-powered hybrid Hyper Deuterion System, and it reuses Second Stage Series technology based on the Justice Gundam for an overall improvement in design.
For the lifter unit on the back, this unit uses the Fatum-01, an improved version of the Fatum-00, that utilizes variable wings and includes Beam Blades to increase both attack power and maneuverability.
The armaments include a Griffon Beam Blade mounted on each leg, and the Beam Carry Shield compound weapon with two types of melee weapons.
All these enhance its close-quarters combat capabilities.
The suit was originally developed by ZAFT, but its design data was stolen by the secret Clyne Faction organization Terminal.
It was adjusted based on the assumption that Athrun Zala would pilot it, based on Kira Yamato's suggestions during its development.

One of the equipment configurations for the ZAKU Warrior.
Using the Gunner Wizard gives this suit access to powerful ranged weapons.
Equipped with the powerful Orthros High-Energy Long-Range Beam Cannon and energy tanks to match, suits with this loadout are typically used for rear support and point defense.

ZAFT pilot Lunamaria Hawke's ZAKU Warrior, equipped with the Gunner Wizard.
It is the exclusive machine of Lunamaria Hawke, a pilot stationed aboard the cutting-edge ZAFT warship Minerva, and has been given a distinctive red paint job.
However, it has not received any other special modifications, and performs exactly the same as a standard ZAKU Warrior.
Lunamaria was mainly assigned to defend the Minerva, and launched in this configuration countless times.

ZAFT pilot Dearka Elthman's ZAKU Warrior, equipped with the Gunner Wizard.
As the exclusive machine of Joule Team member Dearka Elthman, it has been given a black paint job.
However, it has not received any other special modifications, and performs exactly the same as a standard ZAKU Warrior.
During the "Break the World" incident, Dearka launched with the Gunner Wizard equipped in an attempt to destroy Junius Seven.

One of the equipment configurations for the ZAKU Warrior.
Using the Blaze Wizard gives this suit superior combat speed.
The Blaze Wizard's two large thrusters allow for high-speed flight in both air and space, and its Firebee Guided Missiles provide extra firepower.

One of the equipment configurations for the ZAKU Warrior.
Using the Slash Wizard optimizes this suit for close- quarters combat.
Its two Hydra Gatling Beam Cannons provide a rapid-fire barrage, while the Falx G7 Beam Axe mounted on its waist provides an edge against other Mobile Suits.

A ZAFT command Mobile Suit, the ZAKU Phantom builds on the ZAKU Warrior's design by adding a shield to the right shoulder and a blade antenna to the head, which bolsters command communications.
It also shares weapon and Wizard System compatibility with the ZAKU Warrior.
Issued to squad commanders and ace pilots, the Phantom was used primarily as a command unit.

ZAFT pilot Rey Za Burrel's ZAKU Phantom, equipped with the Blaze Wizard pack.
It is the exclusive machine of Rey Za Burrel, a pilot stationed aboard the cutting-edge ZAFT warship Minerva, and has been given a distinctive white paint job.
However, it has not received any other special modifications, and performs exactly the same as a standard ZAKU Phantom.
Rey primarily launched with the Blaze Wizard configuration to fight against the Alliance and Orb.

ZAFT commander Yzak Joule's ZAKU Phantom, equipped with the Slash Wizard pack.
As the exclusive machine of Joule Team leader Yzak Joule, it has been given a blue paint job.
However, it has not received any other special modifications, and performs exactly the same as a standard ZAKU Phantom.
Yzak launched with the Slash Wizard loadout during "Break the World," where he worked to destroy Junius Seven and fought against the Gundams that had been stolen by Phantom Pain.

A mass-produced ZAFT underwater and amphibious combat Mobile Suit.
Improved over the underwater combat suits used during the previous great war, its arms and legs are wider than those of the ZnO, giving it excellent mobility on land.
Developed with the intent of improving the ZnO's combat capabilities on land, it can also transform into a Mobile Armor mode for high-speed underwater movement.
In addition to an underwater Phonon Maser Cannon and Torpedoes, the ASH is also equipped with numerous Beam Weapons and other ranged armaments.
The Beam Claws on its arms make it an effective melee combatant as well.
Though initial test types were deployed with a green color scheme, the mass production models feature a gray paint job.

One of the configurations for the Dagger L.
Using the Jet Striker pack optimizes it for high-speed aerial combat.
Thanks to the flight unit's wings and thrusters, the Dagger L can now achieve atmospheric flight.
The mount latches beneath the wings can hold propellant tanks, along with a variety of additional equipment and weapons like Missile Launchers to bolster its offense.

One of the configurations for the Dagger L.
Using the long-range artillery Striker Pack optimizes it for anti-ship and rear support combat roles.
With the Doppelhorn 2-Barrel Recoilless Cannon on its back, it can make incredibly accurate and powerful long-range attacks capable of downing a warship in one shot.
Due to the large strain placed on the machine's frame when in gravity, this loadout was primarily deployed in space.

A variant of the Dagger L, the Dark Dagger is painted black for stealth amidst the inky void of space.
However, it is otherwise unmodified, and performs exactly the same as a standard Dagger L.
Used primarily by the Earth Alliance Phantom Pain special forces unit, Dark Daggers were deployed during the operation to steal Gundams from Armory One.

One of the configurations for the Windam.
Using the Jet Striker pack optimizes it for high-speed aerial combat.
Thanks to the flight unit's wings and thrusters, the Windam can now achieve atmospheric flight.
The mount that latches beneath the wings can hold propellant tanks, along with a variety of additional equipment and weapons like Missile Launchers to bolster its offense.

This is the personal Windam of Commander Neo Roanoke of the Earth Alliance forces.
This suit was used in combat before the Windam was officially adopted.
Although its purplish red paint differs from that of the typical model, its performance is the same.
However, the sole customization is that the Jet Striker has had its limiters removed, which enables it to accelerate more explosively than the typical model.
This suit was also used to pursue the ZAFT warship Minerva and held its own against the Impulse Gundam.

A mass production Mobile Suit developed at the same time as the ZAKU Warrior, the GOUF Ignited is a member of the New Millennium Series, and was in contention with the ZAKU Warrior to become the next mainline ZAFT Mobile Suit.
Though not chosen due to adaptability and cost issues, its specs exceed those of the ZAKU Warrior's, thus it was produced in limited quantities first before being mass- produced in earnest once the war situation demanded it.
Specialized for melee combat, its Tempest Beam Sword can also be used as a physical blade, while its Slayer Whip batters enemy units with high frequency vibrations.
The GOUF Ignited is also equipped with a flight unit on its back, boosting its speed and enabling it to fly.

A general-purpose ZAFT Mobile Suit developed as the successor to the Providence Gundam.
Boasting supremely high performance courtesy of the hybrid-nuclear Hyper Deuterion System, it possesses even higher performance than other Second Stage Series suits.
It carries forward the Providence Gundam's DRAGOON System, with a more powerful Beam Cannon and the ability to have the two large DRAGOONs form spike-like beam blades to penetrate enemy defenses.
The Legend is also installed with a next-generation control system that even pilots lacking finely-honed spatial awareness skills can handle.
With defensive capabilities that include the Solidus Fulgor beam shield generators on its hands, the Legend has a staggeringly high level of overall performance.

A GOUF Ignited used by Heine Westenfluss, ZAFT's top gun.
Though the ZAKU Warrior was chosen over the GOUF Ignited as ZAFT's next-generation mainline unit, its high performance led to a number of units being produced as early prototypes.
This machine in particular was reserved for Heine and other ace pilots, and painted in Heine's personal orange colors.
Heine and this machine were stationed aboard the cutting- edge warship Minerva, and fought against Alliance forces at the Dardanelles strait.

A form of the M1 Astray with additional equipment.
The Shrike, with its large rotor and thrusters for atmospheric flight capabilities, was developed to defend Orb, which is surrounded by the ocean.
Although there are no particular differences in terms of the suit's armaments or frame, its aerial mobility has been greatly increased, for superior aerial combat capabilities for intercepting enemies.
Along with the Murasame, Orb's main second-generation suit, many Shrikes were deployed for Orb's defense, combat in the Dardanelles strait, and other purposes.

The personal Murasame of former ZAFT Commander Andrew Waldfeld, who acts alongside the Archangel.
Unlike the general type, this suit is painted yellow, and its frame has been improved in line with Waldfeld's superior physical abilities and piloting skill to achieve the design's maximum possible performance.
After it was assigned to the Archangel and used in combat in the Dardanelles strait, it was given to Orb's Ledonir Kisaka.

A special variant of the ZAKU Warrior modified for propaganda purposes.
Appearing at one of Lacus Clyne impersonator, Meer Campbell's live concerts, it has been redone in a bright shade of pink, and its entire body is covered in distinctive markings.
First appearing at a relief concert at Diocuia base, this model was later equipped with the Blaze Wizard pack and participated in the Battle of Messiah as a rear support unit.

ZAFT pilot Dearka Elthman's ZAKU Phantom, equipped with the Blaze Wizard pack.
As the exclusive machine of Joule Team member Dearka Elthman, it has been given a black paint job.
However,  it has not received any other special modifications, and performs exactly the same as a standard ZAKU Phantom.
Dearka launched with the Blaze Wizard configuration for the Battle of Requiem, then worked alongside the Archangel— now affiliated with Orb's military— to help prevent it from firing after it came under ZAFT control.

A GOUF Ignited used by ZAFT commander Yzak Joule.
Though his ZAKU Phantom was painted blue, Yzak chose to go with a white color scheme this time around.
The machine itself is unmodified, and performs exactly the same as a standard GOUF Ignited.
After participating in the Battle of Requiem, Yzak fought alongside the Archangel and others as a member of Orb's forces in a desperate bid to prevent ZAFT from firing its weapon of mass destruction.

The personal Gaia Gundam of former ZAFT Commander Andrew Waldfeld, who acts alongside the Archangel.
This Second Stage Series Mobile Suit was stolen from Armory One.
It was later recovered by ZAFT after being damaged in combat in Lodonia, was subsequently acquired by the Clyne Faction, and became Waldfeld's personal unit.
The suit was adjusted in line with Waldfeld's requirements, and its color became vermilion due to a change in the voltage of the Variable Phase Shift Armor.

An enhanced form of the Strike Freedom Gundam.
This is the result of the Strike Freedom Gundam docking with the large armament module METEOR.
The METEOR's basic specifications are essentially the same as those used during the last great war, and it offers extremely high firepower through its multiple high-energy Beam Cannons and many other heavy weapons.
When undocked, the METEOR module can dock itself with the Eternal, a ship designed specifically to support the ZGMF-X Series, and serve as a gun turret.

An enhanced form of the Infinite Justice Gundam.
This is the result of the Infinite Justice Gundam docking with the large armament module METEOR.
The METEOR's basic specifications are essentially the same as those used during the last great war, and it offers extremely high firepower through its multiple high-energy Beam Cannons and many other heavy weapons.
When undocked, the METEOR module can dock itself with the Eternal, a ship designed specifically to support the ZGMF-X Series, and serve as a gun turret.

One of the Impulse Gundam's parts-swapped configurations.
It uses the Destiny Silhouette, an all-purpose armament developed to be able to handle any situation in a battle.
Parts of the suit itself also have added improvements.
It has all the features of the Force, Sword, and Blast Silhouettes combined, along with a Beam Shield generator on the back of its hand for defense, giving it incredible offensive and defensive capabilities.
However, its heavy equipment is taxing on the machine.
It uses Beam Weapons like the Telescopic Barrel Extension Type Beam Turret, which requires multiple deuterium recharges in a single battle.
Due to this, development and production were suspended after producing just four prototypes.
Instead, its name and concepts were carried over to the newly-developed Destiny Gundams.

A customized version of the Gundam Astray Red Frame upgraded by Lowe Guele after the Red Frame suffered major damage defending against an attack by the mystery organization known as the Librarian Works.
This is considered the culmination of Lowe's work for the Junk Guild, freely utilizing tech from all of the many Mobile Suits that he tinkered with thus far.
The Tactical Arms IIL affixed to its back is a unit capable of transforming into different configurations to adapt to various situations, such as a Flight Form for flight and a Sword Form in which it turns into a giant solid sword.
Including parts from the Turn Delta also enables it to utilize the special propulsion system, Voiture Lumiere.
It is also equipped with two swords, Gerbera Straight and Tiger Pierce, made using lost technology.

A Mobile Suit upgraded from the damaged Gundam Astray Gold Frame Amatsu Hana to protect Fairness Zweigle.
The Rare Metal Ω was originally planned to be used on Lord Astray, instead it is used to remodel this unit and most of it is used for the independent support unit on its back, Yatagarasu.
Before the modification, the main body has a two-seater  seat, but now it's modified into a one-seater each for the main body and the Yatagarasu.
Both units can be piloted independently after separating.
The wings of the Yatagarasu are called Ameno Hazuchi and can cover the machine to increase its defense.
Furthermore, the built-in Voiture Lumiere system allows the energy produced by the wings to be converted into a thrust power, using it as a propulsion attack.

A Mobile Suit upgraded from the damaged Gundam Astray Gold Frame Amatsu Hana to protect Fairness Zweigle.
The Rare Metal Ω was originally planned to be used on Lord Astray, instead it is used to remodel this unit and most of it is used for the independent support unit on its back, Yatagarasu.
Before the modification, the main body has a two-seater  seat, but now it's modified into a one-seater each for the main body and the Yatagarasu.
Both units can be piloted independently after separating.
The wings of the Yatagarasu are called Ameno Hazuchi and can cover the machine to increase its defense.
Furthermore, the built-in Voiture Lumiere system allows the energy produced by the wings to be converted into a thrust power, using it as a propulsion attack.

A Mobile Suit upgraded from the damaged Gundam Astray Gold Frame Amatsu Hana to protect Fairness Zweigle.
The Rare Metal Ω was originally planned to be used on Lord Astray, instead it is used to remodel this unit and most of it is used for the independent support unit on its back, Yatagarasu.
Before the modification, the main body has a two-seater  seat, but now it's modified into a one-seater each for the main body and the Yatagarasu.
Both units can be piloted independently after separating.
The wings of the Yatagarasu are called Ameno Hazuchi and can cover the machine to increase its defense.
Furthermore, the built-in Voiture Lumiere system allows the energy produced by the wings to be converted into a thrust power, using it as a propulsion attack.

A mobile suit designed by Orb using the operational data from the Strike Freedom Gundam and the variable technology developed with the Murasame.
The cockpit features a panoramic monitor, and it comes equipped with several weapons for long-range combat and VPS Armor.

A Celestial Being 3rd-generation Gundam, developed for actual combat based on the 2nd-gen Gundams' test data.
