With a solar-powered GN Drive to provide virtually limitless amounts of energy and heretofore unseen handheld Beam Weapons, its performance far exceeds any current Mobile Suit.
It is also equipped with a Trans-Am System, which releases stored, highly-compressed particles from every surface to boost its output to nearly three times its specifications for a short time.
The GN Particles generated by the Drive make the Exia an astoundingly agile close-combat machine even among other Gundams of its generation.
Specialized for close fights, its weaponry includes four Beam Sabers and three swords, a combination which gave it the development code "Seven Swords."
The swords' ability to cut through a defensive GN Shield makes it evident that this model was made to fight Gundams.

A Celestial Being 3rd-generation Gundam, developed for actual combat based on the 2nd-gen Gundams' test data.
With a solar-powered GN Drive to provide virtually limitless amounts of energy and heretofore unseen handheld Beam Weapons, its performance far exceeds any current Mobile Suit.
It is also equipped with a Trans-Am System, which releases stored, highly-compressed particles from every surface to boost its output to nearly three times its specifications for a short time.
The GN Particles generated by the Drive make the Exia an astoundingly agile close-combat machine even among other Gundams of its generation.
Specialized for close fights, its weaponry includes four Beam Sabers and three swords, a combination which gave it the development code "Seven Swords."
The swords' ability to cut through a defensive GN Shield makes it evident that this model was made to fight Gundams.

A Celestial Being 3rd-generation Gundam, developed for actual combat based on the 2nd-gen Gundams' test data.
With a solar-powered GN Drive to provide virtually limitless amounts of energy and heretofore unseen handheld Beam Weapons, its performance far exceeds any current Mobile Suit.
It is also equipped with a Trans-Am System, which releases stored, highly-compressed particles from every surface to boost its output to nearly three times its specifications for a short time.
Out of all the 3rd-generation Gundams, the Dynames excels most as a long-range support unit.
It is equipped with a variety of weapons for engaging hostiles at mid-to-long ranges depending on the mission, including the GN Sniper Rifle, which fires a high-powered beam of compressed GN Particles.
In situations demanding that extra level of precision, the head's camera eye can extend in its Gun Camera Mode, with the pilot using a specialized rifle-shaped controller from the cockpit to make the shot.

A Celestial Being 3rd-generation Gundam, developed for actual combat based on the 2nd-gen Gundams' test data.
With a solar-powered GN Drive to provide virtually limitless amounts of energy and heretofore unseen handheld Beam Weapons, its performance far exceeds any current Mobile Suit.
It is also equipped with a Trans-Am System, which releases stored, highly-compressed particles from every surface to boost its output to nearly three times its specifications for a short time.
Kyrios is the only 3rd-generation Gundam capable of transforming into a flight-focused fighter mode, allowing it to use its superior speed for hit-and-run tactics and unthinkable blitzkriegs.
It can also add components to its flight mode-exclusive tail unit (located toward the machine's rear) to accommodate a variety of weapons for different mission conditions.
Its main weapon is the GN Beam Machine Gun, which can be fired during flight mode, boasting superior accuracy during high speed engagements.

One of Celestial Beings' 3rd-generation Gundams, developed for actual combat based on 2nd-generation Gundam test data.
With a solar-powered GN Drive capable of generating a near-infinite supply of energy, it outstrips existing Mobile Suits to a shocking degree, and is equipped with beam weaponry and other capabilities that other countries have yet to develop.
It's also equipped with the Trans-Am System, which can temporarily increase machine performance threefold by releasing highly compressed GN Particles throughout the frame.
Among the 3rd-generation Gundams, the Virtue is specialized for defense, with thick armor plating and the ability to generate a GN Field capable of defending against beam weaponry and live ammunition.
From an offensive standpoint, the Virtue excels at base suppression and cleanup operations, and its main weapon, the GN Bazooka, can use its Burst Mode to connect directly to the GN Drive, firing a staggeringly powerful particle beam at maximum output.

The form taken by Gundam Virtue, one of Celestial Being's 3rd-generation Gundams, after purging its exterior.
Normally hidden away within the Virtue, the Gundam Nadleeh is a unique machine known of by a select few members of Celestial Being.
It's most unique feature is known as the Trial System, which allows the Nadleeh to link with Veda to gain control over the other Gundams— a safety measure implemented by Aeolia Schenberg to ensure an unstoppable advantage in the event that a Gundam goes rogue.
The red cables resembling long strands of hair extending from the head are for GN particle transfer.

Celestial Being's 1st-generation Gundam, and the base upon which all future Gundams would be built.
The first Mobile Suit to be equipped with a solar-powered GN Drive, the 0 Gundam perfected most of the key Gundam systems thanks to its virtually limitless amounts of energy and heretofore unseen handheld Beam Weapons.
Still highly experimental in many ways, it is armed with only essentials like a Beam Gun and Beam Saber.
In 2301 AD, a still young Setsuna F. Seiei witnessed this machine when it performed an armed intervention test in the war-torn Republic of Krugis, leading him to one day join Celestial Being.

The first unit in the Gundam Throne series, used by Team Trinity, Celestial Being's second team.
Installed with a pseudo solar reactor, the Throne series was developed in strict secrecy by Celestial Being Observers Laguna Harvey and Alejandro Corner.
Each Throne machine is a customized version of the same base design, with the Eins specialized for long-range bombardments.
The GN Launcher on its right shoulder is a Beam Cannon connected directly to the solar reactor.
Other Thrones can also be connected as well.
Two Thrones provide enough power for the GN Mega Launcher, while a trio enables the GN High Mega Launcher for increasingly greater long-range firepower.

The second unit in the Gundam Throne series, used by Team Trinity, Celestial Being's second team.
Installed with a pseudo solar reactor, the Throne series was developed in strict secrecy by Celestial Being Observers Laguna Harvey and Alejandro Corner.
Each Throne machine is a customized version of the same base design, with the Zwei designed to excel at mid-range combat.
Its GN Buster Sword combines the traits of both a physical sword and a Beam Saber, while the unique GN Fangs on its waist can launch remote attacks on foes from multiple directions.

The third unit in the Gundam Throne series, used by Team Trinity, Celestial Being's second team.
Installed with a pseudo solar reactor, the Throne series was developed in strict secrecy by Celestial Being Observers Laguna Harvey and Alejandro Corner.
Each Throne machine is a customized version of the same base design, with the Drei designed to support the Eins and Zwei.
Its back is equipped with a large GN Particle generator, and its GN Stealth Field can cover the battlefield in GN Particles to disrupt enemy communications.
Because of its intended support role, the Drei's weaponry is limited to only the bare essentials.

A support craft for Celestial Being's Gundams, the GN Arms Type-E is intended to be used specifically to power up the Gundam Exia.
Though not equipped with a GN Drive, the accumulated particles in its GN Condensers can be used to fire its GN Cannons.
By docking with the Gundam Exia, its powerful Large GN Swords and GN Beam Guns can be used without risk.
For prolonged engagements, GN Particles need to be supplied by the Gundam.
When out of service, the GN Arms is stored in an Assault Container onboard the Ptolemaios to serve as a ship weapon.
Lasse Aeon piloted it alongside the Gundam Exia in the final battle against the UN Forces.

A support craft for Celestial Being's Gundams, the GN Arms Type-D is intended to be used specifically to power up the Gundam Dynames.
Though not equipped with a GN Drive, the accumulated particles in its GN Condensers can be used to fire its Large GN Cannons.
By docking with the Gundam Dynames, its powerful long-range armaments like the GN Twin Rifle and GN Missiles can be used even more effectively.
For prolonged engagements, GN Particles need to be supplied by the Gundam.
When out of service, the GN Arms is stored in an Assault Container onboard the Ptolemaios to serve as a ship weapon.

Formed when the Gundam Exia and GN Arms Type-E support craft combine.
Designed for melee combat, it adds two Large GN Swords with internal GN Beam Guns for ranged attacks.
It can also deploy a GN Field to bolster the Exia when attacking and defending.
It was used in the final battle against the UN Forces, engaging in fierce combat with Alejandro Corner's Alvatore.

Formed when the Gundam Dynames and GN Arms Type-D support craft combine.
Designed under the assumption that it would be used in fleet combat, the GN Arms Type-D's additional GN Twin Rifle and GN Missile containers boost its firepower.
It can also deploy a GN Field, providing an all-round improvement to the Gundam Dynames.
It destroyed numerous Mobile Suits and battleships in the final battle against the UN Forces.

A mass-produced Mobile Suit that makes up the brunt of the Human Reform League's mechanized forces.
Though markedly behind the times technologically, its dependability and ubiquity are second-to-none.
A wide variety of variant models exist, as well.
Extremely heavy due to its E-Carbon plating, the Tieren is significantly less mobile than suits from the other major powers, but is much more durable as well.
Its weapons are relics of the past, consisting mainly of solid ammunition-based weaponry and Carbon Blades.
This gives lower cost and higher dependability, which is not necessarily a bad thing.
Its name "Tieren" is derived from the Chinese word for "Iron Man."

A variation of the Tieren developed for use in space.
Thrusters have been added across the frame to aid control in the vacuum of space.
Two massive propellant tanks have been added to the legs, which double as shielding.
Among the improvements to its main weapon, the 200mm x 25mm Smoothbore Gun, are a newly added pair of heat sinks that can double as Carbon Blades in close-range combat.
During a Sergei Smirnov-led mission to capture a Gundam, Tierens equipped with special Carbon Nets and Carbon Wires designed to restrict Mobile Suit movement were used.

A command model variant of the Tieren Space Type.
Though nearly identical in performance to standard Tierens, the head and shoulders have been installed with additional sensors to assist with search functions and wireless comms.
The Human Reform League's Sergei Smirnov piloted one during the operation to capture a Gundam.

A high-mobility variant of the Tieren.
The wings from the Tieren High Mobility Type have been removed, leaving only the jet engines on the legs, which allow the Type B to hover.
Though dustproofed for desert battles, the Type B's high mobility has led to it being deployed in other terrains as well.

A command model variant of the Tieren High Mobility Type.
Though nearly identical in performance to standard Tierens, the head and shoulders have been installed with additional sensors to assist with search functions and wireless comms, much like the Space Type command model.
The Human Reform League's Sergei Smirnov piloted one during combined military exercises with the three major powers.

The High Mobility Type variant of the Tieren was developed to take advantage of the Tieren's durable armor while offsetting its low mobility with a number of modifications and improvements.
It can hover thanks to the foldable wings on its back and additional jet engines on its legs.
It is still not suited for flight, however; its distinctive wings are typically limited to transport drop operations or to quickly adjust course when hovering.

A long-range sniping variant of the Tieren.
Using the ground model as a base, the addition of a 300mm x 50mm Soothbore Gun conceals its head completely.
The Smoothbore Gun can fire at enemy units from outside their range with absolute precision, making it the perfect choice for strikes on enemy installations and Mobile Suits alike.
Conversely, its perilous lack of mobility means it has to be defended during operations.

A variant model of the Tieren piloted exclusively by Human Reform League super soldier Soma Peries.
Unlike other Tieren models, the Tieren Taozi was designed to excel both on Earth and in space.
It boasts astonishing maneuverability thanks to the thrusters lining its frame, and the large thrusters attached to its legs and movable shoulder plates.
The Taozi's cockpit is equipped with a panoramic monitor display that provides unified data from the head-mounted camera eye and various sensors, allowing its pilot to perform faster and more accurately.
Taozi means "peach" in Chinese, hence the machine's paint job.

An old-model Mobile suit powered by fossil fuels.
Originally developed by the Human Reform League under the name "Fanton," it fell out of use following the abandonment of fossil fuels.
It is currently manufactured and sold primarily to third-world countries, including those in the Middle East.
Long obsolete, the Anf falls short in nearly every aspect, and is outclassed in mobility even when compared to the HRL's Tieren mainline Mobile Suits.
It also uses an internal combustion engine, making it likely to explode after taking a hit.
Anf means "nose" in Arabic; a fitting name, considering the machine's distinctive, nose-like head unit.

A Union Flag used exclusively by Union ace Graham Aker.
It has received special modifications courtesy of Professor Ralph Eifman, developer of the Union Flag.
The flight unit on its back has been replaced with a high-power version, giving it roughly twice the speed of a normal Flag.
This comes at a cost: turning at full speed subjects the pilot to a staggering force of 12 Gs.
It also boasts reinforced joints, and the surface of the frame has received a thorough Anti-Beam Coating, giving it a fighting chance against Gundams.
There have also been additional modifications made to accommodate Graham's left-handedness.
This Flag even carries its main weapon, a Prototype Linear Rifle, in its left hand.

The machine used exclusively by ace pilots in the Union's Over Flags squad.
Professor Ralph Eifman, developer of the Flag, has made additional improvements based on data gathered by Graham's custom Flag.
Over Flags carry over the same improvements from Graham's unit, including a high-output flight unit and surface Anti-Beam Coating, and far surpass the capabilities of standard Flags.
To prioritize pilot survivability, several dangerous features have been omitted, like the ability to disable the G-limiter, leading to slight reduction in performance compared to Graham's unit.
Its New Linear Rifle can switch between a devastating 200mm large-caliber mode and a 60mm rapid fire mode as the situation demands.
14 of these machines were made for the Union's 8th Independent Tactical Fighter Squadron, the Over Flags.

A space variant of the Union Flag.
Its rear flight unit is swapped out for a thruster, allowing it to be used in space environments.
It was deployed in fewer numbers than the Union Realdo, and it uses the same armaments as a standard Flag.
These were later used by the anti-federalist organization Katharon.

A ground variant of the Union Flag.
This is an improved Flag variant with the rear flight unit removed and extra armor all over its body.
It is referred to as the Shell Flag.
It uses a 200m Linear Cannon and a Rocket Launcher, along with an enlarged Defense Rod among other weapons, giving it better firepower and defenses.

A flight-capable Mobile Suit of the Advanced European Union.
Though the AEU's orbital elevator is still not fully operational, the Enact is a cutting-edge machine that attempts to demonstrate the AEU's superiority by merit of it being solar-powered.
It resembles the Union Flag in many ways, from its frame design to its weapons, and even its ability to transform independently.
Equipped with an external antenna that can receive power directly from the orbital elevator's energy generation system, the Enact is theoretically capable of operating indefinitely, so long as it remains within the elevator's transmission range.

A command model for the mass-produced AEU Enact.
Though the antenna on its head differs in shape from ordinary Enacts and has a boosted signal range, it is nearly identical in every other way, performance-wise.
AEU ace Patrick Colasour piloted one when he participated in military exercises with the Republic of Moralia.

A demonstration model for the AEU Enact.
Though the AEU's orbital elevator is still not fully operational, the Enact is a cutting-edge machine that attempts to demonstrate the AEU's superiority by merit of it being solar-powered.
It has been given a special coat of paint for its demonstration exercise debut, but is otherwise functionally identical to the Enact Commander Type.
AEU ace Patrick Colasour piloted one during a demonstration held to commemorate the 10th anniversary of the Human Reform League's orbital elevator, and almost immediately came under attack during Celestial Being's first armed intervention.

An upgraded AEU Enact developed by PMC Trust, the Ali al-Saachez Custom has larger shoulder and head power antennas, vastly improving the amount of energy it can receive.
On PMC pilot Al-Saachez's request, it also has lighter armor for improved mobility.
Its main weapons are a Blade Rifle that combines a Linear Rifle with a Large Carbon Blade, and a Missile Launcher that can strike multiple targets across a large area.
This gives the Custom significantly more firepower than the standard unit.
It fought against Setsuna F. Seiei's Gundam Exia during the joint military exercise in Moralia, where Al-Saachez's skilled piloting allowed it to get the edge against the Exia in spite of the Custom's inferior specs.

A massive Mobile Armor used in the 5th Solar Wars that has been tuned to dock with the cutting-edge AEU Enact Custom.
The Plasma Field generators in its legs can inflict damage on enemy Mobile Suits and pilots.
Both the Agrissa's surface and the Enact comprising its core unit have a special coating to avoid damaging itself with plasma.
It was used by AEU pilot Ali al-Saachez during a joint exercise involving the three superpowers.

A pseudo solar reactor Mobile Suit contained within the massive Mobile Armor Alvatore.
The Alvaaron is the main part of the machine, with the Alvatore essentially serving as a glorified outer shell.
Its main armaments are a pair of GN Beam Rifles, which harnesses condensed GN Particles from the Alvaaron's wings to boost their firepower.
Much like the Alvatore, the Alvaaron is nearly impervious to attack, with a powerful GN Field that nullifies beam attacks.
Overall, the Alvaaron is powerful enough to bring even Celestial Being's Gundams to their knees.

A massive Mobile Armor deployed by the UN Forces for the final showdown with Celestial Being.
Developed for Celestial Being observer Alejandro Corner, the Alvatore is equipped with a pseudo solar reactor— one of seven GN Drive Taus— that powers an array of powerful Beam Weapons including the Giant Beam Cannon.
Its powerful GN Field gives it superlative defensive capabilities, nullifying long-range attacks entirely.

A transformable, flight-capable Mobile Suit developed by the Union as a follow-up to the Union Realdo.
Unlike the Realdo, the Union Flag can switch to its flight mode seamlessly.
However, this is a challenging process and only incredibly skilled pilots can pull it off in combat.
The four plasma jet engines used for flight are hydrogen-fuelled.
This is infused into the molecular bonds of the carbon that makes up the machine's frame, eliminating the need for fuel tanks.
With exceptional maneuverability and multi-purpose design, the Union prioritized Flags to members of an elite MSWAD task force, whose pilots were referred to as "Flag Fighters."

Mobile Suits equipped with pseudo solar reactors based off the Gundam Throne series.
Celestial Being observer Alejandro Corner developed them in secret, then provided them to the Union and the other three superpowers.
Though inferior to the original Gundams, they give the three powers access to previously GN engine-dependent Beam Weapons, and far outclass their existing suits in virtually every way.
Their fundamentally high specifications and ease of production led to their becoming the main suit of the ESF Peacekeeping Force, the successor to the UN Forces, and the development of numerous follow-up models.

A transformable, flight-capable Union Mobile Suit.
The Union's main suit prior to the development of the Flag, the Realdo is akin to a fighter jet.
Though it can transform into a flight-capable mode, it cannot do so mid-flight, and must be reconfigured beforehand.
It was already on its way to obsolescence when Celestial Being began its armed interventions.
However, there were insufficient numbers of its successor, the Union Flag, thus Realdos remained a mainstay of Union forces around the globe.

A flight-capable Mobile Suit that served as the Advanced European Union's standard model prior to the Enact.
Though transformable, it cannot do so mid-flight, and must undergo a separate transformation process beforehand much like the Union's Realdo.
Mass-produced for its all-purpose functionality, Hellions saw use outside of AEU countries, as well.
Development of the cutting-edge Enact led to the gradual disappearance of Hellions from the frontlines, but variants with modified head parts and weapon loadouts are still a common sight on battlefields around the globe.

A new and improved Union Flag used exclusively by Union ace Graham Aker.
It is also known as a GN Flag.
Installed with a pseudo solar reactor from a GN-X acquired by Union technical adviser Billy Katagiri, the GN Flag boasts incredible speed and maneuverability.
However, this was only accomplished by ignoring the original design.
The end result is a highly unbalanced machine that is nearly impossible to handle for normal pilots.
Its main weapon is a GN Beam Saber stolen from the Gundam Throne Eins, but using it requires moving the pseudo solar reactor from the GN Flag's back to its left shoulder in order to connect the saber directly.

A third generation Gundam named after an angel whose name means "God is my mystery."
Its model number has been classified, and only a few within the organization responsible for its development know anything about it.
It was created as a "battlefield observer" and operates in places where collecting data directly would prove infeasible for Veda, such as the battlefield.

A prototype developed by the Human Reform League as the successor to the Tieren.
Its most impressive feature is its ability to adapt to different environments such as ground or space without changing any of its equipment.
Its official name is Tieren All-Region Prototype.
Its data was used to develop the MSJ-06II-SP Tieren Super Soldier Type (Tieren Taozi).

A second generation Gundam developed by Celestial Being as a test machine for deploying Gundams into live combat.
The test bed for the third generation Gundam Nadleeh and Gundam Virtue, it is the test for equipping a Gundam with a GN Field.
However, regulating the GN Particles needed to expand the field beyond the unit proved too difficult.
Instead, the unit uses the existing GN composite armor, which generates a field within the armor plating.
The tests also led to the development of the Core Fighter System to allow recovery of the Gundam Meister and GN Drive in emergency situations.

The upgraded 1 Gundam.
After the 1 Gundam was heavily damaged in the battle with Fon Spaak, it was repaired and upgraded.
The twin binders on its back increase its mobility and can also be transformed into a powerful Beam Weapon called the Avalon Cannon, or ground arms called Binder Rifles, increasing its firepower as well.
It was known as the 1.5 Gundam because all of its specs were 1.5 times that of the original.
Since 1.5 looks like the letters "IS," it's also known as the I'S Gundam.

A customized GN-X.
Based on feedback from the battlefield, the later productions of the GN-X were updated.
Defense Rods were attached to the shoulder hardpoints, and Verniers were added to the legs.
The weapon loadout was also improved; including upgrading the GN-X's standard rifle into the Advanced GN Beam Rifle and adding the prototype Proto GN Lance that would be used by the GN-X III.
The unit can also be further tuned to allow the pilot's maximum potential.
The model achieved great results in active duty, but never made up more than 3% of all GN-X units produced.
It was primarily used by the UN's ace pilots.
Combat data from these units was used in the development of the next generation main forces, the GN-X II.

A GN-X specialized for ground assault combat.
It was designed as an offshoot of the GN-X III to be used for water-to-ground ambushes.
However, the Innovades secretly included cutting-edge technology, making its specs a near-match for the next-generation GN-X IV, developed at the same time.
While advancing underwater, it has equipment specialized for assault like sensors, weapons, and hover units which are purged after landfall.
The GN Kunai on its arms are physical blades attached to the GN Vulcans.
It also stores a GN Beam Needle inside the frame for the assault.
The units were used for Special Ops under the command of the A-LAWS.
The pilots were restricted to A-LAWS; Soldiers or Licensors with special authorization to act independently.

A Mobile Suit developed by Flit Asuno in cooperation with the Earth Federation Forces to fight against the Unknown Enemy (UE).
This Gundam was developed by incorporating Flit's theory based on the data from the AGE Device, a device entrusted to him by his mother, Marina Asuno.
One of the biggest features is that the AGE System, installed in the center of the unit, evolves the Mobile Suit by using the living organism mechanism and evolution database.
It is possible to create new Mobile Suit weapons and armaments using the accumulated battle data by linking up to the unit production and analyzer, AGE Builder.
In addition, the power source is a plasma compression furnace similar to the UE, which also uses higher grade armor, allowing its performance to be better than the UE's Mobile Suit.

A Mobile Suit developed by Flit Asuno in cooperation with the Earth Federation Forces to fight against the Unknown Enemy (UE).
This Gundam was developed by incorporating Flit's theory based on the data from the AGE Device, a device entrusted to him by his mother, Marina Asuno.
One of the biggest features is that the AGE System, installed in the center of the unit, evolves the Mobile Suit by using the living organism mechanism and evolution database.
It is possible to create new Mobile Suit weapons and armaments using the accumulated battle data by linking up to the unit production and analyzer, AGE Builder.
In addition, the power source is a plasma compression furnace similar to the UE, which also uses higher grade armor, allowing its performance to be better than the UE's Mobile Suit.

A Gundam AGE-1 ware proposed by the AGE System.
Specialized for melee combat, it is not equipped with any projectile weapons or shields.
However, it is armed with close-combat weapons capable of performing a beam lariat and beam knee kick.

A retrofitted form of the Gundam AGE-1.
Prioritizing maneuverability and acceleration, much of its heavy weaponry has been has been removed.
It is armed with a Shiguru Blade and a needle gun that fires from its knees.

A Mobile Suit developed by the Mobile Suit Technology Research and Development Department of the Earth Federation Forces under the instruction of Flit Asuno, who was the developer of the AGE System and a Gundam pilot.
 Developed as an advanced model of the Gundam AGE-1, this unit transforms into Strider Form that enables high-speed movement, and the AGE System produces data to ensure its superiority on the battlefield.
In this mode, the acceleration speed is three times that of normal speed.
 Also, the Hyper DODS Rifle equipped has twice the power of the conventional armament.
It has the firepower that can overpower the opponent's combat ability.
  Gundam AGE-2 is well-known for its AGE System and later a piece of specialized equipment, Double Bullet was created.
A wear that enhances the power of normal and beam weapons.

A Mobile Suit developed by the Mobile Suit Technology Research and Development Department of the Earth Federation Forces under the instruction of Flit Asuno, who was the developer of the AGE System and a Gundam pilot.
 Developed as an advanced model of the Gundam AGE-1, this unit transforms into Strider Form that enables high-speed movement, and the AGE System produces data to ensure its superiority on the battlefield.
In this mode, the acceleration speed is three times that of normal speed.
 Also, the Hyper DODS Rifle equipped has twice the power of the conventional armament.
It has the firepower that can overpower the opponent's combat ability.
  Gundam AGE-2 is well-known for its AGE System and later a piece of specialized equipment, Double Bullet was created.
A wear that enhances the power of normal and beam weapons.

Woolf Enneacle's specialized unit, the basic framework of which took inspiration from Gundam AGE-1.
The wear system was not implemented, however, in order to prioritize high mobility.
Its main weaponry includes the DODS Rifle, beam saber, and a Shiguru Blade fitted to the shield.

A new mobile suit developed by the EFF, who based its design on Gundam AGE-1.
Performed exceptionally well on operational tests, and was promptly deployed, issued first to squadrons with higher risk of combat engagement.

A mobile suit that was developed to replace the Genoace as a flagship unit.
Its basic frame technology borrows from that of the Gundam AGE-1, allowing it too to use the Titus, Spallow, and other AGE armaments.
Its main weapon, the DODS Rifle, was also newly designed with the AGE-1 version as its base.

A mass-produced Earth Federation Forces mobile suit.
It is highly versatile, with good performance in both gravity and space.
It is armed with a beam spray gun, heat stick, and shield.

A mobile suit specially customized to suit that of Woolf Enneacle, Earth Federation Forces pilot.
It is called the "White Wolf" due to its all-white coloring.

A mobile suit developed by civilian enterprises such as the Madorna Workshop, adopted and improved upon by the military.
Equipped with the beam saber and DODS Gun.

A high mobility Vagan mobile suit.
Its primary weapons are dual-wielded Beam Vulcans and its tail, which can detach to become a saber.
A large backpack is attached to its back, allowing high maneuverability.
Additionally, it can achieve astonishing mobility by transforming.

A high-mobility Vagan mobile suit.
Its primary weapons are dual-wielded beam vulcans and its tail, which can detach to become a saber.
A large backpack is equipped, allowing high maneuverability.
Additionally, its transformation mechanism allows it to switch into a flight form, achieving astonishing mobility.

Mobile suit piloted by members of Magicians 8, an X-Rounder squadron.
Some of Zedas R's features were improved and a partly different coloring was applied to the armor.

A customized mobile suit based on the mass-produced Xeno.
It is piloted by Ract Elfamel, the leader of the Euba.

A customized replica mobile suit based on those used in the Colony Nation War.
It is the personal machine of Don Boyage, the leader of the Zalam.

A mass-produced mobile suit deployed by the Euba Alliance that was adopted by the Euba faction of Fardain.
It resembles the Zila deployed by the Zalam at a glance, but a closer look reveals the differences.

A mass-produced mobile suit piloted by the soldiers under Don Boyage's command.
It was deployed by the Zalam Union during the Colony Nation War.

A mobile suit produced by the Madorna Workshop.
With high basic performance, it served as the basis for the G-Exes.

A mobile suit equipped with a mega beam launcher and Mukured Madorna's personal Shaldoll.
Its power is said to rival a ship's main battery.
This mobile suit is also deployed by the Federation, and as such, is mass-produced.

The primary mobile suit deployed by Vagan, it is equipped with a transformation mechanism.
Typically, it is deployed in large numbers as a primary unit for their advance team.
It is armed with numerous weapons, including a beam rifle, beam saber, beam vulcan, and diffuse beam cannon.

A heavily armored mobile suit deployed by Vagan.
Armed with weapons specialized for close combat, it boasts formidable offensive capabilities, including a beam saber and a beam spike mounted on its chest.
It is equipped with EM armor for defense, allowing it to deflect even DODS Rifle attacks.
In addition, as a transformable mobile suit, it can transform into flight mode.

A mobile suit Vagan developed for X-Rounder use.
It was designed to accommodate pilots who have not received regular training, and in those instances, they only occupy the machine as receptors for remote control.
Its main weapon is the Farsia Bit, which is capable of attacking from any range.

A large mobile weapon piloted by Gerra Zoi.
It was deployed by the space fortress Ambat.
It specializes in operating under gravity and has extremely poor maneuverability in space.
It is armed with a Defurse Cannon, a beam vulcan, and a saber.

A mobile standard for industrial use.
It is equipped with a heat shovel and a heat pickel.
Its engine is hydrogen-powered.

Zeheart Galette's personal machine, developed for him to accommodate his awakened X-Rounder abilities.
It is modeled after the Zedas series and is armed with similar weapons, including a physical sword.
Its chest is equipped with a beam buster.

A mobile suit made easy to produce by removing its transformation mechanism.
It is equipped with a beam rifle on its tail, diffuse beam rifle on its chest, and triple beam vulcans on its arms.

A mobile suit similar in form to Dorado, piloted by Medel Zant.
Equipped with giant claws on both hands and a blade mounted on its tail.

A mobile suit used by the X-Rounder squadron, closely related to the Zeydra in design.
Equipped with two Khronos Cannons for long-range artillery fire, a Khronos Gun held in its right hand, and a beam buster mounted in its chest.

A custom mobile suit completed by the Madorna Workshop under the order of Woolf Enneacle.
It is armed with a sharp beam saber and a high-power beam rifle.

A loadout for the Gundam AGE-2 presented with combat against the X-Rounder in mind, proposed after the battles against Zeydra and Khronos.
Twin DODS Cannons are mounted on its shoulders, along with Calf Missiles built into the legs, allowing for efficient use while in flight form.

An improved version of the Genoace.
Mobile suits belonging to the Diva carrier have a distinctive blue color scheme.

Gundam AGE-2 Normal painted entirely white.
The personal mobile suit of Asemu after becoming the special forces' team leader.

A commander's mobile suit, with distinctive head armor.
Painted in a mostly yellow color scheme.
A 3-tube missile launcher can be mounted on its arm.

A mobile suit designed to provide support, with two DODS Cannons mounted on its shoulders.
It is also equipped with a pair of triple missile pods next to the cannons.

A mobile suit piloted by Flit Asuno, modified variant of the Gundam AGE-1 Normal with the AGE system removed.
Shares much of the same weaponry as the AGE-1 Normal, which includes the DODS Rifle and beam saber.

A Gundam AGE-1 equipped with Swordia ware.
Resembling a medieval knight, its form is designed for close combat, and its weapons are specialized for close combat, including a Liberta Blade, a shield, and a beam saber.
During a graduation ceremony on Tordia, it engaged with the Zedas R piloted by Zeheart.
The ensuing battle was a fierce clash.

An upgraded version of the Gundam AGE-1 Spallow, it is equipped with a physical sword called a razor blade on each limb.
Unlike the Spallow—which maximizes the effectiveness of its main weapon, the Shiguru Blade, by reducing its own weight and increasing the number of its thrusters to improve mobility—the Gundam AGE-1 Razor utilizes the inertia generated by its razor blades to control itself, improving its mobility and offensive capabilities.

A mobile suit manufactured by the Madorna Workshop.
Its default loadout equips the DODS Buster H with a hooked claw attachment in place of the left-hand manipulator, following the pirate's ideology of prioritizing offensive capabilities over versatility.
However, the design is flexible enough to allow changes to the loadout, albeit with some toil. The left arm can be refitted with the high-output heat sword, known to be as destructive as the beam saber, or other specialized melee weapons, if not reimplementing a manipulator when the situation calls for it.

The mobile suit Adele, developed through making improvements to the now-successfully mass-produced basic framework of Gundam AGE-1, is able to be equipped with powerful weapons such as the DODS Rifle, which were previously exclusive to AGE-1 and a few custom mobile suits.
It is worth noting that by implementing the wear system, which allowed AGE-1 to be refitted into Titus and Spallow, Adele made the provisional solution against the versatile Vagan mobile suits into standard tactical practice.
With its efficacy proven on the field, the wear system was also implemented in the succeeding Adele Mk-II, further influencing the EFF's later mobile suit designs.
Starks was a wear system initially proposed for the AGE-1. However, its only combat application on record is of a squadron of Adeles fitted with the wear pack to intercept a Vagan mobile suit.
The giant boosters mounted on its shoulders generate tremendous propulsion, and with the Root Reiber Lance—a weapon unique to this loadout—this Adele boasts the offensive power to deliver decisive blows.

To improve Gundam AGE-1's versatility beyond the wear system, the jacket system was proposed by the military's R&D division as a plan that does not depend on the AGE system.
Its basic concept was formulated by Dique Gunhale, who officially joined the military not long after the battle at Ambat.
The Assault Jacket is a loadout that places emphasis on firepower and defensive capabilities, which also includes improvements to accuracy through additional sensors, as well as adding more durable main thrusters for extended assault operations against strongholds.
As it is merely a prototype test unit, its equipment such as the Twin DODS Rifle, DODS Launcher, and beam vulcans can all be transferred onto other mobile suits. However, the Glastro Launcher, an AGE-1-exclusive weapon, will be later developed to bring about the Full Glansa, the culmination of the jacket system's progress.
Subsequently, the AGE-1 02 that was constructed per development of the jacket system became the template for Adele.

Gundam AGE-2 equipped with a long-range detection system as well as an Artimes Long-Range Rifle allowing engagement from a long distance.
Makes great use of its high mobility and long operational range for reconnaissance missions.

There are several similarities to the AGE-1 in The Gundam's design, layout, and mechanisms.
There is even less information regarding the weapons used by The Gundam than the mobile suit itself, but there remains a record of it wielding a giant sword much like in the Tordian legends, and the Asuno family portrait lost in fire during the Vagan assault depicted a figure equipped with a sword on its back.

A specialized unit developed for Fox by the Madorna Workshop.
It is heavily armed with two massive beam cannons, as well as rocket launchers.

A versatile mobile suit dubbed G-Self (as in "G itself") after the G-series design concept which it strongly reflects.
It has specific activation conditions, and only three pilots can operate it: Bellri and Aida, who are authorized by the Rayhunton Code, and Raraiya, who is officially registered.

The Capital Army, a newly established military organization in Capital Territory, required mobile suits that could withstand long-range attacks.
Elf Bullock, an upgraded mobile suit based on the data collected from the prototype Elf Bull, was deployed to various squads beginning with the Mask's Squadron, but that came at a considerable cost due to its large size.

The Mack Knife piloted by Barara Peor, a lieutenant in the Mask's Squadron.
The Mack Knife is a new mobile suit model developed by the Capital Army for space combat.
The machine that Barara pilots is a command version with twin eye sensors on its head.

A general-purpose mobile suit deployed by the Dorette Fleet.
Its weapons differ depending on affiliation and rank, and ace pilot Rockpie's machine sports a unique color scheme.
In addition, Ringo's machine, which was captured by the Megafauna, is painted red for identification.

A mobile suit designed for solo long-range assaults.
With large thrusters built into the wings mounted on both its shoulders, it boasts high maneuverability in the atmosphere.
It was initially designed solely for Klim, but was later mass-produced for deployment in the stratosphere.

A mobile suit for maintenance inspections of Capital Tower.
It can be equipped with special arms, enabling it to perform various tasks.
The instructor model differs from regular models in its color scheme and was piloted by Dellensen.

A mobile suit modified for combat based on the work mobile suit Recten.
It is equipped with a fixed beam rifle on its shoulder and hover units on its legs, and it was deployed to bolster defense.

The Moran is a general-purpose mobile suit that pilots the Alincato, a mobile suit-operated support unit deployed by the Dorette Fleet.
The Alincato is equipped with a high-output beam cannon and large claws.

A Gundam Frame Mobile Suit owned by Tekkadan.
This is one of the 72 Gundam Frame suits developed near the end of the Calamity War 300 years ago.
It houses two Ahab Reactors which can be run in tandem to generate tremendous output.
This suit is highly versatile compared to other suits with the same frame, and it can be adapted for many situations by exchanging its various armaments.
The suit was discovered abandoned in a desert on Mars, and was used as a power source for a CGS base for many years.
However, during an attack by Gjallarhorn, young members of the Third Group reactivated it and sent it into combat piloted by Mikazuki Augus.
Although it had lost its shoulder armor and its various parts had greatly deteriorated, which prevented it from demonstrating its full strength, it was still strong enough to defeat the enemy Mobile Suit, a Graze.
The newly formed Tekkadan later used it as their main suit.
Upon reactivation, the only armament it had was a thruster-equipped mace.

An improved form of the Gundam Barbatos.
This form is close to the suit's original form from the time of the Calamity War, and it was reproduced by Saisei engineers based on ancient materials recorded in Teiwaz's database.
Although its performance has not been restored to the same level as during the Calamity War, the frame's weight balance and reactors have been readjusted to improve its overall capabilities.
In addition, the Alaya-Vijnana System has been adjusted for use by its pilot, Mikazuki Augus.
It has also been equipped with a new Long Sword made of a rare alloy and is suitable for making targeted attacks.

An improved form of the Gundam Barbatos.
This form has been enhanced to prepare for the final battle in Edmonton.
It has reinforced chest armor, additional shoulder armor from the Graze Ritter, and additional thrusters on its waist for ground-based mobility.
Although the additional armor has compromised some of its mobility, this form's specifications are superior for prolonged battles.
During the battle of Edmonton, although outnumbered by the enemy, it fought like a raging lion, ultimately engaging the Graze Ein in a fight to the death.

An improved form of the Gundam Barbatos.
This form has been enhanced to prepare for the final battle in Edmonton.
It has reinforced chest armor, additional shoulder armor from the Graze Ritter, and additional thrusters on its waist for ground-based mobility.
Although the additional armor has compromised some of its mobility, this form's specifications are superior for prolonged battles.
During the battle of Edmonton, although outnumbered by the enemy, it fought like a raging lion, ultimately engaging the Graze Ein in a fight to the death.

A Gundam Frame Mobile Suit owned by the space pirates known as the Brewers.
This is one of the 72 Gundam Frame suits developed near the end of the Calamity War 300 years ago.
It houses two Ahab Reactors which can be run in tandem to generate tremendous output.
After being discovered in a high-density region of the Debris Belt, this suit was gradually improved while being passed between different pirate groups.
It ultimately ended up in the hands of the Brewers, where it was piloted by Kudal Cadel.
The suit boasts thick armor that enables it to shrug off the Anti-Air Cannon fire of enemy ships, while the high- output thrusters mounted on various parts of its frame give it higher mobility than its bulky appearance suggests.
However, it still consumes a lot of propellant, so its operational time is relatively short.
The suit's Gusion Hammer weighs in at over 15 tons, and the massive weapon has thrusters that can be used to increase the impact of its blows and shatter even large chunks of debris.

A Gundam Frame Mobile Suit owned by Tekkadan.
This suit is the result of Teiwaz engineers improving the Gundam Gusion after it was seized from the space pirates known as the Brewers.
The heavy armor for which the Gusion was known for has been removed except for where it is reused as waist binders, and it has been replaced with spare armor from the Gundam Barbatos to make the suit lighter and increase its operational time.
The backpack houses sub-arms derived from the Graze's arms, and they can also use weapons.
Unlike the Barbatos, which specializes in close-quarters combat, this suit's head includes high-sensitivity sensors geared toward long-range support, and it can be switched to aiming mode to shoot with precision from any range.
The Alaya-Vijnana System seized from a Man Rodi has also been installed into this suit.
Its armaments include a Halberd, which is a melee weapon with a retractable handle; and a Long-Range Rifle, which is an improved version of the Graze's rifle.

A Gundam Frame Mobile Suit owned by the Bauduin family.
This is one of the 72 Gundam Frame suits developed near the end of the Calamity War 300 years ago.
It houses two Ahab Reactors which can be run in tandem to generate tremendous output.
This suit was piloted by Sir Bauduin the First, who played a key role in ending the Calamity War.
After the war, the suit was sometimes exhibited in ceremonies and the like.
Once Gaelio Bauduin piloted it in combat for the first time in 300 years, it became his personal suit.
One of its key features is its overwhelming mobility, which is made possible by its two reactors and boosters mounted on various parts of its frame.
Another key strength of the suit is the combination of the large lance Gungnir, its main weapon, and the thrust of the high-output boosters on its legs enables it to destroy even Nanolaminate Armor, which makes the suit a threat to enemy suits and warships.

A form of the Gundam Kimaris equipped with a high-mobility booster.
A large booster developed to achieve long-distance flight and increase combat capabilities in low orbit has been attached to its back to increase its thrust and enhance its already formidable high-mobility combat capabilities.
Although the increased thrust reduces its ability to turn, this can be compensated through skillful use of the high-output boosters on the legs.
This suit was deployed to prevent Tekkadan from entering the atmosphere and to battle with the Gundam Barbatos in low Earth orbit.

A form of the Gundam Kimaris with an alternate set of equipment for ground combat.
The suit's rear armor has been changed to a hover unit, and the armor for each of its parts has been reduced in size to make it lighter.
In addition, the suit transforms into a four-legged trooper form to achieve the same level of mobility on the ground as in space.
The suit's new armaments include a Destroyer Lance, which is even larger than the Gungnir, a Kimaris shield, and a Kimaris saber.

A high mobility Mobile Suit operated by Teiwaz and its subsidiaries.
Teiwaz, which bases its operations in the Outer Sphere, developed this suit to patrol routes with many obstacles, such as the Debris Belt.
The suit's backpack, which is equipped with a large booster, includes a sensor used to detect Ahab Waves, and it also serves as a weapon container.
The suit can also tuck its arms in and switch to a flight mode that enables it to travel long distances.

The main Mobile Suit operated by Teiwaz and its subsidiaries.
The Teiwaz frame used for this suit is based on a high-output machine that was planned during the late stages of the Calamity War, and it was completed by Teiwaz engineers.
An Ahab Reactor from the Calamity War era is its power source.
The suit's mass production line remains unfinished, and only 44 such suits are in operation.
Because it mainly operates in environments with a lot of obstacles, such as the Asteroid Belt and Debris Belt, it is generally equipped with heavy armor.
The suit's main armaments include a Single-Edged Blade for close-quarters combat as well as a highly versatile 100mm Rifle Cannon.

A Mobile Suit owned by the Turbines, and the personal suit of Amida Arca.
Of the Hyakurens in operation, this is a so-called "Single Number" suit, one of the first nine Hyakurens off the assembly line.
High-purity metal was used for some of the parts of these Single Number suits, and they boast higher maximum output and reaction speed than the Hyakurens manufactured later.
The first nine suits were given to nine Teiwaz subsidiaries by the group's representative, McMurdo Barriston.
The one sent to the Turbines was used as the personal suit of Amida Arca.

An improved form of the Hyakuren owned by the Turbines.
Teiwaz replaced the Hyakuren's usual armor in order to conceal the suit's identity because they could not openly support Tekkadan as the group opposed Gjallarhorn.
This suit has lighter armor than the original Hyakuren, and it has been adjusted for ground combat.
Large thrusters that can be opened and closed have been added to its back, and thrusters for hovering have also been added to the skirt and legs, so it is capable of high maneuverability even under gravity.
The suit's armaments include a Short Rifle developed for the Rouei and a highly destructive Heavy Club for melee combat, and it can use the equipment of the conventional Hyakuren as well.
Two Rouei suits piloted by Lafter Frankland and Azee Gurumin were used to support Tekkadan when the group descended to Earth.
Note that the suit piloted by Lafter was the result of converting Amida Arca's personal Hyakuren.

A mass-produced Mobile Suit commonly used by various organizations.
This suit uses the extremely versatile Rodi frame, which was mass-produced during the middle period of the Calamity War.
It is used not only for combat but also for work in space and other applications.
Its armaments include a rifle with a grenade launcher and a Boost Hammer, which has thrusters that can be fired to increase the impact of the weapon's blows.
A labor union in the Dort Colonies attempted to use these suits for an armed uprising, but their firearms were sabotaged before the battle, and they were then slaughtered by the Gjallarhorn squad sent in to suppress the rebellion.
In addition, Amida Arca piloted a Commander Type Spinner Rodi during her time as a mercenary.

Gjallarhorn's main mass-produced Mobile Suit.
After the Calamity War, Gjallarhorn was the only organization capable of manufacturing new suits, and the Graze was the resulting state-of-the-art machine.
The elaborate design of the suit's internal frame makes it more versatile, and its weapons and armor can be exchanged to adapt it for various environments.
It also offers superior maneuverability and maintainability, and has been praised so highly that some doubt the necessity of developing a next generation main machine.
The suit's standard armaments include a 120mm Rifle and anti-Mobile Suit Battle Axe.
Like any good general-purpose suit, many optional armaments are also available.

Crank Zent's Graze, which is owned by Gjallarhorn's Mars branch.
When Crank was ordered to attack CGS, he used this suit to challenge Mikazuki Augus and his Gundam Barbatos to a one-on-one duel in order to minimize the number of child casualties.
A red cloth is wrapped around the suit's left arm as a symbol of the duel, and its only armaments are a Battle Axe and shield.

A form of the Graze with specifications geared towards space combat.
A booster has been attached to its back for high mobility in space, but its performance is otherwise the same as that of the regular type.
The frame is painted purple to show that it is intended for use in space.

A Graze owned by the Outer Lunar Orbit Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
Although the suit has a dark green color scheme, its performance and armaments are the same as those of the regular Graze Space Type.
When these suits were fielded in the Dort Colonies to put down a rebellion, they decimated the Spinner Rodis there, which had been sabotaged to prevent them from fighting.

A Graze variant deployed with Gjallarhorn's ground forces.
This Graze has been customized for use in ground combat, and the large hover units on its thighs as well as the supplementary hover units on the bottoms of its feet enable it to achieve high maneuverability even while under gravity.
A specially designed Battle Blade is equipped on the suit's back, which is lightweight and easy to handle.
Those used by the forces garrisoned in Edmonton, which engaged Tekkadan's forces, were painted reddish-brown.

An improved form of the Graze owned by Tekkadan.
This suit was built by combining the usable parts of two Graze units captured during combat with Gjallarhorn.
The heavily damaged head and shoulders have been replaced with custom parts and painted with inexpensive white Nanolaminate Armor paint.
In addition, a booster has been added to the back to improve its maneuverability in space.
Because the original Graze offered superior maneuverability and ease of maintenance, this suit is easy to handle even for the members of Tekkadan who are unused to suits.

An improved form of the Graze Custom owned by Tekkadan.
The suit's shoulder armor and rear skirt have been replaced with Hyakuren parts thanks to cooperation from Teiwaz.
In addition, because the Alaya-Vijnana System from a Man Rodi has been installed in the suit, it offers Tekkadan pilots extremely high maneuverability.
The suit's pilot, Norba Shino, calls it the Ryusei-Go II, but the name is not generally used by other members of Tekkadan.
In addition, the suit's gaudy pink color, nose art, and head antenna were all requested by him.

A form of the Graze Ritter with specifications geared towards combat on the ground.
Each part of the suit has been adjusted for ground combat, and its thighs are equipped with the same hover units as those used by the Graze Ground Type.
During the Millennium Island attack, these suits use Earth-landing modules to enter the atmosphere and assault Tekkadan from the sky.

A Graze Ritter Ground Type for use by Commanders.
Like the general type, each part of the suit has been adjusted for ground combat, and its thighs are equipped with the same hover units as those used by the Graze Ground Type.
Carta Issue was piloting one of these suits when she landed on Earth and attacked Tekkadan on Millennium Island.

A Graze Ritter Ground Type for use by Commanders.
This suit was piloted by Carta Issue, Commander of the Outer Earth Orbit Regulatory Joint Fleet.
Carta, who had repeatedly failed against Tekkadan, used this suit when she challenged them to a duel to clear her name.
A red cloth is wrapped around the suit's right shoulder as a symbol of the duel.

A custom suit that uses the same frame as the Graze and is based on a prototype device.
Its design aimed to achieve higher output and mobility, and it has additional shoulder and leg thrusters, as well as a large flight unit on its back.
Unlike a regular Graze unit, which offers superior versatility, this suit is not very stable at low output, which means that the pilot must be skilled.
However, it is capable of extremely high mobility when operated at high output, even within the atmosphere.
The suit's armaments are the same as those of the regular Graze, but pilots of these suits are often fixed. Other features are customized according to pilot preference.
This was the personal suit of Gaelio Bauduin, which was given to Ein Dalton once Gaelio started piloting the Gundam Kimaris; the lance unit was also then removed from the suit's right arm.

A custom suit that uses the same frame as the Graze and is based on a prototype device.
Its design aimed to achieve higher output and mobility, and it has additional shoulder and leg thrusters, as well as a large flight unit on its back.
Unlike a regular Graze unit, which offers superior versatility, this suit is not very stable at low output, which means that the pilot must be skilled.
However, it is capable of extremely high mobility when operated at high output, even within the atmosphere.
The suit's armaments are the same as those of the regular Graze, but pilots of these suits are often fixed, and they are customized according to their pilots' preferences.
This is the personal suit of Gaelio Bauduin. It is painted purple, and its right arm is equipped with a specially-designed large lance unit.

A custom suit that uses the same frame as the Graze and is based on a prototype device.
Its design aimed to achieve higher output and mobility, and it has additional shoulder and leg thrusters, as well as a large flight unit on its back.
Unlike a regular Graze unit, which offers superior versatility, this suit is not very stable at low output, which means that the pilot must be skilled.
However, it is capable of extremely high mobility when operated at high output, even within the atmosphere.
The suit's armaments are the same as those of the regular Graze, but pilots of these suits are often fixed, and they are customized according to their pilots' preferences.
This is the personal suit of McGillis Fareed, and it is painted blue.

A Valkyrja frame Mobile Suit owned by the Montag Company.
This suit uses the Valkyrja frame, which was developed at the same time as the Gundam frame.
It is lighter and more focused on mobility than other suits from the same era.
It boasts excellent maneuverability both in space and on the ground, but its pilot must also be highly skilled as it is necessary to precisely control its center of gravity to land effective blows in melee combat.
The suit's armaments include a 110mm Valkyrja Rifle, which was developed with the suit's weight balance in mind, and a Valkyrja Shield with a built-in Valkyrja Blade on each arm.
This suit was used by McGillis Fareed to support Tekkadan while he was disguised as Montag, and it was used in the battle of Edmonton against Gaelio Bauduin and his Gundam Kimaris Trooper.

A prototype Mobile Suit developed by Gjallarhorn.
This suit was originally an experimental machine used for Alaya-Vijnana System research, but it was improved when Ein Dalton sustained serious injuries.
Ein was incorporated into the suit as a biological unit, and the suit was finally fielded in combat.
In addition, it served as a life support system for Ein, who was beyond any chance of recovery.
Although it is based on the Graze, its arm and leg frames have been lengthened to make it easier for the human brain to recognize the suit as its own body and strengthen the connection between man and machine.
As a result, it is more than 20 meters long, but is still capable of nimble movements thanks to the Alaya-Vijnana System.
The suit's specially designed armaments include large axes and retractable autocannons.
The suit's distinctively long arms and legs can also rotate, enabling the use of Screw Punches and Drill Kicks, which serve as powerful melee weapons.

The main Mobile Suit of the space pirates known as the Brewers.
This is a restored suit that uses the highly versatile Rodi frame, which was mass-produced during the middle period of the Calamity War.
It has also been deployed by organizations other than the Brewers.
The durability of its armor has been maximized to get through the Anti-Air Cannon fire of the merchant vessels that the pirates prey on.
Due to its heavy weight, it has poor fuel efficiency and its operational time is limited, but the thrusters along the length of its frame enable it to move quite nimbly in space.
The suits of the Brewers were also piloted by youths who had undergone the Alaya- Vijnana System process, so they were able to coax even higher maneuverability out of this suit.
Its armaments are a good fit for this general-purpose suit, and they include a Hammer Chopper for melee combat and a 90mm Submachine Gun for close- or mid-range combat.

A Mobile Worker operated by Tekkadan.
Tekkadan has continued to use this machine, which was also used by the private security company CGS.
Because the Alaya-Vijnana System is incorporated into it, older Tekkadan members have often piloted this machine even after the new type of Mobile Worker was introduced.

An improved form of the Mobile Worker operated by Tekkadan.
The main body is the same as the older Mobile Worker model, but the armaments on both sides of its frame have been replaced with 60mm Cannons for better firepower.
During the battle of Edmonton, these Mobile Workers were piloted by Eugene Seven Stark and his companions when they aided the struggling Orga Itsuka.

A Mobile Worker operated by Gjallarhorn.
Developed for military use, this Mobile Worker is larger than general Military Workers and boasts higher output.
It is also well-equipped with armaments, including a Large-Caliber Cannon on the top and an Anti-Personnel Chain Gun on the bottom.
This Mobile Worker was used at the Mars branch, and it was painted reddish-brown.

A rear-support-type Mobile Worker operated by Gjallarhorn.
The Large-Caliber Cannon mounted on top of the regular machine has been replaced with four Missile Pods.
This Mobile Worker was used at the Mars branch, and it was painted reddish-brown.

An improved form of the Gundam Barbatos Lupus.
This suit is the result of a major upgrade to the Barbatos Lupus at Saisei's Mobile Suit workshop after it was severely damaged during its desperate battle against the Mobile Armor Hashmal.
In line with the combat data of Mikazuki Augus, who increased his synchronization with the suit through the Alaya-Vijnana System, its reaction speed has been increased to its theoretical limits.
The damaged internal frame has also been adjusted.
Based on Mikazuki's melee combat abilities, the forearms have been enlarged and sharp claws made of rare metal are attached to the fingertips.
In addition, the Hashmal's tail parts transplanted to its back can be used as a Tail Blade to unleash a diverse array of thrusting attacks.
It also has a new main armament, an Extra Large Mace, which was developed to handle combat with enemy ships.

An improved form of the Gundam Barbatos.
This form is the result of the Barbatos being overhauled at Saisei's Mobile Suit workshop after it sustained too much damage from the fierce battle against the Graze Ein in Edmonton.
Its reaction speed and maneuverability have been increased based on the combat data of Mikazuki Augus, and the frame's movements have been more closely tied to the pilot's senses through the Alaya-Vijnana System.
Based on Mikazuki's preference for close-quarters combat, the exterior has been refitted with curved armor that makes it easier to deflect attacks, and specially designed thrusters have been mounted to the waist.
In addition, many new weapons have been developed for the suit, including a Sword Mace and Twin Maces for melee combat, as well as Arm 200mm Cannons incorporated into its armor.

An improved form of the Gundam Gusion Rebake.
This suit is the result of the Gusion Rebake being overhauled at Saisei's Mobile Suit workshop when the frame, like the Gundam Barbatos, sustained too much damage.
Data recovered from the Calamity War era has been applied to it to restore much of its original performance.
The combat data of its pilot, Akihiro Altland, has also been applied.
Equipment geared towards melee combat has been added while retaining its basic specifications, making it suitable for both close-quarters combat and long-range support.
Its sub-arms have also been enhanced, and they can simultaneously fire its four 110mm Rifles.
Its new rear armor, which doubles as a shield, can also transform into a scissors-like weapon that can even cut through Mobile Suit frames.

A Gundam Frame Mobile suit owned by Tekkadan.
This is one of the 72 Gundam Frame suits developed near the end of the Calamity War 300 years ago.
It houses two Ahab Reactors which can be run in tandem to generate tremendous output.
This suit was discovered in a half-metal mine on Mars.
It was then brought to Saisei's Mobile Suit workshop, where it was reactivated and restored.
Its most distinctive feature are the Railguns mounted on its back, and the output of its two reactors can be used to fire them with explosive speed.
It is also capable of firing the Dainsleif, a banned weapon.
To take full advantage of this feature, it also has a shelling mode, where it transforms into a four-legged form for added stability while firing.
Its pilot, Norba Shino, dubbed it the "Ryusei-Go IV" and naturally had it painted pink, his favorite color.

A mass-produced Mobile Suit developed by Teiwaz.
This suit uses the IO frame, which was designed for mass production, and is Teiwaz's first mass-produced suit.
Although this is a well-balanced general-purpose suit, its individual performance is inferior to the Graze, so it works better in group combat.
The suit's armaments include a Partizan, a striking weapon with a handle that can be retracted or extended.
It is also equipped with a large Riot Shield that is effective for group combat and escort missions.
Teiwaz provided many of these suits to Tekkadan at a low price in recognition of the group's combat strength.

The personal Shiden of Orga Itsuka, the leader of Tekkadan.
The suit has a white color scheme and is called the King's Throne by Tekkadan members.
This Mobile Suit is equipped with a left shoulder shield, and its visor, which differs in shape from that of the regular type, has a blade antenna.
Although Orga never actually piloted the suit in combat, Eugene Seven Stark did in the final battle with Arianrhod.

An improved form of the Shiden owned by Tekkadan.
This suit is customized for Norba Shino's use, and he calls this replacement for the Graze Custom II, the Ryusei-Go III.
A blade antenna and shoulder thrusters have been added, and the left arm is equipped with a gauntlet.
In addition, the Graze Custom II's combat data and Alaya-Vijnana System have been transferred to this suit's cockpit.
Naturally, in line with Shino's tastes, the frame has been painted pink and adorned with nose art.

An improved form of the Shiden Custom owned by Tekkadan.
This suit is the result of further customizing Norba Shino's Shiden Custom (Ryusei-Go III) after it is inherited by Ride Mass.
The suit's visor has been changed, and both arms have been equipped with gauntlets that double as knuckle guards to make the suit more effective in super close-quarters combat.
Ride renamed this suit Riden-Go and changed its color scheme.
During the assault by Arianrhod, Embi piloted the suit in place of the absent Ride.

An improved form of the Man Rodi owned by Tekkadan.
This suit is the result of improving a Man Rodi seized from the space pirates, the Brewers.
Because its leg frames have been replaced and its thrusters repositioned, it can handle operations on the ground.
No other major changes have been made to the suit, including its equipment.
However, because the  Alaya-Vijnana System has been installed it is easy for  Tekkadan members to pilot, which has allowed this  unit to represent an important part of their  fighting strength.

An improved form of the Mobile Worker operated by Tekkadan.
The main body is the same as the older Mobile Worker model, but the armaments on both sides of its frame have been replaced with 60mm Cannons for better firepower.
During the battle of Edmonton, these Mobile Workers were piloted by Eugene Seven Stark and his companions when they aided the struggling Orga Itsuka.

Developed by Teiwaz as the successor of the Hyakuri and Hyakuren.
This high-performance suit was developed with the goal of achieving both the maneuverability of the Hyakuri and the high output of the Hyakuren.
The suit has deployable thrusters on its shoulders and legs, and switches to an assault form that enables it to achieve high mobility and instantly close the gap with enemy machines.
In addition, to make it more effective against opposing machines incorporating the Alaya-Vijnana System, it was made as easy to operate as possible to lighten the burden imposed on its pilots.
The suit's armaments include a specially-designed 110mm Bayonet Rifle as well as the Tobiguchi Blade, a weapon for close-quarters combat with a fire hook at its tip.
Test suits were fielded by the Turbines to collect combat data, but one of them was given to Tekkadan and then assigned to Hush Middy.

A Gundam-frame Mobile Suit owned by the Outer Lunar Orbit Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
The existence of this mysterious suit was kept top secret even within the ranks of Gjallarhorn.
It is actually the Gundam Kimaris damaged during the battle of Edmonton, and it was recovered, repaired, and disguised under the orders of Rustal Elion.
The backpack houses a third Ahab Reactor, and its purpose is to disguise the suit by preventing the reactor's usual frequency from being identified.
In addition, the cockpit incorporates a pseudo Alaya- Vijnana System known as the Alaya-Vijnana Type E.
This system actually consists of the Alaya-Vijnana System that was installed in the Graze Ein as well as Ein Dalton's brain, which can handle the burden imposed on the pilot in order to maximize the performance of the suit.
The suit's main armament is its Burst Saber, from which the sword blade can be detached and detonated after the opponent is stabbed.
The suit is also equipped with handguns, a rifle, and other new armaments intended to conceal the fact that it is actually the Kimaris.

A form of the Gundam Kimaris with specifications geared towards space combat.
This suit is the result of removing the Gundam Vidar's disguise, and it is a form of the Gundam Kimaris restored from Calamity War era data.
Compared to the various other sets of specifications for the Kimaris, this form emphasizes combat in space, and it has thrusters along the length of its frame and shields attached to the sub-arms that extend from its back.
The Alaya-Vijnana Type E system is used for this form as well.
Its main armament is its Drill Lance, a striking weapon with a tip that can be spun to increase its destructive power, as well as built-in Dainsleif launchers.
It also has drill knees that are effective in close-quarters combat, a sword made out of a rare alloy, and other powerful weapons suitable for its combat-oriented specifications.

A Graze variant owned by the Outer Lunar Orbit Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
This suit has been customized to use the Dainsleif, a massive weapon developed for use against Mobile Armors during the Calamity War era.
It is based on the Graze, but its left arm has been replaced with a Dainsleif-type Railgun.
In addition, the head has a built-in sighting camera for precise firing, which is exposed when the suit fires its Dainsleif.
Note that it is not possible for the suit to reload the Dainsleif on its own, so it is necessary to deploy suits to serve as loaders when firing the weapon repeatedly.

The personal Graze of Iok Kujan.
This suit is a Graze Commander Type with a distinctive black and yellow color scheme, and a Battle Blade and ground-use boosters equipped on its back.
Iok piloted this suit when he participated in the assault by Arianrhod, and he charged McGillis Fareed's Gundam Bael completely unarmed to get his forces to attack before McGillis could find a way to break through the surrounding enemy forces.

A custom variant of the Graze owned by the Outer Lunar Orbit Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
This suit specializes in group combat and is generally used in missions to lay siege to cities or bases under gravity.
Because the suit is mainly used for close-quarters combat, a chin guard has been attached to its mask, and it is equipped with a specially designed large halberd and large shield.
Many of these suits were fielded during the assault by Arianrhod, and their mission was to surround Tekkadan's HQ on Mars.

Gjallarhorn's next generation Mobile Suit, developed as a successor to the Graze.
This suit's design inherits the basic performance and high versatility of the Graze while focusing more on combat with other suits.
The newly developed Reginlaze frame is stronger than the Graze frame it is based on, and its structure has been further simplified to increase its maintainability.
The multi-weapon pack, one of the suit's standard armaments, can be used for multiple purposes ranging from melee to ranged combat by combining the included rifle with a rocket launcher, sword unit, or other weapons.

The personal Reginlaze of Julieta Juris.
This is one of the 18 initially manufactured Reginlaze units assigned to the Outer Lunar Orbit Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
This suit has been customized to focus on melee combat in line with Julieta's wishes, and it is capable of higher output and maneuverability than the regular suit.
The suit's specially designed armaments include the Twin Piles, striking weapons with built-in wired anchors.

The personal Reginlaze of Iok Kujan.
This is one of the 18 initially manufactured Reginlaze units assigned to the Outer Lunar Orbit Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
This suit is painted black and yellow to signify its ownership by Iok, and it has been customized to provide long-range support based on the suggestions of Iok's subordinates, who fear for his safety.
The suit's specially designed armaments include a prototype Long-Range Railgun and a Knight Blade.

An improved form of the Grimgerde owned by the Montag Company.
To disguise the Grimgerde, much of the equipment and exterior of the original was replaced based on data on the Helmwige, a heavy Mobile Suit used to battle Mobile Armors near the end of the Calamity War.
The polar opposite of the Grimgerde, which focused on mobility, this suit uses heavy armor and armaments, and the frame output distribution has been adjusted accordingly.
The suit's large Valkyrja Buster Sword was designed for use against Mobile Armors, and the weapon's handle can be detached and used as a Short Mace.
McGillis Fareed gave this suit to his subordinate Isurugi Camice, and it was deployed in the battle against the Hashmal on Mars.

A Hugo operated by JPT Trust.
This suit is used by mercenaries hired by JPT Trust and has a purple color scheme.
Its armaments include a 300mm Long Rifle and Buster Sword.
Many of these were deployed in JPT Trust's battle with Tekkadan.

A Man Rodi operated by JPT Trust.
This suit is used by mercenaries hired by JPT Trust, and its hands and feet are painted purple.
Human Debris subjected to the Alaya-Vijnana System process sometimes pilot these suits as well.
The suit's armaments include a Hammer Chopper and 90mm Submachine Gun.
Many of these were deployed in JPT Trust's battle with Tekkadan.

A highly mobile mass-produced Mobile Suit operated by private companies and space pirates.
This suit uses the Hexa frame, which was developed during the middle period of the Calamity War.
The Hexa frame is intended to be lightweight and highly mobile, so this suit also boasts superior maneuverability, but this comes at the cost of its ability to withstand enemy fire.
Not many of these suits survived after the Calamity War.
Its armaments include Missile Launchers on its head, Leg Claws, a Machine Gun, a Rifle, and waist-mounted Anchor Shot Claws, which can be used to capture enemies.
In addition, the suit piloted by Sandoval Reuters of the Dawn Horizon Corps also has Scimitars, which were standard armaments for the suit during the Calamity War era.

A Hugo operated by the space pirates known as the Dawn Horizon Corps.
This suit was piloted by one of the twin brothers who was a close associate of Sandoval Reuters, the leader of the pirates.
Part of it is painted reddish-purple.
The suit's armaments include a 300mm Long Rifle used to provide artillery support to Sandoval's suit as well as a Buster Sword for close-quarters combat.
During the decisive battle with Tekkadan, it was deployed alongside Sandoval in an attempt to turn the tables.

A Hugo operated by the space pirates known as the Dawn Horizon Corps.
This suit was piloted by one of the twin brothers who was a close associate of Sandoval Reuters, the leader of the pirates.
Part of it is painted green.
The suit's armaments include a 300mm Long Rifle used to provide artillery support to Sandoval's suit as well as a Buster Sword for close-quarters combat.
During the decisive battle with Tekkadan, it was deployed alongside Sandoval in an attempt to turn the tables.

The main Mobile Suit operated by the space pirates known as the Dawn Horizon Corps.
This mass-produced suit uses the Rodi frame and has heavy armor, including armor on its legs and a shield on its left shoulder.
Although its armor is thick, it is capable of hovering even in the gravitational field of Mars and maintaining its maneuverability.
The suit can use a diverse array of weapons depending on the situation, including a Rifle and Boost Hammer.
Thanks to its exceptional versatility, it serves as the main fighting strength of the Dawn Horizon Corps both on land and in space.

A Garm Rodi for use by Commanders operated by the space pirates known as the Dawn Horizon Corps.
This suit has a gray color scheme, a Blade Antenna on its head, and shields on both shoulders.
Its armaments are the same as that of the regular type.

A mass-produced Mobile Suit operated by each economic bloc and many influential groups.
Like the Hugo, this suit uses the Hexa frame, which was developed during the middle period of the Calamity War.
Its extremely high versatility results in its use for both combat and work purposes.
