After the incident in the Dort Colonies, there was a push for independence in the colonies, and it was not rare for this suit to be deployed to fight in armed uprisings despite the fact that it was originally designed for work.
Rebels in the Oceanian Federation colonies also used these suits.
Though armed with wire guns and crowbars, they were no match for Gjallarhorn's suits.

Gjallarhorn's main Mobile Suit before they developed the Graze.
Once a general-purpose suit operated by Gjallarhorn, it was phased out after the Graze was adopted as the main suit.
The few remaining ones are used by regional forces or as training suits.
Some veteran mercenaries who are accustomed to this suit also continue using it.
The suit used by Galan Mossa while he acts under secret orders from Rustal Elion has a large hover unit on its back and a Shield Axe that can be used for both offense and defense.

A variant of the Geirail.
This suit is the result of improving the Geirail with heavy armor and equipment.
The shape of its armored head has been changed dramatically, and its shoulders are equipped with Claw Shields for melee combat.
Like the Geirail, it has many types of armaments, including a Land Mace and Pickel, which are work tools used as melee weapons.
Although this last generation suit no longer fights on the front line, it is still prized by mercenary units and the like.
The unit led by Galan Mossa used this suit as well.

A mass-produced Mobile Suit provided by Gjallarhorn to each economic bloc.
This suit was developed by Gjallarhorn after the battle of Edmonton to satisfy the demand of each economic bloc for suits to shore up their defenses.
Developed as a low-cost version of the Graze, it has been simplified and is relatively small overall.
Although it is far inferior to the Graze in terms of performance, its simplified structure makes it easy to control, so it is easy even for inexperienced pilots to handle.
The suit's armaments include a Submachine Gun and Small Axe, which are compact and easy to use.
Many of these suits were assigned to the Arbrau Defense Force, which serves as the military adviser to Tekkadan.

A Mobile Armor excavated from a half-metal mine on Mars.
The Hashmal is one of the autonomous unmanned weapons that caused the Calamity War.
A highly evolved weapon bent on slaughtering humanity, the Hashmal took the lives of approximately a quarter of the population during the war.
Like suits, this Mobile Armor is powered by an Ahab Reactor, and its exterior is reinforced by Nanolaminate Armor.
Each Hashmal also commands a large number of Plumas, small units manufactured within itself.
Defeating even one requires a large-scale fighting force.
Its armaments include an Anti-Personnel Beam Cannon mounted on its head.
When fighting suits, it fires kinetic energy bullets out of both arms and uses a Superhard Wire Blade that extends from its back.
The Hashmal discovered in the mine was dormant at first, but it woke up when Iok Kujan's Mobile Suit team got too close.
It then resumed its mission to exterminate humanity.

A small sub-unit that accompanies the Hashmal.
This unmanned machine is powered by microwaves received from its Hashmal, and it is used not only to attack but also to collect resources or repair the Hashmal itself.
Plumas are automatically manufactured within the Hashmal as long as there are resources to do so.
Although Plumas are not individually strong in combat, a group of them can overwhelm enemies.
This sub-unit's armaments include a claw on each arm, a drill on the tail, and a Railgun in front.

A Gundam Frame Mobile Suit piloted by Agnika Kaieru, the founder of Gjallarhorn and hero of the Calamity War.
This is one of the 72 Gundam Frame suits developed near the end of the Calamity War 300 years ago.
It houses two Ahab Reactors which can be run in tandem to generate tremendous output.
The suit has wing-shaped thrusters on its back so it can fly within gravitational fields, but its structure is otherwise fairly standard.
The suit's only armaments are two Bael Swords, which have blades made of rare metal, and a Railgun built in to each thruster.

A Graze Ritter Ground Type for use by Commanders.
This is the personal suit of McGillis Fareed, Commander of the Outer Earth Orbit Regulatory Joint Fleet.
When he replaced Carta Issue as Commander after she was killed in action, he had the suit's color changed from green to blue.
The suit is equipped with two Knight Blades.
In addition, this suit's specifications are geared towards combat on the ground, so it has hover units mounted on its thighs.

A high-mobility prototype suit developed based on the Reginlaze.
This suit was developed as a prototype by the Arianrhod Fleet's development team, and it is named after its test pilot, Julieta Juris.
Because it is assumed that this suit will be assigned to a highly skilled pilot, the Reginlaze's good maneuverability has been sacrificed.
However, it is equipped with thrusters on various body parts intended to enhance its performance for highly mobile space combat.
In addition, the Graze Ein's combat data was applied during the development process, resulting in a larger frame and a more organic shape.
The suit's main armaments are the Julian Swords mounted on both arms, and they can be used either as conventional swords or as whips by extending their segmented blades.

A form of the Reginlaze Julia with specifications geared towards combat on the ground.
This form has been customized for ground use and was deployed in the final battle with Tekkadan on Mars.
Although this suit was originally developed with an emphasis on space combat, the suit's high versatility made it possible to replace some of its parts and adapt it for ground combat.
The suit's high maneuverability remains intact on the ground as well, and its leg units can be unfolded to achieve high mobility by hovering.
Because it uses a specially designed large blade, its right arm has been replaced with that of the regular Reginlaze, and its left arm is equipped with a new type of large shield that has a built-in anchor.

This is the Gundam Barbatos Lupus Rex after it was partially destroyed by Dainsleif attacks.
During the operation by Arianrhod to wipe out Tekkadan, the Barbatos Lupus Rex fought as fiercely as it could, but sustained heavy damage from Dainsleif attacks launched from the orbit of Mars.
Its pilot, Mikazuki Augus, was gravely injured.
However, Mikazuki refused to stop fighting, and instead bypassed the Alaya-Vijnana System's safety limiter.
The Barbatos, with its eyes glowing red, fought like the demon it was named after, slaughtering one Arianrhod suit after another.

This is the Gundam Barbatos Lupus Rex after it was partially destroyed by Dainsleif attacks.
During the operation by Arianrhod to wipe out Tekkadan, the Barbatos Lupus Rex fought as fiercely as it could, but sustained heavy damage from Dainsleif attacks launched from the orbit of Mars.
Its pilot, Mikazuki Augus, was gravely injured.
However, Mikazuki refused to stop fighting, and instead bypassed the Alaya-Vijnana System's safety limiter.
The Barbatos, with its eyes glowing red, fought like the demon it was named after, slaughtering one Arianrhod suit after another.

This is the Gundam Barbatos Lupus Rex after it was partially destroyed by Dainsleif attacks.
During the operation by Arianrhod to wipe out Tekkadan, the Barbatos Lupus Rex fought as fiercely as it could, but sustained heavy damage from Dainsleif attacks launched from the orbit of Mars.
Its pilot, Mikazuki Augus, was gravely injured.
However, Mikazuki refused to stop fighting, and instead bypassed the Alaya-Vijnana System's safety limiter.
The Barbatos, with its eyes glowing red, fought like the demon it was named after, slaughtering one Arianrhod suit after another.

Although the Barbatos sustained heavy damage in the battle at Edmonton, Tekkadan's mechanics repaired it as much as they could, after which it continued to be deployed on the front lines as the Barbatos Adapt.
Its swords were supplied by Teiwaz for the purpose of collecting data from the Barbatos.

A Gundam Frame Mobile Suit owned by House Warren.
This is one of the 72 Gundam Frame suits developed near the end of the Calamity War 300 years ago.
It houses two Ahab Reactors which can be run in tandem to generate tremendous output.
This unit went missing after the war, until it was discovered at the bottom of a massive lunar crater by House Warren, who treated it as a family heirloom in the generations to follow.
When House Warren came under accusation of illicit business dealings and was destroyed, it found its way onto the black market.
By the time Tanto Tempo bought it, it had been stripped of its armaments and armor, leaving only the base frame.
In the time following that purchase, it was given a restoration of sorts by cobbling together various Mobile Suit parts, making it asymmetrical in appearance.
It maintains center-of-mass balance with chest-mounted boost armor.
Its weapons include a foldable large close-combat weapon called the Demolition Knife and a 210mm Anti-Material Rifle that is part of the standard loadout of House Warren machines.

Gianmarco Salerno's personally customized Graze.
He acquired it through Teiwaz after Tekkadan captured it, then gave it his own personal modifications.
The head and leg armor has been improved, and it has a heat-resistant red cape on its back, distinguishing it considerably from a regular Graze.
The giant solid Bellows Axe it wields extends its reach by fanning out blade attachments, and delivers heavy impacts by virtue of its centrifugal force.

A customized Man Rodi used by the mercenary Hakuri siblings.
This is one of a pair of scuttled Man Rodis that the Hakuri siblings happened upon in the Debris Belt and brought in to overhaul.
It has the same performance level as a regular Man Rodi.
However, its head and cockpit block was destroyed and replaced with new units, so it does not have an on-board Alaya-Vijnana System.
This is the model used by the older brother, Sampo Hakuri.
Its left shoulder is painted blue, and it uses the same Chopper as the Gundam Gusion.

A customized Man Rodi used by the mercenary Hakuri siblings.
This is one of a pair of scuttled Man Rodis that the Hakuri siblings happened upon in the Debris Belt and brought in to overhaul.
It has the same performance level as a regular Man Rodi.
However, its head and cockpit block was destroyed and replaced with new units, so it does not have an on-board Alaya-Vijnana System.
This is the model used by the younger sister, Yuhana Hakuri.
Its left shoulder is painted yellow, and it uses the same Long Axe as the Gundam Gusion.

An improved version of the Gundam Astaroth.
Astaroth had been restored to 82% of its original state when Nanao Narorina stole it.
It had lost most of its armaments when it was discovered in a Jupiter Sphere black market.
Due to using other Mobile Suits' armaments for its restoration, its left and right halves are asymmetrical and lopsided.
Here, it has been given more upgrades in the form of a Bastard Chopper with an internal explosive Dainsleif, a Sub Knuckle on its left arm that doubles as a shield, and larger weapons across the board.
Due to all of the heavy weapons and heavy armor to suit Argi Mirage's combat specialization, it has been made into an unbalanced machine that only he can use.

An improved version of the Gundam Astaroth.
Astaroth had been restored to 82% of its original state when Nanao Narorina stole it.
It had lost most of its armaments when it was discovered in a Jupiter Sphere black market.
Due to using other Mobile Suits' armaments for its restoration, its left and right halves are asymmetrical and lopsided.
Here, it has been given more upgrades in the form of a Bastard Chopper with an internal explosive Dainsleif, a Sub Knuckle on its left arm that doubles as a shield, and larger weapons across the board.
Due to all of the heavy weapons and heavy armor to suit Argi Mirage's combat specialization, it has been made into an unbalanced machine that only he can use.

A custom Hyakuri owned by Gianmarco Salerno.
It has a large thruster affixed to its backpack, unlike the regular model, it is painted in a blue color scheme.
The Variable Blade Shields on its arms are the shoulder binders from Gundam Astaroth Origin, which this machine uses as effective close-combat weapons.
This was used in the attack when Argi Mirage and his group visited Avalanche Colony, Under 3.
It fought against the Gundam Astaroth.

A customized Rodi Frame owned by Gianmarco Salerno.
It has been independently customized for the purposes of escorting VIPs.
It has extremely thick armor and enhanced sensors in its head capable of two-way communication.
It uses the same Hammer Chopper and 90mm Submachine Gun as a standard Man Rodi's loadout.
This was used in the attack when Argi Mirage and his group visited Avalanche Colony, Under 3.
It fought against the Gjallarhorn Graze team that took the field to stop them.

A Gundam Frame Mobile Suit owned by Rosario Leone.
This is one of the 72 Gundam Frame suits developed near the end of the Calamity War 300 years ago.
It houses two Ahab Reactors which can be run in tandem to generate tremendous output.
Tanto Tempo discovered this machine in a scuttled colony in the course of demolition work.
It was taken away in secret by the man in charge of the site, Rosario.
It was an unarmored frame when first discovered, but Rosario's connection at Gjallarhorn, Ville Klaassen, provided armor from the Gundam Astaroth to restore it.
By outfitting it with a high-output backpack and boosters in its legs, it is now a machine with superior mobility and maneuverability.
In its first battle with Astaroth, it wielded a naginata- style melee weapon called a Glaive and a 210mm Anti- Material Rifle.
In its second battle, it put up a harder fight with a Mining Hammer and a shield.

Following the deployment of seventy-two mobile suits equipped with Gundam Frames, humanity's long and fierce war against the unmanned, large, mobile weapons known as Mobile Armors that had pushed it to the brink of extinction ends in victory.
Three centuries have since passed, and while many mobile suits remain lost to time, the Vual was discovered relatively recently among the remains of a long-forgotten colony that was abandoned during the Calamity War.
Rosario Leone, who was overseeing Tanto Tempo's bid to dismantle the abandoned colony at the time, smuggles the Vual from where it had lain dormant near the colony's power sector and claims it as his own.
Perhaps the groundwork to rebel against Daddy Ted had already been steadily underway around this time.

A large Mobile Suit of unknown origin employed by Nanao Narorina while accompanying Rosario Leone.
It is assumed that this machine utilizes a Graze Frame due to its distinguishing features.
However, it has many parts with unique forms, including its head, shoulders, and backpack.
Much about it remains a mystery, including its performance.
In addition to using the standard Graze armaments of a Battle Blade and 120mm Rifle, its left arm is also equipped with an anti-Mobile Suit Claw tailored for its use.

A customized Hyakuri designed to specialize in VIP assassination missions.
It is designed to be light in order to be more usable in places with gravity.
It emphasizes its mobility in hit- and-run tactics, which it uses for assassination missions.
It also has enhanced sensor units, and its head has a peculiar shape that looks as though it wears a hat.
In addition to its 130mm Assault Rifle, it keeps a Maniago Knife stored within its shoulder armor, a weapon with fine handling that makes it suited for assassinations.
It infiltrated into Avalanche 5 Colony in order to assassinate Ted Morugarton, and it engaged Gundam Astaroth in battle.

The original version of Gundam Astaroth.
This was how Gundam Astaroth looked while it was in the possession of House Warren.
They used unearthed technology from the Calamity War era to restore it to its former glory.
It was originally developed for wide area combat.
It is capable of unassisted long-range movements by deploying its shoulder-mounted Blade Shields into wings and transforming into a flight mode.
It can also maintain flight for long periods of time within atmospheric conditions.
It has a special energy transmission device.
It transmits Ahab particles through its γ Nanolaminate Sword and triggers a γ Nanolaminate Reaction along the blade, which enables it to break down the nanolaminate structure of enemies' armor.
However, Ahab particles are extremely difficult to handle properly, and the technology to do so has been lost in the present day.

A modified version of the Gundam Vual.
This is Vual after being retrieved and given a tuneup following its defeat by the Gundam Astaroth.
The Gundam Astaroth Origin's outer plating used previously has been removed, and it has been reassembled to the specifications of its pilot, Sampo Hakuri.
Besides the 210mm Anti-Material Rifle in its backpack, it has a foldable chainsaw in its arm.
It uses a combined version of a Sword and Chopper called a Halberd.
Its name was registered by Sampo's sister Yuhana without anyone else's approval.

A Gundam Frame Mobile Suit owned by House Zalmfort.
This is one of the 72 Gundam Frame suits developed near the end of the Calamity War 300 years ago.
It houses two Ahab Reactors which can be run in tandem to generate tremendous output.
This was the last out of all of the original Gundam Frames to be completed, and it used the combat data of every other frame fielded in live combat as a basis for making many major spec modifications.
By equipping various optional armaments, it is capable of handling a wide variety of combat situations.
Despite being completed only in the final stages of the war, it yielded extremely high results, making it a House Zalmfort symbol through the generations.
This is its "naked" status, in which it has light armor designed to allow it to fight under gravity and inside colonies.
It exhibits high mobility, adaptability, and martial combat.

This is the Gundam Dantalion with a T Booster attached to its back.
Its large backpack enables unassisted flight over long distances, and its mobility makes it suited for base strikes and hit-and-run tactics.
It can fold its backpack out over its arms to go into Half Cowl T Mode, giving it giant arms suited for close combat.
When it showed up as Argi Mirage's group headed for the Dort Colony Cluster, its color scheme was changed to black, the emblematic color of House Zalmfort.

Among the seventy-two Gundam Frames slated for production, the Dantalion was completed in the final stages of the project.
According to the surviving design data from the Calamity War, it underwent significant revisions multiple times during development.
The goal was to modify its equipment based on logs from other Gundam Frames that had been deployed in combat against Mobile Armors.
As a result, the Dantalion—designed to overcome any battlefield scenario on its own—was only completed near the end of the war.
Nevertheless, the Dantalion proved effective in battle against Mobile Armors. In recognition of its success, the family who owned the Dantalion was bestowed the name Zalmfort.
Within the postwar hierarchy of Gjallarhorn, ruled by the Seven Stars, they began to assert their influence.
Even now, three centuries after the war, the Dantalion remains a symbol of the Zalmfort family.

A Gundam Frame Mobile Suit owned by House Nadira.
This is one of the 72 Gundam Frame suits developed near the end of the Calamity War 300 years ago.
It houses two Ahab Reactors which can be run in tandem to generate tremendous output.
It wears a black armor over its upper body akin to a hooded cowl, made from a Nanolaminate Coat that boasts even greater strength than that of Nanolaminate Armor.
This gives it some of the best defenses out of any suit.
It uses a giant Battle Anchor as its main weapon for close combat.
The Battle Anchor lost one of its halves in battle back in the Calamity War, making it look more like a scythe.
This appearance leads to it giving off the impression of being a grim reaper.

A Valkyrja Frame Mobile Suit owned by House Jizin.
This machine is a House Jizin heirloom currently piloted by Jizil Jizin, commander of Ollerus, Gjallarhorn's internal control squadron.
It uses one of the Valkyrja Frames developed alongside the Gundam Frames.
There are surviving records of it taking part in battles in the latter stages of the Calamity War alongside House Nadira's Gundam Gremory.
Its main weapon is a dual-bladed melee Valkyrja Double Blade, capable of being split into single blades.
It was once equipped with a firing device for the taboo weapon Dainsleif, and the name remains in its Double Blade and left shoulder binder.
It primarily focuses on close combat to emphasize the Valkyrja Frame's signature high mobility.

A custom Graze belonging to Ollerus, Gjallarhorn's internal control squadron.
It is fitted with a Wire Caw on its left arm and fixed arms on its legs in order to allow for attitude control in various places.
It can also move quietly due to the improved shock absorbers in its legs.
It makes use of this stealth capability for hit-and-run tactics.
It uses the standard Graze 120mm Rifle and a Stun Stick designed to disable enemy joints and sensors.

A mobile suit that was hidden within the Radonitsa Colony's power sector.
Demner took over its maintenance after his grandfather passed and continued working on it in secret.
Wistario Afam piloted it when the colony was attacked by an unidentified mobile suit, waking it from a long sleep.

The prototype form of the Gundam Vidar.
Its pilot, Vidar, took the controls to perform test operations and adjustments on the Alaya-Vijnana Type E system with Gjallarhorn technical officer Yamazin Toka.
Yamazin continued to perform adjustments based on the data obtained from these tests, the culmination of which was the Gundam Vidar.

A prototype machine developed by the Ochs Earth Corporation.
This is one of the "GUND-ARM" mobile suits which adopted the innovative technology known as the GUND FORMAT.
It is equipped with a shield made up of swarm weapon systems called GUND-BITs.

A mobile suit developed by Grassley Defense Systems.
The latest technology is incorporated throughout this high-performance machine.
It is assigned to the Mobile Suit Development Council's Dominicus special forces team.

A mass production version of the Gundam Lfrith manufactured by the Ochs company.
Large launchers loaded with GUND-BITs are mounted on its back.

A mass-produced mobile suit produced by the Grassley company.
It is used by the Dominicus team as an escort for the Beguir-Beu.

A mobile suit that Suletta Mercury brings with her to school.
It is equipped with a shield made up of GUND-BITs, a next-generation remote-controlled swarm weapon system.

An external high mobility propulsion unit that the students of Earth House prepared for a duel with Elan Ceres of Peil House.
Built from a scrapped mobile suit flight unit manufactured by Mirasoul, a third party company, various adjustments and improvements have been made for the Aerial, such as tweaking the variable propulsion timing and installing a rack for a beam saber.
The thruster parts on each side are exposed, enabling the Aerial to shift to high maneuver mode for high-speed maneuvering in three dimensions.

A version of the Dilanza tuned for Guel's use.
As well as performance enhancements, it also has a magenta color scheme and splendid decorations.

A GUND-ARM developed by the Peil company.
Thanks to the use of Blast Boosters and the higher-order inertial control provided by the GUND Format, its flight performance is drastically improved.

The Grassley company's new model, piloted by Shaddiq and equipped with Antidote.
The manipulator of its right arm has been replaced with a tactical combination device known as a beam bracer.

A machine belonging to Earth House and piloted by Chuchu.
It is an older-generation model of the Demi Trainer customized by Nika.

A general-purpose mobile suit made by the Jeturk company, with outstanding power.
Thanks to a design policy of actively adding components, its style is heavyweight-class.

A mobile suit developed by the Peil company.
Equipped with a large propulsion unit, it has independent flight capabilities.

A mass-produced mobile suit made by the Grassley company.
It is equipped with a Lantern Shield which incorporates a lance and a handgun.

A mobile suit used for training at the school, manufactured by the Burion company.
It is used throughout the curriculum of the Asticassia School of Technology.

A version of the Demi series specialized for combat.
It has been formally adopted by the front management company as a security mobile suit.

A fifth-generation demonstration machine made by the Jeturk company.
It carries a new type of drone weapon which can operate autonomously thanks to its Decision Making Extension AI.

The successor to the Beguir-Beu, developed by the Grassley company.
Five units are assigned to Sabina and other pilots from Grassley House for the duel with Miorine.

A Gundam-type mobile suit based on the Gundam Lfrith Pre-Production Model, which was developed by the Ochs Earth Corporation before the Vanadis Incident.
It has been provided to the Dawn of Fold, an anti-Spacian organization active on Earth.

A Gundam type provided to the Dawn of Fold, an anti-Spacian organization active on Earth.
Like its sibling, the Lfrith Ur, it is a drastically updated machine based on the Gundam Lfrith Pre-Production Model.

The Aerial has been renovated by the Shin Sei Development Corporation.
As well as repairs to the damage it suffered in the duel with Grassley House, it has received updates and additions to its external armor and equipment.

A mobile suit made by the Jeturk company as the previous generation to the Dilanza.
Shaddiq provides these to the Dawn of Fold in order to cast suspicion on the Jeturk company during the attack on Plant Quetta.

A new concept model in the Demi series which is being planned and developed by the Burion company.
While its specs disregard profitability, it retains the distinctive design and superb maintainability of the series.
It can be equipped with a backpack that functions as a flight unit.

A next-generation concept model that was being developed by the Jeturk company as a successor to the Darilbalde, until its development was frozen.
It incorporates GUND Format technology provided by Prospera, and is equipped with GUND-Bits based on those of the Aerial.

A prototype machine developed by the Ochs Earth Corporation and the Vanadis Institute.
Because it was designed with absolutely no guarantee of preserving the pilot's life, it lost its competition with the Lfrith, and all records of its research and development were subsequently deleted.
During the Vanadis Incident, it was confiscated by the Space Assembly League and secretly kept in storage.

This GUND-ARM was designed in pursuit of the limits of performance rather than preserving the life of its pilot.
The legs are extended to adopt high maneuver mode, specializing as an attitude control unit in space.
The shell unit utilizes a different control method from the Lfrith type. When Suletta boarded it, it displayed not only a red color, but also unique rainbow colors as its score increased.

Its twin beam calivers can also be combined into a beam musket for even greater firepower.
It made an appearance in the Rumble Ring.

Equipped with additional weaponry: the Javelin Bracer, a large lance integrated with a retractable dagger, on its left arm; the Drill Javelin, a wired projectile; and the Strike Brace, which has a built-in beam machine gun.
It fought against Guel in Episode 20.

A new concept model in the Demi series which is being planned and developed by the Burion company.
While its specs disregard profitability, it retains the distinctive design and superb maintainability of the series.
It can be equipped with a backpack that functions as a flight unit.

A mobile suit-type GUND-Bit used by the Lfrith Ur and Lfrith Thorn.
This is an unmanned unit with the GUND Format incorporated into its cockpit.

Like the Lfrith Ur, this is a Gundam-type mobile suit used by the Dawn of Fold.
It has a rare body shape with long arms and short legs to handle advanced combat maneuvers.
The Phased Array Cannon on its back is the same as the Lfrith Ur's and, with a high Permet Score, functions as a Gundvölva operation transmitter.

A mobile suit-type GUND-Bit developed alongside Quiet Zero under Prospera's supervision.
It is designed to serve as a relay to amplify the data storm space, and with optional equipment, can also be operated as a mobile armor.

An important upgrade on the Gundam Lfrith Pre-Production Model, this Gundam-type mobile suit is used by the Dawn of Fold.
The back is equipped with a large Phased Array Cannon that expands in a fan shape and, by increasing the Permet Score, acts as a transmitter to operate the Gundvölva.

The damaged parts and weapons were upgraded after the second duel with Suletta.
The Isvara was changed to the quadruple Gussa Isvara, while the Ambika was replaced with the larger Daya Ambika. A new type of portable weapon called Composite Arms, which is comprised of a beam shot rifle and a folding beam katana, was also installed.

The Dilanza that was sortied in the final battle at Quiet Zero.
Its beam rifle is the standard issue for Jeturk company mobile suits, and is capable of multiple firing modes, including burst mode.
It can also be equipped with the Beam Partizan, a long spear that generates highly focused beam blades from its tip, enabling it to strike multiple targets across a wide area despite being designed for melee combat.

The newest model in the Great K series, developed by Grassley's CE Line.
The GUND Format jamming device, Antidote, also equipped on the Beguir-Beu, is installed on a large non-kinetic shield.
The backpack is also equipped with an auxiliary Antidote unit that slides vertically on startup.
It is equipped with a sniper rifle, as seen in Episode 20.

The newest model in the Great K series, developed by Grassley's CE Line.
The GUND Format jamming device, Antidote, also equipped on the Beguir-Beu, is installed on a large non-kinetic shield.
The backpack is also equipped with an auxiliary Antidote unit that slides vertically on startup.
It is equipped with a sniper rifle, as seen in Episode 20.

The newest model in the Great K series, developed by Grassley's CE Line.
The GUND Format jamming device, Antidote, also equipped on the Beguir-Beu, is installed on a large non-kinetic shield.
The backpack is also equipped with an auxiliary Antidote unit that slides vertically on startup.
It is equipped with a sniper rifle, as seen in Episode 20.

A one-of-a-kind, mysterious Gunpla that Haru happened upon.
It is an original not modeled after any previous designs.
Although it was defeated in battle by Schauer, it was repaired and modded, becoming the Beginning 30 Gundam.

A Gunpla used by Rina based on the HG Acguy.
Kenta and the manager of the hobby shop Axis helped mod it.
When Rina participated with Haru and Kenta in a Gunpla championship, it fought in a fierce battle with the Kampfer, a Gunpla operated by one of Schauer's underlings.

A Gunpla built by Sei.
Initially, Sei only completed the machine itself, but after numerous battles with Reiji, he completed the full package by adding weapons and a booster.
It is also equipped with wholly unique equipment such as a variable output gun and a laminated shield.

The third Perfect Gundam built by titular protagonist of the manga Plamo Kyoshiro.
Its design concept is a departure from the previous two models, which emphasized armor and firepower.
It features enhanced maneuverability and equipment, including built-in, miniaturized, and back-mounted equipment.
With its deep crimson color scheme and sharp mask, it is a fresh take on a familiar image.

One of the Seven Mushas , an elite team within the Gundam Forces.
Shogundam called upon the Seven Mushas when the Darkness Corps, an evil feudal army led by Dark Shogun, invaded.
The first to receive the title musha, he is the strongest musha of the seven, surpassing the others in every martial art.

The good half of the Musha Gundam Mk-III, he was split in two by Sieg Zeon.
Although he lost all memory apart from the name Gundam, he decided to follow his sense of justice and fight the Zeon family.
Since he was also able to pull out the Electrospear said to have been wielded by the hero, he set out against the Demon King as the Lacroan Hero.

The Knight Gundam armed with three legendary relics: the Armor of Mist, Flame Sword, and Shield of Strength.
When three stars came into alignment, the power of the relics increased tenfold, and the Knight Gundam became more powerful than even the Black Dragon.
Unfortunately, this power exacted a heavy toll on his body.

The feared Demon King who is said to have descended upon the Saddrac World in a flash of lightning.
Rarely making an appearance in person, he controls the monsters and knights that comprise the Zeon family from Zeon Black Castle, where he spreads his evil influence throughout the peaceful Lacroa Kingdom.
He attacks by concentrating magical energy into his Demon King Staff, which enables him to launch attacks from a distance.
He is actually the evil half of the Musha Gundam Mk-III. Split in half after his arrival from another world, he is an opposing force to the Knight Gundam.
The true nature of his identity has yet to be revealed, however...

The monstrous form of the Satan Gundam also known as "Fear Itself."
It possesses such overwhelming power that the Knight Gundam has no hope of defeating it without the three relics.

The monstrous form of the Satan Gundam also known as "Fear Itself."
It possesses such overwhelming power that the Knight Gundam has no hope of defeating it without the three relics.

A Lacroan warrior in the service of King Revil that accompanied the Knight Gundam on his quest to vanquish Satan Gundam.
A powerful fighter confident in his own strength, he possesses a spirit made of steel.

A knowledgeable Lacroan priest.
He quested with the Knight Gundam along with the Warrior Guncannon.
He is not only skilled in offensive magic but recovery magic as well, making him an invaluable party member.

A fairy that had been fighting the Zeon family in a Lacroan forest.
When he learned that the Knight Gundam and his party were on a quest to vanquish Satan Gundam, he joined them in order to lend his strength.
He is an expert archer, and his crossbow played a key role in the battle with a legendary giant.

A knight of the Zeon family in the service of Satan Gundam who stood in the way the Knight Gundam and his party on their way to Zeon Black Castle.
He used a glowing red sword as a weapon to attack members of the Union.
His right arm conceals an electromagnetic whip called a heat rod.

A knight of the Zeon family in the service of Satan Gundam.
Prideful and ruthless, there is no low he will not stoop to in order to win.
He usually wields a rapier in battle, but when backed into a corner, he will also attack with missiles installed in his shield.

A warrior of the Zeon family in the service of Satan Gundam who is equipped with an axe and shield.
Having witnessed Satan Gundam fuse with a dragon, he knows the secret of the Black Dragon.

A knight of the Zeon family in the service of Satan Gundam.
His abilities are second only to his retainer, Sazabi, and he stood in the way of the Knight Gundam and his party when they infiltrated Zeon Black Castle.
He is also strong enough to effortlessly swing an oversized sword.

A warrior of the Zeon family who effortlessly swings a long axe.
A powerful fighter confident in his own strength, he uses brute force to bring his axe down in a special move called the Power Slash.
He ambushed the Knight Gundam and his party on their way to Zeon Black Castle.

A knight of the Zeon family who stood in the way of the Knight Gundam and his party in Zeon Black Castle.
He specializes in magical sword attacks that combine magic with swordsmanship and proved a worthy foe for the Knight Gundam's party.

A red knight who serves as a retainer to Satan Gundam.
Excelling in both swordsmanship and magic, he is the most powerful knight under the Demon King, and he stood in the way of the Knight Gundam and his party when they arrived to vanquish Satan Gundam.

A mysterious Gundam that suddenly appeared before a shiftless Gundam team on the Magellan continent following their defeat at the hands of the Zatarion regular army's Bloody Zaku.
He proceeded to whip the Gundam team into shape, forming an elite team called G-ARMS.

He is a member of "Dragon's Watch," a vigilante group in the Shu Area, which is not targeted by Etiolation Trinity MS too often.
After meeting Guan Yu Nu Gundam and Zhang Fei God Gundam, he makes an important decision: To save the world.
He eventually starts to gain comrades who are drawn to his strong passion.

An odd-looking mobile suit with the head of a Psycho Gundam and a die-shaped body.
Posing a sharp contrast to its comical exterior, the suit's fearsome efficiency in battle has earned it the moniker of "Death Cube."

A mobile suit based on the Zeta Gundam III with upgraded firepower.
To compensate for the increase in weight added by its weapons, its mobility was also improved by installing supplemental thrusters on both its legs and a large tail stabilizer that expands outward from each side.
Despite the fact that Gray Wolf is believed to have been its pilot and it is known as the Gray Zeta, its color is yellow due to the Anti-Beam Coating applied across its entire surface.

A mobile suit based on the Zeta Gundam III C Type that was upgraded for Newtypes.
It is equipped with a system known as the Psycho Neutralizer that enables its pilots to operate it psychically, and the fins installed throughout it are said to function as sensors for collecting external data.

A mysterious Mobile Suit bearing the name Gundam.
Several of these were discovered in the hangar of a derelict factory, and since they were fully operational, they were sent straight to various teams.
In addition to standard armaments, its chest has a Diffuse Beam Cannon.
For defense, it has multilayered arm plating that employs a Lunar Titanium alloy and shock-absorbent material, which allows its arms to act as shields.
It is also capable of atmospheric flight, allowing it to be fielded in many places for a wide range of needs.
Since there was no data left by the time these suits were discovered, nothing is known about how or when they were developed.

A mobile suit developed in secret by government forces.
It was stolen from a military testing facility during compatibility tests with the pilot Maria Nichols, who was the test subject for a super sense-enhancing virus.
This machine's fighting abilities are extraordinarily high.
Its body is covered with mega particle cannons, and both arms sport large beam swords.
Excelling in everything from melee combat to long-range attacks, it boasts well-rounded performance in any situation.
After being stolen from the Earth government forces, it was taken to a military base on the moon that was involved in the planetary catastrophe known as the "Flames of Judgment."
Here it was used in an attempt to create an ideal world government led by a new, superior form of humanity.

An early Federation Forces prototype mobile suit created by remodeling captured Zaku II units.
Though some parts are visibly the same as those of the Zaku II, Federation budget problems and technological limitations resulted in the final product leaving much to be desired.
This was originally intended to be more of a testbed for MS data collection than a combat-ready machine.
But as such, it played a foundational role in Federation mobile suit development.

A variation of the Bigro, a mass-produced mobile armor developed by the Principality of Zeon for high-speed space combat.
Its size has been downscaled, its generator output increased, and thruster-equipped legs added for better mobility.
Its armor and armaments have also been improved, further enhancing its inherent strengths as an MA.
High mobility and show-stopping firepower make this a very capable machine.

A prototype version of the Gundam Mk-II, which was developed as the flagship mobile suit of the Titans.
It was designed completely without regard for cost.
It is plagued by many issues with its control system and maintenance, and its operating time is extremely short.
That said, its performance surpasses that of the later Gundam Mk-II.
Its prototype beam sabers and prototype beam rifle make it a well-balanced combat unit.

A crimson Gundam developed for the personal use of Char Aznable, also known as Casval Rem Deikun.
Although its equipment is the same as that of its Federation counterpart, this unit boasts performance that is better overall than that of the Gundam.
This mobile suit can comfortably be considered a super-high performance machine, and one only an ace can properly pilot.
As a mobile suit developed under the concept of a force led by Char that comprises the Newtypes of both Zeon and the Federation, this illusory mobile suit appeared during a fictitious conflict.

Appeared in Mobile Suit Gundam: Gihren's Greed.
The Gyan's final variant, its designation, "Krieger," means "warrior."

Prepared expressly for the use of ace pilots during the final days of the One Year War, the GM Striker, along with the GM Sniper II, boasts the finest specifications of the RGM series.
As evidenced by its Gundam head, the "Striker Custom" is an ace-use mobile suit whose design drew from Gundam units.

A Federation Forces mobile suit developed at the end of the One Year War, based on the high-performance GM variation known as the GM Sniper Custom.
It was created to protect friendly forces, fleets, and military facilities.
The defense of strategic bases was essential for the success of large-scale operations, and this machine was developed for the difficult mission of escorting high-priority assets to the specified mission coordinates.
Compared to the base machine, this type is highly mobile and wields new armament for close combat.
Its massive Guardian Shield uses an ingenious five-layer construction, further increasing its melee combat abilities.
Because it was hard to handle, it was used mainly by veteran pilots.
There were many confirmed post-war sightings of this type.

A mobile suit that appeared in Mobile Suit Gundam: Extreme Vs.
In a distant future in which the Earth was destroyed, it was built to serve as an arbitrator of a "perfect and ideal universe."

A Mobile Suit that seems to have been made with ancient, lost technology, as well as the best technology the present has to offer.
Origin undetermined.
It performs in a manner befitting a suit with the Gundam name.
It has a wide range of weapon options, such as a Beam Saber and Beam Rifle, linked Mega Beam Cannons on its binders, and Feather Funnels that enable attacks at all ranges.
It uses Nanoskin Armor that self-repairs damage using nanomachines.
However, at the time of discovery, all that was left was the vestiges of those nanomachines.
They had lost all function and have not been recovered to this day.
It lacks any maintenance hatches on its surface, as if the Nanoskin armor were designed to also repair internal damages.
It also is equipped with the ability to flight within the atmosphere, but requires no propulsion assistance and can fly for extended periods of time using special technology unknown to the modern world.

Phoenix Gundam, the very embodiment of power unleashed.
Its already high performance has been boosted even further.
With its Nanoskin Armor operating normally to perform self-repairs on damaged areas, it is now capable of unassisted combat for long periods of time.
It can also use the Burning Fire attack, in which it transforms into Bird Mode, coating itself with liquid metal that spouts from the base of its binders, and charges at the target in a ball of energy.
It has regained all of its originally high-offensive capabilities.
The suit’s name “Phoenix” comes from its fiery figure during Burning Fire, where the Nanoskin’s phoenix-like regenerative properties allow it to be covered in molten metal.

Phoenix Gundam, the very embodiment of power unleashed.
Its already high performance has been boosted even further.
With its Nanoskin Armor operating normally to perform self-repairs on damaged areas, it is now capable of unassisted combat for long periods of time.
It can also use the Burning Fire attack, in which it transforms into Bird Mode, coating itself with liquid metal that spouts from the base of its binders, and charges at the target in a ball of energy.
It has regained all of its originally high-offensive capabilities.
The suit’s name “Phoenix” comes from its fiery figure during Burning Fire, where the Nanoskin’s phoenix-like regenerative properties allow it to be covered in molten metal.

A Mobile Suit developed based on analyzing the Phoenix Gundam's data to create the perfect mass production model.
Since the black box technology in the Phoenix Gundam was impossible to understand, it does not have access to Nanoskin Armor or Feather Funnels, and its performance is considerably lower than that of the original.
However, it is still relatively easy and cheap to produce.
With its heavy beam weaponry like the Mega Beam Cannon and atmospheric flight, it still makes for a fine mass production unit that is fielded with many Mobile Suit teams and is a common sight in the field.
Since it is made by a different development team than the original Phoenix Gundam, it has been given a different model number line to distinguish it.

An unknown airframe that has a design resembling the Phoenix Gundam.
It has abilities that greatly exceed those shown by normal MS, and it is equipped with the power to fly in the atmosphere.
It also has the ability to battle in the sky.
The battling side of the model evokes the phoenix as well, and it is equipped with a beam saber and feather funnels just like the other Gundam.
However, the power of its Cross Mega Beam Cannon and Burning Flare are specially equipped to conceal its clearly overwhelming power compared to the Phoenix.
This has led some to believe it is the bases for the design of the Phoenix Gundam, but their relation remains unclear.
Moreover, the “Halphas” it is named after was thought to be one of the 72 demons in the Ars Goetia, an earl of Hell in Roman demonology who was referred to as “The Black Phoenix Devil that Brings War.”

A gigantic weapon of destruction shrouded in complete mystery.
When was it built, and where, and for what purpose? Nobody knows.
It has the form of a Haro, the mascot robot of the Gundam universe, but its cute form belies its extremely high performance.
It has a number of special weapons such as the Haro Drill, Haro Bubble, and Haro Bits, giving it overwhelming power on the field and earning it the nickname "Round Devil."
But nobody knows what it really is...

A gigantic weapon of destruction shrouded in complete mystery, just like the Haro.
 Since it is even more powerful than the Haro, already dreaded as the Round Devil, it has reached a level of strength that no one can ever hope to achieve, making it the ultimate machine and deserving of the title of "ultimate Weapon of Destruction."
 But still, nobody knows what it really is...

A mass-produced version of the prototype heavy mobile armor Big-Zam that was developed by the Principality of Zeon for anti-fortress combat.
This machine was specifically designed to attack the Federation Forces headquarters at Jaburo.
In order to address the Big-Zam's short running time and poor production characteristics, the anti-beam I-Field generator was eliminated, and its armor surface was given an anti-beam coating instead.
This reduced its defenses against beam attacks, but it still retained a large mega particle cannon powerful enough to destroy a warship with a single blast.
By the time this machine was finally ready for mass production, the Principality of Zeon's defeat was already decided, and the war ended before production could begin.

Appeared in the PlayStation game SD Gundam G Generation F.
A prototype unit developed on Axis as part of an effort to create a mobile suit version of the Elmeth.
Owing to the limitations of contemporary technology, miniaturizing the Psycommu System proved difficult, and the mobile suit is enormous as a result.

Appeared in the WonderSwan game SD Gundam G Generation: Gather Beat.
This mobile suit was developed to be able to imitate the all-range attacks that the Principality units were capable of.
As Psycommu had yet to be discovered, it was piloted by an unmanned ball via laser transmissions.

A heavy mobile suit for Newtypes designed as a successor to the Titans prototype MS The-O, and created aboard the resource transportation ship Jupitris.
As with The-O, the thrusters all over its body can adapt to any vector, giving it surprising mobility and acceleration despite its bulk.
It also has sub-manipulator systems that function as hidden arms.
But its most distinctive feature is the ability to perform all-range attacks, using Newtype psycho-waves to remotely control its funnels via a psycommu device.
This machine was expected to have performance worthy of a successor to The-O, but development was halted by the death of its designer Paptimus Scirocco at the end of the Gryps Conflict, and ultimately it was never completed.
Because Scirocco dreamed that a Newtype woman would rule the world, it is said that this machine was designed for such a ruler.

Based on the Principality of Zeon general-purpose prototype mobile suit Gyan, this machine was designed by Axis to further advance the Gyan's design emphasis on melee combat.
It performance is greatly increased from the base machine, and the replacement of the Gyan's distinctive beam saber with a large beam sword makes it even better at melee combat.
This would have been a worthy next-generation mass production MS for Axis, but because it specialized in close combat, its versatility was compromised.
Though its performance surpassed that of the Galluss J and Zssa, it never entered mass production.
However, the data collected from this machine was later used in the development of the R-Jarja.

A custom mobile suit deployed by the Federation Forces.
Created as a "What if...?" unit for a video game, it was modified from the Ez8 piloted by Shiro Amada for space with an emphasis on maneuverability.
Since the original model was designed for ground combat, extensive modifications were made to every aspect of its design in order to adapt it for space combat, giving the custom model a markedly different appearance.
For example, some armor was removed to reduce its weight and a movable vernier unit was installed on its back.

A custom mobile suit deployed by the Federation Forces.
Created as a "What if...?" unit for a video game, it was modified from the Ez8 piloted by Shiro Amada for space with an emphasis on firepower.
It is equipped with two large-caliber beam cannons on its back repurposed from the main batteries of a Salamis-class cruiser, boasting firepower comparable to a warship.

A mobile suit planned for deployment by Zeon forces.
A variant of the Rick Dom, its maneuverability and melee combat capabilities are an upgrade over its predecessor intended to compete with the Gelgoog, a next-generation primary mobile suit replacing the Dom series.
Since it was developed in conjunction with the Dom Tropen, it shares many of its parts with that ground combat mobile suit, giving it a similar appearance.

A mobile suit planned for deployment by Zeon forces.
A variant of the Rick Dom, its maneuverability and ranged combat capabilities are an upgrade over its predecessor intended to compete with the Gelgoog, a next-generation primary mobile suit replacing the Dom series.
Its legs have been removed and replaced with high-mobility thrusters, granting it maneuverability in space comparable to a mobile armor.

A prototype anti-MD (Mobile Doll) Mobile Suit made by the OZ.
It is a sibling unit to Gundam Epyon, with an electronic warfare system called the anti-MD System designed to be a counter to Mobile Dolls.
It sends a computer virus to every Mobile Doll in a 100 kilometers radius that causes them to malfunction, a measure intended to give Epyon time to destroy their manned command unit.
Due to the added weight of the antenna unit affixed, it is not a particularly maneuverable suit, but its high-output verniers give it considerable thrust and flight, which gives it the mobility to keep up with the Epyon and hold its own in combat.
After Treize Khushrenada was removed from command in OZ, his faction of supporters developed this suit.
However, it was never actually fielded in live combat.
Some say it and its developers were consigned by the opposing Dermail faction to the trash heap of history.
This is a unit original to the G Generation series.

Newtypes in both the Federation Forces and the Space Revolutionary Army had become a crucial factor in the latter half of the 7th Space War, and the psychological impact they had on ordinary soldiers in particular was so severe that it could determine the outcome of battle.
As a result, eliminating enemy Newtypes could have greater ramifications than actual military gains.
Recognizing this, the Federation Forces developed a Newtype-use mobile suit for eliminating Newtypes.
In order to achieve its special mission, no expense was spared in the pursuit of performance.
It comes equipped with a set of heat wires to defend against bit weapons, claw arms for close combat, and a sonic smash cannon for artillery combat, boasting formidable capabilities in any combat situation.

Newtypes in both the Federation Forces and the Space Revolutionary Army had become a crucial factor in the latter half of the 7th Space War, and the psychological impact they had on ordinary soldiers in particular was so severe that it could determine the outcome of battle.
As a result, eliminating enemy Newtypes could have greater ramifications than actual military gains.
Recognizing this, the Federation Forces developed a Newtype-use mobile suit for eliminating Newtypes.
In order to achieve its special mission, no expense was spared in the pursuit of performance.
It comes equipped with a set of heat wires to defend against bit weapons, claw arms for close combat, and a sonic smash cannon for artillery combat, boasting formidable capabilities in any combat situation.

Newtypes in both the Federation Forces and the Space Revolutionary Army had become a crucial factor in the latter half of the 7th Space War, and the psychological impact they had on ordinary soldiers in particular was so severe that it could determine the outcome of battle.
As a result, eliminating enemy Newtypes could have greater ramifications than actual military gains.
Recognizing this, the Federation Forces developed a Newtype-use mobile suit for eliminating Newtypes.
In order to achieve its special mission, no expense was spared in the pursuit of performance.
It comes equipped with a set of heat wires to defend against bit weapons, claw arms for close combat, and a sonic smash cannon for artillery combat, boasting formidable capabilities in any combat situation.

Developed by Axis as a successor to the massive space attack mobile armor Neue Ziel, this machine is equipped with a psycommu system and painted in the red personal colors of Char Aznable, the "Red Comet."
Like the Neue Ziel, it has high-output thrusters and powerful powerful beam weapons all over its body, and is equipped with I-Field generators that protect it from beam attacks.
Its fighting strength remains overwhelming, but its greatest feature is the ability to perform all-range attacks, using Newtype psycho-waves to remotely control its funnels via a psycommu system.
Thus, while the Neue Ziel can be used even by Oldtypes, this version was completed as a Newtype machine.
Unfortunately, when development of this machine started at the beginning of the Gryps Conflict, Char had already left Axis to join the AEUG.
Together with its immense production cost, this rendered the project pointless.

A Pegasus-class assault landing ship built under the Federation's Operation V mobile suit development plan.
It can enter the atmosphere, and is equipped with a Minovsky craft system that allows for planetary flight.
It is an all-purpose ship that boasts great functionallity.
The front of the ship is equipped with MS catapults, and as an MS mothership, it incorporates a number of new technologies.
During the One Year War, it served as a mothership for the Gundam, Guntank,  and Guncannon developed by Operation V.

A Principality of Zeon space cruiser that was the first warship designed with mobile suit operation in mind.
Designed to be a versatile ship from the start, it was as adept at anti-ship combat, reconnaissance, and base defense as it was at carrying MS.
In addition to a Komusai atmospheric entry pod in its bow, it could also carry landing capsules between its engines.
Several variations of this ship were produced, and it remained one of the mainline units of the Principality of Zeon's space fleet throughout the One Year War.

The mother ship of the Federation-affiliated Moore Brotherhood.
A Columbus-class supply ship with a structure resembling two conjoined vessels, it is equipped with a total of six large mega particle cannons, with one on each of its top decks, and two on each of its side decks.

Though technically the seventh vessel of the Pegasus class, this Federation Forces assault landing ship featured improvements in terms of appearance and performance.
It could easily be considered an entirely new model.
Built as part of the Gundam Development Project, it incorporated many cutting-edge technologies related to mobile suit operation.
Like other Pegasus-class ships, its Minovsky craft system enabled atmospheric flight, but it was also the first Federation warship equipped with a laser propulsion system.
The addition of mega particle cannons as both main and secondary armaments also served to increase its firepower.

An Anti-Earth Union Group (AEUG) mobile cruiser inspired by the Federation's Pegasus class.
It prioritized mobile suit operations.
For that reason, it lacks the firepower of other warships, with its offense and defense handled by onboard MS.
The residential blocks on its flanks can rotate while in flight to generate artificial gravity, providing better livability compared to other ships of its time.
The addition of ballutes enables atmospheric entry and its Minovsky craft system enables atmospheric flight.
AEUG leader Blex Forer ordered this craft to be built in absolute secrecy.
It was initially called White Base II until Melanie Hue Carbine (the chairman of Anaheim Electronics and the AEUG's biggest investor) renamed it Argama.
After serving as the AEUG fleet's flagship during the Gryps Conflict, it went on to serve in the First Neo Zeon War.

A large Titans battleship built to be a symbol and a feat of strength of the organization.
It was armed with many powerful mega particle cannons and its bombardment capabilities were unrivaled at the time.
Though it was a high-performance battleship, it also had to function as a mobile suit mothership, hence the immense size.
After it was commissioned, it was temporarily entrusted to Jupitris commander Paptimus Scirocco, but it later returned to Titans command and served as their flagship.

A Federation Forces Garuda-class airlifter.
Six of these were assigned to Earth's air defenses as an emergency preparedness system for mobile suit deployment.
As one of the aerial carriers built under this "Garuda Initiative," the Garuda class was required to maintain position over the battlefield to deploy and command troops.
Thus it was given the necessary command facilities, including a bridge in the bow that served as a command center, and a range of other equipment and facilities for communications, search, and control.
With 20 thermonuclear jet engines installed in its wings, this aircraft was able to generate a tremendous amount of thrust.
Because it was designed to be a stratospheric platform, it could cruise without physical constraints and circumnavigate the Earth without resupply or landing.
This aircraft was captured by Karaba during the escape from Jaburo, and was subsequently used to support the AEUG as Karaba's movable base.

A Federation Forces space cruiser built after the One Year War to serve as the flagship of the Titans.
It could carry and support many mobile suits, and had MS catapults on the top and bottom of its bow.
It exhibited excellent MS support capabilities compared to other vessels of its time, and was armed with mega particle cannons and anti-air machine guns that allowed it to perform effectively as a warship.
However, only a few were produced, and it was never able to gain traction as a mainline vessel of the Federation fleet.

An Anti Earth Union Group (AEUG) space battleship.
Redesigned with fleet-scale mobile suit operation in mind, it was the second vessel of the Irish class.
Because MS capabilities were built into the design, it was well-equipped in this regard.
It also fixed the firepower issues that plagued the Argama with added turrets that allowed it to perform well as a battleship.
Because the bridge design emulated that of the Argama, the two ships looked similar, but this ship lacked the structural complications of a variable body.
This ship was built at Granada and paid for with funding from civilian sponsors.
Together with the Argama, it formed the core of the AEUG space fleet, and it fought in battle against the Titans fleet.

An Anti-Earth Union Group (AEUG) mobile battleship.
The AEUG, receiving no support from the Federation Forces, constructed this new mobile suit carrier as a replacement for the mobile cruiser Argama in order to increase its fighting strength.
This vessel was designed to be a successor to the Argama-class assault cruiser and the Pegasus-class assault landing ship.
It was designed for mobile suit operation, with three catapult decks and a factory block underneath the central deck for repair and remodeling.
Another key feature is the ship's overwhelmingly powerful hyper mega particle cannon.
Its regular armament is also an improvement over the base vessel, giving it superior performance as a warship.
Thanks to its enhanced computer support capabilities, it can be operated by a very small crew.
A drum-shaped residential block and solar cells in the main wings provide further improvements.
This ship became the AEUG flagship after the Argama was damaged during the First Neo Zeon War.
Its name means "close to the Argama."

A super-heavy Neo Zeon battleship based on the design concepts of the Gwazine class used by the former Principality of Zeon.
It was built as the flagship of the Neo Zeon fleet and placed under the command of Haman Karn.
Because it was designed with Earth landings in mind, it can be equipped with a ballute for atmospheric entry, and the hull can change shape to fit within the ballute's effective radius.
Designed for all-round performance, this vessel also has excellent firepower and mobile suit support capabilities,  giving it the ability to handle nearly any kind of mission.

Designed as a mobile suit carrier based on lessons learned since the One Year War, this Federation Forces battleship was deployed in the early U.C.0090s.
The flagship of the Federation Forces's Londo Bell, it was the first vessel of the Cailum class, which was a match for the other vessels of the Universal Century.
It could carry mobile suits and perform blitzkrieg bombardment.
Combined with the adoption of heat sinks and other technological innovations, this resulted in a finished vessel that was very well balanced overall.
Because livability was not a consideration in the design, it does not possess a gravity block.
With only the bare minimum of facilities necessary for military personnel, it has almost no superfluous space.
The ship has two bridges, one for navigation and one for combat, and the central captain's seat can travel up and down between them.
The main difference between the two bridges is that there is a navigation screen in the rear of the navigation bridge, and a tactics screen on the forward bulkhead in the combat bridge, as well as a central 3D screen.
This bridge can also function as an emergency escape pod.
Many ships of this class were constructed, and they remained in active service 60 years later, at the time of the war with the Zanscare Empire in U.C.0153.

The space battleship that led the Neo Zeon fleet.
It served as the command ship of Char Aznable, supreme commander of the revived Neo Zeon forces.
This ship followed the design of the Gwazine class and Gwadan class used by the Principality of Zeon.
Its basic design was inspired by the Principality of Zeon.
Using an existing design helped to reduce construction time, the end result was better than other newly designed vessel, with reliable performance for a minimal investment.
It had two mobile suit catapults on the top and bottom of its bow, outstanding MS capacity and support features, and plenty of firepower as well.

An assault landing ship assigned to the Federation Forces' Londo Bell task force.
This is a heavily remodeled version of a space battleship built during the First Neo Zeon War.
With poor handling and an inability to integrate into fleet formations it mainly operated on its own.
This vessel was designed to be a successor to the Argama-class assault cruiser and the Pegasus-class assault landing ship.
It was designed for mobile suit operation, and the three catapult decks on the bow are one of the reasons the Neo Zeon remnants call it the ""Mock Trojan Horse.""
As part of a large-scale modernization, its mobile suit capacity was increased and the MS hangar block was enlarged.
The engine blocks were also updated and refitted to use standard parts from the Cailum and Clop class.
It was further enhanced for solo operations with upgraded weaponry.
It boasted excellent firepower and combat performance for a warship of this type.
In U.C. 0096, this vessel was involved in the upheaval that took place around Laplace's Box, and fought an intense battle against the Neo Zeon remnants known as the Sleeves.

Designed as a mobile suit carrier based on lessons learned since the One Year War, this Federation Forces battleship was deployed in the early U.C. 0090s.
The flagship of the Federation Forces's Londo Bell, it was the first vessel of the Cailum class, which was a match for the other vessels of the Universal Century.
It could carry mobile suits and perform blitzkrieg bombardment.
Combined with the adoption of heat sinks and other technological innovations, this resulted in a finished vessel that was very well balanced overall.
Because livability was not a consideration in the design, it does not possess a gravity block.
With only the bare minimum of facilities necessary for military personnel, it has almost no superfluous space.
The ship has two bridges, one for navigation and one for combat, and the central captain's seat can travel up and down between them. 
The main difference between the two bridges is that there is a navigation screen in the rear of the navigation bridge, and a tactics screen on the forward bulkhead in the combat bridge, as well as a central 3D screen. 
This bridge can also function as an emergency escape pod.
In U.C. 0096, this vessel descended to Earth for functional testing of a new Minovsky craft system that had been installed for use in terrestrial operations.

A space battleship that served as the Neo Zeon flagship during the Second Neo Zeon War.
Afterwards, it was remodeled to become a symbol of the Neo Zeon remnant group known as the Sleeves.
Its basic design was inspired by the Principality of Zeon.
Using an existing design helped to reduce construction time, the end result was better than other newly designed vessel, with reliable performance for a minimal investment.
It had two mobile suit catapults on the top and bottom of its bow, outstanding MS capacity and support features, and plenty of firepower as well.
This ship always possessed flagship-level performance. 
By U.C. 0096, it had still managed to avoid major changes, and had undergone only minor repairs and small adjustments.

Disguised as a civilian transport ship, this space freighter belonged to the Neo Zeon remnants known as the Sleeves, who visited the Industrial 7 colony on a special mission.
 It can carry up to four mobile suits, and also has the ability to enter the atmosphere and fly under gravity.
Originally used to make transport runs between Earth and space, it is equipped with rockets for clearing space debris, but is essentially unarmed and has virtually no combat capabilities.
This ship served as the mothership of the Sleeves' talented Garencieres Team, and fought in the conflict surrounding Laplace's Box.

A Vulture ship captained by Jamil Neate.
An enhanced version of a former United Nations Earth warship, it is classified—compared to other land battleships—as a medium-sized vessel.
It has the structure of a catamaran, with a flight deck and warehouse atop its right-hand hull, and a barracks located inside its left hull.

A former Federation Space Force battleship that was in the hands of an anti-Federation organization.
Carris Nautilus, acting as the commander of a rebel group, entrusted it to Jamil and his crew.
In addition to a Gundam, it carries six mobile suits and a G-Falcon among its payload.
It is equipped with various weaponry, including a beam deflector in the bow, and can produce sufficient thrust to escape the atmosphere with the aid of a large booster.

A customized Archangel-class mobile assault ship used by the Earth Alliance's Atlantic Federation.
A joint development between the Alliance's Atlantic Federation and Morgenroete, Inc.
of the Orbs union, this ship is the Alliance's first-ever Mobile Suit carrier.
Developed with the aim to take the GAT-X series Mobile Suits into the fight against ZAFT's own, it comes equipped with dedicated suit facilities including a dock and two linear catapults.
Its prized weaponry includes Positron Cannon "Lohengrin" and an abundance of artillery and Missile Launchers for enhanced anti-Mobile Suit operations.
The Archangel comes equipped with a wealth of cutting-edge technology, including beam-scattering Laminated Armor and ablative gel that helps to keep it cool when entering the Earth's atmosphere.
Heavy automation also helps keep the ship operational even with a skeleton crew.
Constructed at the neutral Heliopolis colony, it was ambushed by Le Creuset's ZAFT forces immediately after its completion.
Now piloted by the few remaining original crew members and students with military aspirations, the Archangel worked to repel ZAFT's repeated attack.

A ZAFT nuclear-capable dedicated Mobile Suit space carrier.
A new kind of experimental ship designed to carry the nuclear-powered ZGMF-X series Mobile Suits.
Boasting high speeds via 10 nuclear thrusters, it leaves even the fastest of Nazca-class ships in the proverbial dust.
It addition to its Large Beam Cannons, the two METEOR cannons installed in its bow make it pack a serious punch should the need arise.
Shortly after its completion, the ship was captured by a PLANT faction under Lacus Clyne's control, becoming the core of its fleet.

A customized Archangel-class mobile assault ship used by the Earth Alliance's Atlantic Federation.
This ship is the second version of its high performance predecessor, the Archangel.
Not much has changed compared to the Archangel aside from the appearance of its bridge sensors and the layout of its Mobile Suit maintenance deck.
Shortly after its deployment to the Alliance Lunar Base, it was assigned three second-generation GAT-X Mobile Suits and ordered to pursue the Archangel which had defected from the Alliance.

A ZAFT planetary assault landing vessel.
This speedy battleship was developed to carry the new Second Stage Series Mobile Suits. 
Equipment including dedicated catapults as well as a Deuterion Beam Energy Transfer System used to recharge Second Series suits makes it a highly effective support ship. 
It also packs an impressively high amount of firepower, with 2-Barrel Beam Cannons ""Tristan,"" 42cm Cannons ""Isolde,"" as well as a high-output Positron Cannon ""Tannhauser.""
Though anchored at ZAFT's Armory One in anticipation of its launch, it was immediately put into service inhopes of rescuing a set of stolen Gundams.

A Celestial Being space transport vessel.
Designed to carry Celestial Being's Gundam Mobile Suits, the crew affectionately refers to it as the Ptolemy.
With the ability to house up to four Mobile Suits in its containers, it can use the linear catapult on its nose to perform launches during takeoff.
Due to using the Gundam's GN Drive as a power source, its operational time becomes severely limited when all Gundams have left the ship.
Built to provide Gundams with logistical support at most, its weaponry was not the most impressive, but replacing its storage containers with assault containers has made it much more formidable.
Serving as a Gundam support base after the start of Celestial Being's armed intervention, it managed to survive countless fights with the UN including the final decisive battle.

A warship commissioned by the Federation in A.G.115.
With two large decks and each one equipped with a catapult, its structure was designed with mobile suit deployment in mind.
Shortly after its commission, it was enhanced by the Madorna Workshop using data provided by the AGE System, and equipped with a photon blaster cannon and the means to transform into "assault landing mode."

A Tekkadan armored assault ship.
The ship is powered by the same Ahab Reactor used in the Calamity War.
Formerly known as the Will-O'-the-Wisp, it was owned by CGS President Maruba Arkay.
Once seized by Tekkadan, it was rechristened the Isaribi and became the group's flagship.
Though equipped with multiple weapons including 2-Barrel Cannons and Anti-Air Cannons, its Nanolaminate Armor's damage resistance makes it especially suited to be a well-armored spear point in a charge.
It's equipped with Mobile Suit launchers in its belly, allowing it to serve as a Mobile Suit carrier as well as use the Alaya-Vijnana System for enhanced control.

An armed transport vessel owned by Tekkadan.
Given a new task in keeping with Tekkadan's expansion, this ship has the ability to make the trip from Mars to Earth in around three weeks.
It is composed of a main armored assault core with container blocks on each side. These containers are equipped with hangars with a capacity for numerous Mobile Suits. 
While the core of the ship is loaded with Anti-Air Cannons and Missiles, the addition of the containers meant sacrificing the main guns on its flanks.

Mainly used as a training mothership for soldiers with little combat experience, this ship is extremely balanced in terms of weaponry, mobility, and mobile suit capacity.
It lacks the performance of a standard warship, so many soldiers are transferred to other ships after gaining experience.
While it looks similar to the Federation Forces' Clop-class warships, appearance is the only similarity.

An all-purpose warship with a wide range of uses, from a training ship for teams with little live combat experience to a carrier for mercenary teams to get from one battlefield to another.
Equipped with Anti-Air Autocannons, missiles, and linked Beam Cannons all over, it has plenty of firepower in addition to mobility and Mobile Suit storage capacity, making it a well-balanced all-rounded ship for many situations.
However, it has an undeniably low performance relative to standard warships, and due to this, it is often treated as a stopgap ship to be used only until a better option is available.
It uses a wide variety of technology in all aspects, but its relationship with the military and the story behind its development are unclear.
